subject: Republican Party, Congressional Election, New York
description: A political campaign button for William E. Miller's run for the U.S. House of Representatives. Miller represented New York's 40th and 42nd districts and later served as the Republican Vice Presidential nominee in 1964.

name: Save the babies ; I helped - Did you?
title: SAVE THE BABIES I HELPED - DID YOU?
subject: infant mortality, public health, welfare
description: A red and white pinback button with a central image of a baby's face. It was likely produced for one of the various 'Save the Babies' campaigns prominent in the early 20th century, which aimed to educate the public and reduce infant mortality rates through better sanitation and healthcare.

name: Goldwater ; MIller
title: GOLDWATER MILLER
subject: Political Campaign, Election, Presidency
description: A red button with white text saying 'GOLDWATER' and 'MILLER' separated by a white horizontal band with blue stars, used for the 1964 US presidential campaign of Barry Goldwater and William E. Miller.

name: Carter! ; '76
title: Carter! '76

The button features a portrait of the two men with "FREIHEITSGRUPPE" (Freedom Group) in German written above them on a blue banner, likely intended for German-American voters.

name: LBJ in '64
title: LBJ IN '64

name: Deutsch, Amerikanisherverband ; Buffalo, N.Y. ;Deutscher Tag ; Sonntag, Den II. Aug. ; 1912 ; Teutonia Park

The button is organized by the German-American Association (Deutsch-Amerikanischer Verband) and features the horizontal black, white, and red stripes of the German Empire flag.

name: Nixon eats lettuce
title: NIXON EATS LETTUCE
subject: United Farm Workers, Boycott, Richard Nixon
description: A bright green button featuring black text and the eagle logo of the United Farm Workers (UFW). The button was used during the lettuce boycott of the early 1970s to protest President Richard Nixon's administration, which was criticized by the UFW for supporting non-union growers and purchasing non-union lettuce for the military.

name: [yellow missile with lightning bolt through it]

subject: Military, Aviation, World War II

name: [yellow missile with lightning bolt through it]

description: The reverse side of a Kellogg's PEP cereal premium button, featuring the brand logo and identifying the 74th Bombardment Squadron on the rim.

name: Mass War Fund [red cross]

subject: World War II, Red Cross, War Relief
description: A round pinback button for the Massachusetts War Fund, likely from World War II. It features a central red cross on a white square, surrounded by a blue border with the words 'MASS WAR FUND' in white.

name: Reagan - Bush in '80 ; Let's Make America Great Again
title: REAGAN-BUSH in '80 Let's Make America Great Again

The top border is red with white text reading 'REAGAN-BUSH in '80', and the bottom border is blue with white text reading 'Let's Make America Great Again'. Two Republican elephant logos are positioned between the portraits and the outer borders.

name: U for Unity
title: U FOR UNITY

name: Votes for women

subject: Suffrage, Women's Rights, Voting

name: Votes for women

subject: Manufacturing, Advertising, Buttons

title: ABE

This type of button was commonly used for the American Red Cross annual membership drives or 'roll calls' following World War I.

name: Choice
title: CHOICE
subject: abortion, reproductive rights, activism
description: A round white button with the word "CHOICE" printed in purple serif text, enclosed by an orange border. This button is an artifact of the pro-choice movement in the United States, which grew significantly in visibility following the 1973 Roe v. Wade decision and saw major demonstrations in the late 1980s and early 1990s.

name: Bobby come lately
title: BOBBY COME LATELY
subject: Robert F. Kennedy, 1968 Election, Political Criticism
description: A white political button with black text reading "BOBBY COME LATELY". This slogan was used to criticize Robert F. Kennedy for entering the 1968 presidential race only after Senator Eugene McCarthy's strong showing in the New Hampshire primary proved President Lyndon B. Johnson was vulnerable.

name: Business is good

subject: Business, Economics, Boosterism
description: A small round button with a dark blue background and white block text that reads "BUSINESS IS GOOD". This type of button was commonly used for commercial boosterism or during periods of economic recovery to promote confidence in the market.

description: A promotional button for H. Leach Machinery Co. based in Providence, Rhode Island. The inclusion of the postal zone number '4' indicates the button was produced between 1943, when postal zones were introduced, and 1963, when the ZIP code system was established.

name: Stevenson ; Kefauver

name: Dewey
title: DEWEY
subject: Presidential campaign, Thomas E. Dewey, Republican Party

name: [black triangle on white background]

description: White button with an inverted black triangle. This symbol was reclaimed by lesbians in the 1970s and 1980s from its original use in Nazi concentration camps to label "asocial" individuals.

name: PEW [person with gas mask sitting next to a factory and a polluted body of water]

description: A button featuring a parody of Rodin's 'The Thinker' wearing a gas mask while seated in a polluted landscape of industrial smoke and litter. The word 'PEW' appears at the bottom, likely as a pun on the smell of pollution or an acronym for an environmental group.

name: Pell
title: PELL

name: MIA
title: MIA
subject: Vietnam War, Missing In Action, Activism
description: A round black button featuring the letters "MIA" in yellow. This acronym stands for Missing in Action, and buttons like this were frequently worn during the Vietnam War to show support for families of missing military personnel.

name: Titus

name: H2O

subject: War Bonds, WWII, Savings

title: The Mark of Quality
subject: Labor Unions, Printing, Advertising
description: A circular lithographed metal tab with a rectangular attachment, featuring the slogan 'The Mark of Quality' alongside an illustration of a duck. The tab is covered in a pattern of the Allied Printing Trades Council union label for Rochester, N.Y.

name: Thanksgiving Day ; Nov. 5
title: THANKSGIVING DAY NOV. 5
subject: Thanksgiving, FDR, Victory
description: An orange celluloid button with black text commemorating Franklin D. Roosevelt's landslide victory in the 1936 presidential election. Because the election was held on Tuesday, November 3, 1936, supporters of FDR issued these buttons suggesting that the following Thursday, November 5, be celebrated as a day of thanksgiving for his win.

name: Brewster
title: Brewster
subject: Presidential Campaign; Favorite Son; Maryland Politics
description: A blue and gold political campaign button featuring the name 'Brewster'. This button was used during Senator Daniel Brewster's 1964 'favorite son' campaign for President in the Maryland Democratic primary.

name: Brewster

name: WIN
title: WIN

name: Wood
title: WOOD

description: Round campaign button for Leonard Wood's 1920 presidential bid, featuring the name 'WOOD' in white block letters on a blue center, surrounded by a thick red border.

name: Earl

description: A circular red political campaign button featuring the name 'EARL' in bold white capital letters, used for the gubernatorial campaigns of Earl Warren in California.

The organization used this name from 1928 until 1937 when it became the Painting and Decorating Contractors of America (PDCA). The logo features the organization's initials and a folding ruler, which is a common tool for painters and decorators.

title: FIGHT AIDS OUR REAL ENEMY

description: A blue and yellow button with the text 'FIGHT AIDS OUR REAL ENEMY'. The top half of the button is blue with yellow block letters and the bottom half is yellow with blue italic letters.

name: Fight AIDS Not People With AIDS
title: FIGHT AIDS NOT PEOPLE WITH AIDS
subject: AIDS, Advocacy, Health
description: A round pinback button split horizontally with a yellow top and purple bottom. The text reads "FIGHT AIDS" in purple on the yellow background and "NOT PEOPLE WITH AIDS" in yellow on the purple background.

name: Act Up
title: ACT UP
subject: AIDS Activism, LGBTQ Rights, Health Policy
description: A white button with the words 'ACT UP' in white block letters inside a black rectangular box. ACT UP (AIDS Coalition to Unleash Power) is an international grassroots political group working to end the AIDS pandemic.

name: Women + AIDS ; AIDS Action Now!
title: WOMEN + AIDS AIDS ACTION NOW!
subject: AIDS, Women's health, Activism
description: Circular button for the Toronto-based group AIDS Action Now!, featuring text "WOMEN + AIDS" and "AIDS ACTION NOW!" with an image of two hands interlocking in solidarity.

name: Ask Not What A Lesbian Can Do For You But What You Can Do For A Lesbian
title: ASK NOT WHAT A LESBIAN CAN DO FOR YOU BUT WHAT YOU CAN DO FOR A LESBIAN
subject: Lesbianism, LGBTQ rights, Activism
description: A pink round button with black text featuring a parody of John F. Kennedy's 1961 inaugural address, 'Ask not what your country can do for you – ask what you can do for your country,' adapted for lesbian activism and visibility.

name: How Dare You Presume I'm Heterosexual!
title: HOW DARE YOU PRESUME I'M HETEROSEXUAL!
subject: LGBTQ+, Sexual Orientation, Activism
description: A circular pin-back button with a light background featuring the phrase 'HOW DARE YOU PRESUME I'M HETEROSEXUAL!' in black text, with the word 'DARE' highlighted in red. The button challenges heteronormativity and expresses pride in non-heterosexual identities.

name: Don't Believe The Lie The HIV Crisis Is Not Over. ; Stop AIDS
title: DON'T BELIEVE THE LIE The HIV crisis is not over. STOP AIDS
subject: HIV/AIDS, Activism, Public Health
description: A black button with white text that reads "DON'T BELIEVE THE LIE" and "The HIV crisis is not over." It features a graphic element of a stop sign containing a city skyline and the words "STOP AIDS" with a paperclip graphic, which is the logo for the STOP AIDS Project.

name: I [Heart] Being Gay
title: I [heart] BEING GAY
subject: LGBTQ, Gay Pride, Identity
description: A round white pin-back button with the message 'I [red heart symbol] BEING GAY' in black serif font. This design is a variation of the famous 'I Love NY' logo designed by Milton Glaser in 1977.

name: Pass A.B. 1 (Assembly Bill) ; The Agnos Gay Rights Bill
title: PASS A.B.1 The Agnos Gay Rights Bill
subject: Gay rights, Legislation, California
description: A green button with yellow text advocating for the passage of California Assembly Bill 1 (AB 1), a gay rights bill authored by Art Agnos to prohibit employment discrimination.

name: The Names Project
title: THE NAMES PROJECT
subject: AIDS, Memorial, Quilt
description: A button for The NAMES Project, an organization founded in 1987 in San Francisco to create a memorial for people who died of AIDS-related causes, which became the AIDS Memorial Quilt.

name: I'm One Too
title: I'M ONE TOO
subject: LGBTQ+ rights, social movements, visibility
description: A circular pink button with the text 'I'M ONE TOO' in white capital letters. This slogan was widely used in the LGBTQ+ rights movement during the late 20th century to promote visibility and challenge social stigma by indicating that someone in any group or family could be gay.

name: Doing it! ; Lesbian And Gay Liberation In The '80s
title: Doing It! LESBIAN AND GAY LIBERATION IN THE '80s
subject: LGBTQ+ Rights, Activism, Liberation
description: A pink pinback button produced for the 1980 "Doing It! Lesbian and Gay Liberation in the '80s" conference in Milwaukee. It features a large inverted triangle design made of smaller black and pink triangles.

name: Silencio = Muerte (Silence = Death)
title: SILENCIO=MUERTE
subject: AIDS, activism, LGBTQ
description: Black button with a pink triangle and the white text "SILENCIO=MUERTE", which is Spanish for "Silence=Death". This slogan and imagery were widely used by the AIDS Coalition to Unleash Power (ACT UP) to advocate for political and social change during the HIV/AIDS epidemic.

name: Lavender Left
title: LAVENDER LEFT
subject: LGBTQ activism, socialism, social movements
description: A circular purple button featuring a pink inverted triangle with a red star inside, and the words 'LAVENDER LEFT' printed in white along the bottom edge. This button represents the Lavender Left, a caucus of lesbian and gay socialist and communist activists active in the late 1970s and early 1980s.

name: [Pink and Yellow Triangle Identifying Queer, Jewish Men]
subject: LGBTQ+, Jewish identity, Pride
description: A round white button featuring a pink downward-pointing triangle superimposed over a yellow upward-pointing triangle, forming a six-pointed star. This symbol reclaims the badges used in Nazi concentration camps to identify Jewish gay men and is used as a symbol of identity, remembrance, and LGBTQ+ pride.

name: [Pink Triangle With Fist]
title: None
subject: LGBTQ+ Rights, Activism, Resistance
description: A round black button featuring a pink inverted triangle with a white fist inside it. This is a symbol of LGBTQ+ pride and resistance, reclaiming the pink triangle used in concentration camps and combining it with the fist symbol of power and struggle.

name: [Three Interlocking Female Symbols]
title: Three Interlocking Venus Symbols
subject: Feminism, LGBTQ+, Lesbianism
description: An oval red button with three interlocking white Venus symbols, a symbol often associated with lesbian feminism and women's solidarity.

name: Mother Nature Is A Lesbian
title: Mother Nature is a Lesbian
subject: Lesbianism, Ecofeminism, Environmentalism
description: A pin-back button featuring the slogan 'Mother Nature is a Lesbian' in green text along the bottom edge, below a green silhouette of a wooded landscape. This slogan was a popular expression of ecofeminism and lesbian pride in the 1970s.

name: Ban The Marriage of Church and State ; LGBT Rights Now!
title: worldcantwait.org BAN THE MARRIAGE OF CHURCH AND STATE LGBT RIGHTS NOW!
subject: LGBT Rights, Church and State, Activism
description: This button was produced by the activist organization World Can't Wait. It features the organization's website along with slogans advocating for LGBT rights and the separation of church and state.

name: Gays And Lesbians for Hillary For U.S. Senate
title: GAYS and LESBIANS for Hillary For U.S. Senate
subject: LGBTQ+ rights, Election support, Hillary Clinton
description: A pink campaign button with blue text and an American flag icon supporting Hillary Clinton's 2000 run for the U.S. Senate in New York, explicitly appealing to the gay and lesbian community.

name: Hillary For U.S. Senate
title: Hillary For U.S. Senate
subject: LGBTQ+, Senate, Politics
description: A campaign button for Hillary Clinton's 2000 U.S. Senate run in New York. The design features a pink triangle, a symbol of the LGBTQ+ rights movement, suggesting it was aimed at mobilizing gay and lesbian voters.

name: Straight But Not Narrow
title: Straight But not Narrow
subject: LGBTQ+ allies, pink triangle, activism
description: A button featuring the slogan 'Straight But not Narrow' in black text over a pink triangle on a white background. The phrase and the reclaimed pink triangle symbol signify heterosexual support for the LGBTQ+ community and a rejection of homophobia.

name: Straight But Not Narrow ; Vote No On 9
title: Straight But not Narrow Vote NO on 9
subject: LGBTQ+ Rights, Allyship, Oregon Ballot Measure 9
description: This button was produced for the 1992 campaign against Oregon Ballot Measure 9. The measure, which was ultimately defeated, sought to amend the Oregon Constitution to declare homosexuality 'abnormal, wrong, unnatural, and perverse' and would have prohibited state spending on gay rights. The slogan 'Straight But Not Narrow' identifies the wearer as a heterosexual supporter of LGBTQ+ rights.

name: "Stonewall" 20 Yrs. ; 1969-1999 ; N.Y.C.
title: "STONEWALL 20 YRS." 1969-1989 N.Y.C.
subject: Stonewall, LGBTQ, Anniversary
description: This button commemorates the 20th anniversary of the Stonewall Riots. It features a graphic design with a pink triangle and a rainbow flag overlaid on a brick wall background. The text includes the event's name, the duration of the anniversary (20 years), the corresponding dates (1969-1989), and the location (N.Y.C.).

name: Stonewall 10 ; 1969-1979 ; CSLDC (The Christopher Street Liberation Day Committee)
title: CSLDC STONEWALL 10 1969 1979
subject: LGBTQ+, Stonewall, Anniversary
description: A button produced by the Christopher Street Liberation Day Committee (CSLDC) to commemorate the 10th anniversary of the 1969 Stonewall Riots. The button features a pink triangle, which was reclaimed as a symbol of LGBTQ+ liberation in the 1970s.

name: Stonewall 25 ; New York City ; 6-26-94
title: Stonewall 25 new york city 6-26-94
subject: LGBTQ rights, Stonewall riots, anniversary
description: A white button featuring a pink inverted triangle in the center. The text 'Stonewall 25' is written in black script across and within the triangle. Along the lower right edge of the triangle, the text 'new york city' and the date '6-26-94' are also written in black script. The button commemorates the 25th anniversary of the Stonewall Riots.

name: Stonewall Means Fight Back! ; Smash Lesbian & Gay Oppression
title: STONEWALL MEANS FIGHT BACK! SMASH LESBIAN & GAY OPPRESSION
subject: LGBTQ+ rights, Stonewall Riots, activism
description: A circular button with a lavender border and a black center. It features two raised fists in lavender. The text in the center reads 'STONEWALL MEANS FIGHT BACK!' and the text on the border reads 'STONEWALL' and 'SMASH LESBIAN & GAY OPPRESSION'.

name: Stop the OCA (Oregon Citizens Alliance)
title: STOP THE OCA
subject: LGBTQ rights, Oregon Citizens Alliance, activism
description: A political button opposing the Oregon Citizens Alliance (OCA), a social conservative political organization that sponsored several anti-gay initiatives in Oregon during the late 1980s and early 1990s.

name: ["No" Symbol Over OCA] (Oregon Citizens Alliance)
title: OCA
subject: LGBTQ Rights, Ballot Measures, Oregon Citizens Alliance
description: This button features the acronym 'OCA' with a red 'no' symbol (a circle and slash) over it, representing opposition to the Oregon Citizens Alliance. The OCA was a conservative Christian political organization in Oregon known for its anti-gay ballot initiatives in the early 1990s, such as Measure 9 in 1992.

name: Don't Make Discrimination A Consitutional Requirement ; Fight The OCA (Oregon Citizens Alliance)
title: DON'T MAKE DISCRIMINATION A CONSTITUTIONAL REQUIREMENT FIGHT THE OCA
subject: LGBTQ+ Rights, Oregon Citizens Alliance, Anti-Discrimination
description: This pink button with white text advocates against the Oregon Citizens Alliance (OCA), an organization known for sponsoring anti-gay ballot measures in Oregon during the late 1980s and early 1990s, most notably 1992's Ballot Measure 9.

name: [Female and Peace Symbol]
title: Interlocking Female and Peace Symbols
subject: feminism, peace movement, activism
description: A circular purple button featuring two interlocking white symbols: a female gender symbol (Venus) and a peace symbol, representing the overlap of feminism and the peace movement.

name: Dykes In The Street
title: DYKES IN THE STREETS
subject: Lesbian rights, Activism, LGBTQ+ pride
description: A pin featuring the text 'DYKES IN THE STREETS' in pink on a black background, with three interlocking pink Venus symbols and a silver lightning bolt over them.

name: No New Abortion Law ; Choice [Woman's Symbol in letter C]
title: NO NEW ABORTION LAW CHOICE
subject: Abortion rights, Women's rights, Reproductive rights
description: A pink pro-choice button with a central white triangle. The text 'NO NEW ABORTION LAW' arches across the top, while 'CHOICE' is written in the center with a female Venus symbol integrated into the letter 'C'.

name: Choice [Woman's Symbol in letter C]
title: CHOICE
subject: Reproductive Rights, Feminism, LGBTQ+ Advocacy
description: A square button featuring the word 'CHOICE' with a female symbol as the 'C', overlaid on a pink triangle. This design signifies the intersection of reproductive rights and lesbian/feminist activism, likely produced during the pro-choice movements of the late 1980s or early 1990s.

name: CBST (Congregation Beit Simchat Torah)
title: CBST
subject: LGBTQ rights, Judaism, Synagogues
description: A button representing Congregation Beth Simchat Torah (CBST), New York's LGBTQ synagogue. It features the acronym 'CBST' alongside a Star of David and a pink triangle, combining Jewish and LGBTQ symbols.

name: We Are Everywhere
title: WE ARE EVERYWHERE
subject: Lesbianism, Visibility, Activism
description: A blue button featuring the slogan 'WE ARE EVERYWHERE' above an outline of the United States map with an overlaid interlocking double Venus symbol, representing lesbian visibility and identity during the gay liberation movement.

name: Demonstrate April 15 Washington DC ; Overturn Bakke Fight Discrimination ; Medical Committee for Human Rights
title: Demonstrate April 15 Washington DC OVERTURN BAKKE FIGHT DISCRIMINATION Medical Committee for Human Rights
subject: Affirmative Action, Civil Rights, Supreme Court
description: Yellow button with black text advocating for a protest in Washington, D.C., on April 15, 1978, to overturn the Regents of the University of California v. Bakke Supreme Court decision regarding affirmative action. The button features the Medical Committee for Human Rights logo.

name: Christopher Street Gay Pride 72
title: CHRISTOPHER STREET GAY PRIDE '72
subject: LGBTQ+ Pride, Christopher Street, Gay Liberation
description: A purple circular button with white text along the border reading 'CHRISTOPHER STREET GAY PRIDE '72' and interlocking female and male gender symbols in the center.

name: Gay Pride ; Toronto  '85 ; Coming Together
title: GAY PRIDE TORONTO '85 COMING TOGETHER
subject: Gay Pride, LGBTQ, Toronto
description: A yellow circular button produced for the 1985 Gay Pride celebration in Toronto, Canada. The design features the theme 'Coming Together' and an illustration of a 3D pink triangle, which is a historical symbol of the gay rights movement.

name: Lesbian & Gay Liberation Through Socialist Revolution!
title: Lesbian & Gay Liberation Through Socialist Revolution !
subject: LGBTQ+ Rights, Socialism, Gay Liberation
description: A white pinback button featuring the text 'Lesbian & Gay Liberation Through Socialist Revolution!' above an illustration of a pink inverted triangle containing a black raised fist with a star on the wrist.

name: No More Shit ; Stop Police Raids & Harrassment Now!
title: NO MORE SHIT STOP POLICE RAIDS & HARASSMENT NOW! NO MORE SHIT
subject: Police Brutality, Activism, Civil Rights
description: A protest button featuring a red stop sign with the text 'STOP POLICE RAIDS & HARASSMENT NOW!' surrounded by the phrase 'NO MORE SHIT'. This button is likely associated with the early gay liberation movement following the Stonewall Riots in 1969.

name: Rightfully Proud ; Toronto 1987 ; Lesbian And Gay Pride Day
title: Rightfully Proud Toronto Lesbian and Gay Pride Day 1987
subject: LGBTQ+, Pride, Toronto
description: A white button commemorating the 1987 Toronto Lesbian and Gay Pride Day. It features the slogan 'Rightfully Proud' in black and purple script over an inverted pink triangle, a historical symbol of the LGBTQ+ community.

name: AIDS LA ; Let's Face It.
title: AIDS Let's Face It, LA
subject: AIDS awareness, LGBTQ health, Public health
description: A button from the Los Angeles 'Let's Face It' AIDS awareness campaign launched in 1988. The design features purple text and teal graphic panels depicting silhouettes of faces.

name: Overturn the Reagan Progam  ; Oct. 16-18 ; All-People's Congress ; Stop The Family Protection Act ; Lesbian & Gay Focus ; People's Anti-War Mobilization
title: Overturn the Reagan Program Oct. 16-18 All-People's Congress STOP THE Family Protection Act Lesbian & Gay Focus People's Anti-War Mobilization
subject: LGBTQ Rights;Ronald Reagan;Family Protection Act
description: This button was produced for the All-People's Congress held in Detroit from October 16-18, 1981. The button advocates for the overturning of Ronald Reagan's programs and specifically calls for the defeat of the Family Protection Act, a conservative piece of legislation introduced in 1981. It features a pink triangle, a symbol of gay rights, and was organized by the People's Anti-War Mobilization, an organization associated with the Workers World Party.

name: Out Loud + Proud ; New Yokr's Lesbian & Gary Pride ; June 29-30, 1991
title: OUT LOUD + PROUD new york's lesbian + gay pride june 29-30, 1991
subject: Pride, LGBTQ+, New York City
description: A white button with black and pink graphics featuring the theme "OUT LOUD + PROUD" for the 1991 New York City Lesbian and Gay Pride event. It includes a pink triangle outline and the dates June 29-30, 1991.

name: Forward Together ; Gay & Lesbian Pride Day ; June 29, 1986 ; New York City
title: forward together 86 gay & lesbian pride day june 29, 1986 new york city
subject: LGBTQ pride, activism, New York City
description: A circular white button with pink and blue text and illustrations of footprints. The text reads 'forward together 86 gay & lesbian pride day june 29, 1986 new york city'.

name: Gay/Lesbian ; A Heritage of Pride ; CSLDC '82 (Christopher Street Liberation Day Committee)
title: GAY / LESBIAN A Heritage of Pride CSLDC '82
subject: LGBTQ+ Pride, NYC Pride, Gay Rights
description: This pink circular button features the text "GAY / LESBIAN A Heritage of Pride" over an inverted white triangle outline. The acronym "CSLDC '82" at the bottom stands for the Christopher Street Liberation Day Committee, which was the organization responsible for planning the New York City Pride march in 1982.

name: Lesbian & Gay Pride Day '82
title: LESBIAN & GAY PRIDE DAY '82
subject: LGBTQ+, Pride, 1982
description: A button for Lesbian & Gay Pride Day in 1982, featuring blue text and red silhouettes of a crowd of people holding up balloons.

name: Gay People Unite or Die!
title: GAY PEOPLE UNITE OR DIE!
subject: Gay Liberation, LGBTQ+ Rights, Activism
description: A lavender-colored button with black text reading "GAY PEOPLE UNITE OR DIE!" encircling a segmented snake graphic, a parody of Benjamin Franklin's "Join, or Die" political cartoon, likely produced during the early gay liberation movement.

name: Coming Out Strong ; Lesbin Pride Week ; Jun 21-27, 1981
title: Coming Out Strong! Lesbian Pride Week JUN 21-27 1981
subject: Lesbian, Pride, LGBTQ+
description: A circular purple button with white stylized text reading "Coming Out Strong! Lesbian Pride Week" and the dates "JUN 21-27 1981" alongside a double female (venus) symbol.

name: Lesbian And Gay Pride Day '84
title: LESBIAN AND GAY PRIDE DAY '84
subject: Pride, Lesbian, Gay
description: A circular pinback button featuring a yellow background with a faint orange grid pattern. Dark green text in a sans-serif font reads "LESBIAN AND GAY PRIDE DAY '84".

name: Pride = Power ; New York City Gay - Lesbian Pride ; June 27-28, 1992
title: PRIDE = POWER NEW YORK CITY GAY · LESBIAN PRIDE JUNE 27-28, 1992
subject: LGBTQ+, Pride, Activism
description: A button for the 1992 New York City Gay and Lesbian Pride event featuring the slogan 'Pride = Power' repeated in a stacked design behind a pink triangle.

name: Stonewall 69 ; Gay Pride 79
title: STONEWALL 69 GAY PRIDE 79
subject: LGBTQ+ rights, Stonewall Riots, Gay Pride
description: A circular button with a purple and pink geometric starburst pattern. The text commemorates the 10th anniversary of the 1969 Stonewall Uprising, marking Gay Pride in 1979.

name: New York Can ; A Grassroots Project of Gay Men's Health Crisis
title: NEW YORK CAN A GRASSROOTS PROJECT OF GAY MEN'S HEALTH CRISIS
subject: HIV/AIDS, healthcare advocacy, LGBTQ+ rights
description: A square white button with black and red text supporting the City Action Network (CAN), a grassroots advocacy project of the Gay Men's Health Crisis (GMHC) in New York City. The organization was founded in 1988 to advocate for AIDS-related legislation and funding.

name: A More Human Approach to HIV Testing ; David Geffen Center ; GMHC (Gay Men's Health Crisis)
title: A MORE HUMAN APPROACH TO HIV TESTING DAVID GEFFEN CENTER GMHC 212/367-1100
subject: HIV testing, public health, GMHC
description: A square button promoting HIV testing services at the David Geffen Center of the Gay Men's Health Crisis (GMHC) in New York City. The button features a pink coneflower graphic and includes a 212 area code phone number. The David Geffen Center was dedicated in 1997.

name: Act Up ; Fight Back Fight AIDS
title: ACT UP FIGHT BACK FIGHT AIDS
subject: HIV/AIDS, Activism, LGBTQRA
description: A square button for the AIDS Coalition to Unleash Power (ACT UP), an international grass-roots political group working to end the AIDS pandemic. The slogan 'ACT UP FIGHT BACK FIGHT AIDS' was central to the group's protests and advocacy efforts starting in the late 1980s.

name: Stonewall ; 25 ; June 26 1994
title: STONEWALL 25 JUNE 26 1994
subject: LGBTQ+, Stonewall, Anniversary
description: This square button commemorates the 25th anniversary of the Stonewall Riots, an event often cited as the catalyst for the modern LGBTQ+ rights movement. The button features a purple downward-pointing triangle—a symbol once used to identify gay men in Nazi concentration camps and later reclaimed by the community—containing a globe and the number '25'. This central design is encircled by a green laurel wreath, representing victory and honor. The date June 26, 1994, marks the international march on the United Nations in New York City.

name: Gay Lesbian Vote 88
title: GAY LESBIAN VOTE 88
subject: LGBTQ, Voting, Election
description: A square button advocating for gay and lesbian participation in the 1988 elections. It features a pink triangle interlocked with a blue outline of the United States map between the text 'GAY LESBIAN' and 'VOTE 88'.

name: Stonewall 20 ; A Generation of Pride ; New York City Lesbian & Gay Pride Weekend ; June 24-25, 1989
title: Stonewall 20 A Generation of Pride New York City Lesbian & Gay Pride Weekend June 24-25, 1989
subject: Stonewall Riots, LGBTQ+ Pride, 20th Anniversary
description: A square commemorative button for the 20th anniversary of the Stonewall Uprising, produced for the New York City Lesbian and Gay Pride Weekend held on June 24-25, 1989. The design features a pink inverted triangle and teal script text.

name: Unity 94
title: Unity 94
subject: journalism, diversity, conventions
description: A square button for Unity '94, the first joint convention of minority journalist associations, held in Atlanta, Georgia, in 1994.

name: Safer Sex ; Keep It Up!
title: Keep It Up! safer sex
subject: Safe sex, HIV/AIDS, Health promotion
description: A square blue pin-back button featuring the slogan 'Keep It Up!' in large black letters over a repeating background of the phrase 'safer sex' in white.

name: National Coming Out Day ; october 11
title: NATIONAL COMING OUT DAY... OCTOBER 11
subject: LGBTQ+, National Coming Out Day, Keith Haring
description: Square white button with black ink illustration by artist Keith Haring of a stylized figure stepping out of a dark doorway into the light. Text at bottom reads "NATIONAL COMING OUT DAY... OCTOBER 11".

name: Heritage of Pride
title: © K. Haring
subject: LGBTQ+ Rights, Pop Art, Keith Haring
description: A square button featuring Keith Haring's artwork of four colorful dancing figures with heads shaped like interconnected male and female gender symbols on a bright yellow background.

name: [American Flag with Pink Triangle]
title: Pink Triangle on US Flag
subject: LGBTQ+, Patriotism, Activism
description: A square button depicting the American flag with a pink triangle, an established symbol for LGBTQ+ rights and visibility, placed within the field of stars.

name: AIDS 10 Years ; Remember Respond Resolve ; GMHC (Gay Men's Health Crisis)
title: AIDS 10 YEARS REMEMBER RESPOND RESOLVE GMHC
subject: AIDS, health, anniversary
description: A white square button featuring black and yellow blocks with the text 'AIDS 10 YEARS', 'REMEMBER', 'RESPOND', and 'RESOLVE'. The Gay Men's Health Crisis (GMHC) logo is at the bottom.

name: Stop Discirmination ; End The Military Ban
title: STOP DISCRIMINATION END THE MILITARY BAN
subject: LGBTQ Rights, Military Service, Discrimination
description: An octagonal red button in the shape of a stop sign, featuring the text "STOP DISCRIMINATION END THE MILITARY BAN" in white and blue. The button was likely created in the early 1990s during the push to overturn the ban on gay and lesbian individuals serving in the U.S. military.

name: I Helped Make Lesbian, Bi & Gay History ; April 25, 1993 ; Washington D.C.
title: I HELPED MAKE LESBIAN, BI & GAY HISTORY APRIL 25, 1993 WASHINGTON D.C.
subject: LGBT rights, March on Washington, Pride
description: An inverted triangular button with rainbow stripes and a pink triangle at the base. The text commemorates the March on Washington for Lesbian, Gay, and Bi Equal Rights and Liberation held on April 25, 1993.

name: It AIn't Over Yet! ; Help Fight Aids ; GMHC (Gay Men's Health Crisis)
title: GMHC It Ain't Over Yet! HELP FIGHT AIDS
subject: AIDS, HIV/AIDS, healthcare advocacy
description: Rectangular button with rounded edges. The top half is white with the text 'GMHC' and 'It Ain't Over Yet!' in grey. A jagged red line separates the top from the bottom half, which is grey with white text reading 'HELP FIGHT AIDS'.

name: Stonewall 25 ; Keep On Marching! ; June 26, 1994
title: STONEWALL 25 Keep On Marching! June 26, 1994
subject: LGBTQ+ rights, Stonewall Riots, Activism
description: A rectangular black button with silver text commemorating the 25th anniversary of the Stonewall riots. It includes the date June 26, 1994, which was the day of a massive march in New York City for lesbian and gay rights.

name: Queens Lesbian & Gay Pride ; Pride Without Borders; Sunday, June 2nd ; '96
title: QUEENS LESBIAN & GAY PRIDE Pride Without Borders '96 SUNDAY, JUNE 2ND
subject: LGBTQ+ pride, Queens Pride, Pride Without Borders
description: A rectangular button for the 1996 Queens Lesbian & Gay Pride celebration held on June 2nd. The theme is 'Pride Without Borders,' and the design features a pink world map over a blue background with a pink triangle symbol.

name: Equality Begins At Home ; March 21-27, 1999 ; National Gay and Lesbian Task Force ; Federation of LGBT Statewide Political Organizations
title: MARCH 21-27, 1999 equality begins at home National Gay and Lesbian Task Force Federation of LGBT Statewide Political Organizations
subject: LGBT rights, equality, activism
description: A rectangular white button with red and blue text and a star graphic promoting the 'Equality Begins at Home' campaign from March 21-27, 1999. The campaign was a nationwide grassroots initiative organized by the National Gay and Lesbian Task Force and the Federation of LGBT Statewide Political Organizations to advocate for LGBTQ civil rights at the state and local levels.

name: AIDS Cure Now ; Time to Act Up
title: AIDS CURE NOW TIME TO ACT UP
subject: AIDS activism, ACT UP, healthcare
description: A rectangular activist button for ACT UP (AIDS Coalition to Unleash Power). The top half is white with the text 'AIDS CURE NOW' above a graphic of a time bomb. The bottom half is red with the text 'TIME TO ACT UP'. The button reflects the urgency of the AIDS crisis in the late 1980s and early 1990s.

name: Never Again ; Fight Back ; Gays & Lesbians Against The Right Everyw
title: NEVER AGAIN FIGHT BACK! GAYS & LESBIANS AGAINST THE RIGHT EVERYWHERE

description: A rectangular pink button featuring a pink triangle, a person protesting, and the text 'NEVER AGAIN' and 'FIGHT BACK!'. It includes a banner reading 'GAYS & LESBIANS AGAINST THE RIGHT EVERYWHERE' (GLARE), a San Francisco-based activist group formed in 1978 to combat anti-LGBTQ initiatives and right-wing movements.

name: Silent No More
title: SILENT NO MORE
subject: LGBTQ+ Rights; Activism; HIV/AIDS
description: A rectangular button with a black background featuring an upright pink triangle and the text 'SILENT NO MORE' in white. The pink triangle, once used in Nazi concentration camps to identify gay men, was reclaimed as a symbol of LGBTQ+ pride and resistance, and became particularly prominent in activism during the HIV/AIDS crisis of the 1980s.

name: Lesbian & Gay Pride '83
title: LESBIAN & GAY PRIDE DAY '83

description: A rectangular button with rounded corners. The top half features a graphic of a rainbow over a green field inside a square. The bottom half contains the text 'LESBIAN & GAY PRIDE DAY '83' in white font on a light background.

title: LET MY PEOPLE GO! שלח את עמי
subject: Soviet Jewry movement, Human Rights, Emigration
description: A white button with red text that reads "LET MY PEOPLE GO!" along the top left and the same message in Hebrew on the right. The center graphic depicts St. Basil's Cathedral in Moscow behind a red five-pointed Soviet star. Inside the star, a yellow Star of David is shown behind prison bars, symbolizing the restriction of Jewish emigration from the Soviet Union.

name: Freedom For Soviet Jewry ; ??????
title: FREEDOM FOR SOVIET JEWRY СВОБОДА
subject: Soviet Jewry movement, human rights, emigration
description: A white button with red text that reads 'FREEDOM FOR SOVIET JEWRY' and the Russian word for freedom, 'СВОБОДА'. It features a red Star of David in the center. The button is part of the movement to support the rights of Jews in the Soviet Union to practice their religion and emigrate.

name: "Enough Of Blood And Tears, Enough." ; Prime Minister of Israel Yitzhak Rabin
title: "Enough of blood and tears, enough." Prime Minister of Israel Yitzhak Rabin
subject: Peace, Israel, Diplomacy
description: A button featuring a quote from Israeli Prime Minister Yitzhak Rabin's speech at the signing of the Oslo Accords in 1993. The text is light blue on a white background, accompanied by a light blue outline of a dove with an olive branch.

name: Victory
title: VICTORY
subject: Victory, Peace, Protest
description: A blue and white button featuring the word "VICTORY" above stylized hands making various gestures, including the V for victory sign, likely associated with mid-20th-century social or political activism.

name: No Land For Peace
title: NO LAND FOR PEACE
subject: Israel, Peace Process, Zionism
description: A yellow button featuring a map of Israel including the West Bank and Gaza Strip with the text 'NO LAND FOR PEACE'. This button advocates against territorial concessions in exchange for peace with Arab neighbors, a prominent stance of the Israeli right wing during the 1990s peace process era.

name: No Land For Peace
title: NYSUT Vote Yes
subject: Education, Unions, Voting
description: The reverse side of a circular button produced for the New York State United Teachers (NYSUT). The back features a yellow manufacturer's label from 'Button Brokers' located in Rochester, NY, and includes their phone number and union affiliation with the Sign Display Painters and Allied Trades Local 820. The front of this button (ID 501190044) encourages a 'Yes' vote, typically associated with school budget or bond elections endorsed by the union.

name: Democrats For Israel
title: DEMOCRATS for ISRAEL
subject: Israel, Democratic Party, US-Israel Relations
description: A white button featuring a cartoon donkey (mascot of the Democratic Party) in the center, positioned over the combined images of the United States flag on the left and the Israeli flag on the right. The text 'DEMOCRATS for ISRAEL' arches across the top in black capital letters. A union bug for the IPNW AFL-CIO is visible at the bottom.

name: I Put On B'H Tefillin Today Did You?
title: I PUT ON B'H TEFILLIN TODAY DID YOU?
subject: Judaism, Tefillin, Chabad
description: A yellow pin-back button with black text reading 'I PUT ON B'H TEFILLIN TODAY DID YOU?'. This is part of a campaign started by the Lubavitcher Rebbe in 1967 to encourage Jewish men to wear tefillin.

name: Let My People Go! ; ??? ?? ???
title: LET MY PEOPLE GO
subject: Soviet Jewry movement, Emigration, Human rights
description: Red button featuring a Star of David bound by chains and a padlock with a hammer and sickle, representing the struggle of Soviet Jews to emigrate. Text in English and Hebrew translates to 'Let My People Go.' Produced by NCSY/UOJCA.

name: Save Soviet Jewry
title: SAVE SOVIET JEWRY
subject: Soviet Jewry movement, human rights, religious freedom
description: A white button with red text reading 'SAVE SOVIET JEWRY' and a red illustration of a shofar and a torch. This button was part of the international movement to allow Jews in the Soviet Union to emigrate and escape religious persecution.

name: Free Soviet Jews
title: FREE SOVIET JEWS
subject: Soviet Jewry Movement, Human Rights, Soviet Union
description: This button features a red five-pointed star with a yellow Star of David behind black bars, symbolizing the imprisonment of Jews in the Soviet Union. The text "FREE SOVIET JEWS" is written in yellow around the border.

name: Pro-Israel Republican
title: PRO-ISRAEL REPUBLICAN
subject: Israel-United States relations, Republican Party, Foreign Policy
description: A round white button featuring the text 'PRO-ISRAEL' in red and blue at the top and 'REPUBLICAN' in red at the bottom. The center contains a graphic merging elements of the flags of the United States and Israel.

name: Pro-Israel ; Pro-Peace
title: PRO-ISRAEL PRO-PEACE
subject: Israel, Peace, Advocacy
description: A rectangular button with a black background featuring the text 'PRO-ISRAEL PRO-PEACE' in white. A teal arrow points upwards and to the right behind the text. This is the slogan of J Street, a non-profit liberal advocacy group based in the United States.

name: Are We "The Jews Of Silence" ? ; Student Struggle For Soveit Jewry
title: ARE WE "The Jews of Silence" ? STUDENT STRUGGLE FOR SOVIET JEWRY
subject: Soviet Jewry, Activism, Human Rights
description: A black and white circular button issued by the Student Struggle for Soviet Jewry (SSSJ). The text "ARE WE 'The Jews of Silence'?" references Elie Wiesel's 1966 book about the plight of Jews in the Soviet Union, used as a rallying cry to mobilize American Jews against apathy.

name: United We Stand
title: UNITED WE STAND
subject: Israel, United States, Solidarity
description: A button expressing solidarity between the United States and Israel, featuring the slogan 'UNITED WE STAND' with the Israeli flag design on top and the American flag design on the bottom.

name: Israel Must Live
title: Israel must live
subject: Israel, Zionism, International Relations
description: A black and white pin-back button featuring the slogan "Israel must live." This button was likely produced to show support for the state of Israel during or after major conflicts like the Yom Kippur War.

name: I'm Proud To Be A Zionist!
title: I'M PROUD TO BE A ZIONIST!
subject: Zionism, Jewish Identity, Activism
description: A white pin-back button featuring the text 'I'M PROUD TO BE A ZIONIST!' printed in blue capital letters. The slogan became especially prominent in the mid-1970s following United Nations Resolution 3379.

name: Stop Persecution of Soviet Jew! ; (Remember) ????
title: STOP PERSECUTION OF SOVIET JEWS!
subject: Soviet Jewry movement, Human Rights, Antisemitism
description: A red button featuring the text 'STOP PERSECUTION OF SOVIET JEWS!' in black ink. In the center is a yellow Star of David with the Hebrew word 'זכור' (Zakhor), which means 'Remember,' written in red script. This button was likely used as part of the international campaign to support the rights of Jews in the Soviet Union to practice their religion and emigrate.

name: JFREJ ; Jews For Racial & Economic Justice
title: JFREJ Jews for Racial & Economic Justice
subject: Jewish Activism, Racial Justice, Economic Justice
description: A rectangular button for the organization Jews for Racial & Economic Justice (JFREJ). The button features a purple graphic logo with the acronym 'JFREJ' and the full name of the organization at the bottom in purple text against a white background.

name: Exodus March for Soviet Jewry ; Sunday, April 26th
title: EXODUS MARCH for SOVIET JEWRY SUNDAY, APRIL 26th
subject: Soviet Jewry movement, human rights, protest
description: This button was produced for the Exodus March for Soviet Jewry held in New York City on Sunday, April 26, 1970. The event was a major demonstration organized by the Greater New York Conference on Soviet Jewry to advocate for the right of Jews in the Soviet Union to emigrate, particularly to Israel.

name: Soviety Jewry WIll Be Redeemed ; ?? ????? ?? (Israel Will Live)
title: SOVIET JEWRY WILL BE REDEEMED עם ישראל חי
subject: Soviet Jewry, Human Rights, Emigration
description: A white button with blue text reading 'SOVIET JEWRY WILL BE REDEEMED' surrounding the Hebrew phrase 'Am Yisrael Chai' (The People of Israel Live). It was produced as part of the movement to support the rights of Jews in the Soviet Union to emigrate and escape religious and cultural persecution.

name: Stop Contra Aid
title: STOP CONTRA AID
subject: Contra Aid, Nicaragua, Foreign Policy
description: A red octagonal button shaped like a stop sign. It has "STOP" printed in large white letters, with "CONTRA AID" printed over it in a black stencil-style font at an angle.

name: Stop Contra Aid
title: STOP CONTRA AID
subject: foreign policy, Nicaragua, activism
description: A button featuring a stop-sign graphic with the text 'STOP CONTRA AID,' expressing opposition to United States military or financial support for the Contras in Nicaragua during the 1980s.

name: Just Say No to Contra Aid ; Neighbor To Neighbor
title: JUST SAY NO TO CONTRA AID NEIGHBOR TO NEIGHBOR
subject: Nicaragua, Contra Aid, Foreign Policy
description: This green and white button was produced by Neighbor to Neighbor, a grassroots organization that campaigned against United States military aid to the Contras in Nicaragua during the 1980s.

name: Labor Says: End Contra Aid
title: LABOR SAYS: END CONTRA AID
subject: Labor, Nicaragua, Foreign Aid
description: A political button from the 1980s expressing the labor movement's opposition to United States aid for the Contra rebels in Nicaragua.

name: Stop Reagan's Contra War Against Nicaragua Blood Money
title: STOP REAGANS CONTRA WAR AGAINST NICARAGUA BLOOD MONEY
subject: Nicaragua, Anti-war, Ronald Reagan
description: An anti-war protest button expressing opposition to President Ronald Reagan's support of the Contra rebels in Nicaragua during the 1980s.

name: Peace and Justice in Central America
title: Peace and Justice in Central America
subject: peace, justice, central america
description: A round button with horizontal stripes of purple, green, and blue featuring the text "PEACE and JUSTICE in CENTRAL AMERICA" in cream. This button likely dates to the 1980s, a period of significant social and political activism regarding U.S. foreign policy in Central America.

name: Hands Off Central America
title: HANDS OFF CENTRAL AMERICA
subject: foreign policy, central america, anti-war
description: A red button with yellow and green text that reads "HANDS OFF CENTRAL AMERICA" encircling a green map of Central America. This button represents the 1980s protest movement against United States military intervention in countries like Nicaragua and El Salvador.

name: Central America Week ; No More! ; March 18-25 1984
title: Central America Week NO MORE! March 18-25 1984
subject: Foreign Policy, Central America, Protests
description: Red button with green text reading "Central America Week" and "March 18-25 1984" along the edge. In the center is a green map of Central America with the yellow text "NO MORE!" overlaid. The button likely refers to opposition against United States intervention in Central American conflicts during the 1980s.

name: Free All Latin American Political Prisoners ; USLA (United States Committee for Justice to Latin American Political Prisoners )
title: FREE ALL LATIN AMERICAN POLITICAL PRISONERS USLA
subject: human rights, Latin America, political prisoners
description: A button produced by the United States Committee for Justice to Latin American Political Prisoners (USLA). It features a black and white graphic of a person silenced by a chain and padlock across their mouth, symbolizing the plight of political prisoners. The USLA was an organization active in the late 1960s and 1970s that campaigned against human rights abuses in Latin American countries.

name: Support Sanctuary or Refugees of Central American Wars
title: SUPPORT SANCTUARY FOR REFUGEES OF CENTRAL AMERICAN WARS
subject: Sanctuary movement, Central American wars, Refugee aid
description: Button supporting the Sanctuary movement, which provided safe haven for refugees from civil wars in Central America during the 1980s. Features a red heart on an orange background.

name: U.S. Hands of Central America
title: U.S. HANDS OFF CENTRAL AMERICA
subject: Anti-war, Foreign policy, Latin America
description: A button used by activists protesting against U.S. intervention in Central America during the 1980s. It features a portrait of Nicaraguan revolutionary Augusto Csar Sandino.

name: Hands Off Latin America
title: HANDS OFF LATIN AMERICA
subject: foreign policy, anti-imperialism, Latin America
description: A round pinback button with 'HANDS OFF LATIN AMERICA' printed in cream-colored text over a brown textured background. This slogan was commonly used in protests against United States military and political intervention in Latin American nations during the latter half of the 20th century.

name: U.S. Out of North America ; Social-Revolutionary Anarchist Federation
title: U.S. OUT of North America
subject: Anarchism, Anti-statism, Anti-imperialism
description: A red and white button featuring a map of North America with the text 'U.S. OUT of North America' and 'Social-Revolutionary Anarchist Federation' around the bottom edge.

name: The People's Revolution ; Forward Ever- Backward Never ; Bay Area U.S. Grenada Friendship Society ; Grenada ; March 13, 1979
title: THE PEOPLE'S REVOLUTION GRENADA march 13, 1979 FORWARD EVER-BACKWARD NEVER
subject: Grenada Revolution, New Jewel Movement, International Solidarity
description: A button produced by the Bay Area U.S. Grenada Friendship Society commemorating the Grenadian Revolution of March 13, 1979. It features the slogan 'Forward Ever-Backward Never' and a stylized conch shell, the symbol of the New Jewel Movement led by Maurice Bishop.

name: Archbishop Romero ; March to End the U.S. War in Cenral America ; Washington, D.C. ; March 24, 1990
title: Archbishop Romero March to End the U.S. War in Central America Washington, D.C. March 24, 1990
subject: Anti-War, Central America, Human Rights
description: A black button with white and pink text. The text "Archbishop Romero" is curved along the top edge in white. The center of the button features pink text that reads "March to End the U.S. War in Central America". At the bottom, in white text, it says "Washington, D.C. March 24, 1990".

name: Latin America ; The Caribbean ; Nobody's Backyard
title: LATIN AMERICA THE CARIBBEAN NOBODY'S BACKYARD
subject: anti-interventionism, foreign policy, sovereignty
description: A button featuring a map of the Western Hemisphere with the green text 'LATIN AMERICA THE CARIBBEAN NOBODY'S BACKYARD', expressing opposition to United States intervention and geopolitical dominance in Latin America and the Caribbean.

name: Stop U.S. Intervention in Central America
title: STOP U.S. INTERVENTION In Central America
subject: Anti-war, US Foreign Policy, Central America
description: Circular green button with a white dove and black hands advocating for the end of United States intervention in Central American affairs.

name: Boycott Coco Cola ; Coca-Cola: Killing Colombian Labor
title: Boycott Coca Cola Coca-Cola: Killing Colombian Labor
subject: Labor rights, Boycotts, Colombia
description: This button calls for a boycott of Coca-Cola due to the company's alleged connection to the murder of union leaders in Colombia. It shows a 'Killer Cola' can in a pool of blood and includes the website for New Jersey Solidarity.

name: No Vietnam War in Central America
title: No Vietnam War IN CENTRAL AMERICA
subject: Anti-War, Central America, U.S. Intervention
description: A white button with red text reading "No Vietnam War" and "IN CENTRAL AMERICA". The center features a black silhouette of soldiers marching across a map of Central America, a common image from 1980s protest movements against US involvement in the region.

name: Free the Dominican Political Prisoners ; USLA (United States Committee for Justice to Latin American Political Prisoners)
title: FREE THE DOMINICAN POLITICAL PRISONERS
subject: Human Rights, Political Prisoners, Dominican Republic
description: A political button advocating for the release of political prisoners in the Dominican Republic. It features the acronym 'USLA', representing the United States Committee for Justice to Latin American Political Prisoners, alongside a map of the Dominican Republic filled with the national flag and a graphic of hands grasping prison bars.

name: The New Nicaragua is Growing ; Let It Live
title: THE NEW NICARAGUA IS GROWING = LET IT LIVE =
subject: Nicaragua, Sandinista, Agriculture
description: A button supporting the post-revolutionary government of Nicaragua, featuring a woman harvesting corn and the slogan 'The New Nicaragua is Growing - Let it Live'. This button was likely produced by a solidarity organization during the 1980s to oppose United States intervention in Nicaragua.

name: Stop the Invasion of Nicaragua ; CAN
title: STOP THE INVASION OF NICARAGUA CAN
subject: Nicaragua, Anti-war, Sandinista
description: A red and black political button from the 1980s protesting United States intervention in Nicaragua. It features the text "STOP THE INVASION OF NICARAGUA" along the top and "CAN" at the bottom, with a map of Nicaragua containing the silhouette of Nicaraguan revolutionary Augusto César Sandino.

name: Embargo South Africa Not Nicaragua! ; People's Anti-War Mobilization
title: Embargo South Africa NOT Nicaragua! People's Anti-War Mobilization
subject: Anti-Apartheid, Nicaragua Embargo, Peace Movement
description: This yellow and black button features two interlocking clenched fists. It reflects a protest against United States foreign policy in the mid-1980s, specifically opposing the trade embargo against Nicaragua's Sandinista government while advocating for sanctions against the apartheid regime in South Africa. The button was produced by the People's Anti-War Mobilization.

name: No Invasion ; Nicaragua
title: NO INVASIÓN NICARAGUA
subject: Nicaragua, Anti-intervention, Protest
description: A political button protesting military intervention in Nicaragua during the 1980s. It features the Spanish text 'NO INVASIÓN NICARAGUA' in a stylized hand-drawn font over a blue and green watercolor-style background.

name: Stop U.S. War on Nicaragua
title: STOP U.S. WAR ON NICARAGUA
subject: Anti-war, Nicaragua, US Foreign Policy
description: A dark green button with gold text that reads "STOP U.S. WAR ON NICARAGUA". It represents opposition to US intervention in Nicaragua during the 1980s.

name: Stop U.S. War on Nicaragua
title: STOP U.S. WAR ON NICARAGUA
subject: Anti-war, Nicaragua, US Foreign Policy
description: This button features the slogan "STOP U.S. WAR ON NICARAGUA" in dark green brush-style lettering against a yellow background. It refers to the US-backed Contra War in Nicaragua during the 1980s.

name: Nicaragua is Not Our Enemy
title: NICARAGUA IS NOT OUR ENEMY
subject: Anti-war activism, Nicaragua, US Foreign Policy
description: A pinback button with a red and black background, the colors of the Sandinista National Liberation Front (FSLN). It features the slogan "NICARAGUA IS NOT OUR ENEMY" in white text. This button was likely used by American activists protesting U.S. involvement in the Contra War during the 1980s.

name: Nicaragua is Spanish for Vietnam
title: NICARAGUA is SPANISH for VIETNAM
subject: Anti-war, Nicaragua, US Foreign Policy
description: A round black button with pink text that says "NICARAGUA is SPANISH for VIETNAM," expressing opposition to US military involvement in Central America during the 1980s by drawing a parallel to the Vietnam War.

name: Defend Nicaragua! ; Crush the Contras! ; SYC ; Complete, Extend the Revolution! ; Smash U.S. Imperialism!
title: Defend Nicaragua! Crush the Contras! SYC Complete, Extend the Revolution! Smash U.S. Imperialism!
subject: Nicaragua, Anti-Imperialism, Revolution
description: This political button features a red star with text advocating for the defense of the Nicaraguan revolution and the defeat of the Contra forces and U.S. imperialism. It was produced by the Spartacus Youth Club (SYC), the youth wing of the Trotskyist Spartacist League.

name: Free Nicaragua
title: FREE NICARAGUA
subject: Nicaragua, Foreign Policy, Political Activism
description: A blue pinback button with white text reading "FREE NICARAGUA". This button likely dates from the 1980s during the period of intense political and military conflict in Nicaragua, involving the Sandinista government and the U.S.-backed Contra rebels.

name: Support Nicaragua
title: SUPPORT NICARAGUA
subject: Nicaragua, Sandinista, Solidarity
description: A pin-back button featuring a black silhouette of Nicaraguan revolutionary Augusto César Sandino with the text 'SUPPORT NICARAGUA' in red. This button was likely produced during the 1980s to show solidarity with the Sandinista government during the Contra War.

name: CIA Out of Nicaragua Now
title: CIA OUT OF NICARAGUA NOW
subject: Nicaragua, CIA, Foreign Policy
description: This circular red button features black text reading 'CIA OUT OF NICARAGUA NOW'. In the center, there is an illustration of a rat with 'CIA' written on its side, whose head is caught in a mousetrap.

name: Stop U.S. War on Nicaragua
title: STOP U.S. WAR ON NICARAGUA
subject: Anti-war, Nicaragua, Foreign Policy
description: A political protest button calling for an end to United States military intervention in Nicaragua. The button features stencil-style text and silhouettes of military helicopters and soldiers against a background of green, yellow, and red stripes.

name: Freedom for Chile
title: FREEDOM FOR CHILE
subject: Chilean solidarity, Pinochet regime, Human rights
description: A political protest button expressing solidarity with Chile during the military dictatorship of Augusto Pinochet. It features the slogan "FREEDOM FOR CHILE" and an illustration of a figure with flowing hair draped in the Chilean flag.

name: Support Nicaraguan Reconstruction ; National Network in Solidarity With the Nicaraguan People
title: SUPPORT NICARAGUAN RECONSTRUCTION
subject: Nicaragua, Sandinista, Solidarity
description: A red and white button produced by the National Network in Solidarity with the Nicaraguan People (NNSNP) following the Sandinista revolution in 1979. It features an outline of the map of Nicaragua and a stylized portrait of revolutionary leader Augusto César Sandino.

name: El Salvador Nicaragua Grenada Cuba ; U.S. Hands Off! ; YSA
title: EL SALVADOR NICARAGUA GRENADA CUBA U.S. HANDS OFF! YSA

description: A political protest button issued by the Young Socialist Alliance (YSA) calling for an end to United States intervention in El Salvador, Nicaragua, Grenada, and Cuba, likely produced following the 1983 U.S. invasion of Grenada.

name: Stop the U.S. War on Central America; Young Socialist Alliance
title: STOP THE U.S. WAR ON CENTRAL AMERICA Young Socialist Alliance
subject: Anti-war, Central America, Socialism
description: A green circular button featuring the text "STOP THE U.S. WAR ON CENTRAL AMERICA" in bold black letters, with "Young Socialist Alliance" along the bottom edge.

name: U.S. Out of Panama!
title: U.S. OUT of PANAMA!
subject: Anti-war, Panama, Foreign Policy
description: A yellow pin-back button featuring the text 'U.S. OUT of PANAMA!' in dark blue. The bottom half of the button depicts an outline of Panama surrounded by silhouettes of helicopters and naval ships, protesting the 1989 United States invasion of Panama.

name: U.S. Out of Guantanamo ; End the Blockade of Cuba ; Venceremos Brigade
title: U.S. OUT OF GUANTANAMO END THE BLOCKADE OF CUBA VENCEREMOS BRIGADE
subject: Cuba Solidarity, U.S. Foreign Policy, Venceremos Brigade
description: A political button expressing solidarity with Cuba, featuring the Cuban flag and an illustration of a hand holding a tool for sugarcane harvesting. The text calls for an end to the U.S. blockade and the closure of the Guantanamo Bay Naval Base, identifying with the Venceremos Brigade, an organization that has sent volunteers to Cuba since 1969.

name: Your Taxes Pay for Torture, Rape & Murder in Central America
title: YOUR TAXES PAY FOR TORTURE, RAPE & MURDER IN CENTRAL AMERICA
subject: Central America, Foreign Policy, Human Rights
description: A political protest button from the 1980s criticizing United States involvement and funding of military actions and human rights abuses in Central America.

name: October 13 ; Stop Reagan's War in Central America ; Nuclear Freeze Now Meet Human Needs No more Vietnams
title: Stop Reagan's War in Central America
subject: Central America, nuclear weapons, peace
description: A round blue pinback button featuring yellow and white text. The main text reads 'Stop Reagan's War in Central America' in yellow script. Above it is 'OCTOBER 13' in white. Below it are three lines of white text: 'NUCLEAR FREEZE NOW', 'MEET HUMAN NEEDS', and 'NO MORE VIETNAMS'.

name: Ernesto Che Guevara
title: ERNESTO CHE GUEVARA
subject: Revolution, Communism, Cuba
description: This button features the iconic 'Guerrillero Heroico' portrait of Ernesto 'Che' Guevara, a central figure of the Cuban Revolution, set against the design of the Cuban flag.

name: No Military Aid to Guatemala
title: NO MILITARY AID TO GUATEMALA

name: STOP! Laisse L'Amerique- Centrale Tranquille! (Leave Central America Alone)
title: STOP! LAISSE L'AM	RIQUE- CENTRALE TRANQUILLE!

LAISSE L'AM	RIQUE CENTRALE TRANQUILLE!' translates to 'STOP! Leave Central America alone!', protesting Reagan-era US interventionism in Central American countries like Nicaragua.

name: Casa Chile
title: CASA CHILE

description: Round button with a black border and CASA CHILE in white text. The center features the Chilean flag with a circled letter R superimposed.

name: Cuba Solidarity Campaign ; End the U.S. Blockade

description: This button was produced by the Cuba Solidarity Campaign, a British organization that advocates for the end of the United States embargo against Cuba. The design features a waving Cuban flag and text calling for an end to the blockade.

title: I Gave To Free Chile

name: U.S. Hands Off Grenada Now! ; Young Socialist Alliance
title: U.S. HANDS OFF GRENADA NOW! young socialist alliance
subject: Grenada, anti-war, socialism
description: A red button with black text protesting the 1983 United States-led invasion of Grenada, produced by the Young Socialist Alliance.

name: U.S. Out of Grenada Now!
title: U.S. OUT of GRENADA NOW!

description: A red button with white text that reads 'U.S. OUT of GRENADA NOW!', protesting the 1983 United States-led invasion of the Caribbean island nation of Grenada.

name: Chile Fights
title: CHILE FIGHTS

description: A button featuring a stylized Chilean flag with the text 'CHILE FIGHTS'. It was likely produced by international solidarity movements supporting Chilean resistance against the military dictatorship of Augusto Pinochet which began following the 1973 coup d'

name: Chile Si, Junta No ; Suport the Chilean People
title: CHILE SI, JUNTA NO SUPPORT THE CHILEAN PEOPLE
subject: Chilean Coup; Solidarity; Human Rights
description: A yellow protest button with black text. The slogan 'CHILE SI, JUNTA NO' arcs across the top, and 'SUPPORT THE CHILEAN PEOPLE' is displayed in the center. The button was produced in response to the 1973 Chilean military coup and the subsequent junta government led by Augusto Pinochet.

name: Somos Mas (We are More) ; Women in Chile
title: SOMOS MAS WOMEN IN CHILE
subject: Women's rights, Chile, Activism
description: This button represents the 'Somos M

name: 10 Aniversario
title: 10 ANIVERSARIO
subject: Sandinista, Nicaragua, Anniversary
description: A rectangular wooden pin commemorating the 10th anniversary of the Sandinista Revolution in Nicaragua. The design includes the text "10 ANIVERSARIO", a red and black heart, and a figure holding a Sandinista flag.

name: Chile Si, Junta No ; Chicago Peace Council
title: CHILE SI JUNTA NO! CHICAGO PEACE COUNCIL
subject: Anti-Junta, Chile, Chicago Peace Council
description: This button was created by the Chicago Peace Council to express solidarity with the people of Chile and opposition to the military junta that seized power in 1973. It depicts a shouting face silhouetted against a brick wall.

name: US Troops Out of Panama
title: US TROOPS OUT OF PANAMA

description: A round white button with the Panama flag in the center and the text "US TROOPS OUT OF PANAMA" around the border. This button likely refers to the United States invasion of Panama, codenamed Operation Just Cause, which began in December 1989.

name: No Military Aid to Guatemala
title: NO MILITARY AID TO GUATEMALA

description: A yellow pin-back button featuring red stencil-style text that reads 'NO MILITARY AID TO GUATEMALA'. Between the lines of text is a row of eight red silhouette figures marching with rifles. This button was likely produced to protest US involvement and support of the Guatemalan government during its civil war, particularly during the 1980s.

name: Peace & Justice in CEntral America
title: Peace & Justice in Central America
subject: peace, justice, Central America
description: A political button featuring a map of Central America in green against a blue background with the slogan 'Peace & Justice in Central America', likely associated with the 1980s movement against US intervention in the region.

name: Boycott Chilean Goods
title: BOYCOTT CHILEAN GOODS

description: Circular button featuring the Chilean flag in the background. In the foreground, a pair of hands holds a cluster of grapes, with a single drop of red blood falling from them. The text 'BOYCOTT CHILEAN GOODS' circles the border in white on a black background. This imagery was used to protest the human rights violations of the Pinochet regime.

The top band depicts silhouettes of three military helicopters, and the bottom band shows silhouettes of armed individuals marching. This button is representative of 1980s protest movements against U.S. involvement in Central American conflicts.

Bush administration to criticize the US policy of foreign regime change, particularly in Iraq, and to call for political change in the United States during the 2004 election.
