subject: Middle East, Activism, Liberation
description: A red button with black text reading 'SUPPORT MID-EAST LIBERATION'. This button was likely produced in the late 1960s or 1970s by activists supporting Palestinian or general Middle Eastern liberation movements during the height of New Left anti-imperialist organizing.

name: U.S. Out of Middle East ; Bring the Troops Home ; Coalition to Stop U.S. Intervention in the Middle East
title: NO TO GULF WAR CHURCH WOMEN UNITED
subject: Gulf War, Peace, Church Women United
description: This button was produced by Church Women United to protest the Gulf War. It features purple text on a white background.

name: U.S. Out of Middle East ; Bring the Troops Home ; Coalition to Stop U.S. Intervention in the Middle East
title: BUTTON FARM OAKLAWN IL, 60453 312-BUT-TONS
subject: manufacturing, button making, business
description: The reverse side of a pinback button displaying the manufacturer's mark for Button Farm, located in Oak Lawn, Illinois. The use of the 312 area code for this location indicates a date prior to 1989.

name: No to the Gulf War ; Church Women United
title: U.S. OUT of Middle East BRING THE TROOPS HOME
subject: Anti-war, Gulf War, Oil
description: A political button advocating for the withdrawal of United States troops from the Middle East, produced by the Coalition to Stop U.S. Intervention in the Middle East during the lead-up to or during the Gulf War (Operation Desert Shield/Storm). The button features silhouettes of oil derricks.

name: Support the National COuncil of Resistance in Iran ; NCR
title: support the NCR National Council of Resistance in Iran
party: National Council of Resistance of Iran
subject: Iran, Human Rights, Resistance
description: A circular red button with the large white letters 'NCR' in the background and the text 'support the National Council of Resistance in Iran' in black over it. The NCRI is a political organization founded in 1981 in opposition to the Islamic Republic of Iran.

name: US Troops Out of the Middle East
title: US TROOPS OUT OF THE MIDDLE EAST
subject: Anti-war, Middle East, Military
description: A red and white circular protest button with stars at the top, featuring the text 'US TROOPS OUT OF THE MIDDLE EAST'. This button is associated with anti-war protests during the Gulf War era in the early 1990s.

name: Not! [Image of George Bush Sr.]
title: NOT!
subject: George H.W. Bush, 1992 election, Anti-Bush
description: A political button from the 1992 US presidential campaign featuring a halftone portrait of George H.W. Bush with the word 'NOT!' in red ink printed over his face, referencing a popular slang term of the era.

name: Free the Shah's Political Prisioners ; Committee for Artistic and Intellectual Freedom in Iran
title: Free the Shah's Political Prisoners Committee for Artistic and Intellectual Freedom in Iran
subject: Iran, Political prisoners, Human rights
description: A button from the Committee for Artistic and Intellectual Freedom in Iran (CAIFI) calling for the release of political prisoners held by the Shah of Iran during the 1970s.

name: [Image of Muammar Gaddafi]
title: الفاتح أبدا
office: Brotherly Leader and Guide of the Revolution
subject: Muammar Gaddafi, Libya, Green Book
description: A political button from Libya featuring a portrait of Muammar Gaddafi in military uniform. The Arabic text at the top, 'الفاتح أبدا' (Al-Fateh Abadan), translates to 'The Conqueror Always' or 'The Eternal Opener,' referencing the 1969 Al-Fateh Revolution. Below the portrait is a graphic of the three volumes of 'The Green Book', which outlines Gaddafi's political philosophy.

name: [Image of Muammar Gaddafi]
title: Husni Mubarak
subject: President, Egypt, Election
description: The back of a pin-back button for Hosni Mubarak, featuring a manufacturer's label for SBS in Cairo, Egypt.

name: Love Life Stop the Violence [Image of Martin Luther King Jr.]
title: LOVE LIFE STOP THE VIOLENCE
subject: Martin Luther King, Jr., Non-violence, Peace
description: This button features a black-and-white portrait of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. centered on a white background. Around the edge, the text reads "LOVE LIFE" at the top and "STOP THE VIOLENCE" at the bottom. This type of button was produced to commemorate King's legacy and promote his message of non-violence, particularly following his assassination in 1968.

name: Stop Killing Our Children! ; Atlanta Georgia ; 1979-1981
title: Atlanta STOP KILLING OUR CHILDREN! Georgia 1979-1981
subject: Atlanta child murders
description: A lime green button with black text reading "Atlanta STOP KILLING OUR CHILDREN! Georgia 1979-1981". This button refers to the Atlanta child murders, a series of killings of at least 28 children, adolescents, and adults in Atlanta, Georgia, between 1979 and 1981.

name: Save the Black Family
title: SAVE THE BLACK FAMILY
subject: Civil rights, African Americans, Family
description: A round red button with white text that reads "SAVE THE BLACK FAMILY". This button is likely from the mid-1980s, a period when several organizations and leaders, including the Nation of Islam, hosted conferences and campaigns under this theme to address social and economic challenges facing Black families.

name: Defend Black RIghts ; Stop Racist Violence
title: Defend Black Rights Stop Racist Violence NSCAR
subject: Anti-racism, Civil Rights, Student Activism
description: A button from the National Student Coalition Against Racism (NSCAR) advocating for Black rights and an end to racist violence. NSCAR was an organization active in the 1970s, particularly known for its role in the Boston school busing desegregation movement.

name: Now is the Time ; King Day Jan. 15th ; Support the National Holiday ; Rev. Martin Luther King, Jr.
title: NOW is the TIME KING DAY JAN. 15th Support The National Holiday Rev. Martin Luther King, Jr.
subject: Civil Rights, Martin Luther King Jr., National Holiday
description: A campaign button advocating for a national holiday to honor Rev. Martin Luther King, Jr. It features a portrait of King in the center with the slogans "NOW is the TIME" and "Support The National Holiday."

name: March on Washington ; August 27, 1983 ; Shirley Chisholm Harriet Tubman Rosa Parks
title: MARCH ON WASHINGTON August 27, 1983
person: Shirley Chisholm, Harriet Tubman, and Rosa Parks
subject: Civil rights, African American history, Women leaders
description: A circular button commemorating the 20th anniversary March on Washington held on August 27, 1983. It features blue line-art portraits of Shirley Chisholm, Harriet Tubman, and Rosa Parks.

name: Freedom Now
title: FREEDOM NOW
subject: civil rights, activism, freedom
description: A circular pin-back button with 'FREEDOM NOW' printed in blue. The center features a stylized blue torch and wreath silhouette over a red circular background, representing the Civil Rights Movement's demand for immediate equality and freedom.

name: King '68
title: KING '68
subject: Civil Rights, Martin Luther King Jr., 1968 Election
description: A black and white horizontal striped button with the text 'KING '68'. This button is associated with the 1968 election cycle and Martin Luther King Jr., representing support for his civil rights message or a movement to draft him for political office during that year.

name: Black America Third World ; Strike ; NABS
title: BLACK AMERICA THIRD WORLD STRIKE NABS
subject: Student Protests, Black Power Movement, Ethnic Studies
description: This button is associated with the 1968-1969 Third World Strike at San Francisco State University. The strike, led by the Black Student Union and the Third World Liberation Front, was the longest student strike in U.S. history and led to the establishment of the first College of Ethnic Studies. NABS likely refers to the Northern Area Brotherhood of Students, a coalition involved in the activism of the period.

name: March for the Dream ; Aug.27, 1983-Washington, DC
title: MARCH FOR THE DREAM AUG. 27, 1983 - WASHINGTON, DC
subject: Civil Rights, Protest, Anniversary
description: This button was produced for the 20th anniversary of the 1963 March on Washington. It features a line drawing of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and red text noting the date and location of the commemorative 'March for the Dream' event.

name: I Have a Dream
title: I HAVE A DREAM
subject: Civil Rights, Racial Equality, Martin Luther King Jr.
description: A black and white button featuring the phrase 'I HAVE A DREAM' and an illustration of a black hand and a white hand shaking, symbolizing the Civil Rights Movement and Martin Luther King Jr.'s 1963 speech.

name: Second National Celebration ; Dr. M.L.K. King, Jr. ; Jan.1987
title: SECOND NATIONAL CELEBRATION DR. M.L. KING, JR. JAN. 1987
subject: Civil Rights, Martin Luther King Jr., Holiday
description: A button commemorating the second national celebration of Martin Luther King Jr. Day in January 1987, featuring a portrait of Dr. King.

name: January 15th National Holiday ; Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. ; NAACP Youth Council (National Association for the Advancement of Colored People)
title: JANUARY 15th NATIONAL HOLIDAY DR. MARTIN LUTHER KING, JR. NAACP YOUTH COUNCIL
subject: Martin Luther King, Jr. Day, NAACP, Civil Rights
description: A yellow and blue campaign button featuring a portrait of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., advocating for the establishment of a national holiday on January 15th. The button was issued by the NAACP Youth Council.

name: Take the Chisholm Trail to 1600 Pennsylvania Ave.
title: Take the CHISHOLM trail to 1600 Pennsylvania Ave.
subject: Presidential Campaign, Civil Rights, Women in Politics
description: A yellow political button for Shirley Chisholm's 1972 presidential campaign. The button features black text reading "Take the CHISHOLM trail to 1600 Pennsylvania Ave." next to a sketch of the U.S. Capitol dome.

name: Black Votes Mean Power ; New York Operation Big Vote 1984
title: BLACK VOTES MEAN POWER NEW YORK OPERATION BIG VOTE 1984
subject: Voter mobilization, Civil Rights, Black Power
description: A red circular button with black text that reads 'BLACK VOTES MEAN POWER'. On the left side is an outline of an apple (representing the 'Big Apple') with 'NEW YORK OPERATION BIG VOTE 1984' written inside. This button was part of a campaign to increase voter registration and turnout among African Americans in New York during the 1984 election year.

name: 50th Anniversary March on Washington for Jobs & Freedom ; August 1963-2013 ; The Struggle Continues
title: 50th Anniversary March On Washington For Jobs & Freedom August 1963-2013 The Struggle Continues
subject: Civil Rights, Activism, Anniversary
description: A button produced to commemorate the 50th anniversary of the 1963 March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom. It features a handshake between a Black person and a white person, symbolizing unity, and the text 'The Struggle Continues'.

name: Power to the People
title: POWER TO THE PEOPLE
subject: Civil Rights, Black Power, Pan-Africanism
description: A circular button featuring the Black Power slogan 'POWER TO THE PEOPLE' arched over a raised black fist. The background consists of three horizontal stripes in green, yellow, and red, which are the colors often associated with Pan-Africanism and the Ethiopian flag.

name: Make It Happen! ; Africa University
title: Make It Happen! AFRICA UNIVERSITY
subject: Education, Africa University, Zimbabwe
description: A campaign button for Africa University, a private Pan-African university located in Mutare, Zimbabwe, and founded by the United Methodist Church. The 'Make It Happen!' slogan was used during the university's initial fundraising and establishment phase in the late 1980s and early 1990s.

name: Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee ; We Shall Overcome
title: STUDENT NONVIOLENT WE SHALL OVERCOME COORDINATING COMMITTEE
subject: Civil Rights, SNCC, Activism
description: A black and white circular pinback button for the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee (SNCC) featuring the Civil Rights Movement's anthem and slogan "WE SHALL OVERCOME" in the center.

name: We Are Afrikans Working for Black Unity
title: WE ARE AFRIKANS WORKING FOR BLACK UNITY
subject: Pan-Africanism, Black Power, Unity
description: A round pin-back button featuring a green silhouette of the African continent on a black background. Around the edge is the red text 'WE ARE AFRIKANS WORKING FOR BLACK UNITY'. The color scheme of red, black, and green represents the Pan-African flag.

name: Stop Racist Attacks ; Miami Atlanta Buffalo ; YSA
title: MIAMI ATLANTA BUFFALO STOP RACIST ATTACKS YSA
subject: anti-racism, socialism, civil rights
description: A button produced by the Young Socialist Alliance (YSA) featuring Pan-African colors. It calls for an end to racist violence, specifically referencing events in Miami, Atlanta, and Buffalo during the early 1980s, such as the 1980 Miami riots and the Atlanta child murders.

name: Martin Luther King National Holiday March ; January 15, 1982 Washington, D.C.
title: JANUARY 15, 1982 Martin LUTHER King NATIONAL HOLIDAY MARCH WASHINGTON, D.C.
subject: Civil Rights, Martin Luther King Jr., Federal Holiday
description: A blue and white button promoting a march in Washington, D.C. on January 15, 1982, as part of the campaign to establish Martin Luther King Jr. Day as a national holiday.

name: March on Washington for Jobs, Peace & Freedom ; 20th ANniversary l August 28, 1963-August 27, 1983
title: March on Washington for Jobs, Peace & Freedom 20th Anniversary August 28, 1963 August 27, 1983
subject: Civil Rights, March on Washington, Anniversary
description: Yellow and red pinback button commemorating the 20th anniversary of the 1963 March on Washington for Jobs, Peace & Freedom. The button features an image of the U.S. Capitol and Washington Monument.

name: Pro-MARY ; Mary Peltola in Congress
title: Pro-MARY MARY PELTOLA in CONGRESS
subject: Alaska, Congressional Election, Mary Peltola
description: A white campaign button for Mary Peltola's 2022 congressional run in Alaska, featuring the slogan "Pro-MARY" and her name above a multicolored stripe.

subject: 15th Amendment, Voting Rights, Centennial
description: A black button with white text reading 1870, produced in 1970 to commemorate the centennial of the ratification of the 15th Amendment to the United States Constitution.

title: MARY PELTOLA - CONGRESS VOTE FOR FISH. FAMILY. FREEDOM. IDITAROD FISH CREW 2023
subject: Mary Peltola, Iditarod, Alaska Politics
description: A political button for Alaska Congresswoman Mary Peltola, specifically produced for the 2023 Iditarod sled dog race, featuring her slogan "Fish. Family. Freedom." and a howling dog.

name: Sled Dogs for Mary ; Mary Peltola in Congress
title: SLED DOGS for MARY MARY PELTOLA in CONGRESS
subject: campaign, Alaska, sled dogs
description: A blue and gray campaign button for Mary Peltola's 2022 run for the U.S. House of Representatives in Alaska, featuring a treeline silhouette and the slogan 'SLED DOGS for MARY'.

name: Lindsay, Perrotta, Garelik
title: Lindsay, Perrotta, Garelik.
person: John V. Lindsay, Fioravante G. Perrotta, Sanford Garelik
subject: Lindsay, Perrotta, Garelik
description: A pink and white campaign button for the 1969 New York City mayoral ticket of John Lindsay for Mayor, Fioravante Perrotta for Comptroller, and Sanford Garelik for City Council President.

name: Eisenhower ; for President
title: FOR PRESIDENT EISENHOWER

title: ALLIED PRINTING TRADES UNION LABEL COUNCIL CHICAGO ILL 313

It features a repeating pattern of the Allied Printing Trades Union Label for Chicago (shop 313) and the mark of the Graphic Communications International Union (GCIU 534) on the rim, signifying it was union-made.

name: Register for LBJ [image of Stetson LBJ hat]

description: A blue felt button with white text and graphics. It reads 'REGISTER FOR' above a white cowboy hat that contains the initials 'LBJ'. This was used during Lyndon B. Johnson's 1964 presidential campaign.

title: DUBIN RYAN HEDGEMAN

description: A campaign button for the 1965 New York City Democratic primary supporting the 'reform' slate consisting of William Fitts Ryan for Mayor, Melvin Dubin for Comptroller, and Anna Arnold Hedgeman for President of the City Council.

name: Ribicoff cares.
title: Ribicoff cares.

title: Lindsay, Perrotta, Garelik.

subject: New York City, Mayoral Election, Lindsay

description: A red, white, and blue political campaign button from the 1964 United States presidential election supporting the Democratic ticket of Lyndon B. Johnson for president and Hubert Humphrey for vice president. The button features their names in white capital letters on a red background, with the year 1964 in italics within a central blue oval, flanked by two white stars.

name: GOParty ; 1964
title: GOP party 1964

name: I like Ike and Dick

name: We want F.D.R. again

The back contains manufacturing information for the Midwest Badge & Novelty Co., which was located at 1045 Hennepin Ave. in Minneapolis, Minnesota. The front of this specific button features a black-and-white portrait of Eisenhower and the iconic slogan 'I LIKE IKE' surrounded by a blue border with white stars.

name: Shapp
title: SHAPP

subject: Republican Party, Democratic Party, Political Humor
description: A white political campaign button featuring a black-and-white illustration of a donkey, representing the Democratic Party, accompanied by the text "DON'T BE AN ASS! VOTE REPUBLICAN." It is a humorous piece of anti-Democratic propaganda typical of the mid-20th century.

name: [Image of two hands, one black and one white, holding a white dove]
subject: Civil Rights, Peace, Racial Harmony
description: A button featuring a white dove being held by a black hand and a white hand on a light blue background, representing peace and racial integration.

name: קלינטון גור ; Clinton Gore
title: ՑՔթնաոն Գոր Clinton Gore
subject: Clinton, Gore, Hebrew
description: A campaign button for the Clinton and Gore presidential campaign, featuring their names in both English and Hebrew with a stylized American flag graphic.

name: Clinton Gore ; Rapid Repone Team
title: Clinton Gore Rapid Response Team
subject: presidential campaign; political strategy; rapid response
description: A campaign button from the 1992 or 1996 presidential campaign of Bill Clinton and Al Gore, identifying the wearer as part of the "Rapid Response Team," a key component of their campaign communication strategy.

name: Take the Journey with Bernie
title: Take the Journey with Bernie
subject: Presidential Campaign, Bernie Sanders, 2016 Election
description: A circular red, white, and blue political campaign button for Bernie Sanders' 2016 presidential run, with the slogan "Take the Journey with Bernie".

name: Gardenhire ; Go Gary! ; State Senate
title: GARDENHIRE Go Gary! STATE SENATE
subject: State Senate, Gary Gardenhire, Oklahoma
description: Circular red button with white text that reads 'GARDENHIRE Go Gary! STATE SENATE'. This button was used in Gary Gardenhire's campaign for the Oklahoma State Senate.

name: [Putin holding a baby with Trump's head]
title: TP
subject: Satire, Foreign Policy, US Elections
description: A satirical button featuring a parody of Soviet propaganda. It depicts Vladimir Putin in a Soviet military uniform holding Donald Trump as an infant. The baby Trump holds a red flag with the letters 'T' and 'P' stylized together.

name: Green Party Jill Stein for President 2016
title: Green Party Jill STEIN for President 2016
subject: Jill Stein, Green Party, 2016 Election
description: White button with green and blue text supporting Jill Stein for President in 2016 on behalf of the Green Party.

name: Never Trump  [Image of Monopoly Money Man character hauling a money bag]
title: NEVER TRUMP
subject: Donald Trump, Anti-Trump movement, 2016 Presidential Election
description: Yellow button with black graphic showing the Monopoly Man (Rich Uncle Pennybags) carrying a large money bag with a dollar sign on it. The text 'NEVER TRUMP' is printed in black along the lower right curve.

name: [Picture of Bernie Sanders]
subject: Barry Goldwater; 1964 Presidential Election; Silhouette Portrait
description: A campaign button from Barry Goldwater's 1964 presidential campaign featuring a black silhouette of his profile, including his signature glasses and a necktie, on a plain white background.

name: Feel the Bern
title: FEEL the BERN
subject: Bernie Sanders, Presidential Campaign, 2016
description: A campaign button for Bernie Sanders' 2016 presidential run, featuring his popular slogan "FEEL the BERN" in black text with red and blue stripes.

name: Peace [Peace sign over tie dye background]
title: Peace Peace Peace Peace Peace Peace Peace Peace Peace Peace
subject: Peace symbol, Tie-dye, Anti-war
description: A circular button with a black peace symbol centered on a colorful tie-dye background. The word 'Peace' is printed ten times in a circle around the border, separated by small dots.

name: I'm a Political Junkie!
title: I'm a Political JUNKIE!
subject: Politics, Humor, Identity
description: A circular button with a dark blue background featuring the text 'I'm a Political' in white and 'JUNKIE!' in large red stencil-style lettering.

name: It's the Supreme Court, Stupid! ; NARAL
title: It's the Supreme Court, Stupid!
subject: Reproductive Rights, Supreme Court, Abortion
description: A teal button featuring the text 'It's the Supreme Court, Stupid!' in white. Below the main text is the logo for NARAL (National Abortion Rights Action League) and their website address. The slogan is a play on the phrase 'It's the economy, stupid,' popularized during Bill Clinton's 1992 campaign, used here to highlight the importance of presidential appointments to the Supreme Court.

name: Love trumps hate
title: Love trumps hate.
subject: 2016 Presidential Election
description: Round white button with the slogan "Love trumps hate." in red, navy blue, and light blue text. This phrase was a central slogan of Hillary Clinton's 2016 presidential campaign, used to contrast her platform with that of her opponent, Donald Trump.

name: Universal Healthcare ; Bernie 2016
title: UNIVERSAL HEALTH CARE Bernie 2016
subject: Universal Health Care, 2016 Presidential Campaign, Bernie Sanders
description: A campaign button for Bernie Sanders' 2016 presidential run, featuring the slogan 'UNIVERSAL HEALTH CARE'.

name: Bob's Kammettes
title: Bob's Kammettes
subject: Senate campaign, support group, Republican Party
description: A white circular button with green text that reads 'Bob's Kammettes'. The button was produced for the 1978 campaign of Robert Kamm, a Republican running for the U.S. Senate in Oklahoma. 'Kammettes' was the name given to a group of his female supporters.

name: Never Trump [Image of Monopoly Money Man character with a pacifier and baby toys]
title: NEVER TRUMP
subject: Never Trump movement, 2016 Presidential Election, Anti-Trump
description: A yellow political button featuring a black caricature of Donald Trump depicted as a baby wearing a top hat and suit jacket, holding a rattle and a pretzel. The text "NEVER TRUMP" is printed along the bottom right edge.

name: Ask me about Bernie Sanders 2016
title: Ask me about Bernie Sanders 2016
subject: Bernie Sanders, 2016 Presidential Campaign, Democratic Primary
description: A campaign button for Bernie Sanders' 2016 presidential run featuring the text "Ask me about Bernie Sanders 2016" with decorative red and blue stripes.

name: Kennedy
title: Kennedy
subject: Elections; Massachusetts; Politicians
description: A blue campaign button for Ted Kennedy's re-election to the U.S. Senate from Massachusetts. The button features 'Kennedy' in white serif font with a small red star to the right. Smaller text along the bottom border reads 'Paid for by the Committee to Re-Elect Senator Kennedy' followed by a union bug and the number 36.

name: Reagan רייגין
title: Reagan רייגן
subject: Jewish voters, presidential campaign, Ronald Reagan
description: A circular light blue campaign button with the name 'Reagan' printed in white English text at the bottom and white Hebrew characters in the center. This button was designed to appeal to Jewish voters during Ronald Reagan's presidential campaigns.

name: Bernie is my Patronus
title: Bernie is my Patronus
subject: Bernie Sanders, Harry Potter, Campaign
description: A glittery button with white and pink text featuring the slogan 'Bernie is my Patronus', referencing both the politician Bernie Sanders and the Harry Potter fantasy series.

name: Fritz Busters [cartoon of Walter "Fritz" Mondale inside a prohibition sign]
title: FRITZ BUSTERS
subject: Satire, Parody, Election
description: A 1984 political button featuring a caricature of Walter "Fritz" Mondale within a prohibition symbol, parodying the Ghostbusters logo. This item was used by opponents of Mondale during the 1984 presidential election campaign.

name: U.S. Senate Bartlett
title: U.S. Senate Bartlett
subject: Dewey Bartlett, U.S. Senate, Oklahoma
description: A campaign button for Dewey F. Bartlett's 1972 run for the U.S. Senate from Oklahoma. The button features red text on a white background with a stylized 'B' in 'Bartlett' containing stars and stripes.

name: Voting is People Power
title: Voting Is People Power
subject: voting rights, civil rights, empowerment
description: Round black button featuring red text stating 'VOTING IS PEOPLE POWER' with the phrase 'PEOPLE POWER' enclosed in a red burst graphic.

name: Sanders 2016 ; Not Me Us!
title: Sanders 2016 NOT ME US!
subject: Bernie Sanders, Presidential Campaign, Not Me Us
description: Political campaign button for Bernie Sanders' 2016 presidential bid. Features a high-contrast black and white graphic of Sanders' face, with the text 'Sanders 2016' along the left edge and the slogan 'NOT ME US!' in white on a blue diagonal stripe.

name: Save The Planet ; Sanders 2016
title: SAVE THE PLANET Sanders 2016
subject: environment, politics, campaign
description: A round white button with black text reading 'SAVE THE PLANET' above two horizontal stripes, one red and one blue. Below the stripes, the text 'Sanders 2016' is printed in black. This button supports Bernie Sanders' 2016 presidential campaign and highlights his environmental platform.

name: Johnson Weld 2016
title: JOHNSON WELD 2016
subject: 2016 election, Libertarian, Campaign
description: Button for the 2016 Libertarian Party presidential ticket, featuring Gary Johnson for President and Bill Weld for Vice President.

name: Bernie 2016
title: Bernie 2016
subject: Bernie Sanders, Presidential Campaign, 2016 Election
description: A circular political button for Bernie Sanders' 2016 presidential campaign. It features a black stylized silhouette of Sanders' face on a background split vertically between red and blue, with 'Bernie 2016' written diagonally in white text across the left side.

name: Gary Johnson for President
title: GARY JOHNSON for President
subject: Presidential Campaign, Gary Johnson, Libertarian Party
description: A blue circular button with 'GARY JOHNSON for President' in white text, featuring a red horizontal line with a star in the middle.

name: Live Free ; Gary Johnson 2016
title: LIVE FREE GARY JOHNSON 2016
subject: Gary Johnson, Libertarian Party, 2016 Presidential Election
description: A red campaign button with white text for Gary Johnson's 2016 presidential campaign, featuring the slogan 'LIVE FREE'.

name: Livable Wage ; Bernie 2016
title: Livable wage BERNIE 2016
subject: Bernie Sanders, Presidential Election, Labor Rights
description: A political campaign button for Bernie Sanders' 2016 presidential run, emphasizing his stance on a livable wage.

name: Free Tuition ; Bernie 2016
title: Free Tuition Bernie 2016
subject: Bernie Sanders, Education, 2016 Election
description: Campaign button for Bernie Sanders' 2016 presidential run featuring his proposal for free college tuition.

name: Bernie Sanders Consistent since 1981
title: BERNIE SANDERS consistent since 1981
subject: Bernie Sanders, Presidential Election, Political consistency
description: A button supporting Bernie Sanders' 2016 presidential campaign, highlighting his political consistency since his election as Mayor of Burlington, Vermont in 1981.

name: Sanders 2016 ; Not For Sale!
title: Sanders 2016 NOT FOR SALE!
subject: Bernie Sanders, Presidential Campaign, 2016
description: A white political campaign button for Bernie Sanders' 2016 presidential run featuring a black silhouette portrait and the text 'Sanders 2016' and 'NOT FOR SALE!'.

name: Bernie 2016
title: BERNIE 2016
subject: Presidential campaign, Bernie Sanders, 2016 Election
description: A campaign button for Bernie Sanders' 2016 presidential campaign, featuring the text "BERNIE 2016" with red and blue stripes at the top.

name: Reform Wallstreet Sanders 2016
title: Reform wallstreet Sanders 2016
subject: Wall Street, Bernie Sanders, Presidential Election
description: A campaign button from Bernie Sanders' 2016 presidential run featuring the text 'Reform wallstreet Sanders 2016' with red and blue stripes.

name: Johnson Weld 2016
title: JOHNSON WELD 2016
subject: Libertarian, Presidential Election, Third Party
description: A button from the 2016 United States presidential campaign of Gary Johnson and Bill Weld, running as candidates for the Libertarian Party.

name: Political Revolution ; Sanders 2016
title: Political Revolution Sanders 2016
subject: Presidential Campaign, Political Revolution, Bernie Sanders
description: A circular white pinback button supporting Bernie Sanders' 2016 presidential campaign. The top text reads "Political Revolution" in black, separated from the bottom text "Sanders 2016" by two horizontal stripes, one red and one blue.

name: Live Free [Photograph of Gary Johnson]
title: LIVE FREE
subject: Gary Johnson, Libertarian, President
description: Campaign button for Gary Johnson's 2012 presidential run, featuring a black and white portrait of the candidate and the slogan "LIVE FREE".

name: Never Trump [Image of Monopoly Money Man character]
title: NEVER TRUMP
subject: Never Trump movement, wealth, 2016 election
description: A yellow button featuring a cartoon of a wealthy man in a top hat with his pockets turned out and money falling, with the text "NEVER TRUMP" along the bottom edge.

name: DAPL [Prohibition symbol]
title: DAPL
subject: environmentalism, indigenous rights, DAPL
description: A white button with the text 'DAPL' in black. Over the text is a light blue circle and diagonal slash with a geometric pattern, indicating a protest against the Dakota Access Pipeline (NoDAPL movement).

name: Super PACs [Prohibition symbol]
title: Super PACs
subject: campaign finance, political action committee, election reform
description: A white button featuring the words 'Super PACs' in black font, overlaid with a red circle and diagonal line indicating opposition to the influence of Super PACs in politics.

name: Live Free
title: LIVE FREE
subject: liberty, freedom, motto
description: A white pin-back button with the words 'LIVE' in black and 'FREE' in red, block letters.

name: Vote Girlsworld - Holmes 1980 ; People Needs, Yes! Profits, No! ; Workers World Party Presidential Campaign
title: Vote Griswold - Holmes 1980 PEOPLE'S NEEDS, YES! PROFITS, NO! Workers World Party Presidential Campaign
subject: Workers World Party, Socialism, Labor Rights
description: A campaign button for the 1980 presidential election featuring Deirdre Griswold and Larry Holmes of the Workers World Party. The button includes the slogan 'People's Needs, Yes! Profits, No!'

name: Local 509 Service Employees International Union
title: LOCAL 509 Service Employees International Union
subject: labor unions, social services, worker rights
description: A tan circular button for SEIU Local 509, showing the silhouettes of a diverse group of eight workers. Local 509 is the Massachusetts Human Service Workers Union.

name: Will Strike If Provoked ; UFCW 1445
title: WILL STRIKE UFCW 1445 IF PROVOKED
subject: Labor Unions, Strike, Healthcare
description: A round white button with blue text and graphics. It features a central caduceus symbol, representing healthcare workers, and the slogan "WILL STRIKE IF PROVOKED". It identifies UFCW Local 1445, a labor union local based in the Boston area.

name: A New Start
title: A NEW START
subject: John Anderson, 1980 election, Independent
description: A campaign button for John Anderson's 1980 presidential run as an independent candidate. The button features the campaign slogan 'A NEW START' in bold tan (originally white) lettering over a background horizontally split into red and blue halves.

name: Dotterman For Boston City Council-at-Large
title: Dotterman for Boston City Council-at-Large
subject: Local elections, Boston, Massachusetts
description: A blue and white campaign button for Norman Dotterman's run for the Boston City Council-at-Large in 1971.

name: No More Layoffs
title: NO MORE LAYOFFS
subject: Labor Unions, Unemployment, Economic Issues
description: A circular white button with the slogan 'NO MORE LAYOFFS' printed in bold blue capital letters. A small union printer's mark and the number 36 are at the bottom.

name: A Womens Right To Choose ; NARAL National Abortion Rights Action League
title: A WOMAN'S RIGHT TO CHOOSE
subject: abortion rights, women's rights, reproductive freedom
description: A blue circular button featuring a white silhouette of the Statue of Liberty. The text 'A WOMAN'S RIGHT TO CHOOSE' is written in bold white letters, with the 'OO' in 'CHOOSE' interlocking. Below this, it reads 'NARAL National Abortion Rights Action League'.

name: They Say Cutback We say Fuck That
title: THEY SAY CUTBACK WE SAY FUCK THAT
subject: Protest, Reaganomics, Economics
description: A pink button with caricatures of President Ronald Reagan and likely his budget director David Stockman. The text 'THEY SAY CUTBACK WE SAY FUCK THAT' expresses opposition to the fiscal policies and social program budget cuts of the Reagan administration in the 1980s.

name: Vote No On The Antiabortion Referendum
title: VOTE NO ON THE ANTIABORTION REFERENDUM
subject: Abortion rights, Reproductive rights, Referendum
description: This button was used in the 1986 campaign to defeat Massachusetts Question 1, a referendum that sought to amend the state constitution to declare that the right to an abortion was not a fundamental right. The union bug with the number 38 indicates it was printed in Boston.

name: 41st U.S. President George Bush Inauguration Day January 20, 1989
title: 41ST U.S. PRESIDENT INAUGURATION DAY JANUARY 20, 1989 GEORGE BUSH
subject: George H.W. Bush, Presidential Inauguration, Republican Party
description: This button commemorates the inauguration of George H.W. Bush as the 41st President of the United States. The button features a central color portrait of Bush on a blue background, with the text '41st U.S. PRESIDENT' around the top. At the bottom, 'GEORGE BUSH' is printed in yellow text below the Presidential Seal. Along the lower edges, the text reads 'INAUGURATION DAY' and 'JANUARY 20, 1989'.

name: 4 More For 44 ; 2nd Inauguration January 21st 2013
title: 4 MORE FOR 44 2nd INAUGURATION JANUARY 21st 2013
subject: Barack Obama, Inauguration, Election
description: A button celebrating the second inauguration of Barack Obama, the 44th president of the United States, on January 21, 2013. It features a stylized portrait of Obama in red, white, and blue tones.

name: I Am Big Labor
title: I Am Big Labor
subject: Labor unions, Organized labor, AFL-CIO
description: A white button featuring a large pink 'I' in the center and the words 'Am Big Labor' in black serif text along the bottom curve. This slogan was part of a labor movement campaign in the late 1970s aimed at humanizing labor unions; by having individual members declare 'I am Big Labor,' the campaign sought to counter political rhetoric that characterized unions as faceless, monolithic bureaucracies.

name: Republican National Convention New Orleans '88 August 15-18
title: Republican National Convention New Orleans '88 August 15-18
subject: Republican Party, National Convention, New Orleans
description: A round white button for the 1988 Republican National Convention held in New Orleans, Louisiana from August 15-18. The center features a red, white, and blue Republican elephant logo.

name: Bill Campbell Mayor
title: Bill Campbell MAYOR
subject: Mayoral campaign, Atlanta, Georgia
description: Campaign button for Bill Campbell, who served as the 57th Mayor of Atlanta from 1994 to 2002. The button features his name in red above a blue banner containing four stars and the word 'MAYOR'.

name: Vote No On Questions 2,3,5
title: VOTE NO ON QUESTIONS 2, 3, 5
subject: Ballot initiatives; Taxation; Public policy
description: A yellow pinback button with black text urging voters to vote against ballot questions 2, 3, and 5. This most likely refers to the 1980 Massachusetts general election, where Question 2 was the well-known 'Proposition 2 1/2' tax limitation measure.

name: America Works Best When We Say... Union Yes
title: AMERICA WORKS BEST WHEN WE SAY... UNION YES
subject: Labor unions, AFL-CIO, Advocacy
description: White button with red and blue text promoting labor unions. It features the AFL-CIO logo and the slogan 'America Works Best When We Say... Union Yes' with a blue checkmark. This campaign was launched by the AFL-CIO in 1988.

name: Love Is The Greatest
title: LOVE IS THE GREATEST
subject: LGBTQ, Christianity, Healing
description: This button was issued by the Metropolitan Community Church (MCC) of Boston. It features a heart with bandages, symbolizing healing, and the biblical phrase 'Love is the Greatest' (1 Corinthians 13:13). The MCC is an international Protestant denomination founded to serve the spiritual needs of the LGBTQ community.

name: Save Our Communities Coalition ; Vote No On Question 9
title: SAVE OUR COMMUNITIES COALITION VOTE NO ON QUESTION 9
subject: LGBTQ rights, civil rights, Ballot Measure 9
description: This button was produced by the Save Our Communities Coalition to campaign against Oregon's Ballot Measure 9 (often referred to as Question 9) in 1992. The controversial measure sought to amend the state constitution to prohibit civil rights protections for LGBTQ+ people and require state schools and agencies to discourage homosexuality. The measure was defeated at the polls.

name: Defend Abortion Rights!
title: DEFEND ABORTION RIGHTS!
subject: Abortion, Women's Rights, Social Issues
description: A yellow pin-back button featuring red capital letters that state "DEFEND ABORTION RIGHTS!"

name: SEIU ; Remembering and Rebuilding Together September 11 2001
title: Remembering and Rebuilding Together SEIU September 11, 2001
subject: Labor Union, September 11 Terrorist Attacks, Memorial
description: A circular black button produced by the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) to commemorate the September 11, 2001 attacks. It features the SEIU logo in the center with the phrases "Remembering and Rebuilding Together" and "September 11, 2001" printed in yellow text around the border.

name: War Is Not The Answer ; Sane/Freeze
title: WAR IS NOT THE ANSWER SANE / FREEZE
subject: Anti-war, Nuclear Disarmament, Peace
description: A circular button with 'WAR IS NOT THE ANSWER' printed in black over a cream-colored background. At the bottom, 'SANE / FREEZE' is printed, referring to the organization formed in 1987 through the merger of SANE and the Nuclear Weapons Freeze Campaign.

name: Natalie Davis U.S.Senate
title: Natalie Davis U.S. Senate
subject: Political campaign, Alabama, U.S. Senate
description: A campaign button for Natalie Davis, a Democratic candidate for the U.S. Senate in Alabama in 1996. The button features a blue background with a white star border and a red outline of Alabama.

name: Curemos El Sida Ahora
title: Curemos EL SIDA AHORA
subject: AIDS, public health, activism
description: A yellow button with blue text that reads "Curemos EL SIDA AHORA", which translates to "Let's Cure AIDS NOW". This button was used as part of HIV/AIDS advocacy efforts, particularly within Spanish-speaking communities during the height of the epidemic in the 1980s and early 1990s.

name: [Rainbow v sign]
title: Rainbow Peace Sign
subject: Peace, Counterculture, Rainbow
description: A round button featuring a hand in a peace sign gesture (two fingers raised) with a rainbow/tie-dye outline on a white background.

name: No 4&6 ; Mass AFL-CIO
title: NO 4 & 6 MASS AFL-CIO
subject: labor unions, ballot questions, taxes
description: This button was created by the Massachusetts AFL-CIO to oppose two ballot measures in the 1980 state election: Question 4, which aimed to repeal pay raises for state officials, and Question 6, known as Proposition 2½, which limited property taxes.

name: Labor For Gore Lieberman 2000
title: LABOR FOR Gore Lieberman 2000
subject: presidential election; labor unions; 2000
description: A campaign button for the 2000 presidential campaign of Al Gore and Joe Lieberman, showing support from organized labor. The design features an eagle head inside a gear, which is a common symbol for labor organizations.

name: Gang Peace
title: GANG PEACE
subject: gang violence, peace, activism
description: A button advocating for gang peace, likely produced by the Boston-based organization 'Gang Peace' which was founded in 1988 by Tracy Montgomery to address the issue of gang violence through community activism and youth support.

name: Dorchester I.W.D. 1983
title: Dorchester I.W.D. 1983
subject: International Women's Day, Women's Rights, Community
description: A tan and green button commemorating International Women's Day (I.W.D.) in 1983 in Dorchester, Massachusetts. The button features three illustrations of women of various ages and backgrounds.

name: It's Time We Made Smoking History.
title: It's time we made smoking history.
subject: Anti-smoking, Public Health, Tobacco Control
description: A circular white button with bold black text reading 'It's time we made smoking history.' It was likely used as part of a public health campaign to discourage tobacco use.

name: No More Promises! Join 65 LRI- PBS for Real Security
title: No more promises! JOIN 65 LRI - PBS for real security.
subject: Labor union, District 65, Employee benefits
description: This button was produced for District 65, a progressive labor union that represented diverse workers, primarily in the New York City area. The button encourages workers to join the union for 'real security,' referencing its Pension and Benefit System (PBS) and likely its Labor Research Institute (LRI).

name: Jackson in 84
title: Jackson in 84
subject: Jesse Jackson, 1984 Presidential Campaign, Rainbow Coalition
description: A circular campaign button for Jesse Jackson's 1984 presidential bid, featuring the slogan 'Jackson in 84' set against a background with stripes representing the Rainbow Coalition.

name: Mel King for Mayor
title: Mel King for MAYOR
subject: Boston, Mayor, Rainbow Coalition
description: A political campaign button for Mel King's 1983 run for Mayor of Boston, featuring a rainbow design representing his inclusive 'Rainbow Coalition'.

name: Better Health For the Commonwealth ;  Vote Yes 5
title: BETTER HEALTH VOTE yes 5 FOR THE COMMONWEALTH
subject: healthcare, ballot initiative, public health
description: A political button supporting Question 5 on the 2000 Massachusetts ballot, which sought to establish a state-wide health care plan and expand insurance coverage.

name: Liberty and Justice for All... No Exceptions ; Un-do 2
title: Liberty and Justice for All ... No Exceptions. Un-do 2
subject: LGBTQ rights, Civil Rights, Amendment 2
description: A blue button with white text and a star, used in the campaign to overturn Colorado's Amendment 2. Passed in 1992, Amendment 2 aimed to prohibit local and state governments from passing anti-discrimination laws protecting LGBTQ+ individuals. It was eventually ruled unconstitutional by the U.S. Supreme Court in 1996.

name: AFL-CIO For O'Brien
title: AFL-CIO for O'BRIEN
subject: Labor Unions, Elections, Political Campaigns
description: A yellow campaign button with red text from the AFL-CIO's Committee on Political Education (COPE) supporting Charles O'Brien's 1970 campaign for California Attorney General.

name: P9 ; The Struggle Continues ; Faneuil Hall Nov. 23
title: P9 THE STRUGGLE CONTINUES FANEUIL HALL NOV. 23
subject: Labor unions, Hormel Strike, P-9
description: A yellow button with blue text advertising a support rally for the UFCW Local P-9 meatpackers' strike against Hormel, held at Faneuil Hall in Boston on November 23, 1985.

name: Boston Schools Work Because We Do
title: BOSTON SCHOOLS WORK BECAUSE WE DO GUILD, DISTRICT 925, SEIU
subject: Labor Unions, Education, School Employees
description: A round blue button with yellow text advocating for the labor contributions of Boston school workers represented by SEIU District 925.

name: Women Of Steel ; United Steel Workers Of America
title: WOMAN OF STEEL
subject: Labor unions, Women, Steel industry
description: A button for the United Steelworkers of America (USWA) featuring three women's faces in profile. It highlights the 'Woman of Steel' initiative, which supports and advocates for women in the steel industry. The union itself was established in 1936, and this specific program was formally adopted in 1990.

name: Linn Landraitis School Committee District 6 West Roxbury/ Jamaica Plain
title: Linn Landraitis School Committee District 6 West Roxbury/ Jamaica Plain
subject: School Committee, Boston, Election
description: This yellow button supports Linn Landraitis for the Boston School Committee in District 6, which includes West Roxbury and Jamaica Plain.

name: It's A Man's World Unless Women Vote
title: It's a Man's World Unless Women VOTE
subject: Women's Suffrage, Voting Rights, Feminism
description: A political campaign button likely produced by the League of Women Voters or a similar organization in the 1970s to encourage women to exercise their right to vote. The button features a split design with red text on a white background and white text on a blue background.

name: Umass Unions United ; 1776/AFSCME 509/SEIU GEO/UAW ; USA/MTA MSP/ MTA
title: MASS UNIONS UNITED
subject: Labor Unions, Education, Collective Bargaining
description: This button represents 'Mass Unions United', a coalition of labor unions at the University of Massachusetts Amherst. The coalition includes AFSCME Local 1776, SEIU Local 509, the Graduate Employee Organization (GEO/UAW), the University Staff Association (USA/MTA), and the Massachusetts Society of Professors (MSP/MTA). The button was likely produced for a joint labor action or contract campaign in the early 2000s.

name: Our Choice '90 Evelyn Murphy
title: OUR CHOICE '90 EVELYN MURPHY
subject: Gubernatorial election, Women's rights, Labor unions
description: Circular teal button with white text 'OUR CHOICE '90 EVELYN MURPHY'. The letters 'O' in 'OUR' and 'CHOICE' are styled with the female gender symbol. At the bottom is a small logo for the UFCW (United Food and Commercial Workers) union. This button was produced for Evelyn Murphy's 1990 campaign for Governor of Massachusetts.

name: Access for All That's AFSCME's Goal
title: ACCESS FOR ALL THAT'S AFSCME'S GOAL
subject: Labor Unions, Healthcare Reform, Public Services
description: Circular button with a green top half and a tan bottom half. The top half says 'ACCESS FOR ALL' in tan letters, and the bottom half says 'THAT'S AFSCME'S GOAL' in green letters. AFSCME stands for the American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees. This slogan was commonly used in advocacy for universal healthcare and accessible public services.

name: Many Faces... One Union ; GEO UAW
title: many faces... GEO UAW ...one union
subject: Labor unions, Solidarity, Diversity
description: A button produced for the Graduate Employee Organization (GEO) of the United Auto Workers (UAW). The design features a ring of diverse figures holding hands, symbolizing solidarity across a diverse membership with the text 'many faces... one union.'

name: U.S.W.A. Demands Negotiate Now
title: U.S.W.A. DEMANDS NEGOTIATE NOW
subject: Labor unions, Steel industry, Collective bargaining
description: A yellow and green pinback button issued by the United Steelworkers of America (U.S.W.A.) to demand contract negotiations, likely used during a major labor dispute such as the 1959 steel strike.

name: Ralph Nader Vote Green 2000
title: RALPH NADER VOTE GREEN 2000
subject: Ralph Nader, Green Party, 2000 Presidential Election
description: A green campaign button featuring a central sunflower image. The text around the border in white letters reads 'RALPH NADER' at the top and 'VOTE GREEN 2000' at the bottom. This button supported Ralph Nader's bid for the U.S. Presidency as a member of the Green Party.

name: Deval Patrick Governor Tim Murray  LT. Governor ; Together We Can
title: Deval GOVERNOR Patrick Together We Can Tim L T. GOVERNOR Murray
subject: Gubernatorial election, Campaign, Massachusetts
description: A campaign button for Deval Patrick's 2006 run for Governor of Massachusetts with Tim Murray as his Lieutenant Governor running mate. The blue button features white text and a central horizontal white stripe containing the campaign slogan "Together We Can." The bottom of the button notes that it was paid for by The Massachusetts Democratic Party.

name: Unite! Union of Needletrades Industrial And Textile Employess, AFL CIO CLC
title: UNITE UNION OF NEEDLETRADES, INDUSTRIAL AND TEXTILE EMPLOYEES, AFL-CIO-CLC
subject: Labor union, Textile Workers, Needletrades
description: A button for UNITE (Union of Needletrades, Industrial and Textile Employees), which was a labor union in the United States and Canada from 1995 to 2004. The button features a blue 'UNITE' label over a black bar with the full union name and a large red exclamation point in the background.

name: Unity Unidad Unite USWA L. 8751
title: UNITY UNIDAD UNITE CONTRACT '89 BOSTON USWA L. 8751
subject: Labor unions, School bus drivers, Contract negotiations
description: A campaign button for the United Steelworkers of America (USWA) Local 8751, representing Boston school bus drivers. The button refers to the 1989 contract negotiations and features a central illustration of a school bus with 'unity' written in English, Spanish, and French/Creole around the top border.

name: Reagan Bush

It was specifically active within the United Auto Workers (UAW) union to organize opposition against management demands for contract concessions during the economic recession of that era.

The button features an Allied Printing Trades Council union bug for New York, suggesting it originates from a labor dispute in New York City, likely the 1979 school bus strike.

title: Warren Tolman GOVERNOR www.warrentolman.com

name: Election 2000 ; The New York Times

name: Vote Mondale ; At least you might live to regret it

title: AMERICA NEEDS Single Payer Health Care CWA

subject: Education, Labor Unions, Elections

title: SUPPORT WORKING FAMILIES TODAY!

subject: LGBTQ, Square Dancing, Pride

name: Dotterman
title: Dotterman
subject: Local election, New Jersey politics, Republican Party
description: A political campaign button featuring the name 'Dotterman' repeated in a pinkish-white font on a black background, produced for William J. Dotterman's 1971 campaign for Monmouth County Freeholder in New Jersey.

name: Vote Si Nov.6 Por la Paz ; Campana De Referendum Sobre Centroamerica
title: CAMPAÑA DE REFERENDUM VOTE SI NOV. 6 POR LA PAZ SOBRE CENTROAMÉRICA

The referendums were timed to coincide with the general election on November 6, 1984, and appeared on ballots in various cities across the United States. The button features a map of Central America and text in Spanish advocating for a 'Yes' vote for peace.

name: Re-Elect McGuire Boston School Committee
title: Re-Elect McGUIRE BOSTON SCHOOL COMMITTEE

name: Fund The Contract
title: FUND THE CONTRACT

This type of button was commonly used by public sector unions, such as AFSCME or the United Federation of Teachers, during the 1970s and 1980s to demand that government bodies provide the necessary budget to honor newly negotiated labor agreements.

Question 3 was the Public School Facilities Loan Act, which was ultimately defeated by voters amidst a strong anti-tax sentiment in the state during that election cycle.

party: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
subject: USSR, Anniversary, Communism
description: A commemorative lapel pin for the 50th anniversary of the Soviet Union (1922-1972), featuring the hammer and sickle symbol and the text 'USSR 50 YEARS'.

name: The Liberty and Union Taunton flag
title: THE LIBERTY and UNION -- TAUNTON FLAG FIRST RAISED ON OCTOBER 21, 1774
subject: American Revolution, Flag, Local History
description: A white pinback button with red text and graphic depicting the Taunton Flag, also known as the Liberty and Union flag. It commemorates the first raising of the flag on October 21, 1774, in Taunton, Massachusetts.

name: ERA Yes
title: ERA YES
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, feminism, women's rights
description: A green and white button advocating for the ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment (ERA).

name: Idaho potato pin
title: IDAHO
subject: Idaho, Potatoes, Agriculture
description: A brown, oval-shaped pinback button with a texture resembling a potato skin, embossed with the word 'IDAHO' and a registered trademark symbol.

name: Be friendly
title: BE FRIENDLY
subject: Sexuality, Counterculture, Humor
description: White button with black line drawings of four bare feet in a configuration suggesting two people in bed, with the caption 'BE FRIENDLY' printed at the bottom.

name: Progressive Caucus
title: PROGRESSIVE CAUCUS
subject: Progressive movement, US Congress, Democratic Party
description: A round cream-colored button featuring the blue text PROGRESSIVE CAUCUS.

name: I kicked up my Heels in Atlanta (picture of donkey)
title: I Kicked Up My Heels In ATLANTA
subject: Democratic National Convention, Atlanta, 1988
description: White button commemorating the 1988 Democratic National Convention in Atlanta, Georgia. It features a cartoon donkey, the symbol of the Democratic Party, kicking its legs and holding a blue '88' flag in its mouth.

name: Flag pin with gold Lenin profile
title: Vladimir Lenin Profile on Red Flag
party: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
subject: Communism, Lenin, USSR
description: A gold-colored and red enamel lapel pin depicting the profile of Vladimir Lenin on a stylized waving red flag.

name: Boycott Non-UFW Lettuce
title: BOYCOTT LETTUCE NON-UFW
subject: Labor Unions, Boycotts, Agriculture
description: A red, white, and black button advocating for a boycott of non-United Farm Workers (UFW) lettuce. The center features the black Aztec eagle logo of the UFW on a white circular background.

name: Stop the Arms Race! MACCAC
title: STOP THE ARMS RACE! MACCAC
subject: Peace Movement, Arms Race, Nuclear Disarmament
description: A round blue button with white text that reads 'STOP THE ARMS RACE! MACCAC'. The acronym MACCAC stands for the Milwaukee Area Citizens Committee for Arms Control, a local organization active in the anti-nuclear movement during the late 1970s and early 1980s.

name: I am a grassroot
title: I AM A GRASSROOT
subject: Grassroots, Activism, Politics
description: A green pin-back button with the text 'I AM A GRASSROOT' in white. This type of button was popular in the 1970s as a way for individuals to identify themselves as part of a grassroots political or social movement, emphasizing community-level involvement over top-down leadership.

name: Stop the Draft
title: STOP THE DRAFT
subject: anti-war, vietnam war, military draft
description: A circular pinback button featuring the slogan "STOP THE DRAFT" in bold, white, stencil-style capital letters on a solid black background. This button is a piece of anti-war protest memorabilia from the Vietnam War era, advocating against mandatory military conscription.

name: Kentucky (galloping horse)
title: Kentucky
subject: Kentucky, Horse, Souvenir
description: A blue and silver lapel pin featuring the word 'Kentucky' below a silhouette of a galloping horse.

name: Solar Employs Nuclear Destroys
title: SOLAR EMPLOYS NUCLEAR DESTROYS
subject: Solar Power, Nuclear Energy, Environmentalism
description: A circular button divided horizontally into yellow and green halves. The top yellow half features the text 'SOLAR EMPLOYS' in red. The bottom green half features the text 'NUCLEAR DESTROYS' in yellow. This button was likely produced during the height of the anti-nuclear movement in the United States in the late 1970s and early 1980s.

name: Make it in Massachusetts
title: Make it in Massachusetts
subject: Manufacturing, Economic Development, State Promotion
description: A blue button featuring a white thumbs-up icon with an outline of Massachusetts inside it, accompanied by the slogan 'Make it in Massachusetts'. This was the centerpiece of an economic development campaign initiated by Governor Michael Dukakis during the 1980s to encourage businesses to manufacture goods within the state.

name: Fight Pollution Go Hosteling [picture of old bike]
title: FIGHT POLLUTION GO HOSTELING
subject: Pollution, Hosteling, Environment
description: A yellow pin-back button featuring green text that reads 'FIGHT POLLUTION GO HOSTELING' around a central illustration of a penny-farthing bicycle. This button was produced by American Youth Hostels (AYH) around 1970, coinciding with the rise of the modern environmental movement and the first Earth Day, to promote bicycling and hosteling as sustainable alternatives to motorized travel.

name: nuclear moratorium
title: nuclear moratorium
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Peace, Nuclear weapons
description: A white button featuring a blue graphic of a bomb broken in half with a white dove in the center and a white atom symbol in the bottom section. The words 'nuclear moratorium' are printed in green lowercase letters along the bottom curve of the button.

name: Please Don't Fly New York Air
title: Please Don't Fly NEW YORK AIR
subject: labor, airline, boycott
description: Red and white button urging a boycott of New York Air, a non-union airline that operated in the 1980s and was often targeted by labor unions.

name: Moratorium Boston Common May 5 Out Now
title: Moratorium Boston Common MAY 5 Out Now
subject: Vietnam War, Anti-War, Protest
description: A yellow and red pinback button promoting an anti-Vietnam War moratorium rally at the Boston Common on May 5, 1971, featuring the popular protest slogan 'Out Now'.

name: Stop 100% Vote Yes on 1
title: STOP 100% VOTE YES ON 1
subject: Taxation, Referendum, Amendment
description: Political button advocating for a "Yes" vote on Florida's Amendment 1 in 2008, a property tax reform measure. The slogan "STOP 100%" refers to stopping full market value property assessments for tax purposes.

name: Oxfam America
title: oxfam america
subject: Hunger relief, Poverty, International aid
description: A round brown button featuring a white graphic of a stalk of wheat in the center with the words "oxfam america" printed in white along the bottom edge.

name: Stop ABM
title: STOP ABM
subject: Anti-ballistic missile, Protest, Cold War
description: A circular button featuring a red stop sign with the text 'STOP ABM' in white, used to protest the deployment of Anti-Ballistic Missile systems during the Cold War.

name: H20
title: H2O
subject: Barry Goldwater, 1964 presidential election, puns
description: A campaign button from the 1964 presidential election for Barry Goldwater. The button features a glass of gold-colored liquid labeled with the chemical formula for water, H2O, creating a visual pun for the candidate's last name.

name: CPPAX
title: CPPAX
subject: Political activism, Progressive politics, Massachusetts
description: This button is for CPPAX (Citizens for Participation in Political Action), a progressive political organization in Massachusetts formed in 1970 through the merger of Citizens for Participation Politics and PAX.

name: Toimintaan elaman puolesta! (picture of dove and globe)
title: TOIMINTAAN ELÄMÄN PUOLESTA!
subject: Peace movement, Activism, Finland
description: A Finnish political button from the early 1980s peace movement. The slogan 'TOIMINTAAN ELÄMÄN PUOLESTA!' translates to 'Take action for life!' or 'To action for life!' and was associated with a major peace appeal in Finland against the nuclear arms race. The design features a peace dove with an olive branch embracing a globe.

name: Free D.C.
title: Free D.C.
subject: Home Rule, Civil Rights, Statehood
description: A red button with white text that reads "Free D.C." It was used by the Free D.C. Movement, which was organized by Marion Barry in 1966 to advocate for home rule for the District of Columbia.

name: turned off to save energy
title: turned off to save energy
subject: Energy conservation, Environmentalism, 1970s energy crisis
description: A pin-back button promoting energy conservation, likely produced during the energy crises of the 1970s. It features the text 'turned off to save energy' in black on a white circular field, set against a black background.

name: Don't Tread On Me
title: DON'T TREAD ON ME P. B. C.
subject: Gadsden flag, American Revolution, Protest
description: A yellow button featuring a green coiled rattlesnake and the text "DON'T TREAD ON ME" and "P. B. C.", likely produced by the People's Bicentennial Commission in the mid-1970s.

name: Use alternative energy
title: USE ALTERNATIVE ENERGY
subject: Alternative energy, Renewable energy, Environmentalism
description: A yellow pin-back button featuring the text 'USE ALTERNATIVE ENERGY' in red. The center graphic depicts a smiling sun, a hand, and a palm tree in brown line art.

name: I've Got the Power
title: I've got the power
subject: Energy, Empowerment, Utilities
description: A circular yellow button featuring the phrase "I've got the power" in white sans-serif lettering. This slogan was commonly used in the 1970s and 1980s for both energy conservation campaigns by utility companies and as a general message of personal empowerment.

name: I'm for America
title: I'm for America
subject: George Wallace, Patriotism, Presidential Election
description: A political campaign button for George Wallace's 1968 presidential run, featuring the slogan 'I'm for America' in blue text above red and white horizontal stripes.

name: Don't think. Follow! Don't talk, shoot! It's the American Way
title: DON'T THINK, FOLLOW! DON'T TALK, SHOOT! IT'S THE AMERICAN WAY
subject: Anti-war, Satire, Militarism
description: A yellow button featuring the satirical slogan 'DON'T THINK, FOLLOW! DON'T TALK, SHOOT! IT'S THE AMERICAN WAY'. This button was likely produced as a piece of social commentary or anti-war protest during the Vietnam War era.

name: environment!
title: environment!
subject: Environmentalism, Conservation, Activism
description: A circular green button with the text "environment!" printed in a black, lowercase font in the center.

name: Give D.C. the thumb!
title: GIVE D.C. THE THUMB!
subject: Voting Rights, District of Columbia, Constitutional Amendment
description: A white button featuring brown text and a graphic of a hand giving a thumbs-up. This button was used as part of a campaign to support the District of Columbia Voting Rights Amendment, which would have given D.C. residents full representation in Congress.

name: I Go Pogo
title: I GO POGO
subject: satire, comic strip, election
description: White button with red text reading "I GO POGO". Includes a 1980 copyright notice for the Estate of Walt Kelly. The slogan refers to a satirical mock presidential campaign for the comic strip character Pogo Possum.

name: MIA Missing or Prisoner
title: MIA MISSING OR PRISONER
subject: POW/MIA, Vietnam War, Military
description: A black and white pinback button highlighting the issue of American service members who were Missing in Action (MIA) or Prisoners of War (POW) during the Vietnam War. It features a silhouette of a head with a question mark inside.

name: Vietnamnesty Universal Unconditional
title: UNIVERSAL UNCONDITIONAL VIETNAMNESTY
subject: Vietnam War, Amnesty, Protest
description: A white button with blue text calling for universal and unconditional amnesty for Vietnam-era draft resistors and deserters. The button was issued by the National Council for Universal & Unconditional Amnesty, an organization founded in 1973 to advocate for those who had illegally avoided military service during the Vietnam War.

name: Work for Peace Nov 13 & 14 [picture of dove with plant]
title: WORK FOR PEACE NOVEMBER 13-14
subject: Vietnam War, Peace Movement, Moratorium
description: A circular blue pinback button featuring a white graphic of a dove carrying an olive branch. The text "WORK FOR PEACE" appears at the top and "NOVEMBER 13-14" at the bottom, referring to the November 1969 Moratorium to End the War in Vietnam.

name: End the War Now!
title: END THE WAR NOW!
subject: Vietnam War, Anti-war movement, Peace
description: A white circular button with the slogan "END THE WAR NOW!" printed in red block letters. This button is a typical artifact from the protest movements against the Vietnam War in the late 1960s and early 1970s.

name: Re-elect the Congressman
title: RE-ELECT THE CONGRESSMAN
subject: election, politics, congress
description: A circular white campaign button featuring the text 'RE-ELECT THE CONGRESSMAN' in red and blue lettering.

name: cead mile failte
title: céad míle fáilte
subject: Irish greeting, Ireland, Cultural Heritage
description: A green circular button featuring the traditional Irish greeting 'céad míle fáilte' (meaning 'a hundred thousand welcomes') in white lowercase sans-serif lettering.

name: peace symbol
title: Peace Sign
subject: Peace, Anti-war, Activism
description: A circular button featuring a black peace symbol on a white background.

name: Proud to be a Democrat
title: Proud to be a DEMOCRAT.
subject: Democratic Party, political buttons, party pride
description: A circular white button featuring the phrase 'Proud to be a DEMOCRAT.' The word 'Proud' is in red, 'to be a DEMOCRAT.' is in blue, and there is a red horizontal stripe across the bottom.

name: war is not healthy for children and other living things
title: war is not healthy for children and other living things
subject: Anti-War, Peace, Vietnam War
description: A yellow button featuring a black stylized sunflower and the slogan 'war is not healthy for children and other living things,' which was the emblem for the anti-war group Another Mother for Peace founded in 1967.

name: NET [NO]
title: НЕТ
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Cold War, Soviet propaganda
description: A Soviet-era pin featuring the Russian word 'НЕТ' (meaning 'No') in blue lettering over a white globe. Two black missiles point toward each other, one of which is marked with an 'N,' likely representing the 'No to the Neutron Bomb' campaign common in Soviet anti-war propaganda of the late 1970s and early 1980s.

name: ADA
title: ADA
subject: Politics, Liberalism, Advocacy
description: A white pin-back button featuring the acronym 'ADA' in blue text, representing the Americans for Democratic Action, a liberal political organization founded in 1947.

name: I'm for jobs & justice Massachusetts AFL-CIO
title: I'm for Jobs & Justice
subject: Labor Unions, Social Justice, Employment
description: A circular button with a yellow top half and a blue bottom half. The yellow half features the text 'I'm for Jobs & Justice' in blue. The blue half features the text 'Massachusetts AFL-CIO' in yellow above the Massachusetts AFL-CIO logo, which depicts a handshake over the state of Massachusetts.

name: Profile of Lenin pin
title: Vladimir Lenin Profile with Laurel Branch
party: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
subject: Communism, Soviet Union, Vladimir Lenin
description: A rectangular pin with a gold border and a red enamel background. It depicts a gold profile of Vladimir Lenin facing left, with a gold laurel branch positioned below him.

name: Wyoming
title: wyoming
subject: Wyoming, Cowboy, Bucking Bronco
description: A gold-colored souvenir pin shaped like a bucking bronco and rider with the text 'wyoming' across the middle.

name: Bring Justice to America's Fields in '76
title: BRING JUSTICE TO AMERICA'S FIELDS IN '76
subject: United Farm Workers, Agriculture, Labor rights
description: A political button supporting farmworkers' rights in 1976, featuring the United Farm Workers (UFW) eagle logo and an image of a field hand. It likely refers to California's Proposition 14 from that year, which sought to secure collective bargaining rights for agricultural workers.

name: Vermont Green up Environ Month
title: Vermont green up ENVIRON MONTH
subject: Environmentalism, Community, Conservation
description: A green and white button promoting Vermont's Green Up Day initiative, featuring a stylized plant logo and the text 'Vermont green up ENVIRON MONTH'.

name: Indians of all tribes Alcatraz Indian land
title: INDIANS OF ALL TRIBES ALCATRAZ INDIAN LAND NOV 20 1969
subject: Native American activism, Alcatraz occupation, Indigenous rights
description: Red button with black graphics depicting a teepee in front of a suspension bridge. The text commemorates the November 20, 1969, start of the occupation of Alcatraz Island by the activist group Indians of All Tribes.

name: Boycott Non-UFW Grapes
title: BOYCOTT NON-UFW GRAPES
subject: Labor Unions, Agriculture, Boycotts
description: A button supporting the United Farm Workers (UFW) grape boycott, featuring the Aztec eagle logo and calling for a boycott of grapes not produced by UFW union labor.

name: Stop the bombing Out now!
title: STOP THE BOMBING! OUT NOW!
subject: Vietnam War, Anti-war movement, Peace
description: An anti-Vietnam War protest button featuring a silhouette of a bomber plane and two huddled figures within a red target crosshair, accompanied by the slogans "STOP THE BOMBING!" and "OUT NOW!".

name: Smoking Stinks
title: SMOKING STINKS
subject: anti-smoking, health, American Cancer Society
description: An anti-smoking pinback button featuring the slogan 'Smoking Stinks' with a floral and bird design, produced by the American Cancer Society.

name: Jan 20 '73 (dove)
title: JAN 20 '73
subject: Anti-war, Vietnam War, Peace
description: A blue and white pinback button featuring a stylized white dove and the text 'JAN 20 '73'. This button was produced for the anti-war protests that occurred in Washington, D.C. during Richard Nixon's second presidential inauguration.

name: U.S. Senator Blutarsky (picture of John Belushi)
title: U.S. Senator Blutarsky
subject: Animal House, Movie, Comedy
description: A novelty button featuring John Belushi as his character John "Bluto" Blutarsky from the 1978 film National Lampoon's Animal House. The button's text refers to the epilogue of the movie which reveals that the character becomes a U.S. Senator.

name: the year of choices March 13 September 18 November 6 '84
title: the year of choices '84
subject: elections, voting, 1984
description: A red button with white text that reads 'the year of choices '84' and lists three dates: March 13, September 18, and November 6. These dates correspond to the presidential primary (Super Tuesday), the state primary, and the general election day in 1984 for the state of Massachusetts.

name: 1 of a kind
title: 1 of a Kind
subject: Self-worth, Individuality, Novelty
description: A circular novelty button with a white background featuring a large blue number '1' and two red stars, with 'of a kind' written in cursive script below. The design is framed by a blue ring with white dots and an outer red-and-white sunburst pattern.

name: Vote Democratic
title: VOTE DEMOCRATIC
subject: Democratic Party, Donkey, Campaign
description: A vintage die-cut lithographed tin tab button in the shape of a kicking donkey, the symbol of the Democratic Party. The text 'VOTE DEMOCRATIC' is printed in red on the white body of the donkey.

name: I Support Vietnam Veterans Against war
title: I SUPPORT VIETNAM VETERANS AGAINST THE WAR
subject: Vietnam War, Anti-war, VVAW
description: White pinback button with the logo of the Vietnam Veterans Against the War, which consists of a red shield with a yellow border containing a white helmet resting on a rifle. The text 'I SUPPORT VIETNAM VETERANS AGAINST THE WAR' is printed in black around the logo.

name: My Heart Is On The Left. ADA
title: MY HEART IS ON THE LEFT. ADA
subject: Americans for Democratic Action, Liberalism, Progressive politics
description: A red pinback button featuring the slogan 'MY HEART IS ON THE LEFT.' above the acronym 'ADA,' which stands for Americans for Democratic Action, a progressive political organization founded in 1947.

name: Better Home Heat Council
title: BETTER HOME HEAT COUNCIL Oilheat
subject: Oil heat, Industry, Advertising
description: A rectangular promotional pin for the Better Home Heat Council, featuring the Oilheat brand logo. The pin is red, gold, and blue.

name: Congress Lives
title: CONGRESS LIVES
subject: D.C. Statehood, Home Rule, Activism
description: A white button with black text reading 'CONGRESS LIVES'. This button was used by the D.C. Statehood Party in the early 1970s as part of the movement to advocate for home rule and representation for the residents of Washington, D.C.

name: American and Israeli flags
title: None
subject: United States flag, Israeli flag, alliance
description: Metal lapel pin with enamel coloring depicting crossed American and Israeli flags.

name: Help! (picture of crowd)
title: HELP!
subject: alienation, crowd, social commentary
description: A white button featuring a black-and-white cartoon illustration of a dense crowd of people. One person in the middle has a speech bubble containing the word 'HELP!'.

name: April 14-15 [fist]
title: APRIL 14-15
subject: Student strike, Activism, New Left
description: A white button featuring a red clenched fist graphic and the text 'APRIL 14-15' at the bottom. This button is associated with the student strike at Harvard University in April 1969, protesting the Vietnam War and the presence of ROTC on campus.

name: Students Against Driving Drunk SADD
title: STUDENTS AGAINST DRIVING DRUNK SADD
subject: SADD, Drunk Driving, Youth Advocacy
description: A red, white, and black button for the organization Students Against Driving Drunk (SADD), featuring a 'no' symbol over a bottle.

name: When this button melts you are in the midst of an atomic attack
title: WHEN THIS BUTTON MELTS, YOU ARE IN THE MIDST OF AN ATOMIC ATTACK
subject: Anti-nuclear warfare, Cold War, Peace movement
description: A white button with black lettering that delivers a darkly satirical message about the lethality of nuclear weapons. It was a common artifact of the Cold War era anti-nuclear movement, highlighting the futility of survival in the event of an atomic blast.

name: Stop the Draft March 22 Washington DC
title: March 22 STOP THE DRAFT March on Washington
subject: conscription, protest, anti-war
description: This button was produced for the March on Washington held on March 22, 1980, to protest President Jimmy Carter's proposal to reinstitute military draft registration following the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan.

name: Suppose they gave a war â¦and nobody came
title: SUPPOSE THEY GAVE A WAR ....AND NOBODY CAME
subject: Anti-war, Vietnam War, Peace
description: A yellow rectangular pin-back button with the text "SUPPOSE THEY GAVE A WAR ....AND NOBODY CAME" in black. The design features a stylized orange flame and a black silhouette of a person against a black and yellow checkered background. This slogan, originally from a poem by Carl Sandburg, became a popular anti-war sentiment during the Vietnam War era.

name: Save Energy Boston YWCA
title: SAVE ENERGY Boston YWCA
subject: Energy conservation, YWCA, Economy
description: A white button with blue print featuring the text 'SAVE ENERGY' and 'Boston YWCA'. The central graphic is a circle with a diagonal line through it, containing an image of a burning dollar bill, which illustrates the cost of energy waste.

name: Shalom means Peace
title: SHALOM means PEACE
subject: Israel, Peace, Judaism
description: Button featuring light blue stripes on a white background with text "SHALOM means PEACE". The letter 'A' in both words is replaced with a blue Star of David.

name: Ban Handguns
title: BAN HANDGUNS
subject: Gun Control, Handguns, Activism
description: A circular button featuring a stylized silhouette of a handgun with the text 'BAN HANDGUNS' in white.

name: Fight Sexism Support the Cornell 11
title: FIGHT SEXISM Support the Cornell 11
subject: Sexism, Cornell University, Women's rights
description: A button supporting the 'Cornell 11', a group of female faculty members who sued Cornell University for sex discrimination in tenure decisions in the early 1980s.

name: No on One
title: NO ON ONE
subject: Same-sex marriage, Referendum, LGBTQ rights
description: This button was used during the 2009 Maine campaign to oppose Question 1, a ballot referendum that sought to and ultimately did repeal the state's newly enacted same-sex marriage law.

name: I'm voting Democratic on Nov. 5th
title: I'M VOTING Democratic Nov. 5th
subject: election, Democratic Party, voting
description: A white button with blue text that reads "I'M VOTING Democratic Nov. 5th". While several presidential elections have occurred on November 5th, the typography and design of this button are most closely associated with the 1968 campaign season.

name: Ride a Bike for the Retarded May 4, 1980
title: RIDE A BIKE FOR THE RETARDED MAY 4, 1980
subject: Charity, Bicycling, Disabilities
description: A blue button from May 4, 1980, promoting a bicycle ride fundraiser for people with intellectual disabilities, using terminology common at the time.

name: They Lie
title: THEY LIE
subject: anti-nuclear, environmentalism, protest
description: A pink protest button designed by cartoonist Jules Feiffer, featuring the text 'THEY LIE' where the letter 'I' is represented by an illustration of a nuclear cooling tower emitting a cloud of smoke.

name: Why not coal? UMWA (nuclear plant towers)
title: Why Not Coal? UMWA
subject: Coal, Energy, Union
description: A button issued by the United Mine Workers of America (UMWA) advocating for the use of coal over nuclear power, featuring silhouettes of nuclear cooling towers.

name: $2 in '74 for Cope
title: $2 in '74 for COPE
subject: Labor, Fundraising, COPE
description: A button for the AFL-CIO's Committee on Political Education (COPE) for the 1974 election cycle, asking for a $2 donation.

name: No deposit No return! [Statue of Liberty in a landfill]
title: NO DEPOSIT NO RETURN !!
subject: environment, pollution, recycling
description: A pin-back button featuring the Statue of Liberty surrounded by trash with a speech bubble saying 'No Deposit No Return !!' to protest the lack of recycling for single-use containers.

name: MAC
title: MAC
subject: Presidential campaign; Douglas MacArthur; Republican Party
description: A blue and white campaign button for General Douglas MacArthur's 1948 presidential campaign featuring his nickname 'MAC'.

name: If you don't register and Vote Don't Complain
title: If you don't REGISTER and VOTE Don't Complain
subject: Voter registration, Voting, Civic duty
description: A yellow pin-back button featuring black text that reads, "If you don't REGISTER and VOTE Don't Complain," encouraging citizens to participate in the democratic process.
