name: Stop the Draft
title: STOP THE DRAFT
subject: Anti-war, Vietnam War, Conscription
description: A red pinback button with the slogan 'STOP THE DRAFT' in white stencil font. It was commonly worn during the Vietnam War era as a form of protest against the mandatory military draft in the United States.

name: Pro Life is many issues
title: Pro Life is many issues
subject: abortion;social issues;consistent life ethic
description: A red political button with white text that reads 'Pro Life is many issues' in the center. Around the edge, several other political and social issues are listed: environment, elderly, handicapped, energy, and inflation. This suggests a 'seamless garment' or consistent life ethic approach to the pro-life movement, which gained traction in the 1970s and 1980s.

name: Republican
title: REPUBLICAN
subject: Republican Party, voting, elections
description: A vintage political button featuring a white 'X' mark and the word 'REPUBLICAN' on a black background. Buttons like this were used to instruct or remind voters how to mark their paper ballots for the Republican party.

name: Wearing Buttons is not Enough
title: WEARING BUTTONS is not enough
subject: activism, political action, social change
description: A green circular pinback button with the phrase "WEARING BUTTONS is not enough" printed in yellow-gold capital letters. The button serves as a call to action, suggesting that symbolic gestures like wearing pins are insufficient for achieving political or social goals.

name: Massachusetts the Lone Star State
title: MASSACHUSETTS THE LONE STAR STATE
subject: 1972 presidential election, George McGovern, Massachusetts politics
description: This button refers to the 1972 United States presidential election, in which Massachusetts was the only state won by Democratic candidate George McGovern against incumbent Richard Nixon. The phrase "Lone Star State" is traditionally the nickname of Texas, but here it is used ironically to highlight Massachusetts' solitary status in the Electoral College outcome.

name: McCormack Can Win
title: McCORMACK CAN WIN
subject: Edward J. McCormack Jr., Political campaign, 1962 Election
description: Red, white, and blue political campaign button for Edward J. McCormack Jr.'s 1962 U.S. Senate bid in Massachusetts. The button features a sketch of McCormack in the center with his name on a red background and the slogan 'CAN WIN' on a blue background.

name: U.S. Out of El Salvador
title: U.S. OUT OF EL SALVADOR
subject: El Salvador, Foreign Policy, Protest
description: A black and white button with the slogan 'U.S. OUT OF EL SALVADOR' and a stylized portrait of a man, likely a Salvadoran peasant or revolutionary. This button was used to protest United States military involvement and aid during the Salvadoran Civil War in the 1980s.

name: The Man For All Reasons
title: THE MAN FOR ALL REASONS
subject: Canadian Federal Politics, Progressive Conservative Party, Leadership Campaign
description: A rectangular campaign button for Brian Mulroney, likely from his 1983 Progressive Conservative leadership bid or the 1984 Canadian federal election. It features a blue monochrome portrait of Mulroney on the left and the slogan "THE MAN FOR ALL REASONS" in white text over red and white stripes on the right.

name: Zionism Yes Racism No
title: ZIONISM YES RACISM NO
subject: Zionism, Racism, United Nations Resolution 3379
description: A button with blue text on a white background that reads 'ZIONISM YES RACISM NO'. This button likely serves as a response to United Nations Resolution 3379, passed in 1975, which defined Zionism as a form of racism.

name: Fourth Liberty Loan
title: FOURTH LIBERTY LOAN
subject: World War I, Liberty Loans, War Bonds
description: A blue circular button with white text reading "FOURTH LIBERTY LOAN". The central image is a flag with four blue stripes on a white field with a red border, which was the honor flag of the Fourth Liberty Loan campaign during World War I.

name: Оlimpiskie igri Moskva-80 [Olympic games Moscow 1980]  | Olympic games Moscow 1980
title: ОЛИМПИЙСКИЕ ИГРЫ МОСКВА-80
subject: 1980 Summer Olympics, Diving, Soviet Union
description: A red rectangular pin shaped like a banner, featuring the Olympic rings at the top and a circular center piece with a diver. The text around the diver reads 'ОЛИМПИЙСКИЕ ИГРЫ МОСКВА-80' in Cyrillic.

name: Can you do it? 25 or die May 9, 1971
title: CAN YOU DO IT? 25 OR DIE MAY 9, 1971
subject: Hunger, Walk for Development, Activism
description: A yellow button with blue text and a pair of footprints. The button commemorates the Walk for Development held on May 9, 1971, where participants walked long distances to raise money for global poverty and hunger relief.

name: Why do we kill people who kill people to show people that killing people is wrong?
title: WHY DO WE KILL PEOPLE WHO KILL PEOPLE TO SHOW PEOPLE THAT KILLING PEOPLE IS WRONG?
subject: Death Penalty, Capital Punishment, Social Justice
description: A white button featuring a green circular border and green text. The slogan is a rhetorical question frequently used in advocacy against the death penalty.

name: I'm a Freeze voter
title: I'm a FREEZE Voter
subject: Nuclear weapons, Disarmament, Peace
description: This circular button was produced by the Nuclear Weapons Freeze movement, which was a grassroots campaign in the 1980s that advocated for a bilateral agreement between the United States and the Soviet Union to halt the testing, production, and deployment of nuclear weapons. 'Freeze Voter' was a national political action committee formed to organize voters around this issue during the 1984 elections.

name: America: Discover the Great State of Maine
title: AMERICA Discover The Great State of Maine.
subject: Maine, Tourism, United States Bicentennial
description: A circular black button with the word 'AMERICA' in blue and red block letters above the slogan 'Discover The Great State of Maine.' in white text. The patriotic coloring suggests it may be from the United States Bicentennial era.

name: League of Women Voters Debate 82
title: LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS DEBATES 82
subject: Voting, Debates, League of Women Voters
description: Rectangular white button with green and blue text reading 'LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS DEBATES 82' and a blue circular logo.

name: People love our parks.
title: People love our parks.
subject: parks, environment, conservation
description: A circular white button featuring a green silhouette of a tree and the text 'People love our parks.' in black serif font.

name: Remember Oct. 9
title: Remember OCT. 9
subject: Revolution, Activism, Memorial
description: This button commemorates the death of Marxist revolutionary Che Guevara, who was executed in Bolivia on October 9, 1967.

name: People Above Politics
title: PEOPLE ABOVE POLITICS
subject: Slogans, Smiley face, Presidential elections
description: Yellow button featuring a blue smiley face and the slogan 'PEOPLE ABOVE POLITICS', which was the official campaign slogan for George McGovern during his 1972 presidential run.

name: Stop Acid Rain
title: STOP ACID RAIN
subject: Environmentalism, Acid Rain, Pollution
description: A white pinback button with a red stop sign graphic and white text that reads 'STOP ACID RAIN'.

name: Canadian maple leaf
title: Red Maple Leaf
subject: Canada, Symbol, Nationalism
description: A small lapel pin featuring a red enamel maple leaf, the national symbol of Canada, outlined in silver-toned metal.

name: Boycott Non-union Lettuce
title: BOYCOTT NON-UNION LETTUCE
subject: Labor Unions, Farm Workers, Boycotts
description: A red button featuring the black Aztec eagle logo of the United Farm Workers (UFW) with the text 'BOYCOTT NON-UNION LETTUCE'. It was used during the UFW lettuce boycott campaign in the 1970s.

name: Amnesty is Justice
title: AMNESTY IS JUSTICE
subject: Amnesty, Vietnam War, Justice
description: A black pinback button with white text reading "AMNESTY IS JUSTICE", a slogan associated with the movement for unconditional amnesty for Vietnam War draft evaders and military deserters during the mid-1970s.

name: I support Full Funding for IDEA
title: I Support It's A Guarantee Not Just A Promise Full Funding for IDEA
subject: Education, Disability Rights, Federal Funding
description: A white button with red and blue text advocating for full federal funding of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA), featuring the logo of the Council for Exceptional Children.

name: Noise
title: NOISE
subject: noise pollution, environment, activism
description: A white button with the word 'NOISE' in black, crossed out by a red prohibition symbol.

name: The Democratic Agenda
title: THE DEMOCRATIC AGENDA
subject: Progressive politics, Democratic Party, Social reform
description: A circular blue button with white text that reads 'THE DEMOCRATIC AGENDA'. This button represents a coalition of progressive organizations, labor unions, and liberal activists, including the Democratic Socialist Organizing Committee (DSOC), which sought to influence the Democratic Party platform in the late 1970s and early 1980s.

name: Let the People Vote on War Vietnam Referendum 70
title: LET THE PEOPLE VOTE ON WAR VIETNAM REFERENDUM 70
subject: Vietnam War, Peace Movement, Referendum
description: A circular button featuring a peace sign design with yellow, black, and white sections, advocating for a referendum on the Vietnam War in 1970, a movement particularly active in Massachusetts.

name: Let's Stop Paying the Fat Cat's Share. Vote "Yes" on Question 6.
title: LET'S STOP PAYING THE FAT CAT'S SHARE. VOTE "YES" ON QUESTION 6.
subject: Taxation, Ballot Measures, Income Tax
description: This button was used in the 1976 Massachusetts campaign to promote a 'Yes' vote on Question 6, which proposed amending the state constitution to permit a graduated income tax. The imagery of the 'fat cat' was a common symbol used by proponents to suggest that a graduated tax would ensure wealthy individuals paid their fair share.

name: Vietnam for the Vietnamese
title: VIETNAM FOR THE VIETNAMESE
subject: Anti-war, Vietnam, Peace
description: A yellow circular button featuring red text that reads "VIETNAM FOR THE VIETNAMESE" alongside a black graphic illustration of a stylized figure holding a pole with banners.

name: Oxfam America
title: oxfam america
subject: hunger, poverty relief, humanitarian aid
description: An orange round button featuring the logo for Oxfam America, consisting of a white stylized wheat stalk above the lowercase text "oxfam america".

name: eagle and dove
title: [Hawk and Dove]
subject: Peace, Anti-war, Vietnam War
description: A circular black and white button featuring a stylized graphic of a hawk's head. The white negative space within the hawk's silhouette forms the shape of a dove. This design represents the ideological conflict between 'hawks' (those favoring military intervention) and 'doves' (those favoring peace), a prominent theme during the Vietnam War era.

name: A Clean Sweep Vote Democrat
title: A CLEAN-SWEEP! VOTE DEMOCRAT
subject: Democratic Party, Elections, Political Slogans
description: A campaign button featuring a kicking donkey, the symbol of the Democratic Party, with the slogan 'A Clean-Sweep! Vote Democrat' in blue text on a cream background.

name: John Sears for City Council
title: John Sears for City Council
subject: John Sears, City Council, Local Politics
description: Campaign button supporting John Sears for a seat on the Boston City Council. The button features white text on a blue background.

name: My Heart Is On The Left. ADA
title: MY HEART IS ON THE LEFT. ADA
subject: Americans for Democratic Action, Liberalism, Progressivism
description: A blue and white button featuring the punny slogan 'MY HEART IS ON THE LEFT.' above the acronym 'ADA', which stands for Americans for Democratic Action, a long-standing progressive political organization in the United States.

name: Earth Day April 22
title: EARTH DAY APRIL 22
subject: Earth Day, Ecology, Environment
description: A pin-back button for Earth Day, featuring the ecology symbol in blue and green on a white background with the text 'EARTH DAY' and 'APRIL 22'.

name: A AFSCME/SEIU Alliance AFL-CIO
title: AFSCME/SEIU ALLIANCE AFL-CIO
subject: Labor Unions, Collective Bargaining, Public Employees
description: A button for the AFSCME/SEIU Alliance, a coalition between the American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees and the Service Employees International Union. This alliance was prominent in the mid-1970s, specifically representing state workers in California.

name: OKIE
title: OKIE
subject: State Pride, Environment, Natural Resources
description: A gold-toned lapel pin featuring the word 'OKIE'. The letter 'O' contains a blue teardrop, likely representing water resources, and the letter 'I' contains a green pine tree, representing forestry. This pin was part of a campaign popularized by Governor Dewey Bartlett to promote state pride and rebrand the term 'Okie'.

name: Support Public Education Reading, Writing, not Rhetoric! Mta
title: SUPPORT PUBLIC EDUCATION READING, WRITING, NOT RHETORIC!
subject: Public Education, Massachusetts Teachers Association, Union Advocacy
description: A white button with blue text reading "SUPPORT PUBLIC EDUCATION" above a blue chalkboard graphic containing the slogan "READING, WRITING, NOT RHETORIC!". The logo for the Massachusetts Teachers Association (MTA) is at the bottom.

name: Permanent Friends No Common Cause Permanent Enemies No Common Cause
title: PERMANENT FRIENDS NO COMMON CAUSE / NO PERMANENT ENEMIES
subject: Non-partisanship, Political activism, Common Cause
description: A circular button split horizontally into blue and yellow halves, promoting the non-partisan organization Common Cause. The top blue half reads 'PERMANENT FRIENDS NO COMMON CAUSE' in yellow text. The bottom yellow half is inverted and features blue text that reads 'PERMANENT ENEMIES NO COMMON CAUSE', reflecting the organization's philosophy of having no permanent allies or enemies in their pursuit of civic reform.

name: Re-Think Mississippi
title: RE-THINK MISSISSIPPI
subject: State Promotion, Economic Development, Education Reform
description: A button featuring the slogan "RE-THINK MISSISSIPPI" in red and blue. The design includes a blue line that loops back under the state name and forms an arrow pointing upwards. This slogan was associated with Governor William Winter's efforts to modernize Mississippi's image and education system in the late 1970s and early 1980s.

name: Save the libraries
title: SAVE THE LIBRARIES
subject: libraries, advocacy, funding
description: A circular white pin-back button with the text 'SAVE THE LIBRARIES' printed in bold red capital letters.

name: I get a kick out of The Rendon Report Democratic State Convention April 9, 1983
title: I get a kick out of The Rendon Report
subject: Democratic Party, Political media, Massachusetts
description: A white button with blue text and a blue donkey kicking above an outline of Massachusetts. The text reads 'I get a kick out of The Rendon Report' and identifies the 1983 Democratic State Convention. 'The Rendon Report' refers to communications by political strategist John Rendon.

name: Stop Nuclear Power! Demonstrate at Shoreham June 3 SHAD Alliance
title: Stop Nuclear Power! Demonstrate at Shoreham June 3 SHAD Alliance
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Environmentalism, Protests
description: A red button with black and white text encouraging a demonstration against nuclear power at the Shoreham Nuclear Power Plant on June 3rd, 1979, organized by the SHAD Alliance (Sound-Hudson Against Atomic Development).

name: The Right to Abortion can be a Woman's Right to Life
title: THE RIGHT TO ABORTION CAN BE A WOMAN'S RIGHT TO LIFE
subject: Abortion, Reproductive Rights, Women's Rights
description: A white button with green text that states: THE RIGHT TO ABORTION CAN BE A WOMAN'S RIGHT TO LIFE. The phrase 'WOMAN'S RIGHT' is underlined.

name: 2/3 Blind are Women, The Women's Eye Heath Task Force, Let's change it!
title: 2/3 Blind are Women Let's Change It!
subject: Women's health, Blindness, Awareness
description: A red pin-back button created by The Women's Eye Health Task Force to raise awareness that two-thirds of the world's blind population are women. It features the website for the Schepens Eye Research Institute at Harvard.

name: Withdraw All the Troops Now Vote "C" Vietnam Referendum 70
title: WITHDRAW ALL THE TROOPS NOW VOTE "C" VIETNAM REFERENDUM 70
subject: Vietnam War, Peace, Election
description: A button advocating for Proposition C in the 1970 San Francisco election, which called for the immediate withdrawal of all American troops from Vietnam.

name: B.E.R.C. 1933
title: B.E.R.C. 1933
subject: New Deal, NRA, Economy
description: A blue button featuring a white star above the text 'B.E.R.C. 1933'. B.E.R.C. likely stands for the Blue Eagle Recovery Campaign or Council, which was associated with the National Recovery Administration (NRA), a key part of Franklin D. Roosevelt's New Deal program during the Great Depression.

name: Viva la causa
title: VIVA LA CAUSA
subject: Chicano Movement, Labor Rights, Civil Rights
description: A button featuring a portrait of Mexican Revolutionary leader Emiliano Zapata with the text 'VIVA LA CAUSA' (Long Live the Cause). This slogan and imagery were adopted by the United Farm Workers (UFW) and the Chicano movement in the 1960s and 1970s.

name: 59 cents
subject: pay equity, women's rights, gender wage gap
description: A green circular button with white text that reads '59¢'. This button was a popular symbol of the second-wave feminist movement in the late 1970s, representing the statistic that women earned 59 cents for every dollar earned by men.

name: Bike for a better city
title: BIKE FOR A BETTER CITY
subject: Bicycling, Advocacy, Transportation
description: Yellow button featuring a bicycle logo and the text "BIKE FOR A BETTER CITY" in brown. This slogan was used by a New York City bicycle advocacy group active in the early 1970s that campaigned for bike lanes and improved urban cycling infrastructure.

name: It's Your Turn Now Get out and VOTE
title: It's your turn now Get out and VOTE
subject: Voting; Uncle Sam; Get Out The Vote
description: A white round button featuring an illustration of Uncle Sam pointing at the viewer. The text reads "It's your turn now" in blue script around the top and "Get out and VOTE" in red script in the center. The design includes two blue stars.

name: The Pols
title: THE POLS
subject: Music, Band, Power Pop
description: A stick pin featuring the name of the San Francisco power pop band 'The Pols'. The design includes stars and vertical stripes, common motifs in late 1970s and early 1980s graphic design.

name: Help first Fight Drug Abuse
title: HELP HELP FIGHT DRUG ABUSE
subject: Drug abuse, Smiley face, Public health
description: A round white pinback button featuring a yellow smiley face graphic. The word 'HELP' is inscribed within each eye, and the phrase 'FIGHT DRUG ABUSE' is written along the curve of the smiling mouth. This style of button was popular during the early 1970s following the rise of the smiley face icon and the start of the 'War on Drugs' in the United States.

name: Massachusetts Democratic delegation '80
title: '80 MASSACHUSETTS DEMOCRATIC DELEGATION
subject: Democratic Party, 1980 Convention, Massachusetts
description: A circular red and white button for the Massachusetts Democratic Delegation to the 1980 Democratic National Convention. It features a white wedge at the top with the year ''80' in red, and the text 'MASSACHUSETTS DEMOCRATIC DELEGATION' in white around the lower perimeter.

name: Love
title: LOVE
subject: Judaism, Love, Jewish Identity
description: A small white button featuring the word 'LOVE' in blue sans-serif letters, where a blue Star of David is used in place of the letter 'O'.

name: Massachusetts pollution fighter division of water pollution control
title: massachusetts pollution fighter
subject: pollution, water, environment
description: Blue and white button promoting water pollution control in Massachusetts, featuring the state seal and issued by the Division of Water Pollution Control.

name: May 14th Walk for Hunger
title: MAY 14th WALK FOR HUNGER
subject: hunger, charity, social activism
description: A black and white button for a 'Walk for Hunger' event held on May 14th. The center graphic depicts a stylized globe with a bite taken out of it, possibly representing the 'CROP' logo (Christian Rural Overseas Program), which frequently organizes such walks.

name: I support the Acorn commission
title: I support the ACORN COMMISSION
subject: ACORN, internal investigation, community organizing
description: A button supporting the ACORN Commission, an independent panel established in 2009 by the Association of Community Organizations for Reform Now (ACORN) to investigate its internal management and operations following several high-profile controversies.

name: AFL-CIO American Federation Employees (picture of Capitol)
title: AMERICAN FEDERATION EMPLOYEES STATE COUNTY AFL-CIO MUNICIPAL
subject: Labor union, AFSCME, AFL-CIO
description: A round gold-colored lapel pin for the American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees (AFSCME). It features the union's former triangular logo with a capitol dome in the center and the letters 'AFL-CIO' in a green banner.

name: We Are One
title: We Are One
subject: Jewish Unity, Philanthropy, Solidarity
description: A white pinback button featuring the slogan 'We Are One' in blue text, with the Hebrew translation 'עם אחד' (Am Echad) below it. This slogan was a major theme for the United Jewish Appeal (UJA) in the 1970s.

name: Vote for Kindness
title: VOTE FOR KINDNESS
subject: Kindness, Virtue, Novelty
description: A red, white, and blue pinback button featuring the slogan "VOTE FOR KINDNESS" in a style reminiscent of a political campaign. It contains two blue stars and a copyright notice for 1982 by Allan J. Coffee.

name: Third Liberty Loan [Liberty Bell]
title: THIRD LIBERTY LOAN
subject: War Finance, World War I, Liberty Loans
description: A red, white, and blue pinback button from 1918 promoting the Third Liberty Loan war bond drive, featuring an illustration of the Liberty Bell.

name: Nuclear Power? No Thanks
title: NUCLEAR POWER? NO THANKS
subject: Anti-nuclear, Environment, Energy
description: A yellow button featuring the 'Smiling Sun' logo, which consists of a red-orange smiling sun surrounded by the text 'NUCLEAR POWER? NO THANKS'. Designed in 1975 in Denmark, this logo became the international symbol for the anti-nuclear movement.

name: Jay
title: Jay
subject: politics, West Virginia, campaign
description: A circular red button featuring the name "Jay" in large white letters in the center, with the name "Jay" repeated in a smaller blue font throughout the background. The button was used in political campaigns for John D. "Jay" Rockefeller IV in West Virginia.

name: pssu your union.
title: pssu your union.
subject: Labor Unions, Social Services, Pennsylvania
description: A circular yellow button with orange text and an oval border. The text 'pssu' is prominent in the center, with 'your union.' written below it. PSSU refers to the Pennsylvania Social Services Union, which represents social service workers and other public employees in Pennsylvania.

name: coyote: a loose woman's organization
title: COYOTE A LOOSE WOMAN'S ORGANIZATION
subject: sex worker rights, feminism, prostitution
description: Button for COYOTE (Call Off Your Old Tired Ethics), a sex workers' rights organization founded in San Francisco in 1973. It features the group's name and the phrase 'A Loose Woman's Organization' with a stylized coyote graphic.

name: No Hunger
title: HUNGER
subject: hunger, poverty, activism
description: A pin-back button featuring the word "HUNGER" in bold black letters with a red prohibition sign (circle and slash) over it, symbolizing the campaign to end hunger.

name: Catholics for a Free Choice
title: Catholics for a Free Choice
subject: Abortion, Catholicism, Reproductive rights
description: A blue button with white text reading 'Catholics for a Free Choice', representing the pro-choice advocacy organization founded in 1973 that later changed its name to Catholics for Choice in 2007.

name: Boycott J.P. Stevens
title: Boycott J.P. STEVENS
subject: Labor unions, Boycott, Textile industry
description: A red and white button from the late 1970s calling for a boycott of J.P. Stevens & Co., a major textile manufacturer, due to its anti-union practices and poor working conditions.

name: soldier
subject: LGBTQ+ Rights, Military, Pink Triangle
description: This button features a pixelated graphic of a soldier's profile wearing a green helmet with a pink triangle symbol. The pink triangle is an emblem used to represent the LGBTQ+ community, originally a Nazi concentration camp badge that was reclaimed as a symbol of pride and activism. The use of this symbol on a military helmet likely references LGBTQ+ service members and the debates surrounding their service, such as the 'Don't Ask, Don't Tell' policy in the United States.

name: Make It In Massachusetts
title: Make it in Massachusetts
subject: Economic development, Manufacturing, State promotion
description: A circular blue button with white text 'Make it in Massachusetts' surrounding a graphic of a thumbs-up hand that is also shaped like the state of Massachusetts. This was part of a major state economic development campaign launched in the late 1970s under Governor Michael Dukakis.

name: Medical Aid for Indochina
title: MEDICAL AID FOR INDOCHINA
subject: Vietnam War, humanitarian aid, anti-war movement
description: A button promoting medical aid for Indochina, featuring the colors and star of the National Liberation Front (Viet Cong) flag. This button was likely produced by the organization Medical Aid for Indochina (MAI), founded in 1971 to send medical supplies to areas of Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia affected by the war.

name: Profile of Lenin pin
title: Portrait of Vladimir Lenin
party: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
subject: Communism, Soviet Union, Vladimir Lenin
description: A rectangular enamel lapel pin featuring a silver-colored profile of Vladimir Lenin wearing a cap and a red bow on his lapel, set against a red textured background.

name: Vsegda gotov! [Always ready!]  | Pioneer pin with profile of Lenin
title: ВСЕГДА ГОТОВ!
party: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
subject: Young Pioneers, Communism, Soviet Union
description: A Soviet Young Pioneers pin featuring a profile of Vladimir Lenin inside a red star, topped with three tongues of flame representing the three generations of Communists, Komsomol, and Pioneers. The text 'ВСЕГДА ГОТОВ!' translates to 'Always Ready!'.

name: Boycott Grapes
title: BOYCOTT GRAPES
subject: labor union, boycott, agriculture
description: This button features the United Farm Workers (UFW) eagle logo and was produced during the Delano grape strike and subsequent boycott to advocate for better working conditions for farm laborers.

name: We Can Make A World of Difference
title: We Can Make A WORLD OF DIFFERENCE
subject: Anti-bias, Diversity, Tolerance
description: A circular white button with blue text reading "We Can Make A WORLD OF DIFFERENCE" above five overlapping silhouettes of human profiles in yellow, pink, purple, blue, and green. This is a promotional button for the Anti-Defamation League's (ADL) "A World of Difference" program, an initiative started in 1985 to promote diversity and combat prejudice through education.

name: Liberty Loan V
title: LIBERTY V LOAN
subject: World War I, Victory Loan, War Bonds
description: A blue circular button with white text reading "LIBERTY V LOAN" and a large white "V" in the center, signifying the Fifth or Victory Liberty Loan campaign of 1919.

name: Boycott Scab Lettuce
title: BOYCOTT SCAB LETTUCE
subject: Labor rights, United Farm Workers, Boycott
description: A red and black button featuring the United Farm Workers (UFW) eagle logo. This button was used during the labor movement's boycotts of the 1970s to protest unfair working conditions and encourage consumers not to buy lettuce harvested by non-union workers, known as 'scabs'.

name: Peoples Peace Thru Peoples Power
title: PEOPLES PEACE THRU PEOPLES POWER
subject: Anti-war movement, Vietnam War, Peace
description: Pinback button featuring a fist holding a scroll with the text 'PEACE TREATY' against a pink circle, surrounded by the slogan 'PEOPLES PEACE THRU PEOPLES POWER'. This button refers to the 1971 People's Peace Treaty, an initiative by American and Vietnamese students to end the Vietnam War.

name: Boston College Women's Caucus 1985 Legislature
title: Boston College Women's Caucus 1985 Legislature
subject: Women's Caucus, Education, Student Politics
description: A white pin-back button with black text for the Boston College Women's Caucus 1985 Legislature.

name: March 28 March on Harrisburg Keep TMI Shut Down
title: march 28 march on harrisburg keep tmi shut down
subject: nuclear power, Three Mile Island, anti-nuclear movement
description: A yellow button with text advertising a march on the capital of Pennsylvania on March 28, the anniversary of the Three Mile Island nuclear accident, to keep the plant closed.

name: nea era '82
title: nea era '82
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, National Education Association, Women's rights
description: A white button featuring the blue 'nea' logo of the National Education Association and the red text 'era '82', signifying the organization's support for the ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment by the June 1982 deadline.

name: March on Washington November 15 Stop the Death Machine Stop the War Stop the War Machine
title: MARCH ON WASHINGTON NOVEMBER 15 STOP THE WAR STOP THE DEATH MACHINE
subject: Vietnam War, Anti-war movement, Activism
description: A yellow political button with orange text promoting the March on Washington on November 15, 1969, as part of the Moratorium to End the War in Vietnam protests. The center text identifies the event and date, while the border repeatedly calls to 'STOP THE WAR' and 'STOP THE DEATH MACHINE'.

name: I'm for jobs & justice Massachusetts AFL-CIO
title: I'M FOR JOBS & JUSTICE MASSACHUSETTS AFL-CIO
subject: Labor Unions, Economic Justice, Employment
description: A white button with blue text from the Massachusetts AFL-CIO advocating for labor rights and economic justice, featuring the common slogan 'Jobs and Justice'.

name: Make it in Massachusetts
title: Make it in Massachusetts
subject: Manufacturing, Business, Massachusetts
description: A promotional button from the 'Make it in Massachusetts' campaign, an economic development initiative launched by Governor Edward J. King to attract and retain business in the state.

name: PEACE is a Woman's Issue
title: Women's Int'l League for Peace & Freedom PEACE is a Woman's Issue 1213 Race St., Phila., Pa. 19107
subject: peace, women, activism
description: A blue pinback button produced by the Women's International League for Peace and Freedom (WILPF). The button features white text that reads "PEACE is a Woman's Issue" in the center, surrounded by the organization's name and their Philadelphia address at 1213 Race St.

name: Unicef
title: UNICEF
subject: UNICEF, smiley face, humanitarian aid
description: A white button featuring a black smiley face and the word 'UNICEF' in an arc across the top.

name: Elect COAL in '80
title: Elect COAL In '80
subject: Energy, Coal, Election
description: Red, white and blue button featuring stars and the text "Elect COAL In '80", referencing the energy policy debates during the 1980 US election.

name: peace symbol
title: Peace Sign
subject: Peace movement, Anti-war, Symbolism
description: A button featuring a black peace sign (CND symbol) on a white background. Text on the bottom edge indicates it was produced in Philadelphia, PA.

name: The Future is Education I'm mta
title: THE FUTURE IS EDUCATION I'm mta
subject: Education, Teachers, Unions
description: A rectangular blue advocacy button featuring the white text 'THE FUTURE IS EDUCATION' written in a chalk-like font. Below the text, a hand holds a piece of chalk that has underlined the phrase. Underneath the line is the text 'I'm' followed by the stylized logo of the Massachusetts Teachers Association (MTA).

name: Massachusetts The One and Only
title: MASSACHUSETTS THE ONE AND ONLY
subject: Tourism, State Pride, Massachusetts
description: A white and blue button with the text "MASSACHUSETTS THE ONE AND ONLY". This slogan was part of a tourism campaign for the state of Massachusetts launched in 1987 during the administration of Governor Michael Dukakis.

name: Total Amnesty Now!
title: TOTAL AMNESTY NOW!
subject: Vietnam War, amnesty, draft resisters
description: A red button with black text that reads "TOTAL AMNESTY NOW!" sponsored by the National Council for Universal and Unconditional Amnesty (NCUUA), an organization that advocated for the return of Vietnam War draft resisters and deserters.

name: I'm Helping Smokey Prevent Forest Fires
title: I'M HELPING SMOKEY PREVENT FOREST FIRES
subject: Fire prevention, Smokey Bear, Conservation
description: A yellow and red lithographed tin tab button for the Smokey Bear fire prevention campaign. It features an image of Smokey Bear and the text 'I'M HELPING SMOKEY PREVENT FOREST FIRES'. The button was produced by the Green Duck Company of Chicago.

name: theta
title: Ecology Symbol
subject: ecology, environment, earth day
description: A green and white button featuring the ecology symbol, which is a combination of the lowercase letters 'e' and 'o' to represent ecology and organism. The symbol was designed by Ron Cobb in 1969 and became a widely used emblem for the environmental movement and Earth Day in the 1970s.

name: Save our trains
title: SAVE OUR TRAINS
subject: railroads, transportation, activism
description: A white button featuring blue flocked text that reads 'SAVE OUR TRAINS'. It was likely produced during the 1970s as part of a grassroots campaign to support passenger rail service and prevent route cuts.

name: Caution: Your Smoking May Be Hazardous to My Health
title: CAUTION: YOUR SMOKING MAY BE HAZARDOUS TO MY HEALTH
subject: Smoking, Health, ASH
description: A white button with black and red text reading 'CAUTION: YOUR SMOKING MAY BE HAZARDOUS TO MY HEALTH' created in 1972 by the organization Action on Smoking and Health (ASH).

name: Strength in Unity Local 285, C.A.C.
title: STRENGTH IN UNITY LOCAL 285, C.A.C.
subject: labor unions, collective action, worker solidarity
description: A white pin-back button with blue text reading 'STRENGTH IN UNITY' and 'LOCAL 285, C.A.C.' The central graphic depicts a school of small fish forming the shape of a large fish to confront a single large fish, symbolizing the power of a union.

name: Clean Coal A Cheap Turn-On
title: Clean COAL A Cheap Turn-On
subject: Coal, Energy, Industry
description: A green pin-back button featuring the text "Clean COAL A Cheap Turn-On" in beige lettering, with a small lightbulb graphic inside the letter "O" of the word "Turn-On".

name: 1980 Moscow Olympics bear mascot
title: Misha
subject: Olympics, Mascot, Misha
description: An enamel pin depicting Misha, the official mascot of the 1980 Summer Olympics held in Moscow. The bear is shown smiling and wearing a belt featuring the five interlocking Olympic rings.

name: "There's blood on those grapes!"
title: "THERE'S BLOOD ON THOSE GRAPES!"
subject: Labor rights, Boycotts, United Farm Workers
description: This button was used during the United Farm Workers (UFW) grape boycott to draw attention to the violence and poor working conditions faced by agricultural workers in California.

name: Better active today than radioactive tomorrow
title: Better active today than radioactive tomorrow
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Environmentalism, Activism
description: A black button featuring the yellow slogan 'Better active today than radioactive tomorrow'. This was a popular slogan used by anti-nuclear power and anti-nuclear weapons activists during the late 20th century.

name: Vote Yes
title: Vote Yes.
subject: Referendum, Socialism, Elections
description: An orange circular button featuring the text 'Vote Yes.' in black. Between the words is a black cross containing the handshake logo of the Democratic Socialists of America (DSA). The button was likely used to support a ballot initiative or referendum endorsed by the DSA in the 1980s.

name: I am an Effete Snob for Peace
title: I AM AN EFFETE SNOB FOR PEACE
subject: Vietnam War, Peace Movement, Spiro Agnew
description: This button is a satirical response to a October 1969 speech by Vice President Spiro Agnew, in which he characterized anti-war activists as an 'effete corps of impudent snobs.' Anti-war protesters adopted the phrase as a badge of honor.

name: Almost Heaven West Virginia
title: Almost Heaven WEST VIRGINIA
subject: West Virginia tourism
description: A blue button shaped like the state of West Virginia with the text "Almost Heaven WEST VIRGINIA" in yellow. The phrase "Almost Heaven" refers to the popular John Denver song "Take Me Home, Country Roads," which became an unofficial anthem and eventually a state song for West Virginia.

name: Can David Still Beat Goliath?
title: CAN DAVID STILL BEAT GOLIATH?
subject: Presidential Campaign, Eugene McCarthy, Vietnam War
description: A white button with red text reading 'CAN DAVID STILL BEAT GOLIATH?'. This slogan was used during Eugene McCarthy's 1968 presidential campaign, framing his anti-war primary challenge against incumbent President Lyndon B. Johnson as an underdog 'David vs. Goliath' battle.

name: Work for Peace Nov 13 & 14 [picture of dove with plant]
title: WORK FOR PEACE NOV 13 & 14
subject: peace, anti-war, vietnam war
description: A blue and white button featuring a dove with an olive branch, referencing the November 1969 moratorium to end the Vietnam War.

name: theta
title: Θ
subject: ecology, environment, activism
description: A circular button featuring the ecology symbol, which combines the letters 'e' for environment and 'o' for organism to form the Greek letter theta (Θ). The symbol is depicted in yellow on a green and white background, following the design of the Ecology Flag created in 1969.

name: register for peace Indianapolis May 22
title: REGISTER FOR PEACE INDIANAPOLIS - MAY 22
subject: Peace, Anti-war, Vietnam War
description: A blue and white circular button promoting the 'Register for Peace' campaign in Indianapolis on May 22, likely 1971. The movement focused on registering voters who opposed the Vietnam War and supported the peace movement.

name: Vote Group 2 Brookline Democratic Comm.
title: VOTE GROUP 2 BROOKLINE DEMOCRATIC COMM.
subject: Local elections, Massachusetts, Democratic Party
description: A white button with green and blue text advocating for voters to choose 'Group 2' in the Brookline Democratic Committee election.

name: NO Masco Power Plant
title: NO Masco Power Plant
subject: Energy, Environmentalism, Public Health
description: This button was produced to voice opposition to the Medical Area Total Energy Plant (MATEP) proposed by the Medical Academic and Scientific Community Organization (MASCO) in Boston's Longwood Medical Area. The project faced years of legal battles and community protests due to concerns about air pollution and public health impacts on the surrounding neighborhoods.

name: No Matep
title: NO MATER
subject: Environmentalism, Anti-Nuclear, Pollution
description: A black and white circular button with the text "NO MATER" and a stylized illustration of a factory or power plant emitting smoke. MATER was the acronym for the Madison Anti-Thermal Energy Resource, an environmental activist group in Wisconsin that opposed the construction of nuclear power plants.

name: Blue Star of David with Hebrew and blood dripping
title: זכור
subject: Holocaust, Remembrance, Judaism
description: A white circular button featuring a blue Star of David with a red blood drop and the Hebrew word 'זכור' (Zakhor), meaning 'Remember'. The design is a symbol for Holocaust remembrance.

name: Boycott Non-union Lettuce
title: BOYCOTT NON-UNION LETTUCE
subject: labor unions, boycotts, agriculture
description: This button was produced by the United Farm Workers (UFW) to promote the boycott of lettuce that was not harvested by union workers. The center of the button features the black eagle logo of the UFW.

name: AFT Be A Winner
title: AFT Be A Winner
subject: Labor Unions, Education, Teachers
description: A red circular pin featuring the logo of the American Federation of Teachers (AFT) inside a shield on the left, with the white text "Be A Winner" on the right.

name: Get Behind The Government Liberty Loan of 1917
title: GET BEHIND THE GOVERNMENT LIBERTY LOAN OF 1917
subject: WWI, War Bonds, Liberty Loan
description: This button from 1917 features the Statue of Liberty and encourages people to 'Get Behind the Government' by participating in the Liberty Loan, which was a series of war bonds sold in the United States to support the Allied cause in World War I.

name: march on new york & L.A. April 22 out of s.e. asia now! - npac
title: march on new york & L.A. APRIL 22 out of s.e. asia now!-npac
subject: Anti-war; Vietnam War; NPAC
description: Yellow circular button with red and black text and graphics. Top text reads "march on new york & L.A." in black. Middle text in large red letters reads "APRIL 22" with black silhouettes of fighter jets flying over it. Bottom text in black reads "out of s.e. asia now!-npac".

name: Radcliffe 1977
title: RADCLIFFE 1977
subject: Radcliffe College, education, graduation
description: A white circular pinback button with the text 'RADCLIFFE 1977' printed in red, likely referencing the Radcliffe College graduating class of 1977.

name: DJD
title: DJD
subject: Anti-war, Draft, Vietnam War
description: A small round red enamel pin featuring the white letters 'DJD', an abbreviation for 'Don't Join the Draft'. This pin was used as a form of protest against military conscription during the Vietnam War era.

name: Gene McCarthy for president in '68
title: GENE MCCARTHY for PRESIDENT in '68
subject: Eugene McCarthy, Presidential Election, 1968
description: Red, white, and blue campaign button for Eugene McCarthy's 1968 presidential campaign featuring a black and white portrait of McCarthy in the center.

name: I Own A Liberty Bond
title: I OWN A LIBERTY BOND
subject: World War I, Liberty Bonds, Homefront
description: A small round button used during World War I to indicate the wearer had purchased a Liberty Bond. It features the Statue of Liberty and the text 'I OWN A LIBERTY BOND' in white on a teal background with a red border.

name: Save Our Shores
title: SAVE OUR SHORES
subject: environment, ocean, conservation
description: A white button with red text and graphics featuring a stylized lighthouse on the left and the words 'SAVE OUR SHORES' stacked vertically. The letters 'S', 'O', and 'S' are enlarged to form the acronym SOS, and waves are depicted at the bottom. This slogan is associated with environmental movements to protect coastal areas and oppose offshore oil drilling.

name: Effete Snobs for Peace
title: Effete Snobs For Peace
subject: Vietnam War, Anti-war movement, Spiro Agnew
description: This button features the slogan 'Effete Snobs For Peace', which was a satirical response to Vice President Spiro Agnew's 1969 speech where he characterized Vietnam War protesters as an 'effete corps of impudent snobs'. The phrase was subsequently adopted by protestors as a badge of honor.

name: Go With Mike on ballot 2
title: GO WITH MIKE on ballot 2
subject: Michael Dukakis, 1970 Election, Massachusetts Politics
description: A square political sticker with a white background and red text and border. The sticker advocates for Michael Dukakis's 1970 campaign for Lieutenant Governor of Massachusetts, indicating his position as number 2 on the ballot.

name: Friends of the Earth
title: FRIENDS OF THE EARTH
subject: Environmentalism, Ecology, Conservation
description: A circular button with a black background featuring a halftone photograph of Earth from space in the center. The white text "FRIENDS OF THE EARTH" is printed in an arc along the top edge.

name: Bangladesh
title: BANGLA DESH
subject: Bangladesh Liberation War, Independence, Sovereignty
description: A simple green pinback button with black text reading 'BANGLA DESH'. This button was likely produced to show support for the independence of Bangladesh during the Liberation War in 1971.

name: April 24 Out Now! NPAC Wash., D.C.
title: APRIL 24 WASH., D.C. OUT NOW! NPAC
subject: Vietnam War, Protest, Anti-war
description: This button was produced for the anti-Vietnam War march held in Washington, D.C. on April 24, 1971. Organized by the National Peace Action Coalition (NPAC), the event was one of the largest demonstrations against the war, featuring the slogan 'Out Now!'.

name: Small round pin with red, blue and gold
title: None
subject: Winterhilfswerk, Kriegsmarine, Battleship
description: A circular enamel pin from the Winterhilfswerk (WHW) series 'Kriegsschiffe' (Warships), issued in Germany circa 1939-1940. It features a stylized silver silhouette of a battleship with four stacks on blue water against a red background.

name: It's polite to wait until you're asked
title: it's polite to wait until you're asked
subject: John F. Kennedy, Presidential Election, Campaign Slogan
description: A white button with black text featuring the slogan 'it's polite to wait until you're asked', which was used during the 1960 presidential campaign in reference to John F. Kennedy's candidacy.

name: Register to Vote
title: REGISTER TO VOTE
subject: Voter registration, Voting rights, Civic participation
description: A white circular button featuring a purple stylized 'V' logo above the text 'REGISTER TO VOTE' in a multi-line, rounded 1970s-style typeface.

name: Boycott Scab Grapes
title: BOYCOTT SCAB GRAPES
subject: Labor Movement, Boycotts, Farmworkers
description: A red button with a white circle in the center featuring the black Aztec eagle logo of the United Farm Workers (UFW). The text "BOYCOTT" is printed at the top and "SCAB GRAPES" at the bottom. This button was used during the Delano grape strike and boycott led by Cesar Chavez and the UFW to protest the working conditions of farm laborers.

name: Vote Democratic
title: VOTE DEMOCRATIC
subject: Democratic Party, Election, Donkey
description: A red, white, and blue button featuring the words 'VOTE DEMOCRATIC' and two silhouettes of donkeys, the symbol of the Democratic Party.

name: let's meet in Springfield Mass.
title: let's meet in Springfield Mass.
subject: tourism, travel, city promotion
description: A white promotional button for Springfield, Massachusetts, featuring the slogan 'let's meet in Springfield Mass.' in black text, surrounded by four red arrows pointing inward towards the center.

name: Crowded Now? Baby, just you wait!
title: CROWDED NOW? BABY, JUST YOU WAIT!
subject: overpopulation, environmentalism, social issues
description: A white button featuring a black and white illustration of two fetuses in a cramped space. A speech bubble from one reads 'CROWDED NOW? BABY, JUST YOU WAIT!', referencing the global concern over overpopulation during the late 1960s and early 1970s. The bottom edge includes a 1970 copyright notice.

name: This is so sudden
title: THIS IS SO SUDDEN
subject: Third term, FDR, 1940 election
description: A blue pinback button with white text that reads 'THIS IS SO SUDDEN'. This slogan was used by opponents of Franklin D. Roosevelt to satirize the 'draft' movement that led to his nomination for an unprecedented third term in 1940.

name: Cambodia No!
title: CAMBODIA NO!
subject: Peace, Anti-war, Cambodia
description: A white button with the text 'CAMBODIA NO!' in black block letters, likely produced in 1970 to protest the U.S. military campaign in Cambodia during the Vietnam War.

name: WHIP
title: WHIP
subject: Political, Convention, Whip
description: A white ribbon with blue vertical text reading "WHIP", used by Hubert Humphrey's floor whips during the 1968 Democratic National Convention in Chicago.

name: Yes Pittsfield
title: Yes PITTSFIELD
subject: referendum, charter, politics
description: A blue and white campaign button with the text 'Yes PITTSFIELD', likely used for a local referendum or charter vote in Pittsfield, Massachusetts, during the 1960s.

name: Delaware: Small Wonder
title: DELAWARE SMALL WONDER
subject: Tourism, Delaware, Slogan
description: A rectangular promotional button for the state of Delaware featuring its tourism slogan 'Small Wonder', which was introduced in 1981. The design incorporates a yellow map outline of the state within the blue letter 'D' of Delaware.

name: USSR Pioneer pin
title: USSR
subject: Soviet Union, Children, USSR
description: A rectangular metal pin depicting two children's faces in front of a white sunburst, with a red flag in the upper right corner bearing the text 'USSR'.

name: Profile of Lenin pin
title: Vladimir Lenin Profile
party: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
subject: Vladimir Lenin, Soviet Union, Communism
description: A gold-colored metal pin with red enamel details. The central medallion features a right-facing profile of Vladimir Lenin, surrounded by five red enamel petals or ribbon loops that form a flower-like shape.

name: Watch Out I Vote
title: Watch Out I Vote
subject: youth vote, voter registration, 26th Amendment
description: A white button featuring stylized red and blue text that reads "Watch Out I Vote". The design includes flower symbols inside the letters "O". Small text at the bottom indicates it was produced by the U.S. Youth Council and Frontlash in 1972, likely targeting newly enfranchised youth voters following the passage of the 26th Amendment which lowered the voting age to 18.

name: donkey in a hat
title: Donkey in Uncle Sam hat
subject: Democratic Party, Donkey, Uncle Sam
description: A circular button featuring a cartoonish brown donkey, the symbol of the Democratic Party, emerging from an Uncle Sam style top hat featuring red and white stripes and a blue band with white stars.

name: Work for Peace October 15 [picture of dove with plant]
title: WORK FOR PEACE OCTOBER 15
subject: Vietnam War, Peace, Moratorium
description: A blue and white button featuring a peace dove with the text 'WORK FOR PEACE OCTOBER 15', likely referring to the Vietnam Moratorium Day on October 15, 1969.

name: KMO SSSR [USSR Committee of Youth Organizations]  | Komitet Molodezhnykh Organizatsii SSSR
title: KMO CCCP
party: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
subject: Soviet youth, Communism, Internationalism
description: A blue pinback button featuring a red stylized hammer and sickle symbol layered over a white globe with grid lines. The white text 'KMO CCCP' is inscribed on the red symbol. KMO CCCP refers to the Committee of Youth Organizations of the USSR, an umbrella organization for Soviet youth groups involved in international activities.

name: The 100 Club Kraus 1982
title: THE 100 CLUB KRAUS 1982
subject: Political campaign, Fundraising, Donors
description: This button is from Richard A. Kraus's 1982 campaign for the Massachusetts State Senate. The '100 Club' likely refers to a donor group for the campaign, signifying a specific level of financial support.

name: You Fight & Die But Can't Vote At 18
title: YOU FIGHT & DIE BUT CAN'T VOTE AT 18
subject: Vietnam War, Voting Rights, 26th Amendment
description: A yellow political protest button from the late 1960s or early 1970s advocating for the reduction of the voting age to 18. This slogan was widely used during the Vietnam War era to point out the hypocrisy of drafting young men into the military while denying them the right to vote. The campaign led to the ratification of the 26th Amendment in 1971.

name: Housing is the right of all - Cowitz
title: Housing is the right of all- Corita
subject: Housing, Human Rights, Social Justice
description: Green button with handwritten-style yellow text reading "Housing is the right of all-" followed by the signature "Corita". Created by artist and activist Corita Kent, it advocates for housing as a fundamental human right.

name: Mental Illness Stop the Stigma
title: MENTAL ILLNESS STOP THE STIGMA
subject: Mental health, Advocacy, Stigma
description: A red circular button featuring a white octagonal border resembling a stop sign, with the words 'MENTAL ILLNESS STOP THE STIGMA' printed in white across the center and top/bottom sections.

name: Smile A.F.S.C.M.E. Delivers
title: SMILE A.F.S.C.M.E. DELIVERS
subject: Labor unions, AFSCME, Smiley face
description: A green button featuring a white smiley face and the text "SMILE" at the top and "A.F.S.C.M.E. DELIVERS" along the bottom. AFSCME stands for the American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees.

name: How many more Hiroshimas before we understand?
title: How many more Hiroshimas before we understand?
subject: anti-nuclear movement, peace, Hiroshima
description: A white button with a speckled pattern featuring the message "How many more Hiroshimas before we understand?" in black text, accompanied by a stylized illustration of green vines and reddish buds.

name: North Carolina ERA '77 First in Freedom
title: North Carolina ERA '77 First in Freedom
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, women's rights, social movements
description: A white button with blue and red text advocating for the Equal Rights Amendment (ERA) in North Carolina in 1977. The text reads 'North Carolina ERA '77 First in Freedom'.

name: Organizing for NMHCA Rights Representation Responsibility Respect
title: ORGANIZING FOR NMHCA RIGHTS REPRESENTATION RESPONSIBILITY RESPECT
subject: Mental health advocacy, Disability rights, Consumer movement
description: A blue button featuring a red map of the United States with 'NMHCA' written in white. The text 'ORGANIZING FOR' curves along the top, while 'RIGHTS REPRESENTATION RESPONSIBILITY RESPECT' is stacked at the bottom. NMHCA refers to the National Mental Health Consumers' Association, an advocacy group founded in 1985.

name: Mylai Kent Jackson Attica
title: Mylai Kent Jackson Attica
subject: Protest; Social Justice; State Violence
description: This black protest button lists four locations associated with significant tragedies and state-sanctioned violence in the late 1960s and early 1970s: the My Lai Massacre in Vietnam (1968), the Kent State shootings (1970), the Jackson State killings (1970), and the Attica Prison riot (1971).

name: Stop the Draft March 22 Washington DC
title: MARCH 22 STOP THE DRAFT WASHINGTON DC
subject: Anti-draft, Protest, Selective Service
description: This button was created for a protest on March 22, 1980, in Washington, D.C., against the reinstatement of draft registration for the Selective Service System.

name: Olimpiada 80  | Olympics 1980
title: ОЛИМПИАДА-80
subject: 1980 Summer Olympics, Moscow, Soviet Union
description: A pin commemorating the 1980 Summer Olympics held in Moscow, Soviet Union. The pin is shaped like an Olympic torch with a red flame and green body, featuring the text 'ОЛИМПИАДА-80' and the Olympic rings.

name: Out now April 15
title: OUT NOW APRIL 15
subject: Vietnam War, Peace Movement, Anti-war Protest
description: A blue and white pinback button featuring a peace dove with an olive branch and the text "OUT NOW APRIL 15". The slogan "Out Now" was popularized by the National Peace Action Coalition (NPAC), which organized nationwide demonstrations against the Vietnam War on April 15, 1972.

name: Suzdal'
title: СУЗДАЛЬ
subject: Tourism, Suzdal, Architecture
description: A rectangular Soviet-era souvenir pin for the city of Suzdal, featuring an illustration of a cathedral with three domes against a red background.

name: No Bullshit Boycott Polaroid
title: NO BULLSHIT BOYCOTT POLAROID
subject: Apartheid, Boycotts, Polaroid
description: A button from the 1970s calling for a boycott of Polaroid due to its business dealings with the South African apartheid government, specifically the use of its camera technology for passbook identification cards.

name: The Year of the Child
title: The Year of the Child
subject: International Year, Children, United Nations
description: A white button featuring a childlike drawing of two figures holding hands, with the text 'The Year of the Child' along the bottom edge. It likely commemorates the 1979 International Year of the Child.

name: Jewish National Fund Children's Forest 30 Years of Israel's Independence
title: JEWISH NATIONAL FUND CHILDREN'S FOREST 30 YEARS OF ISRAEL'S INDEPENDENCE
subject: Israel Independence, Jewish National Fund, Forestry
description: A white button commemorating the 30th anniversary of Israel's independence in 1978, highlighting the Jewish National Fund's Children's Forest program. The button displays stylized drawings of children planting trees and includes text in both English and Hebrew.

name: Meadows for Congress M
title: MEADOWS FOR CONGRESS
subject: campaign, congress, election
description: A political button for Mark Meadows' campaign for Congress, featuring a large red 'M' over a gold field with blue lines, and the text 'MEADOWS FOR CONGRESS' in blue.

name: Thelander United States Congress
title: THELANDER UNITED STATES CONGRESS
subject: Maine, Congressional election, Republican candidate
description: A political campaign button for Ed Thelander's 2022 run for the United States Congress in Maine. The button features the text "THELANDER UNITED STATES CONGRESS" over an outline of the state of Maine filled with an American flag design.

name: Chris Sununu Governor
title: Chris SUNUNU Governor
subject: Chris Sununu, Governor, New Hampshire
description: A rectangular blue lapel pin featuring the profile of New Hampshire's Old Man of the Mountain on the left side. The text 'Chris SUNUNU Governor' is displayed in yellow and white.

name: Elect Tom Isabella City Council
title: ELECT TOM ISABELLA CITY COUNCIL
subject: Local election, Amsterdam NY, City Council
description: Campaign button supporting Tom Isabella for City Council in Amsterdam, New York. Isabella served on the council for many years before being elected as the city's mayor in 1991.

name: Barnes For Sheriff
title: BARNES for Sheriff
subject: Local election, Sheriff, Law enforcement
description: A blue campaign button with white text reading 'BARNES for Sheriff' featuring a single white star at the top.

name: Van Schaick City Council
title: VAN SCHAICK City Council
subject: Municipal elections, Political campaigns, Local government
description: A campaign button for Peter Van Schaick's run for City Council in Jersey City, New Jersey, featuring a monochrome blue-toned photograph of the candidate with a mustache.

name: Re-Elect Tom Isabella Councilman
title: Re-elect TOM ISABELLA COUNCILMAN
subject: Local Government, Elections, Amsterdam
description: A circular campaign button for Tom Isabella's re-election as Councilman in Amsterdam, New York. The button features yellow text on a black background with yellow horizontal stripes.

name: Carney D.A.
title: CARNEY D.A.
subject: Elections, District Attorney, New York
description: This political campaign button is for George M. Carney's run for District Attorney of New York County (Manhattan) in 1941. Carney was initially the Democratic/Tammany Hall nominee for the position before the party withdrew his candidacy in favor of Frank Hogan, who went on to serve as District Attorney for over three decades.

name: Reagan Bush '84
title: REAGAN BUSH '84
subject: Presidential election; Ronald Reagan; George H.W. Bush
description: A white campaign button for the 1984 Reagan-Bush ticket, featuring the names 'REAGAN' and 'BUSH' in blue and ''84' in red, framed by red lines.

name: I heart Schenectady Henry S. De Legge For Mayor
title: I [heart] SCHENECTADY HENRY S. DE LEGGE FOR MAYOR
subject: Schenectady, Mayoral election, 1980s politics
description: Circular white campaign button with red text and horizontal stripes supporting Henry S. De Legge's run for mayor in Schenectady, New York. The design uses the 'I Love' heart logo popularized in the late 1970s.

name: Go! Art Allen Go!
title: GO! ART Allen GO!
subject: Campaign button, Political candidate, Runner
description: A yellow circular campaign button featuring the text 'GO! ART Allen GO!' with an illustration of a runner to the right. The button likely supports Art Allen for an unspecified political office.

name: Tony Insogna For County Legislature
title: Tony Insogna for County Legislature
subject: local politics, election campaign, New York state
description: This campaign button was produced for Tony Insogna, a long-serving Republican member of the Fulton County Board of Supervisors in New York. Insogna represented Gloversville's 4th Ward for 36 years, retiring in 2011.

name: "If Anyone Knows More About Albany Vote For Him." Mayor Corning
title: "IF ANYONE KNOWS MORE ABOUT ALBANY, VOTE FOR HIM." MAYOR CORNING
subject: Mayoral campaign, Albany, Slogan
description: A campaign button for Erastus Corning 2nd, the longest-serving mayor in the history of Albany, New York. Corning served from 1942 until his death in 1983. The slogan on the button highlights his extensive experience and knowledge of the city as a reason for voters to re-elect him.

name: Re-Elect Councilman Vince Di Cerbo
title: Re-Elect Councilman VINCE DI CERBO
subject: local elections, incumbent, Schenectady politics
description: A campaign button for the re-election of Vince Di Cerbo to the city council of Schenectady, New York. Di Cerbo was first elected in 1969 and served for over 25 years.

name: Tell A Friend Vote Mike Andriano For Mayor
title: TELL A FRIEND VOTE MIKE ANDRIANO FOR MAYOR
subject: Mayoral election, Mike Andriano, Utica New York
description: A campaign button for Mike Andriano's run for Mayor of Utica, New York. The button features a red top section with 'TELL A FRIEND VOTE' in white, a white middle section with 'MIKE ANDRIANO' in blue, and a blue bottom section with 'FOR MAYOR' in white.

name: Elect Savage County Board District 2
title: ELECT SAVAGE COUNTY BOARD DISTRICT 2
subject: Political campaign, Local government, Milwaukee
description: A purple and white political campaign button for Frank Savage's candidacy for the Milwaukee County Board of Supervisors, District 2. Savage was a prominent African American leader and politician in Milwaukee who served on the board from 1972 until his death in 1984.

name: Tina Panetta Zaza County Legislator
title: TINA Panetta Zaza County Legislator
subject: Westchester County, Local Politics, Democratic Party
description: A campaign button for Tina Panetta Zaza, a Democrat who served as a Westchester County Legislator in New York in the 1990s.

name: May 13 2010 Buffalo Welcome August 6 2010
title: MAY 13, 2010 BUFFALO WELCOMES OPIC AUGUST 6, 2010
subject: Presidential Visit, Buffalo, Barack Obama
description: A commemorative button for President Barack Obama's visit to Buffalo, New York in 2010. It features a photograph of the President shaking hands, the Buffalo city skyline, and the dates May 13, 2010 and August 6, 2010, along with the acronym 'OPIC' incorporating the Obama campaign logo.

name: DRC Presidential Rural Forum
title: DRC Presidential Rural Forum Lake Placid, May 30-31, 2003
person: Howard Dean, John Kerry, Carol Moseley Braun, Richard Gephardt, Bob Graham, Al Sharpton, John Edwards, Dennis Kucinich, Joe Lieberman
subject: Presidential candidates, Democratic Party, Rural forum
description: A promotional button for the Democratic Rural Conference Presidential Rural Forum held in Lake Placid, New York, in May 2003. The button features portraits of Democratic presidential primary candidates Joe Lieberman, Howard Dean, John Kerry, Carol Moseley Braun, Richard Gephardt, Bob Graham, Al Sharpton, John Edwards, and Dennis Kucinich.

name: Gore Lieberman 2000
title: Gore Lieberman 2000 nysut
subject: presidential election, labor union, education
description: A blue circular button promoting the Gore-Lieberman ticket for the 2000 US Presidential Election, endorsed by the New York State United Teachers (NYSUT).

name: Mondale Ferraro For New Leadership
title: MONDALE - FERRARO FOR NEW LEADERSHIP
subject: 1984 US Presidential Election, Walter Mondale, Geraldine Ferraro
description: A campaign button from the 1984 US Presidential election featuring portraits of Democratic candidates Walter Mondale and Geraldine Ferraro with the slogan 'For New Leadership'.

name: Governor Samuels in Seventy Four
title: Governor Samuels in Seventy Four
subject: gubernatorial election, Howard Samuels, New York
description: A campaign button for Howard Samuels' 1974 bid for Governor of New York, featuring a black and white portrait of Samuels with the text "Governor Samuels in Seventy Four" in blue and red.

name: Mondale Ferraro 1984
title: 1984 MONDALE FERRARO
subject: Presidential Election, Democratic Party, Mondale-Ferraro
description: A 1984 Democratic presidential campaign button supporting Walter Mondale and Geraldine Ferraro, featuring their portraits flanking the U.S. Capitol building.

name: Gore 2000
title: Gore 2000 nysut
subject: Presidential campaign, Labor union, Education
description: A blue and white campaign button for Al Gore's 2000 presidential bid, endorsed by the New York State United Teachers (NYSUT).

name: Nicki Dileva County Legislator
title: NICKI DiLEVA COUNTY LEGISLATOR
subject: Local politics; Campaign button; Schenectady County
description: A white campaign button featuring a black and white photograph of Nicki DiLeva above blue text that reads "NICKI DiLEVA COUNTY LEGISLATOR". Nicki DiLeva served as a Democratic legislator for Schenectady County, New York, for several terms starting in the early 1990s.

name: Elect Carmel Patrick
title: ELECT CARMEL PATRICK for SCHENECTADY CITY COUNCIL
subject: local election, city council, New York
description: A rectangular campaign button supporting Carmel Patrick for a seat on the Schenectady City Council. The design features a large light blue star in the background with the candidate's name in red and blue text.

name: Mondale 84'
title: MONDALE '84
subject: Presidential Campaign, Walter Mondale, 1984
description: A blue rectangular button with 'MONDALE '84' printed repeatedly in two columns. The text colors are light blue, red, and white. It is from Walter Mondale's 1984 presidential campaign.

name: NYSUT For Spitzer Patersom
title: nysut A Union of Professionals FOR SPITZER PATERSON
subject: Labor unions, New York elections, Education
description: A rectangular political button produced by the New York State United Teachers (NYSUT) in support of the 2006 gubernatorial ticket of Eliot Spitzer and David Paterson.

name: Gore 2000
title: Working Labor For Gore AFL-CIO
subject: Labor Unions, Al Gore, 2000 Presidential Election
description: A rectangular blue button featuring an image of Al Gore speaking at a podium with a 'UNION HALL' in the background. The text 'Working' is positioned above Gore, and the 'AFL-CIO' logo is below. To the right, the words 'LABOR FOR GORE' are printed in large, vertical letters.

name: UFCW For Gore
title: UFCW FOR GORE 2000
subject: Labor Union, Presidential Campaign, Al Gore
description: A rectangular yellow campaign button with blue and red text supporting Al Gore for President in 2000. It features the endorsement of the United Food & Commercial Workers International Union (UFCW).

name: Hillary Clinton For Prison 2017
title: HILLARY CLINTON FOR PRISON 2017
subject: anti-Hillary Clinton, 2016 election, political satire
description: A circular orange button with the text "HILLARY CLINTON FOR PRISON 2017" and a graphic of a female figure in a black-and-white striped prison uniform.

name: In New York the Difference is Education New York Student Aid
title: In New York the Difference is Education! New York Student Aid HESC Albany 12255
subject: Education, Student Aid, New York
description: A yellow pin-back button featuring red and black text promoting student aid through the New York State Higher Education Services Corporation (HESC).

name: Jim Kennedy For Congress 2020 Better Together
title: JIM KENNEDY FOR CONGRESS 2020 "Better Together"
subject: election, campaign, Florida
description: A campaign button for Jim Kennedy's 2020 run for Florida's 8th Congressional District as a Democrat. The button features his name, the office he's running for, the year 2020, and his slogan "Better Together" set against an outline of the state of Florida.

name: Hillary Clinton For Prison 2017
title: HILLARY CLINTON FOR PRISON 2017
subject: Hillary Clinton, Presidential Election, Opposition
description: A white button featuring a female stick figure in an orange jumpsuit and the text "HILLARY CLINTON FOR PRISON 2017" in blue.

name: Donna Deegan For Congress
title: DONNA DEEGAN FOR CONGRESS COMMON SENSE FOR FLORIDA'S 4th DISTRICT
subject: Donna Deegan, Congress, Florida
description: Campaign button for Donna Deegan's 2020 run for U.S. Congress representing Florida's 4th district.

name: No On 2 Bad For Our Schools

The slogan 'CENTRAL AMERICA IS REAGAN'S WAR ON POVERTY' sarcastically references Lyndon B. Johnson's domestic 'War on Poverty' program to suggest that Reagan's military interventions were harmful to impoverished populations.

name: Animal Liberation ; Human Liberation

subject: animal rights, human rights, social justice
description: Round button with a diagonal split background of white and black. The top half features a red rat and the words 'Animal Liberation' in red. The bottom half features three red human stick figures and the words 'Human Liberation' in red.

name: ALA is for ERA 38 by '82
title: 38 by '82 AIA is for ERA
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, AIA, Women's rights
description: This oval-shaped button features green text on a white background with the message '38 by '82 AIA is for ERA'. The slogan '38 by '82' refers to the campaign to get the necessary 38 states to ratify the Equal Rights Amendment (ERA) before the June 30, 1982 deadline. AIA refers to the American Institute of Architects, which supported the ratification of the amendment.

name: Laura Jones For U.S. Congress
title: LAURA JONES FOR U.S. CONGRESS
subject: Texas, US Congress, Election
description: A dark blue campaign button for Laura Jones, a candidate for the U.S. Congress in Texas. The button features an orange outline of Texas with a black star marking a location, and white rays emanating from behind the state map.

name: Kathleen Williams for Congress
title: KATHLEEN WILLIAMS FOR CONGRESS
subject: Political campaign, US House, Montana
description: A political campaign button for Kathleen Williams' run for the U.S. House of Representatives from Montana, featuring her name, the office, and a graphic silhouette of the state.

name: Governor Ned Lamont
title: GOVERNOR NED LAMONT
subject: Ned Lamont, Governor, Connecticut
description: White button with "GOVERNOR" in red and "NED LAMONT" in dark blue, underscored by a red horizontal line. It supports Ned Lamont's campaign for Governor of Connecticut.

name: Keep the pressure on apartheid
title: KEEP THE PRESSURE ON APARTHEID

description: A square black button featuring the message 'KEEP THE PRESSURE ON APARTHEID'. The text uses the colors of the Pan-African flag (yellow, green, and red). The word 'ON' is artistically rendered with a red circle containing a map of Africa and a red square with the letter 'N' overlaid with the target logo of the hip-hop group Public Enemy.

name: Fight weld cuts ; lesbians & gays against weld

Produced by the group Lesbians & Gays Against Weld, it protests social service and healthcare budget cuts proposed by Massachusetts Governor William Weld in the early 1990s.

name: Jenny Garcia Sharon For Congress
title: JENNY GARCIA SHARON FOR CONGRESS

description: A political campaign button for Elizabeth Warren's 2020 presidential run. The button features a rainbow gradient background with the word "PERSIST." and the URL for her campaign website. The rainbow colors indicate support for the LGBTQ+ community, while "PERSIST" refers to the popular slogan "Nevertheless, she persisted" associated with Warren.

name: James Lankford US Senate
title: JAMES LANKFORD US SENATE
subject: James Lankford, US Senate, Oklahoma

name: I Like Ike
title: I LIKE IKE

This imagery and slogan were central to the American Civil Rights Movement, symbolizing the call for racial equality and solidarity.

title: TIM SCOTT U.S. SENATE 2022
subject: Tim Scott, 2022 election, US Senate
description: A circular blue button supporting Tim Scott's 2022 campaign for the U.S. Senate. The button features a light blue silhouette of South Carolina in the background, with the candidate's name, the office, and the year in white text, separated by a red horizontal line.

name: National March for Lesbian and Gay Rights ; Oct. 14 [flag with male and female symbols outside capitol building]
title: NATIONAL MARCH FOR LESBIAN AND GAY RIGHTS OCT. 14
subject: LGBTQ Rights, Protests, Activism
description: A circular black and white button featuring the dome of the U.S. Capitol and a flag with intertwined male and female gender symbols, commemorating the National March for Lesbian and Gay Rights on October 14, 1979.

name: Gara Cook ; Governor ; LT. Governor
title: GOVERNOR Gara LT. GOVERNOR Cook
subject: 2022 Alaska Gubernatorial Election, Les Gara, Jessica Cook
description: A purple campaign button for Les Gara and Jessica Cook's 2022 Alaska gubernatorial ticket, featuring a gold stylized fish.

name: Remember Rodney King ; RACIAL JUSTICE NOW ; from Boston to L.A.
title: Remember Rodney King RACIAL JUSTICE NOW from Boston to L.A.
subject: racial justice, police brutality, civil rights
description: A yellow button with black text advocating for racial justice following the beating of Rodney King by LAPD officers in 1991 and the subsequent acquittal of the officers in 1992.

name: Michael Maher ; U.S. Congress ; Businessman ; Veteran
title: MICHAEL MAHER U.S. CONGRESS BUSINESSMAN VETERAN
subject: US House, Republican, Veteran
description: A political campaign button for Michael Maher, a Republican candidate for the U.S. House of Representatives from California. The button highlights his background as a businessman and veteran.

name: Not me ; US ; Bernie ; 2020
title: US Bernie 2020
subject: Bernie Sanders, Presidential Election, Not Me Us
description: Circular button for Bernie Sanders' 2020 presidential campaign featuring silhouettes of a crowd in various colors. The word 'US' is displayed in large blue letters near the top, and 'Bernie 2020' is at the bottom.

name: Women for Maura ; The People's Lawyer
title: WOMEN for MAURA THE PEOPLE'S LAWYER
subject: Women, Attorney General, Campaign
description: A square campaign button from Maura Healey's 2014 campaign for Massachusetts Attorney General, featuring her portrait and the slogans "Women for Maura" and "The People's Lawyer."

name: Not me ; US ; Bernie ; 2020
title: NOT ME US Bernie
subject: Bernie Sanders, Presidential Election, Campaign Slogan
description: Circular campaign button from Bernie Sanders' 2020 presidential campaign. It features the slogan 'NOT ME US' in large letters over a silhouette of Sanders with a raised fist. Below the silhouette is the name 'Bernie'. The background consists of multicolored silhouettes of a crowd.

name: Not me ; US ; Bernie ; 2020
title: NOT ME US
subject: Bernie Sanders, 2020 Election, Campaign Slogan
description: A campaign button for Bernie Sanders' 2020 presidential run, featuring the slogan "Not Me. Us." and a silhouette of Sanders with a raised fist.

name: MG [Image of a blue and red cowboy boot with yellow symbols]
title: M L G
subject: Michelle Lujan Grisham; Governor; New Mexico
description: A blue campaign button for Michelle Lujan Grisham's 2018 run for Governor of New Mexico. The button displays the initials 'MLG' where the 'L' is represented by a cowboy boot. The boot is blue with yellow Zia sun symbols (the state symbol of New Mexico) on the shaft and a red foot. A union bug for Albuquerque, New Mexico is visible at the bottom center.

name: March for peace & justice in southern African & Central America ; April 25, 1987 ; Washington D.C.
title: MARCH FOR PEACE & JUSTICE IN SOUTHERN AFRICA & CENTRAL AMERICA April 25, 1987 Washington, D.C.
subject: Peace, Justice, Activism
description: Button commemorating the April 25, 1987, March for Peace and Justice in Southern Africa and Central America in Washington, D.C. The demonstration protested U.S. involvement in Central America and apartheid in South Africa.

name: Team Issa
title: Team ISSA
subject: California, Election, Politics
description: A rectangular campaign button for Darrell Issa, a Republican politician from California, featuring a yellow map of California and the text 'Team ISSA' in red and blue.

name: Joe Neguse for Colorado
title: JOE NEGUSE FOR COLORADO
subject: Joe Neguse, Colorado, US House
description: Campaign button for Joe Neguse, a Democratic candidate for the U.S. House of Representatives from Colorado. The button features his name in blue and stylized mountains above.

name: The March ; Pride ; D.C. '93
title: The March Pride D.C. '93
subject: LGBTQ Rights, Pride, March on Washington
description: A brown button featuring an orange-pink inverted triangle, a symbol of the LGBTQ rights movement. Text on the button reads 'The March Pride D.C. '93', referencing the 1993 March on Washington for Lesbian, Gay, and Bi Equal Rights and Liberation.

name: Natasha Hill for Congress
title: NATASHA HILL FOR CONGRESS
subject: Election, Washington, Congress
description: Blue and white campaign button for Natasha Hill's 2022 run for the United States House of Representatives in Washington state.

name: Elissa Slotkin for Congress
title: Elissa Slotkin for Congress
subject: Political Campaign, Democrat, Michigan
description: A white round button featuring the text 'Elissa Slotkin for Congress' in red and blue, with a blue and red graphic element in the center.

name: [mountain range and moon inside of a "C"]
title: Cleveland
subject: Cleveland, Ohio, logo
description: A pin-back button featuring the former official logo of the City of Cleveland, Ohio. The logo, adopted in 1970, consists of a white stylized 'C' surrounding a geometric representation of peaks and a sun on a blue background.

name: NOLA Stands with Charlottesville
title: NOLA STANDS WITH CHARLOTTESVILLE
subject: Civil rights, Charlottesville rally, Solidarity
description: A white button with black text expressing support from New Orleans for Charlottesville, Virginia, following the Unite the Right rally in August 2017.

name: There's a new game in town.
title: There's a new game in town.
subject: McGovern, Election, Democratic
description: A green campaign button from George McGovern's 1972 presidential run featuring the slogan 'There's a new game in town.' in white text.

name: Let's put a cap on $pending ; Vote for Marco Rubio
title: LET'S PUT A CAP ON SPENDING VOTE FOR MARCO RUBIO
subject: Fiscal policy, Marco Rubio, Campaign button
description: A campaign button for Marco Rubio's 2010 bid for the U.S. Senate in Florida. The button features a graphic of a bottle cap and the slogan "Let's put a cap on spending," reflecting his fiscal conservative platform.

name: I believe Christine Blasey Ford
title: I BELIEVE CHRISTINE BLASEY FORD
subject: Supreme Court nomination, Sexual assault allegations, Brett Kavanaugh
