description: This pink button expresses support for Christine Blasey Ford, who came forward with allegations of sexual assault against Supreme Court nominee Brett Kavanaugh in 2018.

name: Votes for Women ; WCAS
title: VOTES FOR WOMEN. CWSA
subject: Suffrage, Catholicism, Women's Rights
description: This button was produced for the Catholic Women's Suffrage Alliance (CWSA), a British organization founded in 1911 to advocate for women's suffrage. The button features the organization's initials CWSA arranged in a cross-like design with celtic knots, and the slogan 'VOTES FOR WOMEN' around the perimeter. It uses the colors purple, green, and white, which were synonymous with the suffrage movement.

name: Ask me why I'm voting no ; don't limit the freedom to marry
title: ASK ME WHY I'M VOTING NO DON'T LIMIT THE FREEDOM TO MARRY MNUNITED.ORG
subject: Same-sex marriage, Ballot initiative, Civil rights
description: Circular blue button with orange and white text reading 'ASK ME WHY I'M VOTING NO DON'T LIMIT THE FREEDOM TO MARRY MNUNITED.ORG'. This button was part of the Minnesotans United for All Families campaign to defeat a 2012 state constitutional amendment that would have defined marriage as between one man and one woman.

name: Votes for Women ; WCAS
title: Conn Woman SUFFRAGE ASSN.
subject: Women's Suffrage
description: The reverse side of a pinback button issued by the Connecticut Woman Suffrage Association, based in Hartford. It was manufactured by the Torsch & Franz Badge Company of Baltimore, Maryland.

name: No third internationale Third Reich third term
title: NO THIRD INTERNATIONALE THIRD REICH THIRD TERM
subject: Anti-FDR, 1940 Election, Third Term Tradition
description: This political button from 1940 expresses opposition to Franklin D. Roosevelt's bid for an unprecedented third term as President of the United States. By linking the 'Third Term' with the 'Third Internationale' (Soviet communism) and the 'Third Reich' (Nazi Germany), the button frames a third term as a step toward totalitarianism.

name: Central America ; Peace & Justice ; Southern Africa ; Spring Mobilization ; Washington, D.C. 1987 ; April 25-27
title: APRIL 25-27 PEACE & JUSTICE · SOUTHERN AFRICA · 1987 · WASHINGTON, D.C. · SPRING MOBILIZATION · CENTRAL AMERICA
subject: Peace, Justice, Activism
description: This button was created for the April 1987 Spring Mobilization for Peace and Justice, which took place in Washington, D.C. The protest focused on opposition to United States foreign policy in Central America and the apartheid regime in Southern Africa.

name: Good Team! ; Thatcher Comptroller ; Top Lever!
title: GOOD TEAM! THATCHER COMPTROLLER TOP LEVER!
subject: Elections, Political Campaign, Straight-ticket voting
description: A campaign button for Richard W. Thatcher for the office of Comptroller, likely in Chester County, Pennsylvania. The red, white, and blue button features the slogans 'GOOD TEAM!' and 'TOP LEVER!', which was a common directive in Pennsylvania for voters to cast a straight-ticket Republican vote on mechanical voting machines.

name: I'm With Her
title: I'M WITH HER
subject: Presidential Campaign, 2016 Election, Hillary Clinton
description: A blue and white campaign button for Hillary Clinton's 2016 presidential run featuring her slogan 'I'M WITH HER' and her signature campaign 'H' logo with an arrow.

name: STOP Logging Our National Forests ; Sierra Club
title: STOP Logging Our National Forests SIERRA CLUB
subject: Environmentalism, National Forests, Logging
description: This green button features white text reading 'STOP Logging Our National Forests' and 'SIERRA CLUB' at the bottom, overlaying a graphic of three evergreen trees. It was produced as part of the Sierra Club's campaign to end commercial logging on federal lands, a policy position they formally adopted in April 1996.

name: Mailer ; Breslin ; 51
title: MAILER 51 BRESLIN
subject: Norman Mailer, Jimmy Breslin, Statehood
description: Button from the 1969 New York City mayoral campaign of Norman Mailer and Jimmy Breslin, featuring their proposal for New York City to become the 51st state.

name: [Peace sign in corner of American flag]
title: Peace Symbol on American Flag
subject: Peace, Anti-war, Vietnam War
description: A button featuring a white peace symbol inside a blue canton, accompanied by red and white stripes to mimic the design of the American flag. This was a common symbol of the anti-war movement during the Vietnam War era.

name: Boycott Lettuce ; Non-Union
title: BOYCOTT NON-UNION LETTUCE
subject: Labor Rights, United Farm Workers, Boycott
description: A button produced by the United Farm Workers (UFW) featuring the organization's black eagle logo on a white background, surrounded by a red border with the text 'BOYCOTT NON-UNION LETTUCE' in black. This button was used during the lettuce boycotts of the 1970s to support unionized agricultural labor.

name: Joelle ; People before products
title: JOELLE PEOPLE BEFORE PROFITS
subject: Communism, Labor, Elections
description: A campaign button for Joelle Fishman, a prominent member and leader of the Communist Party USA in Connecticut. The button features her portrait and the slogan 'People Before Profits,' which she used during several runs for public office, including the U.S. House of Representatives and Mayor of New Haven.

name: Hillary 2016
title: HILLARY 2016
subject: Hillary Clinton, Presidential Campaign, 2016
description: A circular political button featuring a profile portrait of Hillary Clinton in blue against a red and white American flag background. The text 'HILLARY 2016' is printed in white at the bottom.

name: It's hell with Mel
title: it's hell WITH MEL
subject: Anti-tax, Gubernatorial Election, Political Opposition
description: A red political button with white text 'it's hell WITH MEL' and a devil's face, used by opponents of Mel Carnahan, likely during his tenure as Governor of Missouri or his campaigns.

name: Marsha Blackburn For U.S. Senate
title: Marsha Blackburn For U.S. Senate
subject: Politics, Elections, Senate
description: A red campaign button for Marsha Blackburn's 2018 election campaign for the U.S. Senate in Tennessee.

name: Ayanna Presslet for Congress
title: AYANNA PRESSLEY FOR CONGRESS
subject: Congress, Elections, Progressive Politics
description: Campaign button for Ayanna Pressley, a Democrat running for the U.S. House of Representatives in Massachusetts's 7th congressional district.

name: Greenmarket ; 10 years ; Council on the Environment of NYC
title: GREENMARKET 10 YEARS COUNCIL ON THE ENVIRONMENT OF NYC
subject: Environment, Farmers Markets, New York City
description: A button celebrating the 10th anniversary of the NYC Greenmarket program, founded in 1976 by the Council on the Environment of New York City (now GrowNYC).

name: Legalize Prostitution
title: LEGALIZE PROSTITUTION
subject: sex work, legalization, social activism
description: A circular red button with the text 'LEGALIZE PROSTITUTION' printed in white sans-serif capital letters. This slogan was popularized by sex worker rights advocacy groups that emerged in the 1970s, such as COYOTE (Cast Off Your Old Tired Ethics).

name: Kara Eastman ; Congress
title: KARA EASTMAN CONGRESS
subject: Election, Nebraska, House of Representatives
description: A campaign button for Democrat Kara Eastman, who ran for Nebraska's 2nd Congressional District in 2018 and 2020. The button features her name, the office she is seeking, and an outline of the state of Nebraska.

name: Devin Nunes ; U.S. Congress
title: DEVIN NUNES U.S. CONGRESS
subject: Devin Nunes, U.S. Congress, California
description: A red, white, and blue political button for Devin Nunes's campaign for U.S. Congress. The button features his name, his campaign website, and six stars.

name: Attica is all of us
title: ATTICA IS ALL OF US
subject: prison reform, protest, human rights
description: This button features the slogan 'Attica Is All of Us', referring to the 1971 prison uprising at Attica Correctional Facility in New York. The slogan was used by supporters of the prisoners to draw attention to the conditions at the facility and systemic issues within the American carceral system.

name: Welfare to What?
title: WELFARE -TO- WHAT?
subject: Welfare reform, Social policy, Poverty
description: This green button features the text 'WELFARE -TO- WHAT?' in black. It is a critical response to the 'Welfare-to-Work' legislative reforms of the mid-1990s, specifically the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Act of 1996. A union bug for the Graphic Communications International Union (GCIU) is visible at the bottom.

name: Free Bobby [black panther]
title: FREE BOBBY
subject: Black Panther Party, Activism, Bobby Seale
description: A button featuring a black panther and the text 'FREE BOBBY' in red on a yellow background, created to support Bobby Seale, co-founder of the Black Panther Party, during his legal battles in the late 1960s and early 1970s.

name: Armenian Power
title: ARMENIAN POWER
subject: Armenian heritage, ethnic pride, social activism
description: A circular button with an orange background and a thick horizontal blue band across the center. The words 'ARMENIAN' and 'POWER' are printed in orange block letters inside the blue stripe.

name: Protect WILD UTAH
title: Protect WILD UTAH
subject: Environment, Conservation, Wilderness
description: Yellow button with black text advocating for the protection of Utah's wilderness areas, commonly associated with the Southern Utah Wilderness Alliance (SUWA).

name: McGovern
title: McGOVERN
subject: McGovern, 1972, President
description: A campaign button for George McGovern's 1972 presidential bid, featuring the candidate's name in white against a blue band, flanked by white and red stripes.

name: GOD BLESS AMERICA ; Land that I love
title: GOD BLESS AMERICA Land That I Love
subject: Patriotism, American Flag, WWII
description: A circular button with a white background featuring the American flag in the center. The blue text 'GOD BLESS AMERICA' is at the top, and 'Land That I Love' is at the bottom.

name: Defeat NAFTA ; Don't let corporate America take U.S. jobs to Mexico
title: DEFEAT NAFTA DON'T LET CORPORATE AMERICA TAKE U.S. JOBS TO MEXICO
subject: NAFTA, Labor Unions, Trade Policy
description: A square political button produced by the IUE (International Union of Electronic, Electrical, Salaried, Machine and Furniture Workers) opposing the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA). The top section is blue with 'DEFEAT NAFTA' in pink text, and the bottom section is white with blue text warning that the agreement would move American jobs to Mexico.

name: garbage in garbage out
title: garbage in garbage out
subject: computer science, data, information
description: A pinback button featuring the phrase "garbage in garbage out" in red text on a white background. This phrase is a common adage in computer science and mathematics.

name: One in nine ; Stop the Epidemic
title: ONE IN NINE STOP THE EPIDEMIC
subject: Breast Cancer, Women's Health, Advocacy
description: A black circular button featuring the silhouettes of nine women. The phrase 'ONE IN NINE' is printed in pink across the center, with a large white '1' behind the silhouettes. At the bottom, the words 'STOP THE EPIDEMIC' curve along the edge in white. The button refers to a 1990s statistic regarding breast cancer risk.

name: James Farmer
title: JAMES FARMER
subject: Civil Rights, Politics, Election
description: A black button with white text that reads "JAMES FARMER". This button was likely used during civil rights leader James Farmer's 1968 campaign for the U.S. House of Representatives in New York's 12th congressional district, where he ran as a Liberal and Republican candidate.

name: From all walks of life ; Sunday June 5, 1988 ; Boston, Massachusetts
title: FROM ALL WALKS OF LIFE
subject: AIDS, Boston, Fundraising
description: A rectangular white button promoting 'From All Walks of Life', an AIDS fundraising walk held on June 5, 1988, in Boston, Massachusetts. The center features an illustration of a diverse group of people walking together.

name: Ted Baldwin for Congress
title: Ted Baldwin for Congress
subject: campaign, election, politics
description: A black and blue political campaign button for Ted Baldwin, a Republican who ran for the U.S. House of Representatives from Nebraska's 3rd district in 1970 and 1972.

name: From all walks of life ; Sunday May 31 1987
title: FROM ALL WALKS OF LIFE SUNDAY • MAY 31 • 1987
subject: AIDS awareness, fundraising, healthcare
description: A square white button featuring an illustration of a diverse group of people walking, with the text "FROM ALL WALKS OF LIFE" and the date "SUNDAY • MAY 31 • 1987". This button commemorates the first From All Walks of Life event, which was the inaugural AIDS Walk in Boston.

name: Angie Craig ; Congress
title: ANGIE CRAIG CONGRESS www.AngieCraig.com
subject: Politics, Elections, United States House of Representatives
description: A rectangular red, white, and blue campaign button for Angie Craig's run for Congress in Minnesota.

name: Make Every Day World AIDS Day
title: MAKE EVERY DAY WORLD AIDS DAY
subject: HIV/AIDS, Global Health, Awareness
description: A button featuring a red awareness ribbon over a blue globe, surrounded by a red border with the text 'MAKE EVERY DAY WORLD AIDS DAY'.

name: equality begins at home ; March 21-27, 1999 ; National Gay and Lesbian Task Force ; Federation of LGBT Statewide Political Organizations
title: Equality Begins at Home
subject: LGBTQ rights, Equality, Activism
description: A rectangular white button with blue and pink text promoting the 'Equality Begins at Home' campaign held March 21-27, 1999. It mentions the National Gay and Lesbian Task Force and the Federation of LGBT Statewide Political Organizations and features a stylized star graphic.

name: Radway US Senate
title: RADWAY US SENATE
subject: US Senate, Campaign, New Hampshire
description: Red, white, and blue campaign button for Laurence Radway's 1982 run for the U.S. Senate in New Hampshire, featuring a blue star and red vertical stripes.

name: Troops out now! No Blood for Oil
title: TROOPS OUT NOW! NO BLOOD FOR OIL
subject: Iraq War, anti-war, protest
description: This anti-war protest button features the slogans 'TROOPS OUT NOW!' and 'NO BLOOD FOR OIL'. The 'No Blood for Oil' slogan was used during both the Gulf War and the Iraq War, and 'Troops Out Now' became a major slogan for anti-war coalitions in the early 2000s.

name: From all walks of life ; June 2nd 1991 ; Boston
title: From all walks of life® June 2nd 1991 Boston
subject: AIDS Walk, HIV/AIDS, Charity
description: A square white button featuring a multi-colored, four-paneled composite face graphic. This button was produced for the 1991 'From All Walks of Life' event, which was the name of the Boston AIDS Walk organized by the AIDS Action Committee of Massachusetts.

name: TAXI DRIVERS for FORD
title: TAXI DRIVERS for FORD
subject: Taxi drivers, Labor, 1976 Presidential Election
description: A circular yellow button with black text that reads 'TAXI DRIVERS for FORD'. This button was used during Gerald Ford's 1976 presidential campaign.

name: Morse for Congress
title: MORSE FOR CONGRESS
subject: Political campaign, Election, Massachusetts
description: A rectangular wooden campaign pin featuring the name 'MORSE' in large letters and 'FOR CONGRESS' in smaller letters, with a star. The text is branded or burned into the wood.

name: Elect Morgenthau ; Governor
title: ELECT MORGENTHAU GOVERNOR
subject: Robert Morgenthau, Gubernatorial Campaign, 1962 Election
description: Political campaign button for Robert M. Morgenthau's 1962 run for Governor of New York. The button has a red top section with 'ELECT' and two stars, a white middle section with 'MORGENTHAU' in blue, and a blue bottom section with 'GOVERNOR' in white.

name: These colors will not run ; Mysticbank Unit, Winchester, Mass
title: THESE COLORS WILL NOT RUN · MYSTICBANK UNIT, WINCHESTER, MASS ·
subject: World War I, Patriotism, Allied Flags
description: World War I era patriotic button featuring an eagle above a US shield with flags of allied nations in the background. The blue border contains the slogan "THESE COLORS WILL NOT RUN" at the top and identifies the "MYSTICBANK UNIT, WINCHESTER, MASS" at the bottom.

name: These colors will not run ; Mysticbank Unit, Winchester, Mass
title: I LIKE IKE
subject: Dwight D. Eisenhower; Presidential Elections; Campaign Buttons
description: The reverse side of an 'I Like Ike' campaign button from Dwight D. Eisenhower's 1952 presidential campaign. It features the manufacturer's imprint for the Manee Co. of Malden, Massachusetts, a union bug for the Amalgamated Lithographers of America, and the number 16.

name: Giaimo
title: GIAIMO
subject: Campaign, Election, Congress
description: A circular black button featuring the name "GIAIMO" in red capital letters.

name: Non-violence is our strength
title: NON-VIOLENCE IS OUR STRENGTH
subject: Labor Movement, Civil Rights, Nonviolence
description: This button features a photograph of labor leader Cesar Chavez and the slogan 'NON-VIOLENCE IS OUR STRENGTH.' The United Farm Workers (UFW) eagle logo is also visible below the text.

name: Adulterers for Clinton
title: ADULTERERS FOR CLINTON
subject: satire, scandal, infidelity
description: A satirical political button from the 1990s featuring a red heart on a white background with the text 'ADULTERERS FOR CLINTON', mockingly referencing Bill Clinton's extramarital scandals.

name: Picture of Ronald Reagan
title: Ronald Reagan in Cowboy Hat
subject: Republican Party, US President, Ronald Reagan
description: A circular button featuring a photographic portrait of Ronald Reagan wearing a cowboy hat. The image evokes Reagan's persona as a Western rancher and was likely produced for one of his presidential campaigns.

name: Assemblyman Vito Battista
title: Assemblyman Vito Battista
subject: New York Politics, Assembly, Republican Party
description: A blue political button with white text for Vito Battista, who served in the New York State Assembly representing Brooklyn from 1969 to 1974.

name: Stokes
title: STOKES
subject: African American politicians, Civil Rights, Local elections
description: A campaign button for Carl Stokes, featuring the word "STOKES" with the letters "OK" in white on a red background. Stokes was the first African American mayor of a major US city.

name: Our common heritage ; Freedom is our job ; Freedom Train
title: OUR COMMON HERITAGE FREEDOM IS OUR JOB FREEDOM TRAIN
subject: Freedom Train, Patriotism, American History
description: Red, white, and blue button featuring the American Freedom Train, which toured the US from 1947 to 1949 carrying historical documents. The button includes the slogans 'Our Common Heritage' and 'Freedom is Our Job'.

name: Our common heritage ; Freedom is our job ; Freedom Train
title: WILLKIE
subject: Presidential Campaign; Republican Party; Wendell Willkie
description: The reverse side of a 1940 presidential campaign button for Republican candidate Wendell Willkie, featuring a fabric ribbon in red, white, and blue tucked into the back.

name: Architects for McGovern Shriver
title: Architects for McGovern Shriver
subject: McGovern, Shriver, Architects
description: A blue campaign button for the 1972 McGovern-Shriver presidential ticket, featuring the text "Architects for McGovern Shriver".

name: I'm for Jonas
title: I'M FOR JONAS
subject: Republican, Campaign, North Carolina
description: A campaign button for Charles Raper Jonas, a Republican politician from North Carolina who served in the U.S. House of Representatives from 1953 to 1973.

name: My study is the heart of the people ; Willie Stark
title: MY STUDY IS THE HEART OF THE PEOPLE WILLIE STARK
subject: Politics, Movies, Fiction
description: A promotional button for the 1949 film 'All the King's Men', featuring actor Broderick Crawford as the fictional Louisiana politician Willie Stark. The character of Willie Stark was based on the life of Huey Long.

name: Vote no ; Nov. 9th
title: VOTE NO NOV. 8th
subject: prohibition, voting, election
description: A white and black celluloid button from 1910 urging a 'No' vote on November 8th, most likely used in campaigns against state-level prohibition amendments, such as the one in Missouri that year.

name: AMA ; Guest meeting
title: AMA GUEST MEETING
subject: American Medical Association, healthcare, medical convention
description: A circular green and white button for guests attending a meeting of the American Medical Association (AMA).

name: Rockefeller for Governor
title: ROCKEFELLER FOR GOVERNOR
subject: Rockefeller, Governor, Election
description: Blue and white button featuring a portrait of Nelson Rockefeller for his gubernatorial campaign in New York.

name: CALL ME MS., NOT Mrs. Him
title: CALL ME MS., NOT Mrs. Him
subject: Feminism, Women's Rights, Social Movements
description: A round yellow button with green text that reads 'CALL ME MS., NOT Mrs. Him'. This button was used during the second-wave feminist movement of the 1970s to advocate for the use of the title 'Ms.', allowing women to have a professional or social title that does not indicate their marital status.

name: No to Amendment #5
title: NO TO AMENDMENT #5
subject: Ballot Initiative, Florida, Taxes
description: A political campaign button using a 'Do Not Enter' sign design to oppose Amendment 5 on the 1994 Florida ballot. Amendment 5 was a proposed constitutional amendment regarding state revenue limitations.

name: Talks not troops
title: TALKS NOT TROOPS
subject: Anti-war, Peace, Diplomacy
description: A circular yellow button with the slogan 'TALKS NOT TROOPS' printed in bold blue letters. This button is a piece of anti-war memorabilia, advocating for diplomatic negotiations as an alternative to military deployment.

name: When we win
title: WHEN WE WIN
subject: Presidential campaign, Shirley Chisholm, 1972
description: A black button with the words 'WHEN WE WIN' in red. This slogan was famously used by Shirley Chisholm during her 1972 presidential campaign.

name: I'm afraid 2 go home in the dark
title: I'M AFRAID 2 GO HOME IN THE DARK
subject: Popular music, Novelty, Humor
description: A circular novelty button from the early 20th century featuring the text 'I'M AFRAID 2 GO HOME IN THE DARK'. This phrase refers to a popular 1907 song of the same name written by Harry Williams and Egbert Van Alstyne.

name: Battle of Britain ; Museum Appeal
title: BATTLE OF BRITAIN MUSEUM APPEAL
subject: World War II, Aviation, Fundraising
description: This circular button features the text "BATTLE OF BRITAIN MUSEUM APPEAL" in dark blue around the perimeter. The center shows a blue sky with white contrails looping around and small silhouettes of aircraft, representing a dogfight from the Battle of Britain. It was likely produced to raise funds for the Battle of Britain Museum at Hendon, which opened in 1978.

name: I'm extremely fond of Barry
title: I'M EXTREMELY FOND OF BARRY
subject: Barry Goldwater, Extremism, 1964 Election
description: A white button with blue text reading "I'M EXTREMELY FOND OF BARRY". The wording is a play on Republican presidential candidate Barry Goldwater's famous quote from his 1964 acceptance speech: "Extremism in the defense of liberty is no vice."

name: Re-Elect Dick Lee ; Mayor
title: Re-Elect DICK LEE Mayor
subject: Mayoral campaign, New Haven politics, Democratic party
description: A red, white, and blue button for the re-election campaign of Richard C. Lee as Mayor of New Haven, Connecticut. Lee served as mayor for eight terms between 1954 and 1970.

name: Stassen
title: STASSEN
subject: Presidential election, Wisconsin primary, Political candidate
description: Campaign button for Harold Stassen's 1948 presidential run, featuring the name 'STASSEN' in blue text across a white silhouette of Wisconsin on a green background.

name: Acquit the Morristown 5 ; VPF
title: ACQUIT THE MORRISTOWN 5 VPF
subject: peace, protest, anti-nuclear
description: A button produced by the Vermont Peace Fellowship (VPF) calling for the acquittal of the 'Morristown 5', five activists arrested for a sit-in at a General Electric plant in Vermont in 1984.

name: Make Sharp Governor
title: MAKE SHAPP GOVERNOR
subject: Milton Shapp, Governor, Pennsylvania
description: A blue and gold political campaign button for Milton Shapp's gubernatorial run in Pennsylvania, featuring the state's keystone symbol.

name: It's Great to Live in Michigan
title: IT'S GREAT TO LIVE IN MICHIGAN
subject: Michigan state pride, Mackinac Bridge, American Robin
description: A yellow pinback button promoting the state of Michigan. It features a cartoon robin, the state bird, standing on the Mackinac Bridge. The bridge was completed in late 1957, making this button a likely promotional item from shortly thereafter.

name: Thank you for not smoking
title: THANK YOU FOR NOT SMOKING
subject: Anti-smoking, Health, American Lung Association
description: A round pinback button produced by the American Lung Association featuring their signature red double-barred cross logo and the text 'THANK YOU FOR NOT SMOKING' in blue. This button was part of public health campaigns in the 1970s to discourage smoking in public spaces.

name: I'm extremely fond of Barry
title: NO FOURTH TERM EITHER
subject: Franklin D. Roosevelt, Presidential Election, Term Limits
description: A political campaign button from 1944 expressing opposition to Franklin D. Roosevelt serving a fourth term as President of the United States.

name: I'm extremely fond of Barry
title: OFFSET GRAVURE CORPORATION
subject: manufacturing, advertising, unionism
description: The reverse side of a pinback button featuring a paper insert for the Offset Gravure Corporation of New York City. The company specialized in celluloid and metal novelties and color offset lithography. The insert prominently displays a union bug for Local No. 1 of the Amalgamated Lithographers of America.

name: Dan MacKinnon ; The Tax Cutter
title: Dan MacKinnon THE TAX CUTTER
subject: Taxes, Politics, Elections
description: A political campaign button for Republican candidate Dan MacKinnon, who ran for California's 44th congressional district in the late 1980s. The button uses a red, white, and blue color scheme with stars and highlights his stance as 'The Tax Cutter'.

name: Clinton ; Gore
title: Clinton Gore
subject: 1992 Presidential Election, Bill Clinton, Al Gore
description: A round campaign button from the 1992 United States presidential election. It features the names "Clinton" and "Gore" in bold text. The top half of the button shows stylized red and white waves representing the American flag, with a blue star dotting the "i" in Clinton. The bottom half of the button is a solid blue field with the name "Gore" in white.

name: I'm for Chris
title: I'M FOR CHRIS
subject: Campaigns, Portraits, Governors
description: A campaign button for Christopher Del Sesto's gubernatorial run in Rhode Island, featuring his portrait over a patriotic shield and the slogan 'I'm For Chris'.

name: We Americans ; WHK ; UBC
title: WHK WE AMERICANS UBC
subject: radio, patriotism, broadcasting
description: A promotional button for the "We Americans" radio program, broadcast on station WHK in Cleveland, Ohio, which was a member of the United Broadcasting Company (UBC).

name: We Americans ; WHK ; UBC
title: STATE NOVELTY CO. CLEVELAND, OHIO.
person: James M. Cox and Franklin D. Roosevelt
subject: manufacturing, unions, elections
description: The reverse side of a 1920 presidential campaign button for the Democratic ticket of James M. Cox and Franklin D. Roosevelt. It displays the maker's mark for the State Novelty Co. of Cleveland, Ohio, and features two union labels (bugs) from the International Photo-Engravers' Union and the Allied Printing Trades Council.

name: Vote for Dick Gregory ; President ; Mark Lane V.P.
title: Vote for DICK GREGORY PRESIDENT MARK LANE V.P.
subject: civil rights activism, presidential candidate, third party
description: This button supports the 1968 presidential campaign of comedian and activist Dick Gregory and his running mate Mark Lane. It features a design of alternating red, black, and yellow horizontal stripes.

name: Sharaf ; for U.S. Congress
title: SHARAF FOR U.S. CONGRESS
subject: politics, election, congress
description: A campaign button for James Sharaf's 1974 run for the United States House of Representatives in Massachusetts's 8th congressional district.

name: Speziale Treasurer ; Good Team! ; Top Lever!
title: GOOD TEAM! SPEZIALE TREASURER TOP LEVER!
subject: Campaign, Election, Connecticut
description: A red, white, and blue campaign button for John Speziale, who was elected State Treasurer of Connecticut in 1958 on the Democratic ticket. The phrase "Top Lever" refers to voting for the straight party ticket on voting machines of the time.

name: Carey ; Krupsak
title: Carey Krupsak
subject: Political Campaign, Election, New York State
description: A blue campaign button for the 1974 New York State gubernatorial election featuring the names of the Democratic candidates Hugh Carey and Mary Anne Krupsak.

name: Sterling & Range
title: STERLING RANGE
subject: Advertising, Manufacturing, Kitchenware
description: An advertising button for Sterling Range stoves, manufactured by the Sill Stove Works of Rochester, New York. The design prominently features a pound sterling (£) symbol as a visual pun on the brand name.

name: Sterling & Range
title: GOLDWATER IN '64
subject: Barry Goldwater, 1964 Presidential Election, Republican Party
description: The reverse side of a 1964 presidential campaign button for Republican candidate Barry Goldwater, showing the manufacturer's mark for Bastian Bros. Co. of Rochester, New York.

name: Lefkowitz ; Attorney General
title: LEFKOWITZ ATTORNEY GENERAL
subject: New York politics, Republican Party, State Attorney General
description: Blue and white striped campaign button for Louis J. Lefkowitz, who served as New York Attorney General from 1957 to 1978.

name: Beame for Mayor
title: BEAME For MAYOR
subject: politics, mayoral election, campaign
description: Campaign button for Abraham Beame's mayoral candidacy in New York City. The button features a portrait of Beame against a red, white, and blue background with the text 'BEAME For MAYOR'.

name: Click with Dick
title: CLICK WITH DICK
subject: Richard Nixon, Presidential Campaign, 1960
description: A campaign button for Richard Nixon's 1960 presidential bid, featuring the catchphrase "Click with Dick" in blue lettering against a white starburst pattern on a red background.

name: Extension Minute Man ; Food for Victory
title: EXTENSION MINUTE MAN FOOD FOR VICTORY
subject: World War II, Food Supply, Extension Service
description: A round white button featuring a large red 'V' for Victory. Over the 'V' is a blue silhouette of a Minuteman on horseback. The words 'EXTENSION MINUTE MAN' and 'FOOD FOR VICTORY' are printed in blue around the edge.

name: Extension Minute Man ; Food for Victory
title: SOMMER BADGE MFG. CO. BADGES, BUTTONS, PREMIUM RIBBONS, BANNERS, ETC. 71 LIBERTY ST. NEWARK, N.J.
subject: Manufacturing; Advertising; Button Back
description: The reverse side of a pinback button featuring the manufacturer's back paper for the Sommer Badge Manufacturing Company, located at 71 Liberty St. in Newark, New Jersey.

name: Say so, say Sasso ; Metro Dist #2
title: SAY SO, SAY SASSO METRO DIST #2
subject: Local elections; Miami-Dade County; Metro Commission
description: Campaign button for Louis J. Sasso, candidate for the Metro Dade County Commission, District 2. The button features a portrait of Sasso and uses a rhyming slogan with his name.

name: Lowenstein
title: LOWENSTEIN
subject: Political Campaign; US Representative; Allard K. Lowenstein
description: A red, white, and blue political campaign button for Allard K. Lowenstein, likely produced for his 1968 run for the U.S. House of Representatives representing New York's 5th congressional district.

name: John Heinz for Senate
title: John Heinz for Senate
subject: Senate, Pennsylvania, Republican
description: A blue button with red and white text supporting John Heinz for the United States Senate in Pennsylvania.

name: Kennedy Action Corps
title: Kennedy Action Corps
subject: Kennedy Action Corps
description: A blue campaign button for Robert F. Kennedy's 1968 presidential run, featuring the text 'Kennedy Action Corps' and a white upward-pointing arrow.

name: Stop the maul
title: STOP THE MAUL
subject: Urban development, Environment, Local protest
description: A white pinback button with the words "STOP THE MAUL" in red and black block letters. The button is a pun on "Stop the Mall", referring to the local opposition to the proposed Pyramid Mall in Williston and Burlington, Vermont during the late 1970s.

name: Don Potter Sheriff ; Good Team! ; Top Lever!
title: GOOD TEAM! DON POTTER SHERIFF TOP LEVER!
subject: Law Enforcement, Elections, Politics
description: A red, white, and blue political campaign button for Don Potter's run for Sheriff of Genesee County, Michigan. The button encourages voters to pull the 'Top Lever,' a reference to straight-ticket voting.

name: The American Way
title: THE AMERICAN WAY
subject: Republican, Slogan, Campaign
description: A white campaign button featuring the slogan "THE AMERICAN WAY" in tall, red, sans-serif lettering. This slogan was a centerpiece of Republican candidate Wendell Willkie's 1940 presidential campaign, used to promote free enterprise and criticize President Franklin D. Roosevelt's New Deal policies.

name: Save Our Children
title: SAVE OUR CHILDREN
subject: LGBTQ rights, Anita Bryant, Save Our Children
description: A button from the 1977 campaign 'Save Our Children' led by Anita Bryant to repeal a Miami-Dade County ordinance protecting gay rights.

name: Nutmeggers for Peanuts
title: NUTMEGGERS FOR PEANUTS
subject: Jimmy Carter, Presidential Campaign, Connecticut
description: A white button with green text 'NUTMEGGERS FOR PEANUTS'. This button was used in Jimmy Carter's presidential campaign, appealing to voters in Connecticut ('Nutmeggers') by referencing Carter's background as a peanut farmer.

name: Roger Hilsman ; Democrat for Congress
title: Roger Hilsman Democrat for Congress
subject: Political Campaign, Congressional Election, Democratic Party
description: A white button with black text reading "Roger Hilsman Democrat for Congress" featuring red and blue diagonal stripes.

name: Stop at two.
title: Stop at two.
subject: population growth, family planning, Zero Population Growth
description: A black button with yellow text reading "Stop at two.", a slogan used by the Zero Population Growth (ZPG) movement to advocate for small family sizes as a means of controlling population growth.

name: Stop at two.
title: ASSOCIATION FOR VOLUNTARY STERILIZATION 14 W. 40TH ST. NYC 10018
subject: reproductive rights, sterilization, family planning
description: The back side of a pin-back button showing the contact information for the Association for Voluntary Sterilization, including their address at 14 West 40th Street in New York City.

name: Orr ; Mutz
title: ORR MUTZ
subject: Indiana, Politics, Elections
description: A campaign button for the 1980 or 1984 Indiana gubernatorial ticket of Robert D. Orr and John Mutz.

name: Attica [several bullet holes]
title: ATTICA
subject: Attica Prison riot
description: A green button with the word 'ATTICA' in red letters, appearing to be riddled with bullet holes. This button commemorates or protests the Attica Prison riot of 1971 and the subsequent violent takeover by state forces.

name: Steve Wareck for Congress
title: Steve Wareck for Congress
subject: Elections; Republican Party; United States House of Representatives
description: Campaign button for Steve Wareck, who ran as a Republican candidate for the United States House of Representatives from Connecticut's 3rd district in 1980.

name: Humphrey ; Muskie
title: HUMPHREY MUSKIE
subject: 1968 election, Hubert Humphrey, Edmund Muskie
description: A campaign button for the 1968 presidential election ticket of Hubert Humphrey and Edmund Muskie. The button is split into a blue top half and red bottom half with a white horizontal stripe in the middle.

name: Walker
title: Walker
subject: Politics, Wisconsin, Elections
description: A magenta campaign button featuring the name 'Walker' in white serif font, associated with Scott Walker's 2012 gubernatorial recall election campaign in Wisconsin.

name: Pillsbury for Governor
title: PILLSBURY FOR GOVERNOR
subject: Republican Party, Gubernatorial elections, Political campaigns
description: A political campaign button for John Pillsbury, the Republican nominee for Governor of New Hampshire in 1964.

name: Dictator? Not for US
title: DICTATOR? NOT FOR US
subject: Anti-Roosevelt, Dictatorship, Third Term
description: A campaign button from the 1940 election cycle, expressing opposition to President Franklin D. Roosevelt's unprecedented pursuit of a third term in office. Critics at the time labeled him a 'dictator' for breaking the two-term tradition established by George Washington.

name: Mills
title: MILLS
subject: campaign, presidential, 1972
description: A red, white, and blue political campaign button for Wilbur Mills' 1972 presidential run.

name: Nixon's the one!
title: NIXON'S THE ONE!
subject: 1968 Election, Richard Nixon, Presidential Campaign
description: A white pinback button with red text that reads 'NIXON'S THE ONE!'. This slogan was widely used during Richard Nixon's successful 1968 presidential campaign.

name: They're for you ; Ike ; Dick
title: THEY'RE FOR YOU IKE DICK
subject: Dwight D. Eisenhower, Richard Nixon, Presidential Campaign
description: A campaign button from the 1952 United States presidential election featuring portraits of Republican candidates Dwight D. Eisenhower and Richard Nixon with the slogan 'They're For You'.

name: Elect Reinhardsen ; Congress
title: ELECT REINHARDSEN CONGRESS
subject: Politics, Election, Congress
description: A circular green button with white text from David Reinhardsen's 1968 campaign for the U.S. House of Representatives in New York's 21st district.

name: Bonee ; Probate Judge
title: BONEE PROBATE JUDGE
subject: Probate Court, Judicial Election, Hartford Politics
description: A campaign button for John L. Bonee for the position of Probate Judge in Hartford, Connecticut. The button features red and blue text on a white background, highlighting the initials 'BPJ'.

name: [clothes hanger with red slash through it]
title: [Graphic of a crossed-out wire coat hanger]
subject: Abortion Rights, Pro-Choice, Women's Health
description: A circular white button featuring a black wire coat hanger with a red diagonal line through it. This symbol was popularized by the pro-choice movement to represent the danger of illegal, unsafe abortions and the necessity for legal reproductive healthcare.

name: Vota Partido Socialista de los Trabajadores ; Gonzalez para vice presidente
title: Vota Partido Socialista de los Trabajadores González para vicepresidente
subject: Socialist Workers Party, Andrea González, Presidential Election
description: A campaign button for Andrea González, the Socialist Workers Party candidate for Vice President of the United States in 1984. The button features a portrait of González and text in Spanish urging voters to support the party's ticket. The edge of the button identifies the Socialist Workers National Campaign Committee.

name: Humphrey ; Muskie ; Pill top lever
title: HUMPHREY PULL TOP LEVER MUSKIE
subject: campaign, voting, presidency
description: A circular blue and white button from the 1968 US presidential campaign supporting Hubert Humphrey for President and Edmund Muskie for Vice President.

name: P.H.B.F.
title: P.H.B.F.
subject: Labor Union, Health Insurance, Pottery Industry
description: A round white button with the red acronym 'P.H.B.F.', which stands for the Potters Health and Benefit Fund. This fund was established in 1949 by the International Brotherhood of Operative Potters to manage health benefits for its members.

name: No Accidents ; Oct. 18-31 ; Eastern Region
title: NO ACCIDENTS OCT. 18-31 EASTERN REGION
subject: Safety, Labor Unions, Industrial Workplace
description: A green and red safety incentive button featuring the slogan 'NO ACCIDENTS' and dates 'OCT. 18-31' for the 'EASTERN REGION'. This button was likely used as part of a workplace safety campaign, common in the railroad industry during the mid-20th century. It bears the union label of the Lithographers and Photoengravers International Union (LP&GA), which dates its manufacture to between 1964 and 1972.

name: Wagner ; U.S. Senate
title: WAGNER U.S. SENATE
subject: Politics, Campaign, New York
description: A political campaign button featuring a portrait of Robert F. Wagner Jr. during his 1956 campaign for the U.S. Senate in New York.

name: Elect Ottinger ; Governor
title: ELECT OTTINGER GOVERNOR
subject: Republican Party, New York, Gubernatorial
description: A 1928 campaign button for Republican Albert Ottinger's run for Governor of New York.

name: Longstreth for Mayor
title: LONGSTRETH FOR MAYOR
subject: Philadelphia, Mayor, Thacher Longstreth
description: Red, white, and blue campaign button featuring a portrait of Thacher Longstreth for his 1955 mayoral campaign in Philadelphia.

name: "Keep The Faith, Baby!"
title: "Keep The Faith, Baby!"
subject: Civil Rights, Politics, Harlem
description: Political button featuring a portrait of Adam Clayton Powell Jr. and his signature slogan 'Keep the Faith, Baby!'. The slogan became a symbol of defiance and resilience for his constituents in Harlem during his legal challenges and exclusion from Congress in 1967.

name: Families need Caroline K. Simon
title: FAMILIES NEED CAROLINE K. SIMON
subject: Women in politics, Republican Party, Campaign button
description: A campaign button for Caroline K. Simon, a New York Republican who served as the Secretary of State of New York from 1959 to 1963. The button features her portrait and the slogan 'Families Need Caroline K. Simon' in a red, white, and blue color scheme.

name: I'M GOIN'
title: I'M GOIN'
subject: Civil Rights Movement, March on Washington, Activism
description: This button was produced for the March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom on August 28, 1963, to indicate that the wearer intended to participate in the event.

name: Babbitt of Arizona ; 1988
title: BABBITT OF ARIZONA 19 88
subject: Presidential campaign, Bruce Babbitt, Arizona
description: A political campaign button for Bruce Babbitt's 1988 run for President. It shows a portrait of Babbitt surrounded by the text "BABBITT OF ARIZONA 19 88" and stars.

name: Bella for senate
title: Bella for senate
subject: Political campaign, US Senate, Bella Abzug
description: Campaign button for Bella Abzug's 1976 bid for the U.S. Senate from New York, featuring her characteristic silhouette with a wide-brimmed hat.

name: Vote Commoner Harris ; Consumer Party
title: VOTE COMMONER HARRIS CONSUMER PARTY
subject: Barry Commoner, Consumer Party, Presidential Election
description: Campaign button for Barry Commoner and LaDonna Harris in the 1980 presidential election. The ticket ran on the Consumer Party line in Pennsylvania.

name: FREE The PANTHERS; SEIZE The TIME [fist holding rifle]
title: FREE The PANTHERS SEIZE The TIME
subject: Black Panther Party, Activism, Liberation
description: A green and black button advocating for the Black Panther Party with the slogans "FREE The PANTHERS" and "SEIZE The TIME". "Seize the Time" is the title of a 1970 book by Bobby Seale, a co-founder of the party.

name: For City Councilman ; Michael J. Quill ; President ; Transport Workers Union
title: FOR CITY COUNCILMAN MICHAEL J. QUILL PRESIDENT TRANSPORT WORKERS UNION
subject: Labor union, Local election, American Labor Party
description: Campaign button for Michael J. Quill, president of the Transport Workers Union, running for New York City Council on the American Labor Party ticket.

name: Smith ; For CONRESS
title: SMITH For CONGRESS
subject: Political Campaign, Iowa, US Congress
description: A campaign button for Neal Smith, a Democratic congressman from Iowa who served in the U.S. House of Representatives from 1959 to 1995. The button features a black and white portrait of Smith on a red, white, and blue background.

name: 1% For Peace [border of people holding hands]
title: 1% FOR PEACE
subject: Peace, Military, Budget
description: This button is from the '1% for Peace' campaign, which was started by Ben Cohen of Ben & Jerry's Ice Cream in 1988. The goal of the campaign was to redirect 1% of the U.S. defense budget to programs that promoted world peace and cross-cultural understanding.

name: Exterminate these 3 rats ; Mussi ' Adolf ; Togo
title: EXTERMINATE THESE 3 RATS MUSSI ADOLF TOGO
person: Benito Mussolini, Adolf Hitler, Hideki Tojo
subject: World War II, Propaganda, Axis Leaders
description: A World War II-era propaganda button featuring the caricatured heads of Axis leaders Benito Mussolini, Adolf Hitler, and Hideki Tojo (labeled as Mussi, Adolf, and Togo). The text calls for their extermination and refers to them as '3 rats,' accompanied by an illustration of a rat.

name: Reelect Harriman ; Governor
title: REELECT HARRIMAN GOVERNOR
subject: New York, Harriman, Reelection
description: A political campaign button for Averell Harriman's unsuccessful 1958 re-election bid for Governor of New York, featuring a portrait of Harriman.

name: My pick is Dick
title: MY PICK IS DICK
subject: Richard Nixon, Presidential Election, Campaign Slogan
description: A campaign button from Richard Nixon's presidential campaign, featuring the slogan 'MY PICK IS DICK' in blue text on a white background.

name: Dictator? Not for US
title: DICTATOR? NOT FOR US
subject: Franklin D. Roosevelt, Presidential Election, Third Term
description: An anti-Franklin D. Roosevelt button from the 1940 presidential election, expressing opposition to his pursuit of an unprecedented third term in office.

name: Tom Salmon for Vermont
title: Tom Salmon for Vermont
subject: Thomas P. Salmon, Governor, Vermont
description: A white and green campaign button for Thomas P. Salmon, who served as the 75th Governor of Vermont from 1973 to 1977. The button features the text 'Tom Salmon for Vermont' and a green mountain silhouette.

name: Scranton for President
title: SCRANTON FOR PRESIDENT
subject: 1964 presidential election, Republican party, William Scranton
description: Round white button with blue text supporting William Scranton's 1964 bid for the presidency. Scranton was the Governor of Pennsylvania at the time.

name: POLES for FORD
title: POLES for FORD
subject: Gerald Ford, Polish Americans, Presidential Election
description: A red circular campaign button with white text reading 'POLES for FORD', used during Gerald Ford's 1976 presidential campaign to appeal to Polish-American voters.

name: Elect Clancy Ferrara ; Selectman
title: ELECT CLANCY FERRARA SELECTMEN
subject: Local Elections, Selectmen, Political Campaigns
description: A white political campaign button advocating for the election of Clancy and Ferrara to the Board of Selectmen. The text is printed in alternating blue and red ink, with 'ELECT' and 'FERRARA' in blue and 'CLANCY' and 'SELECTMEN' in red.

name: HUNGARIANS for FORD
title: HUNGARIANS for FORD
subject: Gerald Ford, 1976 Presidential Campaign, Hungarian Americans
description: A white button with blue text that reads 'HUNGARIANS for FORD', created for Gerald Ford's 1976 presidential campaign to appeal to Hungarian American voters.

name: [silhouette of Bella Abzug]
subject: George McGovern, Presidential campaign, Silhouette
description: A 1972 presidential campaign button for George McGovern, featuring a blue silhouette of the candidate's profile on a white background. This stylized image is often referred to as the 'McGovern Silhouette'.

name: APARTHEID [red slash through text]
title: APARTHEID
subject: Anti-Apartheid, Human Rights, South Africa
description: A circular button with a white background, featuring a red universal "no" symbol (a circle with a diagonal slash) over the word "APARTHEID" in black capital letters, expressing opposition to the system of institutionalized racial segregation in South Africa.

name: Harold Hughed in '72
title: HAROLD HUGHES IN '72
subject: Presidential Election, Democratic Party, Harold Hughes
description: A red, white, and blue campaign button supporting Harold Hughes for the 1972 United States presidential election.

name: It's Quie for me
title: IT'S QUIE FOR ME
subject: Campaign, Albert Quie, Governor
description: A white campaign button with red text reading 'IT'S QUIE FOR ME', supporting Albert Quie for Governor of Minnesota in 1978.

name: Another Yalie for Dukakis
title: ANOTHER YALIE FOR DUKAKIS
subject: Election, Education, Support
description: A blue and white campaign button for Michael Dukakis' 1988 presidential run, specifically aimed at Yale University students or alumni.

name: Lodge for Senator
title: LODGE FOR SENATOR
subject: Political Campaign, US Senate, Massachusetts
description: A blue and white campaign button for Henry Cabot Lodge Jr.'s run for the U.S. Senate from Massachusetts. It features three stars at the top, the name 'LODGE' in the center, and 'FOR SENATOR' below.

name: ELect Zeferetti ; Congress
title: ELECT ZEFERETTI CONGRESS
subject: Election, Campaign, Congress
description: Red, white, and blue campaign button for Leo C. Zeferetti's congressional campaign in New York.

name: I Recycle! Do You?
title: I Recycle! Do You?
subject: recycling, environment, sustainability
description: A round white pinback button featuring a green recycling symbol surrounding the text "I Recycle!" in black and "Do You?" in green.

name: DeGroot For U.S. Congress
title: DeGROOT FOR U.S. CONGRESS
subject: Political campaign, US Congress, 2022 Election
description: White button featuring the name 'DeGROOT' in red and 'FOR U.S. CONGRESS' in blue text, accompanied by a blue graphic of a columned building.

name: Let's go with Curtis [traffic light with green and yellow light]
title: LET'S GO WITH CURTISS
subject: Kenneth M. Curtis; Traffic signals; Maine politics
description: This political pinback button supports Kenneth M. Curtis's 1966 campaign for Governor of Maine. It features a graphic of a traffic signal and the text 'LET'S GO WITH CURTISS', misspelling the candidate's last name.

name: PAT for FIRST LADY
title: PAT for FIRST LADY
subject: Pat Nixon; Presidential Election; First Lady
description: Blue campaign button with white text that reads 'PAT for FIRST LADY', advocating for Pat Nixon's role as First Lady during Richard Nixon's presidential campaigns.

name: Let's go with Curtis [traffic light with green and yellow light]
title: I'M FOR COX AND COCK-TAILS
subject: James M. Cox, Prohibition, Presidential election
description: The reverse side of a political button from the 1920 presidential campaign supporting James M. Cox. It was manufactured by the Green Duck Company of Chicago and features their logo, a patent date of February 3, 1917, and union labels.

name: DIXON
title: DIXON
subject: Political Campaign, Election, Illinois
description: A circular political campaign button for Alan J. Dixon, likely from one of his campaigns for office in Illinois. The button features the name 'DIXON' in white letters against a background split horizontally between black and green.

name: Donald L. O'Toole ; For Congress
title: FOR CONGRESS DONALD L. O'TOOLE
subject: Political elections, US Congress, New York
description: A campaign button for Donald L. O'Toole, featuring his portrait. O'Toole was a Democratic U.S. Representative from New York, serving in the House from 1937 to 1953.

name: Donald L. O'Toole ; For Congress
title: MADE BY Eagle Regalia Co 298 Broadway New York
subject: Wendell Willkie, Presidential Election, Republican Party
description: The reverse side of a campaign button supporting Wendell Willkie in the 1940 United States presidential election. It features the manufacturer's mark for the Eagle Regalia Company of New York City and a union label for the Allied Printing Trades Council.

name: I Like Pat
title: I LIKE PAT
subject: Pat Nixon; Political campaign; Republican Party
description: Round button with a red border and the blue text 'I LIKE PAT' on an off-white background. The button is promotional material for Pat Nixon, the wife of Richard Nixon, during one of his political campaigns.

name: Elect Russell ; President
title: ELECT RUSSELL PRESIDENT
subject: Presidential Campaign, Richard Russell, Democratic Party
description: A red, white, and blue political campaign button produced for Richard Russell Jr., a U.S. Senator from Georgia who sought the Democratic nomination for President in 1948 and 1952. The button was manufactured by the Green Duck Co. in Chicago.

name: Elect Russell ; President
title: KENNEDY FOR PRESIDENT
subject: Presidential Campaign, John F. Kennedy, Green Duck Company
description: The reverse side of a 1960 John F. Kennedy presidential campaign button, showing the Green Duck Company's 'Mark of Quality' logo and an Allied Printing Trades Council union bug from Chicago.

name: Young America ; Wants to help
title: YOUNG AMERICA WANTS TO HELP
subject: World War II, Relief Efforts, Children
description: This button was produced by 'Young America Wants to Help', an organization founded in 1940 to provide relief to children in Europe during World War II. The design features a red, white, and blue target pattern resembling a military roundel.

name: Kennedy for President
title: KENNEDY For PRESIDENT
subject: John F. Kennedy, Presidential Campaign, 1960
description: A political campaign button for John F. Kennedy's 1960 presidential run. It features a portrait of Kennedy against a red, white, and blue background with the text 'KENNEDY For PRESIDENT'.

name: Re-elect Gov. King
title: RE-ELECT GOV. KING
subject: Governor, New Hampshire, Re-election
description: Campaign button for John W. King's re-election as Governor of New Hampshire, featuring a black and white portrait of King with his name in orange at the bottom.

name: Azadootyan khoomp
title: Azadootyan khoomp
subject: Presidential Campaign, Armenian American, Republican Party
description: A 1964 campaign button for Republican presidential candidate Barry Goldwater and vice-presidential candidate William E. Miller. The button includes the phrase 'Azadootyan khoomp', which is Armenian for 'Group of Freedom', as part of an ethnic outreach effort.

name: Peterson
title: Peterson
subject: Political campaigns, Governors, Delaware
description: A campaign button for Russell W. Peterson's gubernatorial runs in Delaware, featuring the name 'Peterson' in a split red and blue font on a white background.

name: Nixon Now!
title: NIXON NOW!
subject: Richard Nixon, 1972 Presidential Election, Campaign Slogans
description: A campaign button for Richard Nixon's 1972 re-election campaign featuring a central portrait of Nixon surrounded by the slogan 'NIXON NOW!' on a red, white, and blue background.

name: Stassen in 1980
title: STASSEN IN 1980
subject: Republican Party, Presidential election, Harold Stassen
description: A circular yellow button promoting Harold Stassen's 1980 presidential campaign. The text "STASSEN" is in blue, and "IN 1980" is depicted with a red and blue stars and stripes pattern.

name: The best news since La Guardia ; Lindsay for Mayor
title: THE BEST NEWS SINCE LA GUARDIA LINDSAY FOR MAYOR
subject: John Lindsay, Mayoral election, New York City
description: A campaign button for John Lindsay's 1965 mayoral campaign in New York City, featuring his portrait and a slogan comparing him to former mayor Fiorello La Guardia.

name: One Issue ; One Platform ; Vote Republican ; Recovery
title: ONE ISSUE - ONE PLATFORM RECOVERY VOTE REPUBLICAN
subject: Great Depression, Republican Party, Economic Recovery
description: A circular political button from the mid-1930s advocating for the Republican party during the Great Depression. The button features a black elephant, the symbol of the Republican Party, carrying a plank in its trunk that reads "RECOVERY" in red text. The top border reads "ONE ISSUE - ONE PLATFORM" and the bottom border reads "VOTE REPUBLICAN".

name: Clinton ; Gore
title: Clinton Gore
subject: Presidency, Election, Democrats
description: A 1992 campaign button for Bill Clinton and Al Gore, featuring their names and stylized red stripes representing the American flag.

name: Peace and Prosperity ; with Eisenhower
title: PEACE AND PROSPERITY With EISENHOWER
subject: Peace and Prosperity, 1956 Election, Dwight D. Eisenhower
description: A campaign button for Dwight D. Eisenhower's 1956 presidential re-election campaign. It features Eisenhower's face in the center, with the slogan 'PEACE AND PROSPERITY With EISENHOWER' in blue banners. Small drawings of the Capitol building and the White House are also present.

name: No Nukes
title: NO NUKES
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Nuclear energy, Environmentalism
description: This button features the text 'NO NUKES' in green, alongside a stylized green pine tree, on a white background. The slogan was central to the anti-nuclear movement of the late 1970s, which protested the use of nuclear power and weapons. A copyright date of 1978 is printed on the edge.

name: Mitchell Zagarell ; Vote Communist
title: MITCHELL VOTE COMMUNIST ZAGARELL
subject: 1968 Election, Communist Party, Presidential Campaign
description: A 1968 campaign button for Charlene Mitchell and Michael Zagarell, the Communist Party candidates for President and Vice President of the United States.

name: Bridges ; For Senator
title: FOR BRIDGES SENATOR
subject: Campaign, Election, Politics
description: A blue and white campaign button for Styles Bridges, who served as a U.S. Senator from New Hampshire from 1937 until his death in 1961.

name: Save the Republic
title: SAVE THE REPUBLIC
subject: anti-third term, presidential election, Republican Party
description: An oval stickpin button with a white face, red border, and blue text reading "SAVE THE REPUBLIC". This slogan was used by the Republican party in the 1940 presidential election as part of their campaign against Franklin D. Roosevelt's unprecedented third-term bid.

name: Save the Republic
title: THE WHITEHEAD & HOAG CO. BUTTONS BADGES NOVELTIES AND SIGNS NEWARK, N.J.
subject: Manufacturing, Advertising, Pinback Buttons
description: The reverse side of an oval pinback button showing the label of The Whitehead & Hoag Company, a major manufacturer of celluloid buttons based in Newark, New Jersey.

name: I'm for Nixon and Lodge
title: I'M FOR NIXON and LODGE
person: Richard Nixon; Henry Cabot Lodge Jr.
subject: Richard Nixon; Henry Cabot Lodge; 1960 US Presidential Election
description: A red, white, and blue political campaign button for Richard Nixon and Henry Cabot Lodge Jr. during the 1960 United States presidential election. It features the slogan 'I'M FOR NIXON and LODGE'.

name: I'm helping Mayor Logue
title: I'm helping Mayor Logue.
subject: Boston, Political campaign, Edward J. Logue
description: A campaign button from Edward J. Logue's 1967 run for Mayor of Boston. The button features the slogan 'I'm helping Mayor Logue.' in orange and green text on a white background.

name: Aircraft Warning Service ; Volunteer Observer
title: AIRCRAFT WARNING SERVICE VOLUNTEER OBSERVER
subject: World War II, Civilian Defense, Aircraft
description: A pinback button for a volunteer observer in the Aircraft Warning Service, a civilian arm of the United States Army Ground Observer Corps active during World War II to monitor enemy aircraft movements.

name: SANTAGUIDA ; Congress
title: SANTAGUIDA Congress
subject: Political campaign, Connecticut, Congress
description: Red, white, and blue pinback button supporting Frank J. Santaguida for the U.S. House of Representatives. The button features his name in white on a red field and 'Congress' in blue on a white field.

name: Ramsey Clark
title: Ramsey Clark
subject: Politics, Election, US Senate
description: A purple circular campaign button with the name 'Ramsey Clark' in white sans-serif text, likely from his 1974 campaign for the United States Senate in New York.

name: Bill Cohen
title: Bill Cohen
subject: Republican Party; Maine; Political campaigns
description: A red political campaign button with white text that reads "Bill Cohen". William Cohen was a Republican politician from Maine who served in the U.S. House of Representatives and the U.S. Senate before becoming the U.S. Secretary of Defense.

name: Edward L. Logan ; For Congress
title: FOR CONGRESS EDWARD L. LOGAN
subject: US House, Political Candidates, Elections
description: Campaign button for Edward L. Logan's run for Congress in Massachusetts, likely around 1906. Logan was a prominent Massachusetts politician and military figure for whom Logan International Airport is named.

name: Edward L. Logan ; For Congress
title: FOR GOVERNOR EBEN S. DRAPER
subject: politics, elections, Massachusetts
description: The reverse side of a political button for Eben S. Draper's campaign for Governor of Massachusetts, showing the manufacturer's mark for W. C. Dorrety of Boston and union labels from the Allied Printing Trades Council and the Paper Box, Bag and Novelty Workers' International Union.

name: Stop ; Vote Yes! ; Civilian Review Board
title: STOP VOTE YES! CIVILIAN REVIEW BOARD
subject: Police Oversight, Referendum, Civil Rights
description: This button was used during the 1966 New York City referendum regarding the Civilian Complaint Review Board (CCRB). The Patrolmen's Benevolent Association (PBA) campaigned for a 'Yes' vote to pass a measure that would effectively stop civilian participation on the board, restricting it to police department employees.

name: Elect Mitchell ; Governor
title: ELECT MITCHELL GOVERNOR
subject: James P. Mitchell, New Jersey, gubernatorial election
description: A red, white, and blue political campaign button supporting Republican candidate James P. Mitchell for Governor of New Jersey in 1961.

name: McCarthy for President
title: MCCARTHY FOR PRESIDENT
subject: Presidential Election, 1968, Eugene McCarthy
description: A campaign button for Eugene McCarthy's 1968 presidential bid. It features a halftone portrait of McCarthy in the center, surrounded by a green border with the text 'MCCARTHY FOR PRESIDENT' in white capital letters.

name: Ike ; Dick
title: IKE DICK
person: Dwight D. Eisenhower and Richard Nixon
subject: Eisenhower, Nixon, Election
description: A political campaign button for the Republican ticket of Dwight 'Ike' Eisenhower for President and Richard 'Dick' Nixon for Vice President. The button is split horizontally, with a red top half featuring white text and a white bottom half featuring red text.

name: Ike ; Dick
title: NIXON
subject: Nixon, Republican, Presidential Election
description: The reverse side of a 1960 presidential campaign button for Richard Nixon, showing various union labels from Chicago.

name: Victory Boys ; I am enrolled
title: VICTORY BOYS I AM ENROLLED
subject: World War I, United War Work Campaign, Victory Boys
description: This button was issued to boys who participated in the Victory Boys program, a branch of the United War Work Campaign during World War I. Participants pledged to earn and donate money to support American troops.

name: DAIR
title: DAIR
subject: Democratic Party, Inflation, Recession
description: A white political button with the acronym 'DAIR' in blue, which stands for 'Democrats Against Inflation and Recession', an advocacy slogan/group active during the mid-1970s economic downturn.

name: DAIR
title: I'M FOR HUMPHREY
subject: Hubert Humphrey, 1968, Presidential Election
description: The back of a campaign button for Hubert Humphrey's 1968 presidential campaign, featuring the manufacturer's mark for the All-Around Import & Novelty Co. located at 4 W. 33 St., New York City. A union label for the Allied Printing Trades Council is also visible.

name: Senator BILL BENTON ; I'm for him
title: Senator BILL BENTON I'm for him
subject: Politics, Campaign, Elections
description: Red, white, and blue campaign button supporting William 'Bill' Benton for U.S. Senator from Connecticut.

name: Podell for Congress ; Feb. 20th
title: FEB. 20th PODELL for CONGRESS
subject: election, politics, campaign
description: This campaign button is from the February 20, 1968, special election in which Bertram L. Podell was elected to the U.S. House of Representatives from New York's 13th district, filling a vacancy left by Edna F. Kelly.

name: Ike ; Dick ; They're for you
title: IKE THEY'RE FOR YOU DICK
person: Dwight D. Eisenhower and Richard Nixon
subject: Presidential Election, Republican Party, Eisenhower and Nixon
description: A red, white, and blue campaign button supporting the Republican ticket of Dwight D. Eisenhower ('Ike') and Richard Nixon ('Dick') for the United States presidential elections in the 1950s.

name: Ike ; Dick ; They're for you
title: I LIKE IKE
subject: Dwight D. Eisenhower, Presidential Elections, Campaign Buttons
description: The reverse side of an 'I Like Ike' campaign button from Dwight D. Eisenhower's 1952 presidential bid, featuring the Green Duck Company's manufacturer mark and the Allied Printing Trades Council union label from Chicago.

name: Grabowski ; U.S. Congress
title: GRABOWSKI U.S. CONGRESS
subject: election, candidate, portrait
description: A red, white, and blue campaign button for Bernard F. Grabowski for U.S. Congress, featuring a centered photographic portrait of the candidate.

name: Gerosa ; Wagner ; Stark
title: GEROSA WAGNER STARK
person: Robert F. Wagner Jr., Lawrence E. Gerosa, Abe Stark
office: Mayor, Comptroller, and City Council President
subject: Political campaign, New York City, 1953 election
description: A red, white, and blue political button for the 1953 New York City municipal election campaign of Robert F. Wagner Jr. for Mayor, Lawrence E. Gerosa for Comptroller, and Abe Stark for President of the City Council.

name: Re-elect Bertini ; Mayor
title: RE-ELECT BERTINI MAYOR
subject: Political Campaign; Mayors; Local Elections
description: A campaign button for a candidate named Bertini running for re-election as mayor. The button features a standard red, white, and blue patriotic design with horizontal bands of color and block lettering.

name: I'm for Lodge
title: I'M FOR LODGE
subject: Republican, Presidential campaign, Henry Cabot Lodge Jr.
description: Red, white, and blue campaign button with the text 'I'M FOR LODGE' and a white star, supporting Henry Cabot Lodge Jr.'s 1964 presidential campaign.

name: Sarasin ; Congress
title: Sarasin CONGRESS
subject: Political Campaign, Republican Party, US House
description: A white button with the text 'Sarasin CONGRESS' in blue and red, featuring a stylized red and blue flag motif above the name. It promotes the congressional campaign of Ronald A. Sarasin from Connecticut.

name: Lehman
title: LEHMAN
subject: Politics, Election, New York
description: A red, white, and blue political campaign button for Herbert H. Lehman, who served as the Governor of New York and a U.S. Senator. The button features his last name in blue block letters with a blue star above and below.

name: The rock of integrity
title: THE ROCK OF INTEGRITY
subject: Nelson Rockefeller, political campaign, integrity
description: A campaign button for Nelson Rockefeller's 1970 re-election campaign for Governor of New York, featuring a portrait of Rockefeller and the slogan 'The Rock of Integrity.'

name: Gregg for Governor
title: FOR GREGG GOVERNOR
subject: Judd Gregg, Governor, New Hampshire
description: A campaign button for Judd Gregg's gubernatorial campaign in New Hampshire.

name: Junior Citizen ; Community ; Chautauquas
title: COMMUNITY JUNIOR CITIZEN CHAUTAUQUAS
subject: Chautauqua movement, education, youth programs
description: A blue and white oval-shaped pin for the Community Chautauquas, identifying the wearer as a 'Junior Citizen'. This refers to the youth programs that were part of the circuit Chautauqua movement, which brought adult education and entertainment to rural communities in the early 20th century.

name: Passenger Service Employee Association ; CWA (Communications Workers of America Union) ; Our Voice
title: CWA Passenger Service Employee Association OUR VOICE
subject: Labor union, Airlines, CWA
description: Wing-shaped pin with a red central circle featuring 'CWA' and 'Passenger Service Employee Association', with 'OUR VOICE' at the bottom.

name: [Donkey with Two Horseshoes]
title: W
subject: Woodrow Wilson, Democratic Party, Presidential Election
description: A gold-toned political pin featuring a donkey head, the symbol of the Democratic Party, set atop two horseshoes arranged to form the letter 'W' for presidential candidate Woodrow Wilson.

name: Vermont AFL-CIO 50th Anniversary (American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations)
title: VERMONT AFL-CIO 50th ANNIVERSARY
subject: Labor union, Anniversary, AFL-CIO
description: A green and gold lapel pin shaped like the state of Vermont, commemorating the 50th anniversary of the Vermont State Labor Council AFL-CIO.

name: Newfoundland Association of Public Employees ; Altoria et Melora
title: NEWFOUNDLAND ASSOCIATION OF PUBLIC EMPLOYEES ALTIORA ET MELIORA
subject: Labor union, Newfoundland, Public employees
description: A green and gold lapel pin for the Newfoundland Association of Public Employees (NAPE). The center features a green maple leaf containing the outline of Newfoundland and Labrador. The words 'NEWFOUNDLAND ASSOCIATION OF PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' wrap around the top, and 'ALTIORA ET MELIORA' (higher and better) is on a banner at the bottom.

name: Local 500 SEIU (Service Employees International Union)
title: LOCAL 509 SEIU
subject: Labor Unions, Workers, Solidarity
description: A lapel pin for the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) Local 509, featuring a red face with gold lettering and silhouettes of people standing together. SEIU Local 509 is a Massachusetts-based union representing human service workers and educators.

name: N.J. AFL-CIO Votes (American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations)
title: N.J. AFL-CIO VOTES
subject: Labor unions, Voting, Elections
description: Gold-colored lapel pin featuring the text 'N.J. AFL-CIO VOTES' with a red checkmark in the 'O' of 'VOTES'.

name: Vancouver Firefighters Union  Local 18
title: VANCOUVER FIREFIGHTERS UNION LOCAL 18
subject: Firefighters, IAFF, Labor Unions
description: An oval-shaped badge or button for the Vancouver Firefighters Union Local 18. It features the International Association of Fire Fighters (IAFF) logo in the center, which includes a firefighter helmet and crossed axes, along with the text 'AFL-CIO CLC' indicating affiliation with both the American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations and the Canadian Labour Congress.

name: 100 Years of Progress ; International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers ; Local 353 ; Toronto l Chartered 1903
title: 100 YEARS OF PROGRESS LOCAL 353 INTERNATIONAL BROTHERHOOD OF ELECTRICAL WORKERS TORONTO Chartered 1903
subject: Labor Unions, Electrical Workers, Anniversaries
description: An oval gold-toned button commemorating the 100th anniversary of the International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers (IBEW) Local 353 in Toronto, chartered in 1903.

name: Independent Canadian Transit Union ; ICTU ; Local 6
title: INDEPENDENT CANADIAN TRANSIT UNION LOCAL 6
subject: Labor Union, Transit, Canada
description: An oval-shaped pin for Local 6 of the Independent Canadian Transit Union (ICTU), featuring a central red maple leaf above the stylized ICTU logo, with the union name and local number around the edge.

name: AFL -  CIO (American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations)
title: AFL-CIO
subject: Labor unions, AFL-CIO, Merger
description: A gold-tone lapel pin for the AFL-CIO, featuring an eagle at the top and the organization's name in a red rectangle. Below is a shield with two hands shaking over a dark blue silhouette of North America.

name: IBPAT (International Union of Painters and Allied Trades) 100th
title: IBPAT 100TH
subject: Labor union, anniversary, Painters
description: A gold-colored lapel pin in the shape of an artist's palette and brushes, commemorating the 100th anniversary of the International Brotherhood of Painters and Allied Trades (IBPAT) in 1987.

name: SEIU (Service Employees International Union) Union Steward
title: UNION STEWARD
subject: Labor Unions, AFL-CIO, SEIU
description: A shield-shaped lapel pin for a union steward of the Service Employees International Union (SEIU). The central logo features a handshake over a globe, surrounded by the acronyms for the AFL-CIO, SEIU, and CLC (Canadian Labour Congress).

name: Solidarno?? (Solitary)
title: SOLIDARNOŚĆ
subject: Labor Unions, Anti-communism, Polish History
description: Rectangular lapel pin featuring the red stylized logo of the Polish trade union Solidarność (Solidarity) on a cream-colored background. The logo features a red flag rising from the 'n'.

name: SEIU (Service Employees International Union) 75
title: SEIU 75
subject: Labor Unions, SEIU, Anniversary
description: A purple, red, and orange enamel lapel pin for the 75th anniversary of the Service Employees International Union (SEIU). The SEIU was founded in 1921.

name: SEIU (Service Employees International Union) Leading the Way
title: SEIU Leading the Way
subject: Labor unions, SEIU, Workers' rights
description: A square purple pin featuring the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) logo and the slogan "Leading the Way" in white and gold lettering.

name: SEIU (Service Employees International Union) for Kerry
title: SEIU for Kerry
subject: Labor Unions, Presidential Elections, John Kerry
description: Lapel pin with white and purple enamel and gold-colored trim featuring the text SEIU for Kerry, representing the Service Employees International Union's support for John Kerry's 2004 presidential campaign.

name: CGTP Intersindical Nacional (Confederação Geral dos Trabalhadores Portugueses/General Confederation of Portuguese Workers)
title: CGTP INTERSINDICAL NACIONAL
subject: Trade Unions, Portugal, Labor
description: A pin for the CGTP-IN (Confederação Geral dos Trabalhadores Portugueses — Intersindical Nacional), a Portuguese trade union federation. The graphic includes a wrench, wheat, and a pen nib representing the unity of various sectors of labor.

name: Service Employees International Union ; AFL-CIO ; 1 ; '93
title: SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION AFL-CIO 1 '93
subject: Labor Unions, AFL-CIO, SEIU
description: A 1993 lapel pin for the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) AFL-CIO, featuring a central globe with the number 1 and a background of city buildings.

name: Union Strong
title: UNION STRONG
subject: Labor Unions, Workers Rights, Solidarity
description: A gold-toned metal lapel pin featuring an eagle with spread wings and a white head. At the top of the pin, a rectangular section contains the words UNION STRONG in black capital letters. The eagle's wings have alternating red and black stripes, and its chest features a blue shield-like shape with a gold star in the center.

name: [Number five with a Cuban flag] (Free the Cuban Five)
subject: Cuban Five, Political Prisoners, Cuba
description: A lapel pin in the shape of a blue number 5, topped with a yellow star and the red triangle and blue and white stripes of the Cuban flag. This logo was used in the international campaign to release the Cuban Five, five Cuban intelligence officers arrested in the United States in 1998.

name: SID
title: SID
subject: Eugene McCarthy, Students, 1968 Election
description: A square enamel pin featuring the letters "SID" inside a circle, representing "Students in Detroit" or "Students for Eugene McCarthy" during his 1968 presidential campaign.

name: Carpenters for Clinton
title: CARPENTERS for CLINTON
subject: Labor unions, Presidential elections, Bill Clinton
description: A rectangular yellow campaign button with black and orange text reading 'CARPENTERS for CLINTON', indicating support from the Carpenters union for Bill Clinton's presidential campaign.

name: CUT (Central Única dos Trabalhadores/ Unified Workers' Central)
title: CUT.
subject: Labor Unions, Brazil, Worker's Rights
description: A rectangular red enamel pin with the white and gold letters "CUT." representing the Central Única dos Trabalhadores (Unified Workers' Central), a major Brazilian trade union federation founded in 1983.

name: SEIU Cope (Service Employees International Union Committee on Political Education )
title: SEIU 1994 COPE
subject: Labor union, political action, SEIU
description: A blue and gold square pin for the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) Committee on Political Education (COPE) for the year 1994.

name: ODT Organisation Démocratique du Travail (Democratic Confederation of Labour)
title: Odt Organisation Démocratique de Travail
subject: Morocco, Trade Union, Labor
description: A square metal pin for the Organisation Démocratique du Travail (ODT), a Moroccan trade union federation founded in 2006. The pin displays the organization's initials and logo—a handshake and bar graph—along with its name in both Arabic and French.

name: Industri All
title: industriall
subject: Labor Unions, Manufacturing, Workers' Rights
description: A square button for IndustriALL Global Union, featuring the organization's logo with overlapping profiles of people in gold and red against a black background. IndustriALL represents 50 million workers in mining, energy, and manufacturing sectors worldwide.

name: ACTU (Australian Council of Trade Unions)
title: ACTU
subject: Trade Unions, Labor Rights, Australia
description: A green and gold button featuring the logo of the Australian Council of Trade Unions (ACTU), which includes a stylized kangaroo outline.

name: SIPTU Ireland (Services, Industrial, Professional and Technical Union)
title: SIPTU IRELAND
subject: Trade Union, Labor, Ireland
description: A gold-colored stick pin for the Services, Industrial, Professional and Technical Union (SIPTU) in Ireland. The pin features the acronym SIPTU in gold lettering on a green background, with the word IRELAND embossed below.

name: SIPTU Skill (Services, Industrial, Professional and Technical Union)
title: SIPTU SKILL
subject: Trade Unions, Healthcare Workers, Training
description: A green and gold rectangular lapel pin featuring the logo of SIPTU (Services, Industrial, Professional and Technical Union) and its SKILL program, which provides education and training for health service support staff in Ireland.

name: SIPTU Local Authorities (Services, Industrial, Professional and Technical Union)
title: SIPTU LOCAL AUTHORITIES
subject: Labor Union, Local Government, Ireland
description: A rectangular metal pin badge for the Irish trade union SIPTU (Services Industrial Professional Technical Union), specifically for members working in local authorities. It features the text 'SIPTU' and 'LOCAL AUTHORITIES' in red, and an embossed hand logo with a spiral palm.

name: IWI[Shaped like a wrench]
title: IWI
subject: Labor Unions, Ironworkers, Spud Wrench
description: Gold-toned tie tack or lapel pin in the shape of a spud wrench, a tool used by ironworkers, with the initials "IWI" for Iron Workers International.

name: Honor Work
title: Honor WORK
subject: Labor, Employment, Ethics
