description: A vintage metallic pin featuring the slogan 'Honor WORK'. The word 'Honor' is rendered in a red script font, while the word 'WORK' is in a bold, red sans-serif block font. The pin has an irregular, rounded rectangular shape.

name: Respect Workers
title: Respect WORKERS
subject: Labor, Unions, Worker Rights
description: An irregularly shaped silver and purple pin that says "Respect WORKERS". The word "Respect" is in a purple cursive font and the word "WORKERS" is in a bolded purple font.

name: SIPTU Representative (Services, Industrial, Professional and Technical Union)
title: SIPTU REPRESENTATIVE
subject: Labor unions, Worker representation, Ireland
description: A gold-colored rectangular pin for a representative of the Services, Industrial, Professional and Technical Union (SIPTU), which is the largest trade union in Ireland. The pin features the acronym 'SIPTU' at the top and 'REPRESENTATIVE' running vertically down a teal-colored bar on the left. The center-bottom displays a stylized hand logo with a spiral palm.

name: Kinleith Branch ; PPWU(Pulp & Paper Workers Union)
title: KINLEITH PPWU BRANCH
subject: Labor unions, Pulp and paper industry, New Zealand
description: A square enamel lapel pin for the Kinleith branch of the New Zealand Pulp and Paper Workers' Union (PPWU). The pin displays the union name and branch with illustrations of wood logs and a paper bale.

name: C.S.T. ; FSLN (Frente Sandinista de Liberación Nacional/Sandinista National Liberation Front)
title: C.S.T. FSLN
subject: Sandinismo, Labor Unions, Revolution
description: Lapel pin for the Central Sandinista de Trabajadores (CST) and the Frente Sandinista de Liberación Nacional (FSLN). It features revolutionary symbols including a machete, hammer, rifle, and the silhouette of Augusto César Sandino's hat.

name: NUS (National Union of Students)
title: NUS
subject: Student Unions, Education, United Kingdom
description: An oval-shaped lapel pin for the National Union of Students (NUS) in the United Kingdom. The pin features the acronym 'NUS' in black, set against a central yellow vertical bar on a black background.

name: PT (Partido dos Trabalhadores/ Workers' Party)
title: PT
subject: Brazil, Workers' Party, Socialism
description: A stick pin featuring a red flag with a white star containing the letters PT, which is the logo of the Brazilian Workers' Party (Partido dos Trabalhadores).

name: FSU Australia (Finance Sector Union)
title: FSU AUSTRALIA
subject: Labor unions, Finance sector, Australia
description: An oval-shaped lapel pin for the Finance Sector Union (FSU) of Australia, featuring the organization's name in green over a black background with a green border.

name: [Image of Vladmir Lenin]
title: Profile of Vladimir Lenin
party: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
subject: Communism, Soviet Union, Vladimir Lenin
description: An octagonal lapel pin featuring a gold-toned profile portrait of Vladimir Lenin on a dark red background with a gold border.

name: [Image of Vladmir Lenin]
title: Portrait of Vladimir Lenin
subject: Lenin, Soviet Union, Communism
description: A rectangular metal pin depicting a profile of Vladimir Lenin in gold over a black background with gold horizontal stripes.

name: Che [Image of Che Guevara]
title: CHE
subject: Che Guevara, Marxism, Cuban Revolution
description: An enamel pin depicting the iconic stylized portrait of Argentine Marxist revolutionary Che Guevara. The image features Guevara's face in gold on a black background, wearing his signature beret with a small red star. The name "CHE" is written in large red letters across the bottom of the pin.

name: SOGAT (Society of Graphical and Allied Trades)
title: SOGAT
subject: Labor union
description: This pin features the acronym SOGAT for the Society of Graphical and Allied Trades, a major trade union in the United Kingdom printing industry.

name: SIPTU (Services Industrial Professional and Technical Union)
title: SIPTU
subject: Labor Union, Worker Rights, Ireland
description: A green rectangular pin with the gold letters SIPTU above a gold stylized hand logo. SIPTU (Services, Industrial, Professional and Technical Union) is Ireland's largest trade union, formed in 1990 through the merger of the Irish Transport and General Workers' Union and the Federated Workers' Union of Ireland.

name: From the Year of eace - to the Century of Peace ; 1986 - 2000
title: 1986 FROM THE YEAR OF PEACE- TO THE CENTURY OF PEACE! 2000
subject: Peace, Disarmament, International Year of Peace
description: A rectangular pin-back button supporting nuclear disarmament, created for the 1986 International Year of Peace. The design shows a timeline across four globes, with a large missile in 1986 shrinking by 1995 and being replaced by a dove in a gold circle by the year 2000.

name: ?????-????? ???? (Moscow- City of the World]
title: МОСКВА-ГОРОД МИРА
party: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
subject: Moscow, Peace, USSR
description: A metal pin with white, red, and silver enamel colors. It depicts the Spasskaya Tower in the center, a hammer and sickle symbol to the right, and a peace dove with an olive branch to the left. The text at the bottom says 'МОСКВА-ГОРОД МИРА' which translates to 'Moscow-City of Peace'.

name: XII MOCKBA 1985 (12th World Festival of Youth and Students - Moscow)
title: XII МОСКВА 1985
subject: World Festival of Youth and Students, Moscow, Peace
description: A rectangular gold-toned metal commemorative pin for the 12th World Festival of Youth and Students held in Moscow in 1985. The pin features the festival's official logo, a five-petaled flower with a peace dove at its center, alongside the Roman numeral XII and the city and year of the event.

name: [globe surrounded by festival flower/ 11th World Festival of Youth and Students]
title: World Association of Girl Guides and Girl Scouts
subject: Scouting, Girl Scouts, Internationalism
description: A lapel pin featuring the logo of the World Association of Girl Guides and Girl Scouts (WAGGGS). The design includes a central globe surrounded by five stylized petals in different colors (red, green, maroon, blue, and yellow), which represent the five geographic regions of the world association.

name: MOCKBA - 1985 ??? (Moscow 1985 World)
title: МОСКВА-1985 МИР
subject: World Festival of Youth and Students, Peace, Moscow 1985
description: A commemorative pin for the 12th World Festival of Youth and Students, held in Moscow, Soviet Union, in 1985. It features the Russian text for 'Moscow-1985' and 'MIR' (Peace), alongside the festival's signature five-petaled flower and dove logo.

name: FSG
title: FSG
subject: Sam Gibbons, Florida, US House
description: A square political button with the initials 'FSG' in gold on a red background. The initials likely refer to 'Friends of Sam Gibbons,' the campaign organization for Sam Gibbons, a Democratic politician who served in the U.S. House of Representatives from Florida.

name: Union Yes
title: UNION YES
subject: Labor unions, AFL-CIO, Labor movement
description: A rectangular pin featuring the slogan "UNION YES" with the AFL-CIO logo embedded in the letter "O". A checkmark is placed in a box next to the word "YES", symbolizing support for unionization. This design was part of a national advertising campaign launched by the AFL-CIO in 1988 to improve the public image of labor unions.

name: Organising for Fairness at Work and Justice in Society ; 1909 - 2009 ; SIPTU

The design features a red hand symbol in the center, the years 1909 and 2009, and the slogan 'Organising for fairness at work and justice in society'.

name: We are One ; Respect Our Rights
title: WE ARE ONE RESPECT OUR RIGHTS
subject: Labor, Unions, Civil Rights
description: This rectangular button features the slogan 'WE ARE ONE' in large block letters above the phrase 'RESPECT OUR RIGHTS'. It is representative of the 'We Are One' campaign launched in 2011 by labor unions and civil rights groups to advocate for collective bargaining and workers' rights, specifically in response to anti-union legislation in Wisconsin and other states.

name: SEIU COPE 1992 (Service Employees International Union Committee on Political Education)

description: A square, gold-colored pin with blue lettering that reads 'SEIU COPE 1992'. SEIU refers to the Service Employees International Union, and COPE stands for the Committee on Political Education.

subject: Trade Union, Labor Rights, Portugal
description: This is a lapel pin for the General Confederation of the Portuguese Workers (CGTP-IN), which is the largest trade union federation in Portugal. The federation adopted the 'Intersindical Nacional' suffix in 1977.

name: PT (Partido dos Trabalhadores/ Workers' Party)
title: PT
subject: Brazil, Politics, Workers' Party
description: A red plastic five-pointed star-shaped button with the white letters 'PT' in the center, representing the Brazilian Workers' Party (Partido dos Trabalhadores).

name: xxv ????? ???? (25th Congress of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union)
title: XXV СЪЕЗД КПСС
party: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
subject: Soviet Union, Communism, CPSU Congress
description: A commemorative pin for the 25th Congress of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union, featuring a red flag with a hammer and sickle and the text 'XXV СЪЕЗД КПСС'.

name: Skilled Traders ; UAW

title: U.M.W.A.
subject: Labor unions, Coal mining, UMWA
description: A lapel pin for the United Mine Workers of America (UMWA) featuring a miner's hard hat with a lamp over the union's initials.

name: Stronger Together ; SEIU 2004 Convention
title: STRONGER TOGETHER SEIU 2004 CONVENTION
subject: Labor union, SEIU, Convention
description: A metal pin issued for the 2004 Service Employees International Union (SEIU) Convention. The pin features silhouettes of workers beneath the phrase 'STRONGER TOGETHER' and above a purple bar that reads 'SEIU 2004 CONVENTION'.

name: Operative Plasterers' & Cement Mason's Int' Ass'n ; Local 924 ; Organized 1864
title: Operative Plasterers' & Cement Masons' Int'l Ass'n Local 924

name: Fritz/Gerry
title: Fritz / Gerry
subject: 1984 Presidential Election, Walter Mondale, Geraldine Ferraro
description: A gold-colored script pin with the names Fritz and Gerry, referring to the 1984 Democratic presidential ticket of Walter Mondale and Geraldine Ferraro.

name: PSAC ; AFPC ; Syndicate Agriculture Union
title: Syndicat Agriculture Union PSAC AFPC
subject: labor union, agriculture, Canada
description: A lapel pin for the Agriculture Union, a component of the Public Service Alliance of Canada (PSAC/AFPC). The pin features the union's name in both English and French, a maple leaf, silhouettes of faces, and the PSAC and AFPC acronyms within gear-like borders.

name: Lockout ; SIPTU ; I.T. 1913 W.U. ; 1913 - 2013

title: SPÖ 1. mai 2012

name: AFSCME (American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees)
title: AFSCME

title: LABOR COUNCIL FOR LATIN AMERICAN ADVANCEMENT YOUR CHANCE FOR CHANGE

name: Boston Carmen's Union ; Amalgamated Transit Union ; 589 ;  1912 - 1987
title: BOSTON CARMEN'S UNION AMALGAMATED TRANSIT UNION 1912-1987

The center emblem features a shield divided into four quadrants representing the four provinces of Ireland: Ulster, Munster, Leinster, and Connacht, topped with a torch of knowledge.

name: Maritime Machinists

The design is framed by a white outer border containing sixteen gold stars. The button most likely represents a labor union, such as the Aluminum Workers Union or the Amalgamated Workers Union, and served as a membership or promotional item.

title: FOR

description: A circular pin for For

title: UNITED STEELWORKERS OF AMERICA DUES PAID BY 1947 CHECK OFF C.I.O.

title: Don't Mourn~ Organize
subject: Labor unions, Social activism, Industrial Workers of the World

This slogan is a famous quote attributed to Joe Hill, a labor activist and songwriter for the Industrial Workers of the World (IWW), who allegedly sent it in a telegram to Bill Haywood prior to his execution in 1915. The button includes a small union bug at the bottom.

The acronyms FISEMARE and AKFM-KDRSM refer to the Federation of Revolutionary Trade Unions of Madagascar and its associated political party, the Congress Party for the Independence of Madagascar.

name: AFI-CIO CLC for Kerry

To the right of the logo, the words 'FOR KERRY' are printed in large, bold block letters. This button represents the IAFF's endorsement of John Kerry during the 2004 United States presidential election.

title: VETERANS FOR PEACE, INC. ABOLISH WAR

It features a stylized white dove carrying an olive branch against a black background shape resembling a soldier's helmet. The text 'VETERANS FOR PEACE, INC.' arcs across the top, and 'ABOLISH WAR' is printed at the bottom.

title: 2004 When BUSH comes to SHOVE!

name: Zero Nuclear Weapons ; Ban Nuclear Power
title: ZERO NUCLEAR WEAPONS BAN NUCLEAR POWER

title: STOP NUCLEAR WAR

name: Question Authority
title: Question Authority
subject: Anti-authoritarianism, Counterculture, Social Activism

title: NO U.S. WAR IN THE MIDDLE EAST NO BLOOD for OIL

The button displays three oil barrels, one marked with a skull and crossbones, alongside the slogans 'NO U.S. WAR IN THE MIDDLE EAST' and 'NO BLOOD for OIL'. It identifies the 'Campaign for Peace in the Middle East' along the bottom edge.

name: Apartheid ["No" symbol]
title: APARTHEID

description: A white button with the word 'APARTHEID' in black, crossed out by a red circle with a diagonal line through it, indicating a stance against the apartheid system.

person: Al Gore, Joe Lieberman, Hillary Clinton

name: Stevenson
title: STEVENSON 1960
subject: Presidential election, Adlai Stevenson II, Political campaign
description: Yellow campaign button with blue text "STEVENSON 1960" and a circle of blue stars, supporting Adlai Stevenson II for President in 1960.

The button features a graphic of four concentric colored arcs resembling a rainbow above the words "NATIONAL RAINBOW," where the letters in "RAINBOW" contain stylized human profiles. The word "COALITION" appears at the bottom in white letters on a purple rectangular background.

name: Question Authority
title: Question Authority

The design incorporates the London Underground's roundel logo and the activist slogan 'Can't Pay, Won't Pay' to protest the subsequent fare hikes after the policy was ruled illegal.

name: Re-Elect Assemblyman O.K.! ; Oliver Koppell

name: Boycott Non-Union Lettuce

title: 2 CONVENCION CONSTITUCIONAL FRESNO 1975 AÑO DE VICTORIA

subject: Richard Nixon, Lettuce boycott, United Farm Workers
description: A fluorescent green button with black text and the eagle logo of the United Farm Workers (UFW). The button was produced as a form of protest against President Richard Nixon, who publicly opposed the UFW's boycott of non-union lettuce in the early 1970s.

name: Re-elect Mike Shulman
title: RE-ELECT MIKE SHULMAN

description: A square button featuring artwork by Keith Haring for the 'City Kids Speak on Liberty' project in 1986, which celebrated the centennial of the Statue of Liberty.

title: my family votes

title: my neighbors vote

name: Moratorium
title: moratorium
subject: Vietnam War, Anti-war, Protest
description: A circular blue button with the lowercase word "moratorium" in white, signifying the Moratorium to End the War in Vietnam which took place in late 1969.

title: moratorium III
subject: Vietnam War, Anti-war movement, Protest
description: A round blue button with white text reading 'moratorium III'. This button likely refers to the third series of nationwide protests against the Vietnam War, part of the Moratorium to End the War in Vietnam, which took place in December 1969.

name: McKinley
title: MCKINLEY 1896

title: ATREVETE EN EL '89 COMMONWEALTH OF PUERTO RICO
subject: voter registration, Puerto Rican empowerment, political participation
description: This button was part of the 'Atrevete' (Dare) voter registration campaign, a non-partisan effort launched by the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico's Migration Division in the late 1980s to increase the political influence of Puerto Ricans living in the United States.

name: On Nov.7, 2017 Vote No! Constitutional Convention ; Ask Me Why
title: On Nov. 7, 2017 VOTE NO! Constitutional Convention ASK ME WHY UFT

name: March for Peace ; June 11 ; New York City
title: march for peace! JUNE 11 NEW YORK CITY SUPPORT THE UN THIRD SPECIAL SESSION ON DISARMAMENT!

name: Boycott Scab Lettuce ; Farmworkers
title: BOYCOTT FARMWORKERS SCAB LETTUCE

description: A circular red button with the black eagle logo of the United Farm Workers (UFW). The text reads "BOYCOTT" at the top, "FARMWORKERS" in the center within the eagle, and "SCAB LETTUCE" at the bottom. The button was used to promote a boycott of lettuce harvested by non-union labor during the Salinas lettuce strike.

name: Boycott Grapes
title: BOYCOTT GRAPES
subject: Labor movement, Boycott, Agriculture
description: This button was produced by the United Farm Workers (UFW) to promote the boycott of grapes during the Delano grape strike in California. The button features a black eagle, the symbol of the UFW, on a blue background.

name: Stop the War & Racism! ; SDS ; Jan 20 ; Washington, D.C.

description: A red and black protest button produced by the Students for a Democratic Society (SDS) for the counter-inaugural demonstrations in Washington, D.C. on January 20, 1969. The protest targeted the Vietnam War and domestic racial issues during the inauguration of Richard Nixon.

name: Vietnam Moratorium
title: vietnam moratorium
subject: Vietnam War, Anti-war protest, Peace

The associate member program was established in 1986 to organize workers in related fields and maintain ties with former members. The button features the slogan 'Unite & Fight!' on a red field at the bottom.

name: Enough [Vietam Protest Button]
title: ENOUGH
subject: Vietnam War, Anti-war, Presidential Campaign
description: This black button featuring the word 'ENOUGH' inside a white rectangle was designed by Pulitzer Prize-winning cartoonist Jules Feiffer for Eugene McCarthy's 1968 presidential campaign. It was used as a succinct protest against the Vietnam War and the policies of the Lyndon B. Johnson administration.

name: June 19 Solidarity Day

description: A pinback button from the June 19, 1968 Solidarity Day rally in Washington, D.C., which was the culmination of the Poor People's Campaign for economic justice.

name: Getting it Together [Various protest symbols]

subject: Peace, Solidarity, Resistance
description: A yellow button featuring a black four-leaf clover. Each leaf contains a different social or political symbol: a raised fist (Solidarity/Black Power), a peace sign, an infinity symbol (Unity), and an omega symbol with a silhouette (Draft Resistance). The text 'GETTING IT TOGETHER' appears at the bottom.

name: Sick of the War ; Vietnam Peace Parade Committee
title: SICK OF THE WAR FIFTH AVE. VIETNAM PEACE PARADE COMMITTEE
subject: Vietnam War, Peace movement, Anti-war protest

name: United Nations ; Outlaw Nuclear Weapons!

The text "UNITED NATIONS" arches across the top in black, while "OUTLAW NUCLEAR WEAPONS!" is printed across the middle and bottom in black capital letters. This button reflects international advocacy for the prohibition of nuclear weapons, likely associated with UN Special Sessions on Disarmament in the late 1970s or early 1980s.

name: End Racist Attacks in Boston! ; Young Socialist Alliance
title: End racist attacks in Boston!
subject: Racism, Socialism, Civil Rights
description: A pin-back button produced by the Young Socialist Alliance calling for an end to racial violence in Boston, likely in response to the protests and attacks surrounding court-ordered school busing in the mid-1970s.

name: Boycott Fur-Bearing People
title: BOYCOTT FUR-BEARING PEOPLE

description: An anti-fur button issued by Friends of Animals Inc. featuring a black and white image of a seal pup and the slogan 'Boycott Fur-Bearing People.'

name: Bella for Senate

description: A blue and white campaign button for Bella Abzug's 1976 U.S. Senate run in New York, featuring her signature silhouette with a wide-brimmed hat.

name: WSP (Women's Strike for Peace)

name: CUNY Divest from South Africa

In September 1984, CUNY became one of the first major American universities to vote for full divestment.

name: No Nukes Under the Sun
title: NO NUKES under the sun

The button advocates for redirecting military funding towards social services like education and employment while protesting the military draft.

Bush, who served only one term (1989-1993), advocating that the younger Bush should also be limited to a single term.

The button shows significant signs of age with brown foxing or rust spots across the surface.

name: Like a Rock Only Dumber [Image of George W. Bush]

name: George You're Fired! ; November 2, 2004 ; [Image of George W. Bush with îNoî symbol]

The circular button features a photo of Bush with a red 'no' sign overlaid, featuring the text 'YOU'RE FIRED!'—a reference to the then-popular catchphrase from 'The Apprentice'. The election date, November 2, 2004, is printed at the bottom.

name: G.W. Bush in 200
title: G.W.BUSH IN 2000

description: A blue and white campaign button for Jan B. Lehman, the Libertarian candidate for the U.S. House of Representatives from California's 22nd district during the 1980 and 1982 elections.

name: Elizabeth Dole 2000
title: Elizabeth DOLE 2000
subject: Presidential Campaign, Elizabeth Dole, Republican
description: A political campaign button for Elizabeth Dole's 2000 presidential bid. The button features a portrait of Dole on the left, with her name 'Elizabeth DOLE' and the year '2000' prominently displayed. Dole ran for the Republican nomination before withdrawing from the race in October 1999.

name: [Picture of George W. Bush wearing a Stetson
subject: Republican party, George W. Bush, Texas

description: A circular political campaign button for Mitt Romney's 2008 presidential bid. The button features a portrait of Romney on the left, with the text "Mitt Romney for President in" on a white background at the top, and "2008" in large white numerals on a blue background at the bottom. The button shows significant signs of rust and wear.

name: Steve Forbes 2000
title: Steve FORBES 2000
subject: Presidential Campaign, Republican Party, Steve Forbes
description: A campaign button for Steve Forbes' 2000 presidential run featuring a photo of Forbes and the text 'Steve FORBES 2000' with red and white stripes within the numbers.

description: A round yellow button featuring the slogan 'WOMEN: DON'T AGONIZE, ORGANIZE' in black text. This phrase was a popular call to action during the second-wave feminist movement and is attributed to activist Florynce 'Flo' Kennedy.

name: Wausau's Got It!

name: Romney
title: ROMNEY

name: Seth Moulton For America 2020

name: Coalition For Keep The Heat On! Basic Human Needs
title: COALITION FOR BASIC HUMAN NEEDS KEEP THE HEAT ON!
subject: welfare rights, energy assistance, social justice
description: This button was issued by the Massachusetts-based Coalition for Basic Human Needs (CBHN). The CBHN was a welfare rights organization led by low-income women that advocated for better social services and economic justice. The 'Keep the Heat On!' slogan likely refers to campaigns for fuel assistance and against utility shut-offs for poor families during the winter months.

name: Sheriff Waldron

description: A gold-colored, five-pointed star-shaped campaign pin for a candidate named Waldron running for the office of Sheriff. The text 'SHERIFF WALDRON' is printed in black, bold, sans-serif font across the center of the star.

name: Honest Don

The design features a yellow background with blue bars at the top and bottom, with the text 'GORE 2000' prominently in the center.

The oval-shaped button is split horizontally, with a yellow top half and blue bottom half. George McGovern was the Democratic nominee for President and Sargent Shriver was his running mate.

title: RO KHANNA FOR CONGRESS

It features portraits of both candidates flanking an image of the White House, with the text "BUSH QUAYLE" at the top and "1988" at the bottom against a red, white, and blue background.

title: BACH MAI
subject: Vietnam War, Humanitarian Relief, Peace

title: HUMPHREY

description: A yellow pinback button with bold black text that reads 'I AM A SHAMELESS AGITATOR'. This type of button was popular in the 1960s and 1970s as a way for individuals to express their commitment to social and political activism.

name: Kunin! Governor

title: FOOD FOR PEOPLE NOT FOR PROFIT

The text '1984 AQUÍ NO SE RINDE NADIE!' translates to 'No one surrenders here!', a slogan of resistance associated with the Nicaraguan Revolution and used by the Sandinistas during the 1984 general election.

name: Alternative Energy Now Greenpeace

name: Ike Nixon

The button is divided horizontally, with 'VOTE' in green on a white background on the top half, and 'CARTER' in white on a green background on the bottom half. This color scheme was commonly used in Jimmy Carter's 1976 presidential campaign.

description: A rectangular tag with red, white, and blue horizontal stripes. The middle white stripe features the text "WAR CHEST" in blue. This tag was likely used to acknowledge a contribution to a local War Chest fund, which supported war-related charities and social services during World War II.

title: For President Wm. H. Taft / For Vice President James S. Sherman

title: HARDING AND COOLIDGE
person: Warren G. Harding and Calvin Coolidge

subject: Patriotism, World War II, Home Front
description: A patriotic button from 1942 featuring a red and white striped shield with a blue star, likely produced during World War II to show support for the United States.

name: Hillary Scholten For Congress
title: HILLARY SCHOLTEN FOR CONGRESS

description: Round white button with blue and red text featuring 'ALEK' in blue with a green evergreen tree as the 'E'. Below it, a blue bar says 'FOR CONGRESS' flanked by '20' and '22' in red boxes, representing his 2022 campaign for Congress in Oregon's 4th district.

name: Donald J. Trump Supports Leon For Our Next Congressman
title: DONALD J. TRUMP SUPPORTS LEON FOR OUR NEXT CONGRESSMAN
subject: Donald Trump, US House of Representatives, Election

title: STANDING UP FOR TEXAS FOR CONGRESS JIMMY LEON (R) TEXAS DIST 9 20 22
subject: Congressional Election, Texas Politics, Republican Party

name: Danielle Hawk For US Congress Florida District 1 Integrity Job Creation Affordable Health Care danniellehawk.com
title: Integrity Job Creation Affordable Health Care DANIELLE HAWK FOR US CONGRESS FLORIDA DISTRICT 3 daniellehawk.com

title: MELTON FOR IOWA
subject: LGBTQ, Election, Democratic Party
description: A campaign button for Brent Melton's 2018 run for the Iowa House of Representatives. The rainbow-colored text 'MELTON' highlights the candidate's identity as an openly gay man and his commitment to LGBTQ+ rights.

name: Melton For Iowa
title: MELTON FOR IOWA
subject: Election, Campaign, Iowa
description: A black campaign button for Brent Melton's 2018 run for the Iowa State Senate, featuring the text 'MELTON FOR IOWA' in white and a graphic of a waving American flag ribbon.

name: Melton For Iowa
title: MELTON FOR IOWA
subject: Campaign, Election, Governor
description: A circular campaign button for John Melton's 2022 gubernatorial bid in Iowa. The button features the text 'MELTON FOR IOWA' in blue and a waving American flag icon on a white background.

name: Boycott Grapes
title: BOYCOTT GRAPES
subject: labor rights, United Farm Workers, boycotts
description: A button advocating for the boycott of grapes in support of the United Farm Workers (UFW) labor movement, led by Cesar Chavez. It features the UFW's black Aztec eagle logo.

name: President Helms '84 Right From The Start
title: PRESIDENT HELMS '84 RIGHT FROM THE START
subject: Jesse Helms, Presidential campaign, 1984 election
description: A red and white political campaign button for a potential 1984 presidential run by North Carolina Senator Jesse Helms, featuring a silhouette of the United States and the slogan 'Right From the Start'.

name: Heretic
title: Heretic
subject: Feminism, Religion, Dissent
description: A pink pinback button with the word "Heretic" centered in a black floral wreath. Such buttons were common during the second-wave feminist movement as a symbol of defiance against patriarchal religious and social institutions.

name: I am a Shameless Agitator
title: I am a shameless agitator
subject: Social activism, Feminism, Protest
description: A round button featuring a blue to purple gradient background with black text stating "I am a shameless agitator." This phrase is a shortened version of a quote by labor and community organizer Mary Harris "Mother" Jones and was popular among social activists and feminists in the mid-20th century.

name: United Sisterhood of Carpenters & Joiners of America
title: United Sisterhood of Carpenters & Joiners of America
subject: Carpentry, Women in Trades, Labor Unions
description: A pin-back button featuring the text 'United Sisterhood of Carpenters & Joiners of America' on a striped blue and white background. The text is a play on the official name of the United Brotherhood of Carpenters and Joiners of America (UBC) and likely represents the 'Sisters in the Brotherhood' (SIB) initiative which supports women in the carpentry trade.

name: Marjorie O'Neil Clapprood '90 Lieutenant Governor
title: Marjorie O'Neill Clapprood '90 Lieutenant Governor
subject: political campaign, state elections, candidate
description: A green and white campaign button for Marjorie O'Neill Clapprood's 1990 run for Lieutenant Governor of Massachusetts.

name: You gotta have Art
title: you gotta have ART
subject: Art, Culture, Museum
description: A circular white button featuring the phrase 'you gotta have ART' in black stylized lettering. This slogan was the centerpiece of a successful 1977 marketing campaign for the Detroit Institute of Arts.

name: Steward ; American Federation of State, County & Municipal Employees ; AFL-CIO
title: American Federation of State, County & Municipal Employees Steward AFL-CIO
subject: Labor Unions, AFSCME, Steward
description: A round yellow button with green text and graphics for the American Federation of State, County & Municipal Employees (AFSCME). It features a large triangle with the word 'Steward' across the bottom and the union's logo in the center.

name: George Bush for President
title: George Bush for President.
subject: Presidential Campaign, Republican Party, George H.W. Bush
description: Circular political button for George H.W. Bush's presidential campaign. The button features three horizontal bands: red at the top, white in the center, and blue at the bottom. The text "George Bush for President." is printed in black italicized font within the white band, enclosed by two thin black horizontal lines.

name: War [Prohibition symbol]
title: war
subject: anti-war, peace, protest
description: A white pin-back button with the word 'war' in black lowercase letters. A red circle with a diagonal slash is superimposed over the word, representing an anti-war sentiment.

title: UNITED STEELWORKERS OF AMERICA VOTE USWA AFL-CIO CLC

description: A circular button from the United Steelworkers of America (USWA) urging members to vote, featuring the union's name, its establishment date of May 22, 1942, and its affiliation with the AFL-CIO and the CLC.

name: Lieberman US Senate
title: LIEBERMAN US SENATE
subject: Election, US Senate, Connecticut
description: A blue campaign button for Joe Lieberman featuring the text 'LIEBERMAN US SENATE' in white with six red stars arranged in a row.

name: For Hart From The Re-Start
title: FOR HART FROM THE RE-START
subject: Presidential election, Gary Hart, 1988
description: A green and white campaign button for Gary Hart's 1988 presidential campaign. The slogan "FROM THE RE-START" refers to Hart's re-entry into the Democratic primary race in December 1987 after having previously withdrawn.

name: Festival Of Peoples Festival Etnico ; June 9 Hennigan School
title: Festival of Peoples June 9 Hennigan School Festival Etnico
subject: Multiculturalism, School events, Community
description: A round yellow button with red text advertising a "Festival of Peoples" and "Festival Etnico" held on June 9 at the Hennigan School in Boston.

name: Use Me [Image of a hand removing a condom from a packet]
title: USE ONE
subject: Safe sex, HIV/AIDS awareness, Public Health
description: Yellow button featuring the text "USE ONE" and a graphic of hands holding a condom package and a condom, promoting safe sex to prevent the spread of HIV/AIDS.

name: 1-800-Leahy '86
title: 1-800-LEAHY '86

title: A Woman's Place is in her Union

description: This blue and white button was produced for the United Steelworkers of America (USWA) to promote women's involvement in the labor union. It features the USWA logo and the slogan 'A Woman's Place is in her Union,' which was a popular feminist slogan in the 1970s and 80s adapted from 'A woman's place is in the home.'

name: National Steering Committee Meeting February 5-7 1993 ; The 1993 March on Washington for Lesbian, Gay and Bi Equal Rights and Liberation April 25
title: National Steering Committee Meeting February 5-7, 1993 THE 1993 MARCH ON WASHINGTON FOR LESBIAN, GAY AND BI EQUAL RIGHTS AND LIBERATION APRIL 25
subject: LGBTQ Rights, Activism, Protests
description: A white button with blue and pink text and graphics. It commemorates a National Steering Committee Meeting held in February 1993 to plan the March on Washington for Lesbian, Gay and Bi Equal Rights and Liberation, which took place on April 25, 1993. The central graphic is a pink inverted triangle containing an illustration of the U.S. Capitol building.

name: Jamaica Plain no está a la venta ; Vida Urbana
title: Jamaica Plain no está a la venta VIDA URBANA
subject: Housing, Gentrification, Community Organizing
description: A button from the Boston-based community organization City Life/Vida Urbana, advocating against gentrification in the Jamaica Plain neighborhood. The Spanish text translates to 'Jamaica Plain is not for sale'.

name: Vote Nader & LaDuke
title: vote Nader & LaDuke www.VoteNader.org
subject: Election, Green Party, Third Party
description: A circular green campaign button for the 2000 presidential run of Ralph Nader and Winona LaDuke on the Green Party ticket.

name: These Are Our Schools
title: THESE ARE OUR SCHOOLS
subject: Education, Civil Rights, Community Control
description: A white pinback button featuring the slogan "THESE ARE OUR SCHOOLS" in bold black sans-serif text. This phrasing was widely used during the late 1960s movement for community control of public schools, most notably during the Ocean Hill-Brownsville crisis in New York City.

name: Jamaica Plain Green-Lite Luz Verde
title: JAMAICA PLAIN GREEN-LITE LUZ VERDE
subject: women's safety, community organizing, neighborhood watch
description: This button represents the Green Light (Luz Verde) program in the Jamaica Plain neighborhood of Boston. The program encouraged residents to place green lights in their windows to indicate their home was a safe haven for anyone feeling threatened or in need of help, with a particular focus on preventing violence against women.

name: The Voters' Mandate [3 inside a prohibition symbol]
title: THE VOTERS' MANDATE
subject: Taxation, Ballot measures, Property taxes
description: A white button with black text that reads 'THE VOTERS' MANDATE'. The letter 'O' in 'VOTERS' is replaced by a red 'no' symbol (a circle with a diagonal slash) over the number '3'. This button was created to oppose Oregon's 1982 Ballot Measure 3, a property tax limitation initiative promoted by its supporters as 'The Voters' Mandate'.

name: Vote Janet Royston For a Change
title: vote JANET ROYSTON For a Change
subject: Election; Politics; State Government
description: A white pin-back button featuring purple text that says 'vote JANET ROYSTON For a Change' with a union bug at the bottom.

name: Save Your Ugly Face! Buckle Up
title: Save Your Ugly Face! BUCKLE UP.

description: A pink circular button featuring the humorous public service message 'Save Your Ugly Face! BUCKLE UP.' in black text.

name: Canned Food Drive we CAN do it "I Did" Contract '91
title: CANNED FOOD DRIVE we CAN do it "I DID" CONTRACT '91

description: A yellow button with orange text promoting a canned food drive in 1991 with the slogans "we CAN do it" and "I DID".

subject: LGBTQ+, Spirituality, Religion
description: This button features the words 'GAY SPIRIT' where a triangle symbol replaces the 'Y', and 'mcc/boston' at the bottom. It represents the Metropolitan Community Church (MCC) of Boston, an LGBTQ+-affirming church founded in 1972.

name: Mass PIRG
title: MASS PIRG
subject: consumer advocacy, environmentalism, student activism
description: A green circular button featuring the white text 'MASS PIRG', which stands for the Massachusetts Public Interest Research Group, a non-profit advocacy organization founded in 1972.

name: Don't Be An [Image of a Donkey] Vote For Dewey
title: DON'T BE AN [donkey symbol] VOTE FOR DEWEY

description: This political campaign button for Thomas E. Dewey features a donkey, the symbol of the Democratic Party, to create a punning message that discourages voting for the Democratic ticket.

name: BU Be United Twice as Strong ; District 65/UAW
title: BE UNITED TWICE AS STRONG DISTRICT 65/UAW.
subject: Labor unions, UAW, District 65
description: A promotional button for District 65 of the United Auto Workers (UAW) union featuring the slogan "BE UNITED TWICE AS STRONG". District 65, which represented a wide range of workers in retail, distribution, and offices, affiliated with the UAW in 1979 and was a prominent labor organization in New York City until the early 1990s.

name: Win With Rockefelller For President
title: WIN WITH ROCKEFELLER FOR PRESIDENT
subject: Nelson Rockefeller, Presidential campaign, Republican Party
description: A red and blue political campaign button supporting Nelson Rockefeller's bid for the presidency, most likely from his 1968 campaign.

name: New Hampshire's Favorite ; Dukakis 1988
title: New Hampshire's Favorite DUKAKIS 1988
subject: 1988 Presidential Election, New Hampshire Primary, Michael Dukakis
description: White circular campaign button for Michael Dukakis's 1988 presidential bid. It features a black and white photograph of Dukakis, the text 'New Hampshire's Favorite' in an arc at the top, 'DUKAKIS' in large blue letters, and '1988' to the right. A blue outline of the state of New Hampshire is positioned above the name, flanked by blue stars.

name: SEIU Yes
title: SEIU Yes
subject: Labor Unions, Organizing, SEIU
description: A white button featuring the acronym 'SEIU' (Service Employees International Union) in black block lettering above a stylized red 'Yes' with a checkmark in a box, likely used during a union representation election or organizing drive.

name: Hospital Workers = Quality Care
title: HOSPITAL WORKERS = QUALITY CARE
subject: Labor Unions, Healthcare, Workers' rights
description: A circular yellow button with dark green text that reads 'HOSPITAL WORKERS = QUALITY CARE'. This slogan has been a long-standing rally cry for healthcare labor unions, such as SEIU and District 1199, to highlight the essential role of staff in providing high-quality patient care.

name: MIA Missing or Prisoner
title: MIA MISSING OR PRISONER
subject: Vietnam War, POW/MIA, Activism
description: A black and white button from the Vietnam War era featuring a profile of a head with a question mark inside, with the text 'MIA' and 'MISSING OR PRISONER'. It refers to the issue of military personnel missing in action or being held as prisoners of war.

name: People Before Profits ; Polly Halfkenny City Council
title: People Before Profits Polly Halfkenny City Council
subject: Local politics, Socialism, City Council
description: A blue and yellow campaign button for Polly Halfkenny's run for Boston City Council. Halfkenny was a candidate for the Socialist Workers Party who ran on a platform of "People Before Profits."

name: Local 26 Justicia Justice Justica
title: LOCAL 26 JUSTICIA JUSTICE JUSTIÇA
subject: Labor Unions, Immigrant Rights, Workers' Rights
description: A yellow button from UNITE HERE Local 26 (formerly HERE Local 26) in Boston, Massachusetts. The button features the word "justice" in Spanish, English, and Portuguese, reflecting the diverse demographics of the hotel and food service workers the union represents.

name: We stand with Cindy
title: We stand with Cindy
subject: Anti-war protest, Iraq War, Cindy Sheehan
description: A green circular button with white text expressing solidarity with anti-war activist Cindy Sheehan. Sheehan gained national attention in August 2005 for her extended protest outside President George W. Bush's ranch in Crawford, Texas, following the death of her son in the Iraq War.

name: U.S. Troops Out Of The Middle East
title: U.S. TROOPS OUT OF THE MIDDLE EAST
subject: anti-war, Middle East, Gulf War
description: A white circular pin-back button with black block text that reads 'U.S. TROOPS OUT OF THE MIDDLE EAST'. This button is representative of the anti-war movement during the Persian Gulf War era in the early 1990s.

name: For Youth's Right To Earn Learn & Live ; YWLL
title: FOR YOUTH'S RIGHT TO EARN, LEARN, & LIVE YWLL
subject: Youth rights, labor movements, socialism
description: This button was produced for the Young Workers Liberation League (YWLL), which was the youth wing of the Communist Party USA from 1970 to 1982. The design features four raised fists of various colors, representing racial diversity and solidarity, and the organization's slogan focusing on economic and educational rights for youth.

name: Záppenin'
title: ZAPPENIN
subject: Presidential Campaign, Frank Zappa, Musician
description: A campaign button from musician Frank Zappa's potential 1992 run for President of the United States, featuring his stylized 'Zappenin' slogan.

name: Coalition For Basic Human Needs
title: COALITION FOR BASIC HUMAN NEEDS
subject: welfare rights, poverty, social justice
description: A round blue button with white text for the Coalition for Basic Human Needs, a grassroots welfare rights organization based in Massachusetts.

name: Faith & Family MMOW [Prohibition sign] ; Hell no! I Won't go!
title: Faith & Family MMOW Hell no! I won't go!
subject: Gun control, Million Mom March, Faith and Family
description: A round, tan-colored button with a red prohibition sign over the black letters "MMOW", standing for the Million Mom March on Washington. The words "Faith &" are above "MMOW" and "Family" is below. Inside the diagonal red slash of the prohibition sign, the slogan "Hell no! I won't go!" is written in white. This button was created to express opposition to the Million Mom March, a rally for stricter gun control laws held on May 14, 2000.

name: Saints
title: SAINTS
subject: Sports, Fan, Spirit
description: A circular pink button featuring the word 'SAINTS' in bold black capital letters.

name: Clerical Technical Union District 65 Boston University
title: CLERICAL - TECHNICAL UNION DISTRICT 65 BOSTON UNIVERSITY
subject: Labor Unions, Higher Education, Clerical Workers
description: A button for the Clerical and Technical Union, District 65, at Boston University. District 65 was a distributive workers union that organized university staff and later merged with the United Auto Workers (UAW).

name: Attica Is All of Us
title: ATTICA IS ALL OF US
subject: prison reform, civil rights, Attica riot
description: A circular button with the text 'ATTICA IS ALL OF US' printed in yellow. The slogan was used by activists in the wake of the 1971 Attica Prison riot to show solidarity with inmates and advocate for improved conditions and civil rights within the penal system.

name: Hospital Workers Local 767 ; We Strike From Within
title: HOSPITAL WORKERS WE STRIKE FROM WITHIN LOCAL 767
subject: Labor, Strike, Healthcare
description: A labor union button from SEIU Local 767, representing healthcare workers in Massachusetts, featuring the slogan 'We Strike From Within'. Local 767 was a chapter of the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) based in Cape Cod.

name: Vote No on #2
title: VOTE NO ON #2
subject: Ballot measure, Voting, Referendum
description: A white political button with blue text that reads 'VOTE NO ON #2'. The text is arranged vertically, with 'VOTE' at the top, 'NO' in large letters, 'ON' between two horizontal lines, and '#2' at the bottom.

name: Experience Counts Elect Gerald R. Ford in '76
title: EXPERIENCE COUNTS Elect Gerald R. FORD in '76
subject: Gerald Ford, 1976 Election, Presidential Campaign
description: A red, white, and blue political campaign button for Gerald R. Ford's 1976 presidential run featuring his portrait and the slogans 'EXPERIENCE COUNTS' and 'Elect Gerald R. FORD in '76'.

name: Welcome Home Nelson Mandela ; Forward To Freedom
title: Welcome Home Nelson Mandela FORWARD TO FREEDOM
subject: Anti-Apartheid Movement, South Africa, Human Rights
description: This button commemorates the release of Nelson Mandela from Victor Verster Prison on February 11, 1990, after 27 years of imprisonment. The phrase 'Forward to Freedom' was a widely used slogan by the African National Congress (ANC) and its supporters during the struggle against apartheid.

name: Henry Allen School Committee West Roxbury Jamaica Plain
title: HENRY Allen SCHOOL COMMITTEE WEST ROXBURY JAMAICA PLAIN
subject: Education, Boston, Local Elections
description: A campaign button for Henry Allen's candidacy for the Boston School Committee, specifically representing the West Roxbury and Jamaica Plain neighborhoods.

name: NO 3
title: NO 3
subject: Water Resources, Ballot Initiative, Proposition 3
description: A political campaign button opposing California Proposition 3, the Water Resources Initiative, in the November 1982 election. The button features a graphic 'NO' where the 'O' is a red prohibition symbol containing the number '3'. The Allied Printing Trades Council union label for San Francisco is visible at the bottom.

name: Count me for Carl.
title: Count me for Carl.
subject: Mayor, Election, African American
description: White button with blue text used in the mayoral campaign of Carl Stokes in Cleveland, Ohio. Stokes was the first African American mayor of a major United States city.

name: Spring Susan Saxe
title: spring susan saxe
subject: radicalism, activism, legal defense
description: Circular white button with blue text 'spring susan saxe' and a blue illustration of a witch flying on a broomstick. The word 'spring' is used here as a verb meaning to free from prison.
