title: no more HIBAKUSHA
subject: nuclear disarmament, peace movement, survivors
description: A circular metal pin featuring the embossed slogan 'no more HIBAKUSHA' in lowercase script and uppercase sans-serif. The term Hibakusha refers to survivors of the atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, and the slogan is a common plea for global nuclear disarmament.

name: No More Hibakusha
title: None
subject: Peace, Anti-nuclear, Japan
description: An oval-shaped pin featuring a white origami crane against a red background. The origami crane, or orizuru, is a traditional Japanese symbol that became an international symbol of peace and nuclear disarmament following the story of Sadako Sasaki, a girl who attempted to fold 1,000 cranes while dying from leukemia caused by the Hiroshima atomic bomb.

name: Stop the Bombing in El Salvador
title: STOP THE BOMBING IN EL SALVADOR
subject: Salvadoran Civil War, Peace activism, Anti-war movement
description: A circular button from the 1980s protesting the Salvadoran Civil War. The button depicts a stylized dove with an olive branch in its beak, a symbol of peace. The text 'STOP THE BOMBING IN EL SALVADOR' is printed around the dove on a green background. The button likely voices opposition to military actions and U.S. involvement during the conflict.

name: Jose Marti
title: JOSE MARTI
subject: Cuban independence, Jose Marti, National hero
description: A circular button featuring a green shield-like shape with a torch, a five-pointed star inside a triangle, and a banner with the name "JOSE MARTI".

name: 95th Anniversary ; United Association of Plumbers & Pipefitters ; 1901-1996 ; #276
title: 95th ANNIVERSARY UNITED ASSOCIATION OF PLUMBERS & PIPEFITTERS 1901 1996 # 276
subject: Labor Unions, Plumbers, Pipefitters
description: A circular maroon and gold pin commemorating the 95th anniversary of the United Association of Plumbers & Pipefitters Local 276, established in 1901. The pin includes trade-related icons and a shoe symbol, referencing Brockton's history as a center for shoe manufacturing.

name: [Shaped like a Dove 19th Annual World Conference Against Atomic and Hydrogen Bombs]
title: 1973.8 ナガサキ 第19回世界大会 北海道原水協
subject: Anti-nuclear, Peace, Nagasaki
description: A silver-colored dove-shaped pin commemorating the 19th World Conference against Atomic and Hydrogen Bombs held in Nagasaki in August 1973. The pin was issued by the Hokkaido Council Against Atomic and Hydrogen Bombs (Hokkaido Gensuikyo).

name: [Shaped like a Dove 19th Annual World Conference Against Atomic and Hydrogen Bombs]
title: Peace Dove Pin
subject: Peace, Dove, Anti-war
description: A silver-colored metal pin shaped like a dove, a common symbol of the peace movement during the mid-20th century.

name: [Odessa Coat of Arms]
subject: Odessa, Hero City, Soviet Union
description: A Soviet-era commemorative lapel pin for the city of Odessa. The top section depicts a stylized monument or industrial scene, a red flag, and the Gold Star medal awarded to Hero Cities of the Soviet Union. The bottom section features the city's coat of arms, a four-fluked anchor, on a red background.

name: 1921-1966 ; 45 ; FJC
title: 1921 1966 45º FJC
subject: Communist Youth, Argentina, Anniversary
description: A triangular commemorative pin marking the 45th anniversary of the Federación Juvenil Comunista (FJC) of Argentina, which was founded in 1921. The pin features a red star, the acronym FJC, and the years 1921-1966.

name: ????????????? ????? ; ????? (Patriotic War) (Luga)
title: ЛУГА
party: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
subject: Soviet Union, WWII, Commemoration
description: A Soviet-era lapel pin commemorating the city of Luga. It features the name of the city in Cyrillic characters on a red flag background, with an image of the Order of the Patriotic War on the left. Luga was awarded the Order of the Patriotic War, 1st Class, in 1984 for its role in the defense of Leningrad during World War II.

name: JIS (Juventud Socialista de Chile)
title: JIS
subject: Labor unions; Janitors; SEIU
description: A pin shaped like a waving Texas flag with a red circle in the center containing the letters 'JIS' and a stylized cross or broom symbol. This pin is associated with the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) 'Justice in Service' campaign, which was active in Texas, particularly during the Houston janitors' strike of 2006.

name: 1969 Trabajo Agricola
title: 1969 TRABAJO AGRICOLA
subject: Agriculture, Labor, Texas
description: A metal pin shaped like the state of Texas with the year '1969' and the Spanish phrase 'TRABAJO AGRICOLA' (Agricultural Work) embossed on it.

name: AFGE TSA (American Federation of Government Employees)
title: AFGE TSA
subject: Labor Union, Federal Government, TSA
description: This button features the abbreviations for the American Federation of Government Employees (AFGE) and the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) over a map of the United States. It likely relates to AFGE's long-term campaign to gain collective bargaining rights for TSA workers, which resulted in the union being certified as their exclusive representative in 2011.

name: Claridad ; 25 Anos de Lucha ; 1959-1984 (25 Years of Struggle/Fighting)
title: 25 AÑOS DE LUCHA Claridad 1959-1984
subject: Puerto Rican independence, journalism, Claridad
description: This button commemorates the 25th anniversary of Claridad, a pro-independence Puerto Rican newspaper founded in 1959. The text '25 AÑOS DE LUCHA' translates to '25 Years of Struggle,' reflecting the paper's role as the official publication of the Puerto Rican Socialist Party (PSP).

name: Federacio of Mujeres Cubanas
title: FEDERACION DE MUJERES CUBANAS
subject: Women, Cuba, Revolution
description: This button is a pin for the Federation of Cuban Women (Federación de Mujeres Cubanas). It depicts the organization's logo: a woman in a militia uniform with a rifle slung over her shoulder, holding a baby, set against a stylized globe.

name: 26 [ 26th of July Movement ]
subject: Cuban Revolution, M-26-7, Fidel Castro
description: Enamel pin shaped like a flag with horizontal red and black bands and the number '26' in the center, representing the 26th of July Movement led by Fidel Castro in Cuba.

name: CWA Organize (Communications Workers of America)
title: C.I.O. Organize!
subject: Labor Unions, CIO, Organizing
description: A black and gold pin representing the Congress of Industrial Organizations (CIO) encouraging workers to organize into unions.

name: [Lenin]
title: Vladimir Lenin Profile Pin
party: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
subject: Vladimir Lenin, Communism, USSR
description: A bronze-colored metal pin depicting the profile of Vladimir Lenin, the Soviet revolutionary and politician who served as the first and founding head of government of Soviet Russia.

name: Lenin
title: LENIN
party: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
subject: Soviet Union, Communism, Vladimir Lenin
description: A rectangular red enamel pin featuring a gold-toned profile portrait of Vladimir Lenin with his name written at the bottom.

name: [Cuban Flag]
title: Cuban Flag
subject: Cuba, Flag, Spanish-American War
description: A small metal charm or pin in the shape of a waving Cuban flag. This type of item was commonly produced around the time of the Spanish-American War to signify support for Cuban independence from Spain.

name: L.I. Workers' Memorial Day ; April 28 (Labor and Industries)
title: L.I. WORKERS' MEMORIAL DAY APRIL 28
subject: Labor unions, Workplace safety, Memorials
description: A lavender circular button with a stylized green candle and white flame in the center. The text 'L.I. WORKERS' MEMORIAL DAY' and 'APRIL 28' arches around the top and bottom. It commemorates workers injured or killed on the job in Long Island, New York.

name: Cuarto Grado ; UPC (Unión de Pioneros de Cuba/Union of Pioneers of Cuba)
title: CUARTO GRADO UPC
subject: Education, Cuba, Pioneers
description: A blue and silver circular pin for a fourth-grade student in the Union of Pioneers of Cuba (UPC). The pin features a five-pointed star containing the portrait of Paquito González Cueto, the first pioneer martyr of the Cuban Revolution.

name: Unity in Action! ANC ; 1912-1982 (African National Congress)
title: UNITY IN ACTION! ANC 1912-1982 70
subject: Anti-Apartheid, Human Rights, South Africa
description: A rectangular pin commemorating the 70th anniversary of the African National Congress (ANC) in 1982. The pin features the ANC's traditional colors of black, green, and gold, along with an assegai (spear), a clenched fist, and the slogan 'Unity In Action!'.

name: PCD
title: PED
subject: Spanish politics, Political parties, Labor
description: A square white button with a gold border, featuring the text "PED" in gold. The imagery includes a red silhouette of a fist holding a hammer and a gold outline of an open book, symbolizing the union of labor and knowledge. This button is for the Partido de la Estabilidad Democrática (PED), a minor political party active during the Spanish Transition to democracy in the late 1970s.

name: Independence Socialism People's Progressive Party of Guyana
title: SOCIALISM PEOPLE'S PROGRESSIVE PARTY OF GUYANA INDEPENDENCE
subject: Socialism, Independence, Guyana
description: A circular political button for the People's Progressive Party of Guyana. The button features a green map of Guyana in the center with a pair of brown hands breaking silver shackles, symbolizing liberation and independence. A small red five-pointed star sits at the top right of the map. The border contains the text 'SOCIALISM PEOPLE'S PROGRESSIVE PARTY OF GUYANA INDEPENDENCE'.

name: Quinto Grado ; UPC (Unión de Pioneros de Cuba/Union of Pioneers of Cuba)
title: QUINTO GRADO UPC
subject: Cuba, Education, Che Guevara
description: A green and silver metal pin for a fifth-grade student (Quinto Grado) in the Unión de Pioneros de Cuba (UPC), a Cuban youth organization. The pin features a star with the portrait of revolutionary leader Ernesto "Che" Guevara.

name: [Woman in front of outline of Vietnam]
subject: Vietnam, Anti-war, Solidarity
description: An oval-shaped metal pin with a blue enamel-like background featuring a gold-toned map of Vietnam and the portrait of a person in a traditional conical hat. Beside the face is a stylized depiction of a traditional musical instrument. The pin is a piece of political memorabilia likely from the Vietnam War era, used to express solidarity with the Vietnamese people or as part of the anti-war movement.

name: [Shaped like a Dove 19th Annual World Conference Against Atomic and Hydrogen Bombs]
title: 1973.ナガサキ 第19回世界大会
subject: peace, nuclear disarmament, Nagasaki
description: The reverse side of a bird-shaped (likely a dove) pin commemorating the 19th World Conference against Atomic and Hydrogen Bombs, held in Nagasaki in 1973. The Japanese inscription on the left notes the event and year, while the text on the right identifies a Hokkaido-based organizational branch.

name: [Shaped like a Dove 19th Annual World Conference Against Atomic and Hydrogen Bombs]
subject: Peace, Anti-War, Elections
description: A white dove-shaped pin associated with Eugene McCarthy's 1968 presidential campaign, representing his anti-Vietnam War platform and the broader peace movement.

name: [14th Annual World Conference Against Atomic and Hydrogen Bombs]
subject: peace, doves, anti-war
description: A vertical rectangular blue pin featuring two white doves in flight and a gold-colored sunburst symbol. This design is a common emblem of the peace movement that was prominent during the late 1960s and early 1970s.

name: Plumbing Trades Employees ; Proud to Be Union ; Retired Member
title: PLUMBING TRADES EMPLOYEES UNION PROUD TO BE UNION OF AUSTRALIA RETIRED MEMBER
subject: Labor Unions, Plumbing, Australia
description: A circular badge with a flared base, identifying the wearer as a retired member of the Plumbing Trades Employees Union of Australia. The badge features depictions of plumbing tools such as a pipe wrench, pliers, and a hammer, along with the slogan "Proud to be Union".

name: Region 9 ; UAW (United Auto Workers)
title: REGION 9 UAW
subject: Labor Unions, UAW, Regional Organization
description: A lapel pin for Region 9 of the International Union, United Automobile, Aerospace and Agricultural Implement Workers of America (UAW). The pin is shield-shaped with an eagle at the top, the words "REGION 9", and the UAW logo depicting a circle of workers holding hands.

name: Mobilization CWA Coordinator (Communications Workers of America)
title: MOBILIZATION CWA COORDINATOR
subject: labor union, communications workers of america, collective bargaining
description: A square enamel pin for a Communications Workers of America (CWA) mobilization coordinator, featuring a red triangle with the CWA logo and 'COORDINATOR' in a red bar at the bottom.

name: NNEC & SEIU ; Local 560, 603, 1984, 1989
title: NNEC - SEIU LOCALS 560, 603, 1984, 1989
subject: Labor unions, Service Employees International Union, Northern New England Council
description: An oval gold-colored pin for the Northern New England Council of the Service Employees International Union (SEIU), mentioning Locals 560 and 603 and the years 1984 and 1989. A handshake is featured in the center.

name: 100 [Italian Mak P pin]
subject: United Way, Charity, Award
description: A rectangular lapel pin with a red upper field and an off-white lower field. Three stylized black figures are depicted holding up the numbers 1, 0, and 0 in off-white. This pin was commonly awarded for 100% participation in United Way fundraising campaigns.

name: CGT La Metallurgie (General Confederation of Labour)
title: la cgt métallurgie
subject: Labor union, Metallurgy, France
description: A red rectangular button for the French trade union federation CGT (Confédération Générale du Travail), specifically representing the metalworking sector (métallurgie). It features the union's acronym in large letters and three stylized faces of workers at the bottom.

name: [International Brigade Flag]
subject: Spanish Civil War, International Brigades, Second Spanish Republic
description: A rectangular enamel pin featuring the tricolor flag of the Second Spanish Republic (red, yellow, and purple) with a red three-pointed star, which is the emblem of the International Brigades that fought in the Spanish Civil War.

name: [Gender Equality Symbol]
subject: gender equality, feminism, civil rights
description: A metal lapel pin representing gender equality, showing the intertwined symbols for male and female with a red equals sign in the center.

name: Unite Scotland ; Your Union Your Voice ; Policy Conference 2016
title: UNITE SCOTLAND YOUR UNION, YOUR VOICE Policy Conference 2016
subject: Trade Union, Scotland, Labor
description: A button produced for the Unite Scotland Policy Conference in 2016. The button features the Unite the Union logo, a map of Scotland, and the slogan 'Your Union, Your Voice'.

name: PATCO (Professional Air Traffic Controllers Organization)
title: patco
subject: Labor Unions, Air Traffic Control, PATCO Strike
description: A cast metal lapel pin featuring the lowercase text 'patco', representing the Professional Air Traffic Controllers Organization. This union is most famous for its 1981 strike which led to its decertification by the Reagan administration.

name: Confederaz Italiana Sindacati Lavoratori ; C.I.S.L. (Italian Confederation of Workers' Unions)
title: CONFEDERAZ. ITALIANA SINDACATI LAVORATORI CISL
subject: Labor Unions, Italy, Workers
description: A metal lapel pin representing the Confederazione Italiana Sindacati Lavoratori (CISL), an Italian trade union federation. The pin features a globe design with the organization's full name and its acronym.

name: Community Action Program ; UAW (United Auto Workers)
title: COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM UAW
subject: Labor Unions, Community Activism, United Auto Workers
description: A circular pin featuring the logo of the UAW Community Action Program (CAP). The design includes an outer blue ring with the program name and a yellow ring depicting silhouettes of people holding hands around the central letters 'UAW'.

name: UGT (Unión General de Trabajadores/ General Union of Workers)
title: UGT
subject: Labor unions, Spain, Workers' rights
description: A circular pin badge for the Unión General de Trabajadores (UGT), a major Spanish trade union. The design features the initials 'UGT' and a handshake symbol, both in silver/black outline, against a solid red background.

name: Carpenters Make A Difference
title: CARPENTERS MAKE A DIFFERENCE
subject: Labor Unions, Carpenters, United Brotherhood of Carpenters
description: A rectangular yellow button promoting the United Brotherhood of Carpenters and Joiners of America with the slogan 'Carpenters Make a Difference' and the union's emblem in the center featuring the motto 'Labor Omnia Vincit'.

name: IBEW Loval 1499 ; 1946-1996 (International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers
title: LOCAL 1499 1946 1996 IBEW
subject: Labor unions, IBEW, 50th Anniversary
description: A commemorative pin for the 50th anniversary of IBEW Local 1499, which was established in 1946. The pin features a fist clutching a lightning bolt, a diamond-shaped IBEW logo, and an outline of the state of Massachusetts.

name: New England Regional Council of Carpenters ; Connecticut Maine Massachusetts New Hampshire Rhode Island Vermont
title: New England Regional Council of Carpenters
state: Connecticut, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, Rhode Island, Vermont
subject: Labor union, Carpentry, New England
description: A square lapel pin for the New England Regional Council of Carpenters, depicting a hammer, square, and level on a black background. The pin lists the six states served by the council: Connecticut, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, Rhode Island, and Vermont. The NERCC existed from 1996 until it merged into the North Atlantic States Regional Council of Carpenters in 2020.

name: No Pasaran ; Brigadas Internacionales
title: NO PASARAN BRIGADAS INTERNACIONALES
subject: Antifascism, Spanish Civil War, International Brigades
description: An enamel pin featuring the three-pointed star of the International Brigades, the slogan "No Pasarán", and the text "Brigadas Internacionales". It commemorates the Republican forces and international volunteers who fought against fascist forces during the Spanish Civil War.

name: Forth Valley Health Branch ; Unison
title: FORTH VALLEY UNISON HEALTH BRANCH
subject: Trade union, Healthcare, Scotland
description: An oval pin badge for the UNISON Forth Valley Health Branch, featuring the union's name and stylized logo on a green and purple background.

name: CWA (Communications Workers of America)
title: 50 CWA
subject: Labor union, Anniversary, CWA
description: A rectangular gold-colored commemorative pin for the 50th anniversary of the Communications Workers of America (CWA) labor union, featuring the number 50 and the acronym CWA with stylized geometric shapes.

name: UGT (Unión General de Trabajadores/ General Union of Workers)
title: UGT
subject: Labor union; Spain; Worker solidarity
description: A silver-toned metal lapel pin for the Unión General de Trabajadores (UGT), a major Spanish trade union. The pin features the letters 'UGT' above a handshake symbol, representing the union's commitment to worker solidarity.

name: World Congress Devoted to the International of Peace ; Copenhagen, October 1986
title: WORLD CONGRESS DEVOTED TO THE INTERNATIONAL YEAR OF PEACE Copenhagen, October 1986
subject: Peace, International Year of Peace, Global Cooperation
description: A rectangular white pin featuring a blue globe and a gold graphic of a dove and hands within a wreath. The text commemorates the World Congress Devoted to the International Year of Peace held in Copenhagen in October 1986.

name: Kilbourn for Congress ; Staff
title: KILBOURN FOR CONGRESS STAFF
subject: Politics, Election, Campaign
description: A red rectangular campaign sticker with white text that reads "KILBOURN FOR CONGRESS STAFF" from John J. Kilbourn's 1974 run for the House of Representatives in South Dakota.

name: We're Your Bugs! ; Union Label Allied Printing Trades Council
title: WE'RE YOUR BUGS!
subject: Labor Unions, Printing, Union Label
description: A button promoting the use of union labels, which are colloquially known as "bugs" in the printing industry. The button features two logos: the Allied Printing Trades Council label and the International Typographical Union label. The placeholder "NAME OF CITY" suggests this was a generic or sample design.

name: Support the Steelworkers ; USX
title: SUPPORT THE STEELWORKERS
subject: Labor, Union, USX Lockout
description: A button supporting the United Steelworkers during the 1986-1987 USX lockout, featuring a chain and a padlock with the 'USX' logo.

name: Fed Up With 59¢ Join AFSCME ( American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees)
title: FED UP WITH 59¢ JOIN AFSCME
subject: Gender pay gap, AFSCME, Labor union
description: This button was produced by the American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees (AFSCME) to advocate for pay equity. The "59¢" refers to a statistic widely cited in the late 1970s and early 1980s indicating that for every dollar a man earned, a woman earned only 59 cents.

name: Drug & Hospital Employees ; Local 1199 Guild Delegate ; AFL-CIO (American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations)
title: DRUG & HOSPITAL EMPLOYEES UNION LOCAL 1199 GUILD DELEGATE AFL-CIO
subject: Labor Unions, Healthcare Workers, AFL-CIO
description: A red button for a delegate of the Guild division within Local 1199, the Drug and Hospital Employees Union, affiliated with the AFL-CIO.

name: SEIU Yes (Service Employees International Union)
title: SEIU Yes
subject: Labor Union, SEIU, Unionization
description: A black square button with yellow text "SEIU" and turquoise cursive text "Yes" with a checkmark inside a yellow box, advocating for union representation by the Service Employees International Union.

name: New Jersey Anti-Apartheid Mobilization ; Free South Africa ; November 9, 1985
title: NEW JERSEY ANTI-APARTHEID MOBILIZATION
subject: Anti-Apartheid, Human Rights, South Africa
description: A red button with a green map of Africa in the center that says "FREE SOUTH AFRICA". The outer edge of the button reads "NEW JERSEY ANTI-APARTHEID MOBILIZATION" and the bottom reads "November 9, 1985". It was likely produced for the March on Trenton organized by the group on that date.

name: Respect 32BJ
title: RESPECT 32BJ
subject: Labor Union, Airport workers, SEIU
description: A yellow button with a purple airplane icon and text reading RESPECT 32BJ. This button is associated with the SEIU Local 32BJ union's campaign for airport workers' rights, likely in the New York/New Jersey area.

name: We'll Be There ; Day of Outrage ; June 30 ; CWA (Communication Workers of America)
title: WE'LL BE THERE Day of Outrage June 30 CWA
subject: Labor Unions, Protest, Budget
description: A button from the Communications Workers of America (CWA) for a 'Day of Outrage' protest on June 30, 1992. The protest was held in Trenton, New Jersey, where thousands of state employees demonstrated against proposed budget cuts and layoffs by the state government.

name: We're Taking a Stand ; Contract '89 ; CWA (Communications Workers of America)
title: WE'RE TAKING A STAND CONTRACT '89 CWA
subject: Labor, Union, CWA
description: A button produced for the Communications Workers of America (CWA) during their 1989 contract negotiations and strikes against various regional Bell companies.

name: Free Nelson Mandela ANC (S.A.) (African National Congress South Africa)
title: FREE NELSON MANDELA ANC (S.A.)
subject: Apartheid, Mandela, Anti-Apartheid Movement
description: A yellow and black button featuring a portrait of Nelson Mandela, calling for his freedom from prison during the South African apartheid era. It includes the acronym for the African National Congress (South Africa).

name: Self-Determination for All People
title: Self-Determination For All People
subject: Self-determination, Human rights, Sovereignty
description: An oval green button with white text that reads "Self-Determination For All People." This slogan is a central tenet of various political and social movements seeking autonomy and the right to self-governance.

name: SEIU Yes (Service Employees International Union)
title: SEIU Yes
subject: Labor union, SEIU, Union organizing
description: A button for the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) featuring the text 'SEIU Yes' with a checkmark, typically used during union representation elections or organizing campaigns.

name: Vote NO on Question 3. ; It Goes Too Far.
title: Vote NO on Question 3. It goes too far.
subject: Ballot measure, Taxation, Labor unions
description: This blue political button was produced for the 1990 Massachusetts election to oppose Question 3, a high-profile ballot initiative that proposed rolling back state taxes. Labor unions, including the United Food and Commercial Workers (UFCW) whose logo appears at the bottom, campaigned against the measure using the slogan 'It goes too far' to argue that the resulting budget cuts would severely damage public services.

name: FTAA ["No" Symbol] (Free Trade Area of the Americas)
title: FTAA
subject: Free Trade, Globalization, Activism
description: A button featuring the acronym FTAA (Free Trade Area of the Americas) with a red prohibition symbol through it, signifying opposition to the proposed trade agreement during the late 1990s and early 2000s.

name: Vote Contract AFA (Association of Flight Attendants)
title: DAVE THE BUTTON MAN of Daytona Beach
subject: advertising, manufacturing, buttons
description: A button back containing a promotional sticker for Dave The Button Man, a button manufacturer in Ormond Beach, Florida.

name: Vote Contract AFA (Association of Flight Attendants)
title: VOTE Contract AFA
subject: Labor Unions, Aviation, Contract Ratification
description: Round white button with blue and red text reading "VOTE Contract AFA" featuring a red checkmark over the letter 'O' in 'VOTE'.

name: South Afria Freedom Fund ; Service Employees International Union
title: South Africa Freedom Fund
subject: Anti-Apartheid, Labor Unions, South Africa
description: A square button with rounded corners supporting the South Africa Freedom Fund, sponsored by the Service Employees International Union (SEIU). The button displays a stylized graphic of a hand holding a torch against a red and yellow background.

name: Proud to be Union! ; Pittsburgh CLUW (The Coalition of Labor Union Women)
title: WAR IS GOOD BUSINESS INVEST YOUR SON
subject: Vietnam War; Anti-war; Protest
description: The reverse side of a pinback button produced by Button Farm in Oak Lawn, Illinois. The front of the button features the slogan 'WAR IS GOOD BUSINESS INVEST YOUR SON', a common anti-Vietnam War message from the late 1960s.

name: Proud to be Union! ; Pittsburgh CLUW (The Coalition of Labor Union Women)
title: PROUD TO BE UNION! PITTSBURGH CLUW
subject: Labor Unions, Women's History, Labor Activism
description: A red button with white text promoting the Pittsburgh chapter of the Coalition of Labor Union Women (CLUW), expressing pride in being part of a union.

name: Support Strawberry Workers ; 5¢ for Fairness ; UFW AFL-CIO (United Farm Workers American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations)
title: Support Strawberry Workers 5C for fairness UFW AFL-CIO
subject: labor rights, agricultural workers, United Farm Workers
description: A button supporting strawberry workers, specifically the campaign for a 5-cent-per-pint increase to improve workers' lives, led by the United Farm Workers (UFW) and the AFL-CIO in the late 1990s.

name: Fight Back  ; Build Our Union ; CWA (Communications Workers of America)
title: Fight Back CWA Build Our Union
subject: Labor unions, CWA, Protests
description: A red circular button featuring a graphic of three hands holding up picket signs. The signs display the slogans 'Fight Back' and 'Build Our Union', along with the initials 'CWA' for the Communications Workers of America.

name: Stop the U.S, Intervention in El Salvador ; Coordinating Committee in Solidarity with the People of El Salvador
title: STOP U.S. INTERVENTION in EL SALVADOR COORDINATING COMMITTEE in SOLIDARITY with the PEOPLE of EL SALVADOR
subject: El Salvador Civil War, US Foreign Policy, Protest
description: Button produced by the Committee in Solidarity with the People of El Salvador (CISPES) to protest United States military intervention and aid during the Salvadoran Civil War.

name: Boycott Campbell's Libby's ;  FLOC - Union Recognition (Farm Labor Organizing Committee)
title: FLOC - UNION RECOGNITION BOYCOTT CAMPBELL'S LIBBY'S
subject: Labor Unions, Farmworkers, Boycotts
description: A button issued by the Farm Labor Organizing Committee (FLOC) calling for a boycott of Campbell Soup Company and Libby's products to support migrant farmworkers' efforts for union recognition and better working conditions.

name: Free Nelson Mandela
title: FREE NELSON MANDELA
subject: Apartheid, Human Rights, South Africa
description: A circular yellow button with green text reading 'FREE NELSON MANDELA' and a black and white halftone portrait of Nelson Mandela. This button was part of the international campaign to release Mandela from prison in South Africa.

name: Drop the Charges ; Stop Union Busting! ; USWA School Bus Union 3 (United Steelworkers of America)
title: DROP THE CHARGES Stop Union Busting! USWA School Bus Union 3
subject: Labor Unions, Bus Drivers, Protests
description: A yellow and black button advocating for the dropping of legal charges and an end to union-busting activities against members of the USWA (United Steelworkers) school bus union, likely associated with the 2013 Boston school bus drivers strike and the subsequent defense of the 'Boston School Bus 5'.

name: Stop Apartheid Rugby Tour
title: STOP APARTHEID RUGBY TOUR
subject: Apartheid, Rugby, Protest
description: A black circular button with white text that reads 'STOP APARTHEID RUGBY TOUR'. This button was used by activists protesting the South African national rugby team (the Springboks) during their international tours, which were a major focus of the anti-apartheid movement, especially during the 1981 tour of New Zealand and the United States.

name: Vote No 3 ["No" Symbol over 3] ; Ques. #3 ; Nov. 6th
title: Barney Frank for Congress
subject: Political campaigns, Elections, Barney Frank
description: A political campaign button supporting Barney Frank for the United States Congress. The back of the button features a manufacturer's sticker for G & G Design in New Bedford, MA.

name: Vote No 3 ["No" Symbol over 3] ; Ques. #3 ; Nov. 6th
title: VOTE NO QUES. #3 Nov. 6th
subject: Ballot measures, Taxation, Massachusetts
description: A political button from 1990 urging a 'no' vote on Question 3, a Massachusetts ballot initiative that proposed a significant tax rollback. The button features a union label from Somerville, Massachusetts.

name: Hands Off El Salvador ; No Mas Vietnams
title: HANDS OFF EL SALVADOR NO M
subject: Anti-war, El Salvador, Foreign Policy
description: A political button protesting United States military intervention in El Salvador during the Salvadoran Civil War. It features a map of Central America with El Salvador highlighted, a hand reaching down towards it, and the slogans 'HANDS OFF EL SALVADOR' and 'NO M

name: Covert Action Information Bulletin ; ["No" symbol over CIA]
title: CovertAction Information Bulletin CIA
subject: CIA, intelligence, covert action
description: A white button featuring a red prohibition symbol over the letters 'CIA' in black. Around the top curve is the text 'CovertAction Information Bulletin' and around the bottom curve is the address 'Box 50272 Washington DC 20004 USA'.

name: Hospital Division Local 1199 ; AFL-CIO (American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations)
title: HOSPITAL DIVISION LOCAL 1199 AFL-CIO
subject: Labor Unions, Healthcare, Worker Rights
description: A blue and white pinback button from Local 1199's Hospital Division. Local 1199 is a prominent healthcare workers' union, and the button indicates its affiliation with the AFL-CIO. A small LPIU union bug is visible at the bottom.

name: NO 3 ["No" symbol over 3]
title: NO 3
subject: Ballot measure, Salaries, Voting
description: A white button with the word 'NO' in large red letters, featuring a black number '3' inside the 'O' with a red slash through it. This button was used in the campaign against Massachusetts ballot Question 3 in 1980, which concerned salary increases for state officials.

name: Madrea ; Joining Hands & Hearts with the Mothers & Children of Central America & the Caribbean
title: Joining hands & hearts with the mothers & children of Central America & the Caribbean MADRE
subject: Human Rights, Central America, International Solidarity
description: This button represents MADRE, an international women's human rights organization founded in 1983. The button displays a silhouette of a woman against a map of Central America, advocating for solidarity with mothers and children in that region and the Caribbean during a period of significant political unrest.

name: GUild of Professional Techinical & Office Employees 1199
title: Guild of Professional Technical & Office Employees 11 99
subject: union, labor, employees
description: A white circular button with red text and imagery. Around the border is written "GUILD OF PROFESSIONAL TECHNICAL & OFFICE EMPLOYEES". In the center is an image of an open book with the numbers "11" and "99" on the pages. This button represents a division of the 1199 National Union of Hospital and Health Care Employees.

name: Taft Can Mine It... Hartley Can Haul It... I Support the Miners
title: Taft can mine it... Hartley can haul it... I SUPPORT THE MINERS
person: Robert A. Taft; Fred A. Hartley Jr.
subject: Coal Mining, Labor Unions, Taft-Hartley Act
description: This button supports coal miners during the labor disputes following the passage of the Taft-Hartley Act in 1947. The text 'Taft can mine it... Hartley can haul it' was a popular slogan among union members, suggesting that if the politicians wanted the coal mined under the restrictive conditions of the act, they should perform the labor themselves.

name: Abortion ; A Right Not S Privilege
title: ABORTION A RIGHT NOT A PRIVILEGE
subject: abortion rights, reproductive rights, feminism
description: Green circular button with black text reading 'ABORTION A RIGHT NOT A PRIVILEGE' surrounding a central decorative horizontal line.

name: District 65 of The UAW Cornell Chapter (United Auto Workers)
title: District 65 of The UAW Cornell Chapter
subject: Labor Unions, Cornell University, UAW
description: A white button with red text and graphics for the Cornell Chapter of District 65, UAW. The central graphic is Cornell University's McGraw Tower clock tower.

name: Alliance ; AFSCME/SEUI AFL-CIO (American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees , Service Employees International Union, American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations)
title: ALLIANCE AFSCME / SEIU AFL-CIO
subject: Labor Unions, Collective Bargaining, Workers Rights
description: A promotional button for The Alliance, a coalition between AFSCME and SEIU AFL-CIO, which worked together to represent public employees in Illinois starting in the mid-1970s.

name: Contras No Nicaragua Si
title: CONTRAS NO Nicaragua SÍ
subject: Nicaragua, Contras, Sandinistas
description: A circular button with a horizontal wavy division; the top half is yellow with the words 'CONTRAS NO' in brown, and the bottom half is blue with the words 'Nicaragua SÍ' in yellow. The button expresses opposition to the Contras and support for the Nicaraguan revolution during the Contra War.

name: The Union for Concerned Women
title: THE UNION FOR CONCERNED WOMEN
subject: Labor Unions, Women's Rights, UFCW
description: A white button with blue and red text that reads "THE UNION FOR CONCERNED WOMEN". The first letters of the main words are highlighted in red to spell out the acronym "UFCW", which stands for the United Food and Commercial Workers International Union. The letter "O" in the word "WOMEN" is replaced with a female gender symbol.

name: Sheet Metal Workers ; Gore 2000 ; Local 17
title: SHEET METAL WORKERS GORE 2000 LOCAL 17
subject: Labor Unions, Presidential Election, Al Gore
description: A campaign button supporting Al Gore's 2000 presidential run, produced by the Sheet Metal Workers International Association Local 17 based in Boston.

name: Namibia & South Africa One Struggle
title: Namibia & South Africa One Struggle
subject: Anti-apartheid movement, Namibia independence, African liberation
description: A red, white, and blue button featuring a torch with a red flame over a green map of Namibia and South Africa. The text 'NAMIBIA & SOUTH AFRICA' appears at the top and 'ONE STRUGGLE' at the bottom, signifying solidarity between the liberation movements in both countries during the 1980s.

name: SEIU Local 509 (Service Employees International Union) ; Stand with Sandy
title: SEIU LOCAL 509 STAND WITH SANDY
subject: Labor Unions, Workers Rights, SEIU
description: Purple and white button produced by SEIU Local 509 to support social worker Sandy Feltz, who was fired from the Massachusetts Department of Children and Families in 2010. The union campaigned for her reinstatement, which was achieved in 2012.

name: Social Secuirty, Education, Healthcare ; I'm Voting on Nov. 3rd ; Labor AFL_CIO 98 (American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations)
title: Social Security Education Health Care I'm voting on Nov. 3rd Labor AFL-CIO '98
subject: Labor, Education, Healthcare
description: A square black button with yellow and white text encouraging people to vote on November 3rd, 1998, based on the issues of Social Security, Education, and Healthcare. It features the AFL-CIO logo.

name: 21/81 T.E.A.M. ; UFCW (United Food and Commercial Workers)
title: 21/81 T.E.A.M.
subject: Labor unions, UFCW, Chicago
description: A red button with yellow text that reads 21/81 and T.E.A.M. Below the main text is a small logo for the UFCW (United Food and Commercial Workers) Union Chicago.

name: SLATS
title: SLATS
subject: Education, Elections, Illinois Politics
description: A campaign button for Richard J. "Slats" Martwick, a Democratic politician from Chicago who served as the Regional Superintendent of Schools for Cook County from 1971 to 1991. The simple blue and white design with his nickname was a hallmark of his campaign materials.

name: TEAM ; Tax Justice Campaign
title: TEAM TAX JUSTICE CAMPAIGN
subject: Tax reform, Taxation, Justice
description: White button with blue text that reads 'TEAM' in the center and 'TAX JUSTICE CAMPAIGN' in a semi-circle along the bottom edge. There is also a small blue union bug logo at the top.

name: America Works Best When We Say... Union Yes
title: AMERICA WORKS BEST WHEN WE SAY... UNION YES
subject: Labor unions, AFL-CIO, Union Yes campaign
description: A square white button with red and blue text reading "AMERICA WORKS BEST WHEN WE SAY... UNION YES". It features the AFL-CIO logo (American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations) and a blue checkmark. This button was part of a major public relations campaign launched by the AFL-CIO in 1988 to improve the public image of labor unions.

name: Vote Pro-Choice ; No on #1
title: VOTE Pro-Choice NO ON #1
subject: Abortion, Reproductive Rights, Ballot Initiative
description: This button was used during the 1986 campaign in Massachusetts to defeat Question 1, a proposed state constitutional amendment that would have given the state legislature the power to restrict or prohibit abortion. The pro-choice movement campaigned successfully for a 'No' vote, and the measure was defeated.

name: Bread & Roses Heritage Committee
title: BREAD & ROSES HERITAGE COMMITTEE
subject: Labor, Strike, Heritage
description: A button from the Bread and Roses Heritage Committee, a non-profit organization based in Lawrence, Massachusetts, dedicated to honoring the history and legacy of the 1912 Lawrence Textile Strike, also known as the Bread and Roses strike.

name: SEUI  (Service Employees International Union) 1199 ; Unity is Strength
title: 1199 SEIU UNITY IS STRENGTH
subject: Labor Unions, Healthcare Workers, Solidarity
description: A green button featuring the logo of the 1199SEIU United Healthcare Workers East union. The logo shows four interlocking hands with the text '1199', 'SEIU', and the motto 'UNITY IS STRENGTH'.

name: The Only Way to Win is Not to Play ["No" symbol over mushroom cloud]
title: THE ONLY WAY TO WIN IS NOT TO PLAY
subject: anti-nuclear, disarmament, WarGames
description: A circular anti-nuclear protest button featuring a red circle with a diagonal slash over a silhouette of a mushroom cloud on a blue background. The text around the border reads 'THE ONLY WAY TO WIN IS NOT TO PLAY', which is the famous concluding quote from the 1983 film WarGames.

name: "Read my lips." ["No" symbol] T.E.A.M.
title: "Read my lips." T.E.A.M.
subject: Taxes, Campaign Promises, George H.W. Bush
description: This button features the phrase "Read my lips," a direct reference to George H.W. Bush's 1988 campaign pledge not to raise taxes. The phrase is covered by a red "no" symbol, indicating a criticism of Bush for breaking that promise during his administration. The acronym T.E.A.M. likely refers to a taxpayers' advocacy group such as the Taxpayers' Economic Action Movement.

name: Cut Ties to Apartheid
title: CUT TIES TO Apartheid U.S. PEACE COUNCIL
subject: Apartheid, Anti-Apartheid, Social Justice
description: A black and white circular button produced by the U.S. Peace Council. It features the text 'CUT TIES TO Apartheid' in white over a black background. Below the text is an emblem showing a dove integrated into a hand, and at the bottom edge, it says 'U.S. PEACE COUNCIL'.

name: No NAFTA ; AFL-CIO (American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations)
title: NO NAFTA AFL-CIO
subject: Labor unions, Trade agreements, NAFTA
description: A red and white button from the AFL-CIO expressing opposition to the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA).

name: Get Serious About America's Families! ; Employees International Union, AFLC-CIO, CLC ( American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations, Central Labor Council)
title: Get Serious About America's Families!
subject: Labor Unions, Family Leave, SEIU
description: A button produced by the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) featuring the slogan 'Get Serious About America's Families!' as part of their campaign for the Family and Medical Leave Act.

name: Health Care That's Always There.
title: HEALTH CARE THAT'S ALWAYS THERE.
subject: Healthcare, Reform, Clinton
description: Yellow button with blue text reading "HEALTH CARE THAT'S ALWAYS THERE." featuring the logo of the Clinton administration's 1993 health care reform plan.

name: Throw the Bums Out ; Union Busters, Lawbreakers, Warmongers, Gun Runners, Racists
title: THROW THE BUMS OUT
subject: Ronald Reagan, Iran-Contra Scandal, Political Protest
description: An anti-Ronald Reagan political button from the late 1980s. It features a caricature of Reagan being thrown out, accompanied by a list of grievances: 'UNION BUSTERS', 'LAWBREAKERS', 'WARMONGERS', 'GUN RUNNERS', and 'RACISTS'. The 'gun runners' and 'lawbreakers' text likely refers to the Iran-Contra scandal that broke in 1986.

name: Local 840 Teamsters ; I.B. of T.C. W. & H. of A. (International Brotherhood Of Teamsters, Chauffeurs, Warehousemen, And Helpers Of America)
title: LOCAL 840 TEAMSTERS
subject: Labor Unions, Teamsters, Local 840
description: A gold-colored button representing Local 840 of the International Brotherhood of Teamsters (IBT), showcasing the union's traditional logo of two horse heads and a wagon wheel and the full name: I. B. of T. C. W. & H. of A.

name: Support Sandy Stop Weld ; SEIU Local 509 (Service Employees International Union)
title: SUPPORT SANDY SEIU LOCAL 509 STOP WELD
subject: Labor, Union, Protest
description: This pinback button was produced by SEIU Local 509, a Massachusetts union for human service workers, to protest the policies of Governor William Weld and support a worker named Sandy, likely during a period of budget cuts or privatization.

name: Workers of the World Unite
title: WORKERS OF THE WORLD UNITE
subject: Labor, Socialism, Solidarity
description: This button features the 'arm and hammer' symbol, which is the logo of the Socialist Labor Party of America, and the slogan 'Workers of the World Unite', a famous socialist rallying cry.

name: Jobs, Not Bombs ; April 25 - May Day
title: APRIL 25-MAY DAY JOBS, NOT BOMBS
subject: Peace, Employment, Military spending
description: A white button featuring a dove with an olive branch and a tail made of red tulips. The text promotes 'Jobs, Not Bombs' during a series of protests from April 25th to May Day, 1981, protesting military spending and advocating for economic justice.

name: Justica ; Dignidad
title: JUSTICIA DIGNIDAD
subject: Labor, Agriculture, UFW
description: This button was produced for the United Farm Workers (UFW) campaign to organize strawberry workers in California during the late 1990s. It features the UFW's black Aztec eagle logo alongside a strawberry, with the Spanish words for justice and dignity.

name: Re Elect Fred Trusten
title: FRED ELECT TRUSTEN
subject: School committee, Socialist Workers Party, Boston politics
description: A white campaign button with black text for Fred Trusten's 1973 campaign for the Boston School Committee. Trusten ran as a candidate for the Socialist Workers Party.

name: Lick Bush
title: LICK BUSH
subject: George H.W. Bush, Presidential Election, Anti-Bush
description: A white political button with the text 'LICK BUSH' in dark brown block lettering. This button was used as an anti-campaign slogan against George H.W. Bush, where 'lick' is meant as a synonym for 'defeat'.

name: 509 Templeton President
title: 509 TEMPLETON President
subject: Labor Union, Election, SEIU
description: A campaign button for a candidate named Templeton running for President of SEIU Local 509. The button features the purple and yellow color scheme characteristic of the Service Employees International Union. The number 36 in the union bug at the bottom indicates it was likely printed in Boston, Massachusetts, which is the headquarters for Local 509.

name: Pro Union Pro Choice
title: PRO Union PRO Choice CLUW
subject: Labor Unions, Pro-Choice, Women's Rights
description: A square button advocating for both labor union support and reproductive rights, issued by the Coalition of Labor Union Women (CLUW).

name: SEIU Local 509 AFL-CIO (Service Employees International Union American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations)
title: seiu local 509 afl-cio
subject: Labor Unions, Service Employees International Union, Collective Bargaining
description: A yellow button for SEIU Local 509, an affiliate of the AFL-CIO that represents human service workers and educators in Massachusetts.

name: Respected the Work ; Respect the Workers ; SEIU Local 509 (Service Employees International Union)
title: Respect the Work SEIU Local 509 Respect the Workers
subject: Labor unions, Service Employees International Union, Workers' rights
description: A circular purple button with yellow text reading "Respect the Work" along the top edge and "Respect the Workers" along the bottom edge. In the center, the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) Local 509 logo is featured in yellow and white.

name: Special Interests Make Me Sick. ; Support the American Health Security ACt HR 1200 & S 491
title: Special interests make me sick. Support the American Health Security Act HR 1200 & S 491
subject: Healthcare Reform, Legislation, Single-payer System
description: A purple button with yellow and white text advocating for the American Health Security Act (HR 1200 and S 491), which proposed a single-payer health insurance system in the United States during the 103rd Congress.

name: Labor Solidarity Has No Borders ; Coalition of Labor Union Women
title: LABOR SOLIDARITY HAS NO BORDERS COALITION OF LABOR UNION WOMEN
subject: Labor, Women's Rights, Solidarity
description: A circular blue pinback button featuring white text that reads "LABOR SOLIDARITY HAS NO BORDERS" in the center, and "COALITION OF LABOR UNION WOMEN" along the bottom arc. It includes the CLUW logo.

name: Willy Weld The Governor From Hell! ; Support State Employees
title: WILLY WELD THE GOVERNOR FROM HELL! SUPPORT STATE EMPLOYEES
subject: Labor Unions, State Employees, Protest
description: A button used by state employees in Massachusetts to protest the policies of Governor William Weld, who was often in conflict with labor unions over budget cuts and privatization.

name: Back Yards Not Back Yards for People With Mental Illness
title: BACK YARDS NOT BACK WARDS FOR PEOPLE WITH MENTAL ILLNESS
subject: Mental Health, Deinstitutionalization, Disability Rights
description: A square white button advocating for community-based care for the mentally ill, featuring a green house outline and the slogan 'BACK YARDS NOT BACK WARDS FOR PEOPLE WITH MENTAL ILLNESS' in blue text. The slogan highlights the shift from institutionalized 'back wards' of state hospitals to residential living.

name: Boycott Coors
title: BOYCOTT COORS
subject: Labor Unions, Boycott, Coors Brewing Company
description: This button was produced by the Michigan State AFL-CIO to support the nationwide boycott of the Coors Brewing Company, which took place from 1977 to 1987 due to disputes over labor practices and union-busting.

name: Jobs with Peace
title: JOBS with PEACE
subject: Peace movement, Labor, Economic justice
description: This button is from the Jobs with Peace campaign, a nationwide initiative in the 1980s that advocated for redirecting federal military spending towards domestic needs such as employment, housing, and education. The design features a peace dove inside a industrial gear, representing the union of labor and peace activists.

name: A Woman's Place is in Her Union ; CWA (Communications Workers of America)
title: A Woman's Place Is In Her Union CWA
subject: Labor Unions, Women's Rights, Communications Workers of America
description: A circular purple button with white text that reads 'A Woman's Place Is In Her Union CWA'. The slogan is a play on the traditional phrase 'A woman's place is in the home' and was popularized by the Coalition of Labor Union Women (CLUW) in the mid-1970s to encourage women's involvement in labor unions like the Communications Workers of America (CWA).

name: Solidarity with the People of Guatemala ; U.R.N.G. (Unidad Revolucionaria Nacional Guatemalteca/Guatemalan National Revolutionary Unity)
title: SOLIDARITY WITH THE PEOPLE OF GUATEMALA U.R.N.G.
subject: Guatemalan Civil War, URNG, Solidarity
description: A political button showing solidarity with the Guatemalan people and the URNG (Unidad Revolucionaria Nacional Guatemalteca), a coalition of guerrilla groups active during the civil war. The design features a quetzal bird and profiles of people.

name: I'm Marching on June 12
title: I'm marching on JUNE 12
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Anti-Nuclear Protest, Peace
description: This button was created for the anti-nuclear rally held on June 12, 1982, in New York City. The event is one of the largest political demonstrations in United States history, with an estimated one million people gathering in Central Park to advocate for nuclear disarmament and an end to the Cold War arms race.

name: International Workers' Holiday ; Jobs...Peace...Equality ; 18886-1986 May Day ; Made in the U.S.A.
title: INTERNATIONAL WORKERS' HOLIDAY 1886-1986 MAY DAY MADE IN U.S.A. JOBS...PEACE...EQUALITY
subject: Labor movement, May Day, Racial equality
description: This button commemorates the 100th anniversary of May Day (1886-1986), also known as International Workers' Holiday. It features a handshake between a white hand and a Black hand over a globe, symbolizing international labor solidarity. The text highlights demands for jobs, peace, and equality.

name: MASS Mental Give Away? No WAY!
title: MASS MENTAL GIVE AWAY? NO WAY!
subject: Mental Health; Privatization; Healthcare
description: This button was used to protest the sale or 'giveaway' of the land and facilities of the Massachusetts Mental Health Center (MMHC) in Boston to private entities like Partners Healthcare in the early 2000s.

name: Liberty & Union ; ILGWU (International Ladies' Garment Workers' Union)
title: LIBERTY & UNION ILGWU
subject: Labor unions, ILGWU, Apparel industry
description: White circular button featuring a red illustration of the Statue of Liberty's head within a blue scalloped border. Blue text reads "LIBERTY & UNION" above and "ILGWU" below the central image.

name: A Partnership ; Community Services ; Working - Serving - Giving ; AFL-CIO (American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations)
title: A PARTNERSHIP
subject: Labor Unions, United Way, Community Service
description: A rectangular blue and gold lapel pin commemorating the partnership between the United Way and the AFL-CIO's Department of Community Services. The pin displays the logos of both organizations.

name: Jobs with Justice
title: JOBS WITH JUSTICE
subject: Labor rights, Economic justice, Workers rights
description: A blue button with the text 'JOBS WITH JUSTICE' in white. It represents the Jobs with Justice national campaign, which was founded in 1987 to advocate for workers' rights and economic justice.

name: Saint Gobain/Norton ; Stop Union Busting
title: Saint Gobain/Norton STOP UNION BUSTING
subject: Labor unions, Union busting, Manufacturing
description: An octagonal red button with white text advocating against union busting at Saint Gobain/Norton, featuring a union bug for Teamsters Local 445.

name: Jobs Peace Equality  ; Dump Reagan ; May Day '84
title: JOBS • PEACE • EQUALITY DUMP REAGAN MAY DAY '84
subject: Anti-Reagan, nuclear disarmament, labor rights
description: A red anti-Reagan political button from May Day 1984 featuring a caricature of Ronald Reagan being kicked while holding a nuclear missile. It advocates for 'Jobs, Peace, Equality' and includes the text 'Dump Reagan'.

name: CWA ; Pay Equity... I'm Worth It (Communications Workers of America Union)
title: CWA PAY EQUITY... I'm Worth It.
subject: Labor Unions, Gender Equality, Pay Equity
description: A blue circular button with white text that reads 'CWA PAY EQUITY... I'm Worth It.', produced by the Communications Workers of America to advocate for equal pay.

name: 99 AFL-CIO Hospital Division (American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations)
title: HOSPITAL DIVISION 1199 AFL-CIO
subject: Labor Unions, Healthcare Workers, 1199
description: A blue and white button from the 1199 Hospital Division, which became part of the AFL-CIO after 1955. The button features icons representing the medical and pharmaceutical professions, reflecting the union's history as a pharmacy workers' union before expanding to represent hospital workers.

name: Local 509 SEUI AFL-CIO Together (Service Employees International Union American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations)
title: local 509 seiu afl-cio together
subject: Labor unions, SEIU, Workers' rights
description: A yellow semicircular button for SEIU Local 509, featuring the text "local 509 seiu afl-cio" and the word "together" in blue. This local represents human service workers and educators in Massachusetts.

name: May Day ; Peace Jobs Equality ; Celebrate the Struggle for the 8 Hour Day ; 1886 Union Square NYC May 1st 1985
title: PEACE JOBS EQUALITY MAY DAY CELEBRATE THE STRUGGLE FOR THE 8 HOUR DAY 1886 • UNION SQUARE • NYC • MAY 1st • 1985
subject: Labor Rights, May Day, Union Square
description: A 1985 button commemorating May Day and the struggle for the eight-hour workday, featuring a globe graphic and listing Union Square in NYC as the location of the celebration.

name: 99 Delegate ; AFL-CIO Hospital DIvision (American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations)
title: 1199 DELEGATE AFL-CIO HOSPITAL DIVISION
subject: Labor unions; Healthcare; Workers' rights
description: This blue and white button identifies a delegate of the Hospital Division for the 1199 union, affiliated with the AFL-CIO. The button features icons of a mortar and pestle and a caduceus, representing the pharmaceutical and medical professions within the union.

name: Como Siempre
title: Como Siempre
subject: Labor, United Farm Workers, Civil Rights
description: A red pin-back button featuring the United Farm Workers (UFW) black eagle logo and the Spanish phrase 'Como Siempre', meaning 'As Always'.

name: Local 420 Says Save Our City Hospitals
title: LOCAL 420 SAYS SAVE OUR CITY HOSPITALS
subject: Labor Unions, Healthcare, Public Hospitals
description: A button issued by AFSCME Local 420, representing New York City hospital workers, as part of a campaign to prevent the closure of municipal hospitals in the late 1970s and early 1980s.

name: Taft can mine it... Hartley can haul it... ; I Support the Miners
title: Taft can mine it . . . Hartley can haul it . . . I SUPPORT THE MINERS
person: Robert A. Taft; Fred A. Hartley Jr.
subject: Labor unions; Mining; Taft-Hartley Act
description: This button expresses opposition to the Taft-Hartley Act of 1947, which restricted the power and activities of labor unions. The slogan 'Taft can mine it... Hartley can haul it' was a popular refrain among striking coal miners and other union workers who felt the law was an attack on their rights.

name: 65 U.A.W.
title: 65 U.A.W. DISTRICT
subject: Labor unions, UAW, District 65
description: A green and white button for District 65 of the United Auto Workers union. District 65 was a progressive labor union representing workers in various sectors, primarily in New York City, that joined the UAW in 1987.

name: African National Congress ; South Africa
title: AFRICAN NATIONAL CONGRESS SOUTH AFRICA
subject: Anti-Apartheid, Liberation, ANC
description: A yellow circular button featuring a black silhouette of a warrior with a spear and shield in the center. Green text wraps around the border reading 'AFRICAN NATIONAL CONGRESS' at the top and 'SOUTH AFRICA' at the bottom. This imagery is associated with the Umkhonto we Sizwe, the military wing of the ANC.

name: Justice ; SEIU Local 509 (Service Employees International Union)
title: JUSTICE SEIU LOCAL 509
subject: Labor Unions, Social Justice, Human Services
description: Round white button featuring the word JUSTICE in red letters over a group of grey silhouettes. Below the silhouettes, text reads SEIU LOCAL 509. SEIU Local 509 is the Massachusetts union for human service workers and educators.

name: Grenada is Nobody's Backyard ; U.S. Out Now!
title: GRENADA IS NOBODYS' BACKYARD U.S. OUT NOW!
subject: Grenada invasion, Anti-war, US foreign policy
description: A yellow political button featuring the text 'GRENADA IS NOBODYS' BACKYARD U.S. OUT NOW!' The slogan 'Grenada is nobody's backyard' was famously used by Grenadian Prime Minister Maurice Bishop in 1979 to assert independence, but the specific call for 'U.S. OUT NOW!' identifies this as a protest button from the 1983 United States invasion of Grenada.

name: Stop Scabs
title: STOP SCABS
subject: Labor Unions, Strikes, Labor Rights
description: An octagonal red button designed to resemble a stop sign with white text reading 'STOP' and black text reading 'SCABS' printed over the bottom half. The term 'scab' refers to workers who cross picket lines during a strike.

name: Boycott South Africa not Nicaragua ; Clergy & Laity Concerned
title: BOYCOTT SOUTH AFRICA NOT NICARAGUA Clergy & Laity Concerned
subject: Apartheid, Nicaragua, Boycott
description: This button, produced by the social justice organization Clergy & Laity Concerned (CALC), advocates for economic pressure against South Africa to end apartheid while simultaneously opposing the United States' trade embargo against the Sandinista government in Nicaragua.

name: March Against Apartheid June 14 ; Remember Soweto
title: MARCH AGAINST APARTHEID JUNE 14 Remember Soweto
subject: Apartheid, Soweto, Activism
description: This yellow button was produced for the March Against Apartheid held on June 14, 1986, in New York City. The march was organized to mark the tenth anniversary of the Soweto Uprising (June 16, 1976) and to call for an end to apartheid in South Africa. The design features a graphic of broken shackles symbolizing liberation.

name: A Woman's Place is in Her Union
title: A WOMAN'S PLACE IS IN HER UNION
subject: women's rights, labor unions, feminism
description: A pinback button from the Coalition of Labor Union Women (CLUW) featuring their logo and the slogan 'A WOMAN'S PLACE IS IN HER UNION'. CLUW was founded in 1974 to promote the interests of women within the labor movement.

name: American Family Celebration Working for Change ; May 7, 1988 ; Be There
title: AMERICAN FAMILY CELEBRATION MAY 7, 1988 CLUW BE THERE WORKING FOR CHANGE
subject: Labor Unions, Family Rights, Women's Activism
description: A red pinback button for the 'American Family Celebration: Working for Change' rally held on May 7, 1988, in Washington, D.C., and sponsored by the Coalition of Labor Union Women (CLUW).

name: National Federation of Republican Women
title: NATIONAL FEDERATION OF REPUBLICAN WOMEN
subject: Republican women, political organization, NFRW
description: A circular enamel pin for the National Federation of Republican Women (NFRW) featuring an eagle with a quill pen perched on a ballot box. The pin includes a gold chain attached to a gavel-shaped charm, which typically designates a leadership role such as a club president within the organization.

name: Mim
title: MIM
subject: Maoism, Revolutionary, Internationalism
description: A circular button featuring the acronym 'MIM' in white sans-serif letters on a dark blue background, surrounded by a gold or brass-colored outer ring. 'MIM' refers to the Maoist Internationalist Movement, a revolutionary organization founded in the United States in 1983.

name: Erin Go Bragh
title: ERIN GO BRAGH
subject: Irish Heritage, St. Patrick's Day, Ireland
description: A white celluloid button with the text 'ERIN GO BRAGH' arched at the top. The central design features a large green shamrock with a winged maiden harp in the center. The shamrock is flanked by the United States flag on the left and a green flag with a harp on the right, representing Irish-American heritage.

name: [Image of Elephant with American Flag]
title: None
subject: Republican Party, Elephant, American Flag
description: A plastic button in a shield shape, featuring a blue graphic of an elephant, the symbol of the Republican Party, holding an American flag in its trunk.

name: Okay American You Bet - I'm With You ; Inauguration March 4, 1933 ; Our President Franklin D. Roosevelt
subject: Election, Campaign, 1980
description: The reverse side of a circular pinback button, featuring a repeating pattern of the year "1980" on a gray background, with a visible red, white, and blue rim.

name: Okay American You Bet - I'm With You ; Inauguration March 4, 1933 ; Our President Franklin D. Roosevelt
title: OKAY AMERICA INAUGURATION MARCH 4, 1933 OUR PRESIDENT FRANKLIN D. ROOSEVELT YOU BET-I'M WITH YOU
subject: Franklin D. Roosevelt, Presidential Inauguration, 1933
description: Red, white, and blue campaign-style button commemorating the first inauguration of Franklin D. Roosevelt on March 4, 1933. The button features a central portrait of Roosevelt with the U.S. Capitol dome superimposed on his head, set against a background of the Capitol building.

name: Reagan ; Bush '84
title: REAGAN BUSH '84
person: Ronald Reagan and George H.W. Bush
subject: Ronald Reagan, George H.W. Bush, 1984 Presidential Election
description: White button with red horizontal lines and the text REAGAN BUSH '84 in blue and red. This button supported the re-election of Ronald Reagan as President and George H.W. Bush as Vice President.

name: Nixon
