title: FOR NONVIOLENCE PEACE PLEDGE UNION
subject: Pacifism, Nonviolence, Peace
description: A black and white button from the Peace Pledge Union (PPU) featuring a stylized dove and the text 'FOR NONVIOLENCE'. The PPU is the oldest secular pacifist organization in Britain, advocating for the total abolition of war.

name: For Peace & Disarmament ; International Solidarity ; June 12
title: FOR PEACE & DISARMAMENT international solidarity JUNE 12
subject: Nuclear disarmament, Peace movement, Protest
description: A button advocating for nuclear disarmament and peace, featuring a peace symbol and the date June 12. This date corresponds to the large-scale protest held in New York City's Central Park in 1982.

name: Free at Last [Image of Nelson Mandela]
title: FREE AT LAST NELSON MANDELA
subject: Nelson Mandela, Anti-Apartheid, South Africa
description: A button celebrating the release of Nelson Mandela from prison in February 1990. It features Mandela's portrait against a background of black, green, and gold, the colors of the African National Congress (ANC).

name: From the River to the Sea, Palestine Will Be Free
title: from the river to the sea, PALESTINE will be free!
subject: Palestine, activism, liberation
description: A political button expressing support for Palestinian liberation, featuring the slogan 'from the river to the sea, PALESTINE will be free!' The design includes an assault rifle wrapped in a ribbon in the colors of the Palestinian flag, set against a colorful circular background. The website address for the International Solidarity Movement is visible at the bottom.

name: Free Palestine
title: Free Palestine
subject: Palestine, Middle East, Activism
description: A circular button featuring the Palestinian flag with the words "Free Palestine" written in black text across the white center stripe.

name: Long Live the Intifada
title: Long Live the Intifada
subject: Palestine, Intifada, Activism
description: A white circular button featuring the Palestinian flag in the center, with the slogan 'Long Live the Intifada' printed in black sans-serif font above and below the flag. This button was likely produced during or in support of the First Intifada which began in late 1987.

name: Enough Free Palestine Now!
title: ENOUGH FREE PALESTINE NOW!
subject: Palestine, Middle East conflict, Peace
description: A button advocating for the freedom of Palestine, featuring the colors of the Palestinian flag and a drop of blood dripping from the red section. The text reads 'ENOUGH FREE PALESTINE NOW!'.

name: No Justice No Peace
title: No Justice No Peace
subject: Palestine, Social Justice, Activism
description: A white circular button featuring the Palestinian flag in the center with the text 'No Justice' printed above and 'No Peace' printed below in black sans-serif font. The button is a piece of political activism expressing solidarity with the Palestinian cause and utilizing a popular slogan from civil rights movements.

name: Hasta La Victoria Siempre (Until Victory Always) ; Revolution Till Victory
title: HASTA LA VICTORIA SIEMPRE REVOLUTION TILL VICTORY!
subject: Revolution, Palestine, Cuba
description: This button features the portraits of revolutionary icons Che Guevara and Leila Khaled against the background of the Cuban and Palestinian flags. It displays the slogan "Hasta la victoria siempre" and its English translation, "Revolution till victory!" The organization's website, www.njsolidarity.org, refers to New Jersey Solidarity - Activists for the Liberation of Palestine, a group active in the early 2000s.

name: Stop Nuclear Arms Production ; SNAP
title: SNAP STOP NUCLEAR ARMS PRODUCTION FLATBUSH-MIDWOOD
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Anti-Nuclear Movement, Peace
description: A white political button featuring the acronym SNAP (Stop Nuclear Arms Production) written vertically in blue on the left and horizontally in red across the center. It includes a graphic of a falling bomb and specifies the 'FLATBUSH-MIDWOOD' chapter, which is a neighborhood in Brooklyn, NY. This button was likely produced for the 1982 nuclear freeze and disarmament movements.

name: Free Man [Image of Nelson Mandela]
title: FREE MAN
subject: Anti-Apartheid, Human Rights, Nelson Mandela
description: A button celebrating the release of Nelson Mandela from prison in 1990. It features a photograph of Mandela with a raised fist, an outline of the African continent, and the colors of the African National Congress (ANC).

name: Labor Says: Free South Africa!
title: LABOR SAYS: Free South Africa!
subject: Anti-apartheid, Labor unions, Solidarity
description: A political pinback button showing a raised fist with a broken shackle, representing the labor movement's support for ending apartheid in South Africa.

name: No To Apartheid ; Yale Divest
title: NO TO APARTHEID YALE DIVEST
subject: Apartheid, Divestment, Activism
description: A blue and white button from the movement at Yale University urging the school to divest from companies doing business with the apartheid regime in South Africa.

name: No! Nuclear Weapons ; Abolition of Nuclear Weapons Now ; 2004 World Congress to Abolish Nuclear Weapons
title: No! Nuclear Weapons いま、核兵器の廃絶を 原水爆禁止2004年世界大会
subject: nuclear weapons, disarmament, Japan
description: A teal and white button with text in both English and Japanese calling for the abolition of nuclear weapons. The text at the bottom identifies it as being from the 2004 World Conference against Atomic and Hydrogen Bombs.

name: Bush is Sharon's Poodle
title: carolmoore.net/buttons
subject: Libertarianism, Libertarian Party, Political activism
description: The reverse side of a political button featuring a small white sticker with the URL 'carolmoore.net/buttons'. Carol Moore is a well-known libertarian activist who produced and sold various political buttons through her website.

name: Bush is Sharon's Poodle
title: BUSH IS SHARON'S POODLE
subject: Foreign Policy; Middle East; Protest
description: A political button featuring a caricature of President George W. Bush's head on a poodle's body, with the text 'BUSH IS SHARON'S POODLE' criticizing his relationship with Israeli Prime Minister Ariel Sharon.

name: Easter 1988 ; Aldermaston
title: EASTER 1988 ALDERMASTON
subject: Peace, Nuclear Disarmament, Protest
description: A red button featuring a white dove and the Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament (CND) peace symbol, produced to commemorate the 1988 Easter protest march to the Atomic Weapons Research Establishment in Aldermaston, England.

name: Down with the Republikuds!
title: Down with the Republikuds!
subject: Israel, Republican Party, Foreign Policy
description: Anti-war or protest button criticizing the relationship between the US Republican Party and Israel's Likud Party, using the portmanteau 'Republikud'. The design features an Israeli flag motif with a Star of David.

name: Link Arms to End the Arms Race
title: LINK ARMS TO END THE ARMS RACE
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Peace Movement, Arms Race
description: A button advocating for an end to the nuclear arms race. It features a blue semi-circle with the words 'LINK ARMS' and a small bird icon, above a graphic of two hands joining. Green text around the bottom border reads 'TO END THE ARMS RACE'.

name: March for a Nuclear Free Britian ; April 25
title: MARCH FOR A NUCLEAR FREE BRITAIN APRIL 25th
subject: Nuclear Power, Environmentalism, Political Protest
description: A button for the March for a Nuclear Free Britain, which took place in London on April 25, 1987. The button features a cartoon of a two-headed sheep, referencing the concerns over radioactive contamination in British sheep following the Chernobyl disaster in 1986.

name: June 12 ; Stop the Arms Race
title: JUNE 12 STOP THE ARMS RACE
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Disarmament, Peace
description: Yellow button with red text 'JUNE 12 STOP THE ARMS RACE', a peace symbol, and a drawing of the United Nations headquarters. It refers to the massive anti-nuclear demonstration in New York City on June 12, 1982.

name: 50th Anniversary Of The March On Washington ; March On Washington For Jobs & Freedom ; August 28, 1963 ; August 28, 2013
title: 50th Anniversary March on Washington For Jobs & Freedom
subject: Civil Rights, Commemoration, Protest
description: Commemorative button for the 50th anniversary of the 1963 March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom, featuring the dates August 28, 1963 and August 28, 2013 and a graphic of two hands shaking.

name: Disarm Venus de Milo Did It
title: design: D. Lathrop & f. Gilbert / CUSTOM-MADE LARRY FOX BOX M VALLEY STREAM NY 11582 (516) 791-7929
subject: manufacturing, environmentalism, Earth Day
description: The back of a pinback button showing a pink circular sticker for Larry Fox, a custom button manufacturer. The yellow rim of the button includes a design credit to D. Lathrop and F. Gilbert, who designed environmental graphics and buttons for the first Earth Day in 1970.

name: Disarm Venus de Milo Did It
title: VENUS de MILO DID IT DISARM
subject: disarmament, peace, anti-nuclear
description: A circular yellow button with a black-and-white illustration of the Venus de Milo statue. The text "VENUS de MILO DID IT" is printed above the statue, and "DISARM" is printed below, creating a pun on the statue's missing arms to promote nuclear disarmament.

name: Lambeth Needs Jobs Not Bombs
title: LAMBETH NEEDS JOBS NOT BOMBS
subject: Anti-nuclear, Unemployment, Pacifism
description: A black pinback button with the text 'LAMBETH NEEDS JOBS NOT BOMBS'. This slogan was used by the Lambeth Council and activists in London during the early 1980s to protest against government spending on nuclear weapons while local employment suffered. It reflects the strong anti-nuclear and socialist political climate of the borough at the time.

name: No Nukes ; September 23
title: NO NUKES September 23
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Protest, Environment
description: An orange and black button featuring a stylized image of the Statue of Liberty and the text "NO NUKES September 23". This button was produced for the massive anti-nuclear rally held in New York City on September 23, 1979, which followed a series of "No Nukes" benefit concerts organized by Musicians United for Safe Energy (MUSE).

name: Protest...and Survive ; New York City ; June 11, 12, 14
title: PROTEST ... AND SURVIVE! New York City JUNE 11, 12, 14
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Protest, Disarmament
description: A pink button with black text and illustration. It displays the slogan "PROTEST ... AND SURVIVE!" above a cartoon of a woman kicking a nuclear missile. Below the illustration, it lists "New York City JUNE 11, 12, 14", referring to the massive 1982 anti-nuclear demonstrations in New York City.

name: Freeze the Arms Race
title: FREEZE THE ARMS RACE
subject: disarmament, peace, activism
description: A black and white circular button promoting the nuclear weapons freeze movement, featuring the slogan 'FREEZE THE ARMS RACE' and a graphic of hands forming a dove.

name: United Campuses to Prevent Nuclear War
title: UNITED CAMPUSES TO PREVENT NUCLEAR WAR
subject: Nuclear weapons, Disarmament, Student movements
description: Button featuring a red mushroom cloud with a black X over it and the text 'UNITED CAMPUSES TO PREVENT NUCLEAR WAR' along the bottom edge.

name: Aldermaston '68
title: ALDERMASTON '68
subject: anti-nuclear, peace movement, CND
description: A button from the 1968 Aldermaston March, a significant anti-nuclear weapons protest in the United Kingdom organized by the Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament (CND). The design features a peace sign overlaying a woman's portrait.

name: Stop Shorehan ; Oct. 7 ; Hauppauge
title: STOP SHOREHAM OCT. 7 HAUPPAUGE
subject: Nuclear Power, Activism, Environment
description: A button produced by the Long Island SHAD Alliance protesting the Shoreham Nuclear Power Plant. It advertises a protest event held on October 7, 1979, in Hauppauge, New York.

name: No More Hiroshimas
title: NO MORE HIROSHIMAS
subject: Nuclear disarmament, Peace movement, Anti-war
description: An anti-nuclear weapons protest button featuring a black background with the slogan 'NO MORE HIROSHIMAS' in red lettering. The button also includes an image of a yellow origami crane, which is a symbol of peace and a reminder of Sadako Sasaki, a victim of the Hiroshima bombing.

name: Jobs Not Bombs
title: JOBS NOT BOMBS
subject: Anti-war, Employment, Economics
description: A round blue button with white text reading 'JOBS NOT BOMBS.' This slogan was popularized during the late 20th century by peace and labor activists advocating for federal budget priorities to shift from military spending toward job creation and social services.

name: No Cruise [Teddy Bear in a Peace Sign T-Shirt]
title: NO CRUISE
subject: Peace Movement, Anti-Nuclear Protests, Cruise Missiles
description: A wooden cutout button in the shape of a teddy bear wearing a yellow shirt with a peace symbol and carrying a suitcase labeled 'NO CRUISE'. This button refers to the international protests against the deployment and testing of cruise missiles during the Cold War in the early 1980s.

name: The Nukes Stop Here ; March on Washington April 26, 1980
title: THE NUKE STOPS HERE MARCH ON WASHINGTON APRIL 26, 1980
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Protest, Environment
description: This green and white button features the dome of the U.S. Capitol building and was produced for the anti-nuclear protest 'March on Washington' held on April 26, 1980. The slogan 'The Nuke Stops Here' is a play on President Harry Truman's 'The Buck Stops Here'.

name: Take Back Seabrook ; Oct 6th
title: Teddy
subject: Theodore Roosevelt, 1912 Election, Reproduction
description: The reverse side of a reproduction Theodore Roosevelt campaign button. The front features a portrait of Roosevelt with the text 'Teddy'. This reproduction was produced by 'Buttons for the People' in 1981, likely for the American Political Items Collectors (APIC) National Convention. It includes a handwritten note on the reverse identifying the collector, Ralph Perroncello, and the date and location of the convention: Los Angeles, August 3, 1981.

name: Take Back Seabrook ; Oct 6th
title: TAKE BACK SEABROOK OCT. 6th
subject: Nuclear Power, Protest, Seabrook
description: A protest button referencing the 'Take Back Seabrook' demonstration against the Seabrook Station Nuclear Power Plant on October 6, 1979.

name: World Peace through Nuclear Disarmament
title: WAGES FOR HOUSEWORK
subject: Feminism, housework, labor rights
description: The reverse side of a pin-back button featuring a manufacturer's label for Mellow Crafts. The front side of the button is magenta and advocates for 'Wages for Housework', a global feminist movement founded in 1972 to recognize domestic labor as paid work.

name: World Peace through Nuclear Disarmament
title: WORLD PEACE through nuclear disarmament
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Peace Movement, Activism
description: A pink circular button with a stylized white dove and a small green dove, featuring the text 'WORLD PEACE through nuclear disarmament'.

name: Women Vote for SUuvival '84 ; WOmen's Action for Nuclear Disarmament
title: WOMEN VOTE FOR SURVIVAL '84 Women's Action for Nuclear Disarmament
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Voting, Women
description: A circular button from 1984 issued by Women's Action for Nuclear Disarmament (WAND). It features a stylized purple figure of a mother and child holding up the Earth, accompanied by a white dove with an olive branch. The border text reads 'WOMEN VOTE FOR SURVIVAL '84' and 'Women's Action for Nuclear Disarmament'.

name: Defend Britian Without the Bomb
title: DEFEND BRITAIN WITHOUT THE BOMB
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Peace Movement, United Kingdom
description: This button was produced by the Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament (CND) in the early 1980s. It features the peace symbol overlaid on the Union Jack, reflecting an argument for nuclear disarmament based on national defense.

name: Yes on 15 Nuclear Safety
title: Yes on Nuclear Safety 15
subject: Nuclear Power, Proposition 15, Environmentalism
description: A button supporting California's Proposition 15 in 1976, also known as the Nuclear Safeguards Act, which aimed to restrict nuclear power plant construction and operation until safety and waste disposal issues were addressed.

name: In Every War There's A Hiroshima Waiting to Happen
title: In Every War There's A Hiroshima Waiting To Happen
subject: Anti-nuclear, Peace movement, Hiroshima
description: Anti-nuclear protest button featuring green text that reads "In Every War There's A Hiroshima Waiting To Happen" alongside the Japanese characters for peace (平和).

name: Stop Diablo [Image of Diablo Canyon Power Plant]
title: STOP DIABLO
subject: nuclear energy, environmentalism, protest
description: An anti-nuclear power button from the late 1970s or early 1980s protesting the Diablo Canyon Power Plant in California, featuring a stylized sun and a cracked reactor dome.

name: Help De Kernwapens De Wereld Uit: Om Te Beginnen Uit Nederland; Help Elminate Nuclear Wearpons from the World : Starting with the Netherlands
title: HELP DE KERNWAPENS DE WERELD UIT: OM TE BEGINNEN UIT NEDERLAND
subject: nuclear disarmament, peace movement, activism
description: A Dutch anti-nuclear protest button featuring the slogan 'Help de kernwapens de wereld uit: om te beginnen uit Nederland' (Help get nuclear weapons out of the world: starting with the Netherlands). It depicts a group of people carrying away a large missile silhouette. This slogan was the centerpiece of the Interkerkelijk Vredesberaad (IKV) campaign launched in 1977.

name: Nuclear Weapon Free Chicago
title: NUCLEAR WEAPON FREE CHICAGO
subject: anti-nuclear movement, disarmament, social activism
description: A white button with the text 'NUCLEAR WEAPON FREE CHICAGO' around the border. In the center is a black silhouette of a bomb with a red 'prohibited' symbol (circle with a slash) over it.

name: Peace is Disarming.
title: Peace is disarming.
subject: peace, disarmament, anti-nuclear
description: A circular white pinback button with the slogan 'Peace is disarming.' printed in green at the bottom. Above the text is a blue illustration of a group of people carrying a large bomb or missile on their shoulders, suggesting a collective effort toward nuclear disarmament.

name: First Strike You're Out! ; [Image of Uncle Sam at Bat, Ball is a Bomb]
title: FIRST STRIKE YOU'RE OUT!
subject: Nuclear Weapons, Peace Movement, Cold War
description: A circular button featuring a cartoon illustration of Uncle Sam dressed in his iconic striped trousers and top hat, holding a baseball bat and preparing to hit a descending nuclear missile. The right side of the button is a solid red vertical band with the text 'FIRST STRIKE YOU'RE OUT!' in white. This button was used as a protest against nuclear first-strike capabilities and the Cold War arms race during the 1980s.

name: I'm Marching on June 12
title: I'm marching on JUNE 12
subject: anti-nuclear, disarmament, protest
description: Circular green button with diagonal blue stripes and white hand-written style text. The text reads 'I'm marching on JUNE 12'. This button was produced for the June 12, 1982 anti-nuclear rally in Central Park, New York City, which was one of the largest political demonstrations in United States history.

name: The Bomb Stops Here
title: THE BOMB STOPS HERE
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Peace symbol, Nuclear disarmament
description: A white button with the phrase 'THE BOMB STOPS HERE' in black capital letters. The 'O' in 'STOPS' is a red peace sign.

name: Write in Dick Greogry President ; Peace and Freedom
title: Write in DICK GREGORY PRESIDENT PEACE AND FREEDOM
subject: Presidential elections, Peace and Freedom Party, Activism
description: A campaign button for comedian and civil rights activist Dick Gregory's 1968 write-in presidential campaign. Gregory was associated with the Peace and Freedom Party in several states during that election cycle.

name: No Draft No Nukes ; Occupy Seabrook ; May 24
title: MAY 24 NO DRAFT NUKES OCCUPY SEABROOK
subject: Anti-nuclear, Anti-draft, Protest
description: Button supporting the occupation of the Seabrook nuclear power plant site on May 24, 1980, while also expressing opposition to the military draft.

name: Together We Can Stop the Bomb ; CND (Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament ; 24 October
title: TOGETHER WE CAN STOP THE BOMB 24 OCTOBER
subject: nuclear disarmament, peace movement, protest
description: A pinback button for the Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament (CND) featuring the slogan 'Together We Can Stop the Bomb' and the date '24 October', most likely referring to the massive anti-nuclear demonstration held in Hyde Park, London, on October 24, 1981.

name: Peace is Everybody's Business ; Candian Peace Congress ; 1981
title: CANADIAN PEACE CONGRESS PEACE IS EVERYBODY'S BUSINESS 1981
subject: Peace, Activism, Canada
description: A round yellow button featuring a red globe graphic. Around the top edge, the text 'CANADIAN PEACE CONGRESS' is printed in red. The center of the button features the text 'PEACE IS EVERYBODY'S BUSINESS' in bold green letters, and the year '1981' is printed in red at the bottom.

name: Stop Cruise & Pershing II ; Greenham Common - Comiso - Seneca - Tornoto - Berlin - Puget Sound... ; Peace Camps
title: Greenham Common • Comiso • Seneca • Toronto • Berlin • Puget Sound... STOP CRUISE & PERSHING II PEACE CAMPS
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Peace Camps, Pacifism
description: A tan button with green and red text and graphics. It features two tents, one with a combined peace and female symbol, representing women-led peace camps. The button lists several international locations of these camps and calls to stop the deployment of Cruise and Pershing II missiles.

name: Mobilise for Survival ; Stop Nuclear Madness
title: MOBILISE FOR SURVIVAL STOP NUCLEAR MADNESS
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Peace, Nuclear disarmament
description: A circular button with a white background featuring a green silhouette of a bomb being broken by a white dove. Red text around the border reads 'MOBILISE FOR SURVIVAL STOP NUCLEAR MADNESS'. This button was likely produced by the Australian anti-nuclear coalition Mobilise for Survival in the late 1970s or early 1980s.

name: Beijing ; Australia ; 1995 [Image of Peace Dove]
title: BEIJING 1995 AUSTRALIA
subject: Women's Rights, Beijing Conference, Australia
description: A white button featuring a purple bird with a female symbol and a sprig of golden wattle, created for the Australian delegation to the 1995 World Conference on Women in Beijing.

name: The Contiental Walk for Disarmament & Social Justice
title: THE CONTINENTAL 1976 WALK FOR DISARMAMENT & SOCIAL JUSTICE
subject: Disarmament, Social Justice, Peace
description: A button from the 1976 Continental Walk for Disarmament and Social Justice, a cross-country protest march that began in San Francisco in January and ended in Washington, D.C. in October to advocate for nuclear disarmament and redirecting military spending toward social needs.

name: Disarm for Peace
title: Disarm for Peace
subject: Disarmament, Peace, Anti-nuclear
description: A circular button with 'Disarm for Peace' in green text. The central graphic features a white dove holding an olive branch superimposed on a blue image of a broken missile, symbolizing the peace movement and nuclear disarmament.

name: Human Race or Nuclear Race ; Britian Can Make a Difference
title: HUMAN RACE OR NUCLEAR RACE BRITAIN CAN MAKE A DIFFERENCE OCT 26
subject: nuclear disarmament, peace movement, CND
description: An anti-nuclear protest button produced for a Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament (CND) rally held in London on October 26, 1985. The design features a yellow peace symbol background with the slogan 'Human Race or Nuclear Race' and silhouettes of people along the bottom.

name: Basingstoke Organization for Nuclear Disarmament ; Bond
title: BASINGSTOKE ORGANISATION FOR NUCLEAR DISARMAMENT BOND
subject: nuclear disarmament, peace, BOND
description: A circular pin-back button for the Basingstoke Organisation for Nuclear Disarmament (BOND). The button features white text and a stylized peace dove inside a circular emblem, supported by a graphic representing many hands or voices, all on a black background.

name: [Shield with Palestinian Flag and Shape of Palestine Outlined]
title: None
subject: Palestine, Nationalism, Geography
description: A shield-shaped metal pin featuring the outline of the map of Palestine over the pan-Arab colors of red, green, black, and white, representing the Palestinian flag.

name: Beware Faulty Goods [Image of Cracked Nuclear Power Plant]
title: BEWARE FAULTY GOODS
subject: nuclear power, environmentalism, safety
description: An anti-nuclear power button depicting a cracked nuclear reactor containment dome with a radiation symbol and the warning 'BEWARE FAULTY GOODS'.

name: Peace ; мир
title: PEACE МИР
subject: Peace, Cold War, Diplomacy
description: Circular wooden button featuring a white dove of peace on a blue background, surrounded by a light blue border with the words PEACE and МИР (Russian for peace).

name: Nuclear Power ; Over My Dead Body
title: NUCLEAR POWER? OVER MY DEAD BODY
subject: Anti-nuclear power, Nuclear energy, Protest
description: An anti-nuclear power button featuring an orange skull whose base is shaped like a mushroom cloud, set against a black background. The text 'NUCLEAR POWER? OVER MY DEAD BODY' surrounds the image in a circular border.

name: President Ford
title: President Ford
subject: Gerald Ford, 1976 Election, Presidential Campaign
description: Two pink circular stickers on a single white backing sheet. The text on the stickers reads 'President Ford' in bold white letters. Fine print along the bottom edge identifies the 'President Ford Committee', with James A. Baker, III as Chairman and Royston C. Hughes as Treasurer.

name: Ford ; Dole
title: Ford Dole
subject: 1976 Election, Republican Party, Ford and Dole
description: A campaign sticker from the 1976 U.S. presidential election for the Republican ticket of Gerald Ford and Bob Dole.

name: For President HUMPHREY ; The Peoples Democrat
title: For President HUMPHREY THE PEOPLES DEMOCRAT
subject: Presidential Campaign, Hubert Humphrey, Democratic Party
description: A campaign button for Hubert Humphrey's presidential run, featuring the text 'For President HUMPHREY THE PEOPLES DEMOCRAT' on a blue and red background.

name: Moynihan
title: Moynihan
subject: US Senate, New York, Election
description: A square red, white, and blue political campaign sticker for Daniel Patrick Moynihan's United States Senate campaign.

name: Obama ; Biden
title: OBAMA BIDEN
subject: Barack Obama, Joe Biden, 2008 Presidential Election
description: A blue circular campaign button or sticker featuring the names of Barack Obama and Joe Biden, the Obama 'O' sunrise logo, and the official campaign website URL for the 2008 US Presidential Election.

name: [image of Hillary Clinton symbol]
title: hillaryclinton.com
subject: Presidential Campaign, Hillary Clinton, 2016
description: Circular blue button featuring the 2016 Hillary Clinton presidential campaign logo, a white letter H with a red arrow pointing right, above the website address hillaryclinton.com.

name: Amigos de Cuomo
title: Amigos de Cuomo
subject: Mario Cuomo, Election, Spanish
description: A blue rectangular sticker or button graphic featuring the white text 'Amigos de Cuomo' and seven white stars along the bottom. This was a common campaign slogan used by Mario Cuomo to reach out to Hispanic voters during his tenure as Governor of New York.

name: 1199 Seiu for Andrew Cuomo
title: 1199 SEIU for ANDREW CUOMO
subject: Labor Unions, Elections, Endorsements
description: A campaign sticker for Andrew Cuomo's gubernatorial run in New York, featuring an endorsement from the 1199 SEIU (Service Employees International Union) United Healthcare Workers East. The sticker uses the union's signature purple and yellow color scheme.

name: Defend Transgender Protections! ; freedom for all ; Massachusetts [image of rainbow with transgender flag colors]
title: DEFEND TRANSGENDER PROTECTIONS!
subject: transgender rights, civil rights, referendum
description: A button from the Freedom for All Massachusetts campaign urging voters to defend the state's transgender anti-discrimination law during the 2018 Question 3 referendum.

name: Gren Stanton ; For Congress
title: GREG STANTON FOR CONGRESS
subject: Arizona, US House, Democrat
description: Campaign sticker for Greg Stanton's run for the United States House of Representatives in Arizona.

name: Kobach ; U.S. Senate; The Consistent Conservative
title: KOBACH U.S. SENATE THE CONSISTENT CONSERVATIVE
subject: Political Campaign, Election, Conservative
description: An oval campaign sticker for Kris Kobach's 2020 U.S. Senate campaign in Kansas. The sticker features the candidate's name, the office sought, and the slogan 'THE CONSISTENT CONSERVATIVE' on a blue background with an American flag icon.

name: Tom ; 2020
title: TOM 2020
subject: Presidential campaign, Tom Steyer, 2020 election
description: A circular button from Tom Steyer's 2020 presidential campaign. It features the name 'TOM' and '2020' repeated multiple times in various colors. The website tomsteyer.com is printed vertically along the left edge.

name: Tom ; 2020 [image of both texts repeated multiple times]
title: TOM 2020
subject: Tom Steyer, 2020 Presidential Campaign, Democratic Party
description: A circular button featuring a repeating pattern of the text "TOM" in blue and "2020" in orange on a white background, representing Tom Steyer's 2020 presidential campaign.

name: Shannon for U.S. Senate
title: SHANNON FOR U.S. SENATE
subject: Political campaign, Election, Senate
description: Purple campaign sticker for Shannon Liss-Riordan's 2020 run for the United States Senate from Massachusetts.

name: Theresa Greenfield ; For Iowa
title: GREENFIELDFORIOWA.COM THERESA GREENFIELD FOR IOWA
subject: campaign, Iowa, senate
description: Round sticker for Theresa Greenfield's 2020 bid for the United States Senate from Iowa. The sticker has a blue center and a green outer ring with the campaign website.

name: Elect Tom Zmich for US Congress ; Take back America
title: TAKE BACK AMERICA Elect Tom ZMICH for US Congress www.zmich2020.com
subject: Congress, Election, Republican
description: A red rectangular campaign sticker for Tom Zmich's 2020 run for U.S. Congress in New York, featuring the slogan "TAKE BACK AMERICA" inside a blue arrow pointing right.

name: Hale Yes ; Congress
title: HALE YES CONGRESS
subject: election, candidate, campaign
description: A campaign button for David Hale's run for the U.S. House of Representatives in 1994. It features a black semicircle on a white background with the punning slogan "HALE YES" in white, followed by "CONGRESS" and three stars in black.

name: Tom ; 2020
title: TOM 2020 tomsteyer.com
subject: Presidential Campaign, Tom Steyer, 2020
description: A campaign button for Tom Steyer's 2020 presidential run, featuring his name and campaign website on a blue background.

name: Curran for U.S. Senate ; Send a Sheriff to Washington D.C.
title: CURRAN for U.S. SENATE Send a Sheriff to Washington D.C.
subject: Law enforcement, Elections, Illinois
description: A campaign sticker for John 'Jack' Curran during his 1984 bid for the U.S. Senate in Illinois. Curran, a former sheriff of Lake County, utilized the slogan 'Send a Sheriff to Washington D.C.' and a green sheriff's star graphic to highlight his law enforcement background.

name: Zoe ; US Congress [image of a dream catacher]
title: ZOE US CONGRESS
subject: U.S. Congress, Election, Democrat
description: A blue and white campaign sticker for Zoe Lofgren's 1994 run for the U.S. House of Representatives, featuring a stylized logo for her name where the 'O' is replaced with a graphic containing a branch, arrow, stars, and feathers.

name: Kendra Horn ; Congress
title: Kendra Horn CONGRESS
subject: Politics, Elections, Oklahoma

title: TROUPES BRITANNIQUES HORS D'IRLANDE!! LES SOCIALISTES INTERNATIONAUX
subject: Ireland, Socialism, Anti-war
description: Green button featuring a map of Ireland and Great Britain with a silhouette of a riot police officer holding a baton over the British mainland. The French text translates to "British Troops Out of Ireland!!" and identifies the group "The International Socialists." It includes a raised fist icon at the bottom.

name: Ad Hoc Committee for Irish Human Rights ; End British Terror in Ireland
title: AD HOC COMMITTEE FOR IRISH HUMAN RIGHTS END BRITISH TERROR IN IRELAND 212-338-0031
subject: Irish human rights, Northern Ireland, The Troubles
description: This pin-back button was produced by the New York-based Ad Hoc Committee for Irish Human Rights. It advocates for an end to British military and police presence in Northern Ireland during 'The Troubles,' featuring a green shamrock with blood dripping from its center.

name: Irish Northern Aid Committee ; Unite Ireland
title: IRISH NORTHERN AID COMMITTEE UNITE IRELAND
subject: Irish Nationalism, Unification, NORAID
description: This button was produced by the Irish Northern Aid Committee (NORAID), an American organization that provided support for Irish republicanism during the Troubles. It features a map of Ireland with Northern Ireland highlighted in orange and the message 'Unite Ireland'.

name: Smash H-Block! ; Politcal Status Now
title: SMASH H-BLOCK! Political status now
subject: Hunger strike, Irish Republicanism, H-Block
description: This button was produced as part of the campaign to secure political status for Irish Republican prisoners held in the H-Blocks of HM Prison Maze in Northern Ireland. The protest, which began with the blanket protest in 1976, escalated into hunger strikes in 1980 and 1981, most notably led by Bobby Sands. The button features a bearded man with a raised fist, a symbol of defiance and solidarity.

name: Unite Ireland
title: UNITE IRELAND IRISH NORTHERN AID
subject: Ireland, Nationalism, Republicanism
description: A green pin-back button with the text 'UNITE IRELAND' in large white block letters, and 'IRISH NORTHERN AID' in smaller white letters at the bottom. This button was produced by the Irish Northern Aid Committee (NORAID), an American-based organization that raised funds for the Irish republican cause during The Troubles.

name: Unity Against Internment ; Irish Anti-Interment Coalition
title: UNITY AGAINST INTERNMENT Irish Anti-Internment Coalition
subject: Northern Ireland, Internment, Civil Rights
description: A political protest button issued by the Irish Anti-Internment Coalition. It features the text 'UNITY AGAINST INTERNMENT' in green and depicts crossed lines of barbed wire in orange and green, symbolizing the opposition to the policy of internment without trial introduced in Northern Ireland in 1971.

name: T.F.U.I [Image of Ireland]
title: T.F.U.I.
subject: Irish reunification, trade unionism, labor activism
description: This button features a green map of Ireland with the text 'T.F.U.I.' below it, which stands for 'Trade Unionists for a United Ireland'. This organization was active in the late 1970s and 1980s, advocating for the reunification of Ireland through the lens of the trade union and labor movement.

name: Ireland Is One Country
title: IRELAND IS ONE COUNTRY
subject: Ireland, Nationalism, Reunification
description: Circular button with three horizontal stripes of green, white, and orange, mimicking the Irish flag. The text reads 'IRELAND IS ONE COUNTRY' across the stripes.

name: Ireland ; England's Vietnam
title: IRELAND england's vietnam
subject: The Troubles, Ireland, Vietnam War
description: A political button comparing the British military presence in Northern Ireland to the Vietnam War. It features a red map of Ireland and a black silhouette of a hand holding an AK-47 on a green background.

name: Putting The Country First ; Fine Gael
title: PUTTING THE COUNTRY FIRST FG FINE GAEL
subject: Ireland, Fine Gael, general election
description: A campaign button for the Fine Gael political party in Ireland, featuring their 1981 general election slogan 'Putting the Country First'.

name: Give Ireland A Hand ; Boycott British Goods
title: Give Ireland a Hand Boycott British Goods
subject: Irish independence, Boycott, British-Irish relations
description: A button advocating for Irish independence and a boycott of British goods. The image shows a map of Ireland with Northern Ireland colored red and the rest green. A hand, with a cuff in the colors of the Irish flag, is removing the red part of the map. A sleeve with the Union Jack is being held back.

name: End British Torture in Ireland
title: END BRITISH TORTURE IN IRELAND
subject: The Troubles, Ireland, Human Rights
description: A political protest button featuring the text 'END BRITISH TORTURE IN IRELAND' in white and red against a black background. It likely refers to the interrogation techniques used by British security forces in Northern Ireland during the early 1970s which led to international legal proceedings.

name: Troops Out Now!
title: TROOPS OUT NOW!
subject: The Troubles, Northern Ireland, British Military
description: A green and black pinback button featuring the text "TROOPS OUT NOW!" below an illustration where the island of Great Britain is depicted as a riot police officer with a baton and shield, standing next to the island of Ireland. The button represents the campaign for the withdrawal of British troops from Northern Ireland during the Troubles.

name: Brits Out!
title: brits out !
subject: Irish Republicanism, Northern Ireland, Decolonization
description: This green button features the slogan 'brits out !' in black text at the bottom. Above the text is a black graphic parodying the 'Keep Britain Tidy' logo, depicting a figure throwing a British soldier in riot gear into a bin labeled 'EIRE'.

name: Remember Bobby Sands ; British Out of Ireland
title: REMEMBER BOBBY SANDS BRITISH OUT OF IRELAND YSA
subject: Irish republicanism, Bobby Sands, hunger strike
description: A button with vertical green, white, and orange stripes representing the Irish flag. It features black text that reads "REMEMBER BOBBY SANDS" along the top edge, "BRITISH OUT OF IRELAND" in the center, and "YSA" (for the Young Socialist Alliance) at the bottom. The button memorializes Bobby Sands, an Irish republican who died during a hunger strike in 1981.

name: Ireland 32
title: IRELAND 32
subject: Irish reunification, United Ireland, Irish nationalism
description: A circular button featuring the Irish tricolor (green, white, and orange) and an outline map of Ireland. The text 'IRELAND 32' refers to the political aim of reuniting all 32 counties of Ireland into a single state.

name: [Union Jack with "No" Symbol Over It]
title: © COPYRIGHT 1996 PRODUCTS OF VISION, 14417 CHASE ST. #166 PANORAMA CITY, CA 91402 (818) 894-9149
subject: Manufacturing, 1996, Buttons
description: The reverse side of a circular button featuring a manufacturer's sticker for 'Products of Vision' in Panorama City, California, dated 1996.

name: [Union Jack with "No" Symbol Over It]
title: [Prohibition sign over Union Jack]
subject: Northern Ireland, Irish Republicanism, Anti-British
description: A button featuring a green universal prohibition sign (a circle with a diagonal slash) over the Union Jack, the flag of the United Kingdom. This imagery was commonly used by Irish Republican groups to express 'Brits Out' sentiment during the Troubles in Northern Ireland.

name: Off!
title: off!
subject: Anti-police, Civil Rights, Black Panther Party
description: A circular green button featuring the word 'off!' in stylized black script. It depicts two black silhouettes: one of a pig dressed in a police uniform holding a baton and another smaller pig figure, referencing the Black Panther Party slogan 'Off the pig'.

name: There's No Excuse ; Wife Assault ; It Is A Crime
title: There's no excuse WIFE ASSAULT It is a Crime
subject: domestic violence, social awareness, law
description: A pink diamond-shaped button with a black band produced by the Ontario government in the 1980s as part of a public awareness campaign stating that wife assault is a crime.

name: Fight The Attacks ; No Turning Back! ; International Women's Day 1990
title: Fight the Attacks No Turning Back! INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY 1990
subject: Feminism, Women's rights, Activism
description: An oval red button featuring three white brush-stroke banners with the slogans "Fight the Attacks" and "No Turning Back!" in black text. Along the bottom edge, the text "INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY 1990" is printed in white.

name: ["No" Symbol over Wife Assault] Wife Assault ; No Man Has The Right
title: Wife Assault No Man Has the Right
subject: domestic violence, women's rights, public awareness
description: Rectangular sticker from the 1980s Ontario government campaign 'No Man Has the Right' aimed at preventing wife assault. It features a red prohibition symbol over the words 'WIFE ASSAULT'.

name: Coyote! ; My Ass is Mine...
title: COYOTE MY ASS IS MINE!
subject: sex worker rights, COYOTE, bodily autonomy
description: This button was produced by the sex workers' rights organization COYOTE (Cast Off Your Old Tired Ethics), which was founded by Margo St. James in San Francisco in 1973. The slogan "My Ass Is Mine!" was a prominent rallying cry for the decriminalization of prostitution and the assertion of individual rights over one's own body.

name: Coyote! ; My Ass is Mine...
title: COYOTE! MY ASS IS MINE...
subject: Sex Workers Rights, Feminism, COYOTE
description: A button produced by COYOTE (Call Off Your Old Tired Ethics), an organization advocating for sex workers' rights founded in 1973 by Margo St. James. The button features a graphic of female anatomy and a slogan emphasizing bodily autonomy.

name: Oh, So That Explains The Difference In Our Salaries
title: Oh, so that explains the difference in our salaries!
subject: pay equity, feminism, gender equality
description: A yellow button featuring a black and white illustration of a male and female toddler looking into their diapers with the caption 'Oh, so that explains the difference in our salaries!', satirizing the gender pay gap.

name: Stop Rape
title: STOP RAPE
subject: Sexual Violence Prevention, Women's Rights, Social Activism
description: A circular button featuring a red hand symbol with the words 'STOP RAPE' in white text across the middle. This pinback was likely produced as part of the anti-rape and sexual assault awareness movements that gained momentum during the 1970s and 1980s.

name: International Women's Day ; March 5, 1983
title: INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY MARCH 5, 1983
subject: International Women's Day, Feminism, Activism
description: This button commemorates International Women's Day, dated March 5, 1983. It depicts an illustration of a woman in a red shirt holding a large purple flag.

name: In Commemoration Of The 14 Women Killed In Montreal, December 1989 ; En Commemoration Des 14 Femmes Assasinees A Montreal le 6 Decembre 1989
title: In commemoration of the 14 women killed in Montreal, December 6, 1989
subject: Montreal Massacre, Violence against women, École Polytechnique
description: A commemorative button with a red rose, created in memory of the fourteen victims of the massacre at École Polytechnique in Montreal on December 6, 1989. The text is in both English and French.

name: "If I Can't Dance"...  I Don't Want To Be Part Of Your Revolution ; Emma Goldman
title: "IF I CAN'T DANCE..." I DON'T WANT TO BE PART OF YOUR REVOLUTION Emma Goldman
subject: Anarchism, Feminism, Revolution
description: A button featuring a portrait of Emma Goldman and a famous paraphrased quote attributed to her: "If I can't dance, I don't want to be part of your revolution."

name: Another Man Against Violence Against Women
title: ANOTHER MAN AGAINST VIOLENCE AGAINST WOMEN
subject: violence against women, men's movement, feminism
description: A circular button divided horizontally; the top half is white with black text that reads "ANOTHER MAN AGAINST" and the bottom half is purple with white text that reads "VIOLENCE AGAINST WOMEN".

name: Keep Your Laws Off My Body
title: KEEP YOUR LAWS OFF MY BODY
subject: abortion rights, reproductive rights, bodily autonomy
description: A button with blue text on a light background reading 'KEEP YOUR LAWS OFF MY BODY'. This slogan emerged as a prominent rallying cry for the reproductive rights and feminist movements in the United States during the late 1960s and early 1970s.

name: Tribunal International Des Crimes Contre La Femme ; Palais Des Congres ; Bruxelles 4-8 Mars 76
title: TRIBUNAL INTERNATIONAL DES CRIMES CONTRE LA FEMME PALAIS DES CONGRES BRUXELLES 4-8 MARS 76
subject: feminism, international law, women's rights
description: A button promoting the International Tribunal on Crimes Against Women, which took place in Brussels, Belgium, in March 1976.

name: Fighting Racism and Sexism Together! ; International Women's Day ; March 7, 1987
title: FIGHTING RACISM AND SEXISM TOGETHER! INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY MARCH 7, 1987
subject: Feminism, Anti-racism, International Women's Day
description: A square blue button featuring a series of pink and white horizontal stripes. In the center is a circle representing the head of a Venus symbol, containing stylized figures of four people of diverse backgrounds and physical abilities, including one in a wheelchair. The text around the symbol reads 'FIGHTING RACISM AND SEXISM TOGETHER!' and at the bottom 'INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY MARCH 7, 1987'.

name: A Single Rape Violates All Women
title: A SINGLE RAPE VIOLATES ALL WOMEN
subject: feminism, sexual violence, women's rights
description: A round blue button with gold text that reads 'A SINGLE RAPE VIOLATES ALL WOMEN' and a gold Venus symbol on the left side.

name: Action Coalition for the ERA ; Vote ERA '76 ; Massachusetts Referendum
title: Action Coalition for the ERA vote ERA '76 Massachusetts Referendum
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, Women's Rights, Political Campaign
description: A blue button with white lettering that reads 'Action Coalition for the ERA vote ERA '76 Massachusetts Referendum'. This button was part of the campaign to pass a state-level Equal Rights Amendment in Massachusetts, which successfully passed on November 2, 1976.

name: Never Another Battered Woman
title: NEVER ANOTHER BATTERED WOMAN
subject: Domestic Violence, Women's Rights, Feminism
description: A black and white button advocating against domestic violence. It features the text 'NEVER ANOTHER BATTERED WOMAN' in a semi-circle at the top. The central graphic shows the face and shoulders of a woman with a jagged lightning bolt-shaped crack running vertically through the middle of her face.

name: Harcèlement Sexuel Pas Question! ; Au Bas de 'Echelle - Rank and File
title: Harcèlement Sexuel Pas Question!
subject: sexual harassment, labor rights, feminism
description: A green button with pink text that reads 'Harcèlement Sexuel Pas Question!' (Sexual Harassment No Way!). The bottom edge includes the name of the organization 'Au Bas de l'Echelle - Rank and File'. This button was part of a major campaign launched in the early 1980s by the Montreal-based non-profit Au Bas de l'échelle to raise awareness about sexual harassment in the workplace, particularly for non-unionized workers.

name: Women Unite to Fight Racism Sexism Economic INequality ; International Women's Day ; Toronto,1988
title: Women Unite to Fight racism sexism economic inequality INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY TORONTO, 1988
subject: feminism, racism, economic inequality
description: White button with pink, purple, and teal text and graphics produced for International Women's Day 1988 in Toronto. The graphics show three people holding streamers that lead to a Venus symbol containing a globe.

name: Women's Political Caucus
title: WOMEN'S POLITICAL CAUCUS
subject: Women's Rights, Political Activism, Feminism
description: A red button for the Women's Political Caucus featuring a white graphic of the Statue of Liberty holding a ballot box instead of a torch, symbolizing the power of women's votes.

name: Women Unite Take Back the Night
title: WOMEN UNITE TAKE BACK THE NIGHT
subject: feminism, sexual violence, activism
description: A circular purple button with white text reading 'WOMEN UNITE TAKE BACK THE NIGHT' and a graphic of two hands joined to look like a bird. The button represents the Take Back the Night movement, which began in the 1970s to protest sexual violence.

name: E.R.A. Yes
title: E.R.A. YES
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, Women's Rights, Feminism
description: A round yellow button with black text reading 'E.R.A. YES', advocating for the ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment to the United States Constitution during the 1970s and early 1980s.

name: DEMS for ERA
title: DEMS FOR ERA
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, Women's Rights, Democratic Party
description: A circular red button featuring the text 'DEMS FOR ERA' in white. This button indicates the support of members of the Democratic Party for the ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment (ERA).

name: E.R.A. in '76! ; Vote Socialist Workers
title: E.R.A. in '76! Vote Socialist Workers
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, Women's Rights, Socialist Workers Party
description: This light blue button features a white female symbol with the text 'E.R.A. in '76!' inside and 'Vote Socialist Workers' to the left. It advocates for the ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment and supports the Socialist Workers Party in the 1976 election.

name: ERA. ; All People Are Created Equal.
title: ERA. All people are created equal.
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, Feminism, Women's Rights
description: A white button featuring the slogan "ERA. All people are created equal." in blue and red text, promoting the ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment.

name: People of Faith for ERA
title: People of Faith FOR ERA
subject: Equal Rights Amendment, religion, feminism
description: Yellow and blue button advocating for the Equal Rights Amendment (ERA) on behalf of religious individuals and organizations.

name: Lesbian Rights are Women's RIghts
title: Lesbian Rights are Women's Rights
subject: lesbian rights, feminism, civil rights
description: A circular purple button with white text that reads "Lesbian Rights are Women's Rights." In the center is an inverted white triangle containing the logo for the National Organization for Women (NOW).

name: Let's Face It... Women Are Smarter
title: LET'S FACE IT... WOMEN ARE SMARTER
subject: Feminism, Women's Rights, Gender
description: A circular pink pin-back button featuring two black silhouettes of women's profiles facing one another. The text 'LET'S FACE IT...' is printed in black between the faces. Below the silhouettes, the word 'WOMEN' is printed in large purple block letters, and underneath that, 'ARE SMARTER' is printed in black.

name: Women Unite Take Back the Night
title: WOMEN UNITE TAKE BACK THE NIGHT
subject: Feminism, Women's Rights, Safety
description: A white circular pinback button for the Take Back the Night movement. It features black text reading "WOMEN UNITE" and "TAKE BACK THE NIGHT" around a central graphic of two female silhouettes kneeling back-to-back in grass under a crescent moon and star. The artwork includes a small signature and the date '80.

name: Support Your Local Feminist
title: SUPPORT YOUR LOCAL FEMINIST
subject: feminism, women's rights, activism
description: A round brown button with the slogan "SUPPORT YOUR LOCAL FEMINIST" printed in white block lettering across three lines.

name: ASP ; Alliance For the Safety of Prostitutes
title: A$P ALLIANCE FOR THE SAFETY OF PROSTITUTES
subject: Sex worker rights, Prostitution, Advocacy
description: A button for the Alliance for the Safety of Prostitutes (ASP), a sex worker rights advocacy group founded in Philadelphia in 1980. The logo features a stylized poppy flower and the acronym A$P.

name: International Women's Tribunal ; October 21-22 New York ; WONAAC (Women's National Abortion Action Coalition)
title: International Women's Tribunal October 21-22 New York WONAAC
subject: Reproductive Rights, Feminism, Activism
description: A pinback button for the International Women's Tribunal, which took place in New York City on October 21-22, 1972. The event was organized by the Women's National Abortion Action Coalition (WONAAC) to advocate for reproductive freedom and women's rights.

name: Women's Liberation
title: WOMEN'S LIBERATION
subject: feminism, women's rights, social movements
description: A tan circular pin featuring a dark brown Venus symbol with a raised fist inside. The text 'WOMEN'S LIBERATION' is printed in an arc at the bottom. This logo was a prominent symbol of the second-wave feminist movement in the late 1960s and 1970s.

name: Stop the War Against Women!
title: STOP THE WAR AGAINST WOMEN!
subject: Women's rights, Feminism, Social activism
description: A square advocacy button featuring the slogan 'STOP THE WAR AGAINST WOMEN!' split between a white top half with red text and a black bottom half with white text. A small union bug with the number 461 is at the bottom.

name: Wages for Housework
title: WAGES FOR HOUSEWORK
subject: Feminism, Labor, Housework
description: A white and red button advocating for the Wages for Housework movement. It features the Venus symbol with a hand grasping paper currency inside, surrounded by the text 'WAGES FOR HOUSEWORK'. The currency depicted includes symbols for both dollars and pounds, reflecting the international nature of the campaign launched in 1972.

name: "Failure is Impossible" ; Susan B. Anthony
title: "FAILURE IS IMPOSSIBLE." Susan B. Anthony
subject: Women's Rights, Suffrage, Feminism
description: A yellow button with red lettering featuring Susan B. Anthony's famous quote, 'Failure is impossible.' This quote was originally from her final public speech in 1906 regarding the women's suffrage movement and was later adopted as a slogan by modern feminist movements in the 20th century.

name: W76 ; Win With Women
title: DON'T BUY GRAPES
subject: boycott, labor, grapes
description: The reverse side of a United Farm Workers (UFW) 'Don't Buy Grapes' boycott button. The back features a metal pin, a union bug from the Allied Printing Trades Council, and a handwritten archival accession mark '1969 ERA 576'.

name: W76 ; Win With Women
title: W76 Win With Women
subject: women in politics, feminism, elections
description: White circular button with blue text 'W76 Win With Women'. The stylized 'W' and '76' represent a 1976 campaign focused on electing women to political office, likely affiliated with the National Women's Political Caucus.

name: A Woman's Right to Work
title: A WOMAN'S RIGHT TO WORK
subject: Women's rights, Employment, Feminism
description: A pink circular button with the text 'A WOMAN'S RIGHT TO WORK' in red. Behind the main text, smaller text repeats the words 'JUNE 21' and 'DEMONSTRATE' in a circular pattern, referring to a national demonstration held in London in 1980.

name: Why Not! ; 1975 International Women's Year
title: 1975 WHY NOT! INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S YEAR
subject: International Women's Year, Feminism, Women's rights
description: A red button with white text commemorating International Women's Year in 1975. The phrase "WHY NOT!" is prominently displayed in the center, with the letter 'O' replaced by a female symbol. The year "1975" is at the top, and "INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S YEAR" is written along the bottom edge.

name: Men Can Stop Rape
title: MEN can stop RAPE
subject: Sexual assault, Activism, Feminism
description: A white button with the slogan 'MEN can stop RAPE' printed on it. The words 'MEN' and 'RAPE' are in black, while 'can stop' is in red script. This button was used in awareness campaigns to involve men in the prevention of sexual violence.

name: Peace ; NOW ; National Organization for Women
title: PEACE NATIONAL ORGANIZATION FOR WOMEN
subject: Peace, Feminism, Activism
description: A circular button with a yellow background. The word 'PEACE' is prominently displayed in black, overlapping a stylized female gender symbol which is the logo of the National Organization for Women. Below it, 'NATIONAL ORGANIZATION FOR WOMEN' is printed in black text.

name: Porn is the Theory Rape is the Practice
title: PORN IS THE THEORY RAPE IS THE PRACTICE
subject: Feminism, Anti-pornography movement, Sexual violence
description: A circular tan button featuring the red-printed slogan 'PORN IS THE THEORY RAPE IS THE PRACTICE'. This phrase was coined by feminist author Robin Morgan in her 1974 essay and became a prominent slogan for the anti-pornography feminist movement during the late 1970s and early 1980s.

name: November 12th ; Mobilize for Women's Lives Across the USA & Washington, DC
title: NOVEMBER 12th Mobilize FOR WOMEN'S LIVES ACROSS THE USA & WASHINGTON, DC
subject: Abortion Rights, Women's Rights, Protest
description: This button was created for the 'Mobilize for Women's Lives' rallies held on November 12, 1989. Organized primarily by the National Organization for Women (NOW), these nationwide demonstrations advocated for reproductive freedom and abortion rights following the Supreme Court's decision in Webster v. Reproductive Health Services.

name: 59 Cents ["No" Symbol over 59 Cents]
subject: Gender pay gap, Women's rights, University of Illinois
description: A button featuring '59¢' with a diagonal line through it, superimposed over a light blue University of Illinois 'I' logo. The '59¢' with a slash was a common symbol used in the 1980s to protest the gender pay gap, specifically the statistic that women earned 59 cents for every dollar men earned. This specific button likely represents a local campaign or protest at the University of Illinois.

name: International Women's Day ; 1975 March 8
title: 1975 INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY MARCH 8
subject: Women's rights, Feminism, International Women's Day
description: A pink and red button commemorating International Women's Day on March 8, 1975, featuring a stylized multi-Venus symbol design.

name: We've Been Too Nice Too Long...
title: NOW We've been too nice too long.....
subject: feminism, women's rights, activism
description: A button featuring the logo of the National Organization for Women (NOW) and the slogan 'We've been too nice too long.....', reflecting the assertive stance of second-wave feminism.

name: NOW / National Organization for Women
title: NOW NATIONAL ORGANIZATION FOR WOMEN
subject: Women's Rights, Feminism, Equality
description: A circular black and white button featuring the stylized acronym 'NOW' and the text 'NATIONAL ORGANIZATION FOR WOMEN' below it.

name: NOW / National Organization for Women
title: NOW NATIONAL ORGANIZATION FOR WOMEN
subject: Feminism, Women's Rights, Advocacy
description: A black and white circular button featuring the stylized logo of the National Organization for Women (NOW) with the full name of the organization written below.

name: Refuse To Be Abused
title: REFUSE TO BE ABUSED
subject: Abuse, Domestic Violence, Activism
description: A purple pin with off-white block lettering that reads 'REFUSE TO BE ABUSED'.

name: La Voix Des Femmes ; Voice of Women ; Peace
title: LA VOIX DES FEMMES VOICE OF WOMEN, CANADA
subject: Women, Peace, Activism
description: A blue and white circular button for the Canadian Voice of Women for Peace. It features the organization's name in French and English with a central graphic of a dove, a female symbol, and an olive branch labeled 'peace'.

name: Stop Sexual Harassment
title: STOP SEXUAL HARASSMENT
subject: sexual harassment, social justice, women's rights
description: A magenta button with the phrase 'STOP SEXUAL HARASSMENT' printed in a hand-drawn style font. 'STOP' is in yellow, while the rest is in white, accompanied by a yellow underline.

name: March 31 International Day of Action ; Safe Contraception and Abortion ; NO orced Sterilization
title: MARCH 31 INTERNATIONAL DAY OF ACTION SAFE CONTRACEPTION AND ABORTION NO FORCED STERILIZATION
subject: Reproductive Rights, Abortion, Contraception
description: A green circular button with black text advocating for reproductive rights, specifically safe contraception and abortion, and opposing forced sterilization, associated with the International Day of Action on March 31, 1979, organized by the International Contraception, Abortion and Sterilisation Campaign (ICASC).

name: Freedom of Choice Now and Forever
title: FREEDOM OF CHOICE NOW AND FOREVER
subject: Reproductive Rights, Feminism, Women's Rights
description: A pink and light blue button featuring the slogan 'Freedom of Choice Now and Forever'. The word 'NOW' is stylized in the center, representing the National Organization for Women. The button was likely produced in the late 1980s or early 1990s during a period of increased activism for reproductive rights.

name: Vote Pro-Choice ; California Abortion Rights Action League
title: VOTE Pro-Choice 90 California Abortion Rights Action League
subject: abortion rights, pro-choice, voting
description: A white button with blue and red text promoting pro-choice candidates in the 1990 elections on behalf of the California Abortion Rights Action League.

name: National People's Campaign 212-633-6646 ; Pro Choice Women Fight Back
title: National People's Campaign 212-633-6646 PRO CHOICE WOMEN FIGHT BACK!
subject: Reproductive Rights, Women's Rights, Activism
description: A purple circular button with white text. It features the contact information for the National People's Campaign and the slogan 'WOMEN FIGHT BACK!' with 'PRO CHOICE' replacing the 'O' in 'WOMEN'. The National People's Campaign was an activist organization based in New York City.

name: Keep Your Laws off My Body
title: KEEP YOUR LAWS OFF MY BODY
subject: abortion, reproductive rights, feminism
description: A yellow circular button with green sans-serif text reading 'KEEP YOUR LAWS OFF MY BODY'. This slogan has been a key rally cry for reproductive rights and bodily autonomy since the late 1960s.

name: BC Coalition Clinics! For Abortion Clinics
title: BC COALITION CLINICS! FOR ABORTION CLINICS
subject: Abortion, Reproductive rights, Canada
description: A yellow pin-back button advocating for the establishment of abortion clinics in British Columbia, produced by the BC Coalition for Abortion Clinics, which was active during the 1980s campaign for reproductive healthcare access.

name: Speak Out for Choice on Abortion [Woman's Symbol in letter C]
title: Speak Out FOR CHOICE ON ABORTION
subject: abortion rights, pro-choice, feminism
description: A circular black button with teal text that reads 'Speak Out FOR CHOICE ON ABORTION'. The letter 'C' in 'CHOICE' is stylized to include a female symbol.

name: Students for Choice [Woman's Symbol in letter C]
title: Students for CHOICE
subject: abortion, feminism, students
description: A red pin-back button with the slogan "Students for CHOICE" in white, featuring a female symbol as part of the letter C.

name: Abortion - A Woman's Right ; Choice [Woman's Symbol in letter C]
title: ABORTION - A WOMAN'S RIGHT CHOICE
subject: abortion rights, feminism, reproductive health
description: A black circular button featuring white text. Around the top curve, it says 'ABORTION - A WOMAN'S RIGHT'. Below that, 'CHOICE' is written in a larger, bold font, with a female symbol integrated into the letter 'C'.

name: Abortion is a Woman's Choice [Woman's Symbol in letter C]
title: ABORTION IS A WOMAN'S CHOICE
subject: abortion, women's rights, pro-choice
description: Circular button with a grid pattern and the slogan 'ABORTION IS A WOMAN'S CHOICE' in black block letters. A female symbol is incorporated into the letter 'C' of 'CHOICE'.

name: Legalize Free-Standing Abortion Clinics ; Choice [Woman's Symbol in letter C]
title: LEGALIZE FREE-STANDING ABORTION CLINICS CHOICE
subject: Abortion Rights, Pro-Choice, Reproductive Freedom
description: A blue and white button advocating for the legalization of free-standing abortion clinics with the word CHOICE featured prominently.

name: Canadian Abortion RIghts Action League ; Choice [Woman's Symbol in letter C]
title: Canadian Abortion Rights Action League CHOICE
subject: abortion rights, pro-choice, Canada

This button was part of a movement in the late 1970s and 1980s to shut down the Rocky Flats nuclear weapons plant in Colorado and repurpose it for peaceful, civilian manufacturing.

name: MLK Day 2017
title: MLK DAY 2017

name: Vote Yes ; Disarmament ; 8 Nov.

subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Nuclear Freeze, Peace Movement
description: A campaign button advocating for a 'Yes' vote on a disarmament initiative or referendum. The date November 8 aligns with the 1983 election day, a year when many local nuclear freeze referendums were held across the United States. The button features a stylized red tower and a ballot marking symbol.

name: I Support Pre-Emptive Peace in Iran!
title: ©2007 RUTH KLEIN buttonbuddha @gmail.com 718-897-4746
subject: button making, contact information, copyright

name: I Support Pre-Emptive Peace in Iran!
title: I Support Pre-Emptive PEACE IN IRAN!
subject: Anti-war, Iran, Peace
description: A round black button with a red border and white text that reads 'I Support Pre-Emptive PEACE IN IRAN!'. The slogan is a play on the term 'pre-emptive war' or 'pre-emptive strike' used during the Bush administration's foreign policy debates regarding the Middle East.

name: Free South Africa!
title: FREE SOUTH AFRICA!
subject: Apartheid, Nelson Mandela, South Africa
description: A political button featuring a portrait of Nelson Mandela with a raised fist and the slogan 'FREE SOUTH AFRICA!'. The button uses the yellow, green, and black color scheme of the African National Congress (ANC).

name: El Mundo Dice No a la Guerra (The World Says No to War) : 15 Feb NY
title: El Mundo Dice No a la Guerra 15 FEB NY
subject: Iraq War, Anti-war, Protest
description: A blue button with a globe graphic and white Spanish text stating 'The World Says No to War' for the global day of anti-war protests held on February 15, 2003, specifically referencing the event in New York City.

name: Want Peace? Get Out Now ; Palestine Afghanistan Iraq
title: Want peace? Get out! Palestine Afghanistan Iraq Now
subject: Anti-war, Middle East, Peace
description: A red and white political button calling for the withdrawal of troops and military intervention from Palestine, Afghanistan, and Iraq, featuring the flags of each country/territory.

name: No War in Iraq ; DallasPeaceCenter.org
title: No War in Iraq
subject: Iraq War, pacifism, protest
description: A yellow button with black text reading 'No War in Iraq' and the website for the Dallas Peace Center.

name: Grandmothers Against the War
title: Grandmothers against THE WAR
subject: Protest, Peace, Anti-war
description: A black button with white and red text that reads "Grandmothers against THE WAR". This slogan is associated with the Grandmothers Against the War movement, which protested the Iraq War.

name: Support the Troops ; Bring Them Home Now!
title: Support The Troops BRING THEM HOME NOW!
subject: anti-war, Iraq War, military withdrawal
description: A circular light blue button with bold black text. 'Support The Troops' is printed in an arch at the top, and 'BRING THEM HOME NOW!' is displayed in large, centered block letters. This slogan was a central message of the anti-war movement during the Iraq War, particularly promoted by organizations like Military Families Speak Out starting in 2003.

name: Nuclear War can Spoil Your Whole Day
title: NUCLEAR WAR can spoil your whole day
subject: Nuclear war, disarmament, satire
description: A circular red button with black text featuring the satirical slogan 'NUCLEAR WAR can spoil your whole day.' This phrase was widely popular during the anti-nuclear proliferation movement and Cold War era of the 1980s.

name: No Cruise Missiles ; Protest and Survive
title: NO CRUISE MISSILES PROTEST and SURVIVE
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Cruise missiles, Peace
description: An anti-nuclear protest button with the text 'NO CRUISE MISSILES' and 'PROTEST and SURVIVE'. It features a blue shield containing a castle, a dove, and an atomic symbol. The 'Protest and Survive' slogan was popularized by E.P. Thompson in 1980 as a response to a government civil defense pamphlet. The castle design indicates this button was likely produced for local protests in Exeter, England.

name: Love the Troops Hate the War
title: VICKI ROVERE War Resisters League
subject: Pacifism, Anti-war, Activism
description: The back of a name tag button for activist Vicki Rovere, showing a sticker with her email address vickirov@nycwhere.com. The front of the button identifies her as being part of the War Resisters League.

name: Love the Troops Hate the War
title: LOVE THE TROOPS HATE THE WAR
subject: Anti-war, Peace movement, Military
description: A pink circular button with black text that reads 'LOVE THE TROOPS HATE THE WAR', a slogan used to distinguish between support for soldiers and opposition to the conflicts in which they serve.

name: Drive Out The Bush Regime!
title: Drive Out the Bush Regime!
subject: Protest, Activism, Environmentalism
description: Square black button featuring a burning Earth with the text "Drive Out the Bush Regime!" in orange and the website "www.worldcantwait.org" in white. This is associated with the "World Can't Wait" movement founded in 2005 to protest the George W. Bush administration.

name: Blockade Diablo
title: BLOCKADE DIABLO
subject: nuclear power, protest, activism
description: A red and white button featuring the words 'BLOCKADE DIABLO' and an image of two hands clasped in front of a nuclear power plant, used during protests against the Diablo Canyon Power Plant.

name: Freeze & Disarm
title: FREEZE & DISARM
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Peace Movement, Cold War
description: This button features a white dove, a symbol of peace, set against a blue background. The words "FREEZE" and "DISARM" are written in white around the edge, separated by an ampersand. It was likely produced during the nuclear freeze movement of the 1980s.

name: Oregon Euromissile Rally ; 22 October 1983
title: OREGON EUROMISSILE RALLY 22 OCTOBER 1983
subject: Euromissile Rally, Peace Movement, Nuclear Disarmament
description: Green and white button for the Oregon Euromissile Rally on October 22, 1983, featuring a peace dove with an olive branch over a map of Europe.

name: End the Occupation ; Support the Intifada
title: END THE OCCUPATION SUPPORT THE INTIFADA
subject: Palestine, Protest, Intifada
description: A circular button featuring the Palestinian flag (black, white, and green horizontal stripes with a red triangle on the left) with the text "END THE OCCUPATION" and "SUPPORT THE INTIFADA."

name: Stop Nuclear War
title: STOP NUCLEAR POWER
subject: anti-nuclear, protest, energy policy
description: A circular pin-back button featuring blue, hand-drawn style lettering on a white background that reads "STOP NUCLEAR POWER". This button reflects the grassroots anti-nuclear protest movements of the 1970s and 1980s.

name: Uranium Leave It in the Ground
title: URANIUM LEAVE IT IN THE GROUND
subject: uranium mining, environmentalism, anti-nuclear movement
description: Circular button featuring a brown mountain silhouette against a yellow background. At the top, "URANIUM" is printed in red arched text. Below the mountains, the phrase "LEAVE IT IN THE GROUND" is printed in white. This button was used by activists protesting against uranium mining, a prominent movement in the late 20th century.

name: No Nukes Now
title: NO NUKES NOW
subject: Nuclear energy, Activism, Environment
description: Round button featuring the phrase 'NO NUKES NOW' in large red capital letters on a dark background. Fine print along the upper edge indicates it was produced by CRC in Santa Cruz, California.

name: No U.S. Missile Defence
title: NO U.S. MISSILE DEFENCE CND

description: A square blue button with the text 'NO U.S. MISSILE DEFENCE' in white stencil font, featuring a graphic of a missile and the Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament (CND) peace symbol and initials.

name: There are Many Roads to Peace Nuclear Weapons is not One
title: There are many roads to peace Nuclear weapons is not one
subject: Peace, Disarmament, Nuclear Protests
description: A blue pinback button featuring a white dove flying over several radiating paths or roads. The text 'There are many roads to peace Nuclear weapons is not one' is printed in white along the top edge.

name: Free the New York 102,624
title: FREE THE NEW YORK 102,624
subject: Prisons, Civil Rights, Black Panthers
description: This button calls for the release of the 102,624 individuals who were processed through the New York City prison system in 1970. It was likely produced in 1971 as part of a campaign by the Black Panther Party to protest mass incarceration and prison conditions in New York.

name: [Image of Hands Releasing Dove]
title: None
subject: Peace, Pacifism, Symbolism
description: Button featuring a black outline of a dove above two cupped hands, encircled by a laurel wreath, with a rainbow-colored chevron at the bottom.

title: Labor, Students Mobilize Our Power For Workers Strikes Against the War!

description: An anti-war button produced by the Internationalist Group featuring a purple fist and text calling for workers' strikes and student mobilization against the war, likely referring to the Iraq War.

title: WORKERS UNITE FOR MAY DAY PEACE, DISARMAMENT & FRIENDSHIP 85
subject: Labor movement, Peace, International Relations
description: A red button featuring white text and a white graphic of a dove-shaped hand holding a map of Australia. The button commemorates May Day 1985 and advocates for worker unity, peace, disarmament, and friendship.

name: End Apartheid Terror! ; Sanctions Against South Africa!
title: END APARTHEID TERROR! SANCTIONS AGAINST SOUTH AFRICA!
subject: Apartheid, Sanctions, South Africa
description: A political button featuring the colors of the African National Congress (black, green, and yellow) with text calling for an end to apartheid and sanctions against South Africa, alongside the United Nations logo.

name: "I Have a Dream" ; Rockland County ; Jan. 15, 1971
title: ROCKLAND COUNTY "I HAVE A DREAM" JAN. 15, 1971
subject: Civil Rights, Martin Luther King Jr., Commemoration
description: A white pinback button with black text that reads 'ROCKLAND COUNTY "I HAVE A DREAM" JAN. 15, 1971'. The button commemorates the birthday of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and refers to his famous 'I Have a Dream' speech.

name: Shut Down [Image of Nuclear Power Plant]
title: SHUT DOWN
subject: nuclear energy, anti-nuclear movement, environmentalism
description: Blue button with red text reading 'SHUT DOWN' superimposed over a white line drawing of a nuclear power plant cooling tower. The button was produced by the Alabama Publishing Co. in Birmingham, Alabama, likely as part of the anti-nuclear power movement of the late 1970s and early 1980s.

name: I'm for Peace
title: I'm for PEACE
subject: Peace, Dove, Anti-war

description: A red pin-back button featuring white serif text that reads 'END ISRAELI OPPRESSION IN PALESTINE'. The button is an example of political protest material from the late 20th century advocating for Palestinian rights.

title: Demand Congress Bring the Troops Home OUT NOW! March on Washington Jan. 27 2007

name: Disarm for Life ; Peace Pledge Union
title: PEACE PLEDGE UNION Disarm for Life

title: MAKE JOBS NOT WAR DUMP BUSH!
subject: Iraq War, Labor, Election
description: A button produced by U.S. Labor Against the War (USLAW) featuring a dove perched on a fist holding a pencil and a wrench, symbolizing the intersection of peace and labor. It advocates for the removal of George W. Bush from office during the 2004 presidential campaign.

name: Support Our Troops ; Bring Them Home
title: SUPPORT OUR TROOPS BRING THEM HOME NOW!

The text 'SUPPORT OUR TROOPS' and 'BRING THEM HOME NOW!' was a common slogan used by anti-war organizations during the Iraq War era.

title: Stop the War Machine MARCH 17
subject: Anti-war, Iraq War, Pentagon
description: This button was produced for the 'Stop the War Machine' march held on March 17, 2007, in Washington, D.C. The event marked the fourth anniversary of the beginning of the Iraq War and included a march to the Pentagon. The graphic depicts three missiles towering over the Pentagon building.

name: Stop The War
title: STOP THE WAR

name: Choose Peace
title: This button, 75+ item wholesale catalog & 100th Monkey $2 Islandia p.o. 1207 Felton CA 95018

name: Globalise Resistance
title: globalise RESISTANCE
subject: Anti-globalization, Activism, Social movements
description: A circular white button featuring a red graphic of a raised fist, a symbol commonly used by activist groups to represent solidarity and resistance. Overlaid on the fist is the text 'globalise RESISTANCE' in black. This button represents Globalise Resistance, a UK-based anti-capitalist and anti-globalization organization that rose to prominence in the early 2000s.

name: Use Alternative Energy

This button was created by Supporters of Silkwood, a group advocating for justice and an investigation into the 1974 death of Karen Silkwood, a union activist and whistleblower at the Kerr-McGee nuclear plant.

The text 'INTERNATIONAL DISARMAMENT MOBILISATION FOR SURVIVAL' is printed in black capital letters around the upper portion of the button. The use of the spelling 'Mobilisation' and the design suggests a likely origin from Australia or the United Kingdom in the late 1970s or early 1980s.

name: Respect and Protect the Black Woman

This button reflects a slogan popularized by the Black Panther Party and the Black Power movement of the late 1960s and 1970s to emphasize the dignity and safety of Black women.

Below this is the website address 'www.enoughoccupation.org'. Encircling the central design are the phrases 'END THE ISRAELI OCCUPATION' and 'JUSTICE FOR THE PALESTINIANS' separated by red dots.

name: Jobs Not Bombs ; U.N. Rally June 12

The red text around the top-left edge reads 'JOBS NOT BOMBS', and the text on the right side reads 'U.N. RALLY JUNE 12', referring to the historic anti-nuclear protest in New York City in 1982.

name: Anti Nuclear Campaign

To the left is the text "RALLY May 1, 1976". The center graphic shows an outline of the continent of Africa containing a halftone image of a person's face behind vertical bars, symbolizing a prisoner.

title: NO NUKES
