title: INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S DAY for DISARMAMENT MAY 24TH
subject: Women's rights, Disarmament, Peace
description: A round button promoting International Women's Day for Disarmament on May 24th. It features a hand-drawn white dove carrying a female symbol against a horizontally split purple and green background.

name: Brooklyn Parents for Peace
title: ©2006 RUTH KLEIN buttonbuddha@gmail.com 718-897-4746
subject: copyright info, Ruth Klein, Button Buddha
description: The back of a button featuring a contact sticker for artist Ruth Klein, dated 2006, with an email and a 718 area code phone number.

name: Brooklyn Parents for Peace
title: Brooklyn Parents For Peace
subject: Peace, Anti-war, Brooklyn
description: Colorful button with peace symbols and rainbows promoting the Brooklyn Parents for Peace organization.

name: [Flag of the Soviet Union on the left and American Flag on the right.]
title: None
subject: Cold War, US-USSR Relations, Peace
description: A button featuring a design that combines the United States and Soviet Union flags with a silhouette of St. Basil's Cathedral in the center. This type of button was commonly produced during the Reagan-Gorbachev summits of the late 1980s.

name: Stop The Euromissiles ; Oct.21-24
title: STOP THE EUROMISSILES OCT. 21-24
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Euromissiles, Peace Movement
description: This button was created to promote and commemorate the international days of protest held from October 21-24, 1983, against the deployment of U.S. Pershing II and cruise missiles in Western Europe. The graphic shows hands held up to block incoming missiles over a map of Europe.

name: Vote Baby Vote
title: VOTE BABY VOTE
subject: Voting Rights, Civil Rights, SNCC
description: A black circular button with white text that reads "VOTE BABY VOTE". This slogan was popularized by civil rights organizations like the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee (SNCC) in the mid-1960s to encourage voter registration and participation in the African American community.

name: Womens Peace Camp ; Greenham Common
title: WOMENS PEACE CAMP GREENHAM COMMON
subject: anti-nuclear, peace, feminism
description: A button for the Greenham Common Women's Peace Camp, established in 1981 to protest the decision of the British government to allow cruise missiles to be based at RAF Greenham Common.

name: Stop U.S. $ For Salvadoran Death Squards
title: STOP U.S.$ FOR SALVADORAN DEATH SQUADS
subject: El Salvador Civil War, US Foreign Policy, Human Rights
description: A grey pin-back button with red text reading "STOP U.S.$ FOR SALVADORAN DEATH SQUADS" superimposed over a tan handprint graphic. The button protests United States financial support for the Salvadoran government during its civil war.

name: Don't Let the Dream Die [Image of Martin Luther King, Jr.]
title: don't let the dream die
subject: Civil Rights, Memorial, Martin Luther King Jr.
description: This button was produced by the Fellowship of Reconciliation following the assassination of Martin Luther King Jr. in 1968. It features a portrait of Dr. King and the text "don't let the dream die" as a call for the continuation of his civil rights work.

name: Paix (Peace)
title: Paix
subject: Peace, Anti-war, Dove
description: Small blue button with a white dove and the French word for peace, 'Paix'.

name: U.S. Hands Off El Salvador
title: U.S. HANDS OFF EL SALVADOR
subject: Anti-interventionism, Foreign Policy, El Salvador
description: This button features red text that reads "U.S. HANDS OFF EL SALVADOR" on a yellow background. The central image includes a red silhouette of the country of El Salvador with a black bird's claw reaching down from above. Buttons like this were produced by anti-war activists in the late 1970s and 1980s to protest U.S. military aid and involvement in the Salvadoran Civil War.

name: El Salvador Vencera (El Salvador Will Win) FMLN (Farabundo Martí National Liberation Front)
title: FMLN EL SALVADOR VENCERA
subject: Salvadoran Civil War, FMLN, Guerrilla Warfare
description: A button supporting the FMLN (Farabundo Mart

name: We're In This Together
title: WE'RE IN THIS TOGETHER
subject: Cold War, US-Soviet Relations, Peace
description: A yellow button featuring the Soviet and American flags juxtaposed, with the text 'WE'RE IN THIS TOGETHER' below. The button promotes cooperation and peace between the two superpowers during the Cold War era.

name: December 1986 Greenham
title: december 1986 Greenham
subject: Anti-nuclear, Feminism, Protest
description: A round yellow button featuring the text 'december 1986 Greenham' in green, with red interlinked female and peace symbols below. The button is bordered by a green chain-link fence pattern, representing the fence around the RAF Greenham Common base where women protested against nuclear weapons.

name: Nuclear-Free Zone
title: NUCLEAR-FREE ZONE
subject: nuclear energy, peace movement, activism
description: A button with a white background and black text reading 'NUCLEAR-FREE ZONE' in a circle around a central image. The image is a black radiation symbol (trefoil) on a yellow circle, covered by a red circle with a slash through it, signifying opposition to nuclear power and weapons.

name: Social Workers for Peace and Nuclear Disarmament
title: SOCIAL WORKERS FOR PEACE AND NUCLEAR DISARMAMENT
subject: peace, nuclear disarmament, social workers
description: A white circular pinback button with blue text and graphics. It features the phrase "SOCIAL WORKERS FOR PEACE AND NUCLEAR DISARMAMENT" and a central graphic showing a peace dove with an olive branch over a bomb.

name: Say No to Radioactive Waste
title: Say NO to Radioactive Waste
subject: Radioactive waste, Nuclear energy, Environmental activism
description: A pin-back button with the message 'Say NO to Radioactive Waste' written in red capital letters on a white background, with a small black and white graphic of a hand giving a thumbs down at the bottom.

name: If You've Seen One Nuclear War You've Seen Them All
title: IF YOU’VE SEEN ONE NUCLEAR WAR YOU’VE SEEN THEM ALL
subject: Anti-Nuclear War, Cold War, Nuclear Weapons Disarmament
description: This button features the text ‘If you’ve seen one nuclear war you’ve seen them all.’ In the center of the button is a graphic illustrating rubble and a pair of cockroaches, a common cultural reference to the idea that only cockroaches would survive a nuclear holocaust.

name: Jews For Nuclear Disarmament ; New Jewish Agenda - Rainbow Sign '83
title: JEWS FOR NUCLEAR DISARMAMENT NEW JEWISH AGENDA • RAINBOW SIGN '83
subject: Nuclear Disarmament, Jewish Activism, Peace Movement
description: A circular green button featuring black text. The center reads 'JEWS FOR NUCLEAR DISARMAMENT'. The bottom edge contains the text 'NEW JEWISH AGENDA • RAINBOW SIGN '83'. The button reflects the involvement of progressive Jewish groups in the anti-nuclear movement of the early 1980s.

name: Free Man Nelson Mandela
title: FREE MAN NELSON MANDELA
subject: Anti-Apartheid, Nelson Mandela, Human Rights
description: A circular button calling for the release of Nelson Mandela from prison. The design features a portrait of Mandela centered over three horizontal stripes of black, green, and gold, which are the colors of the African National Congress (ANC). The text 'FREE MAN' is at the top and 'NELSON MANDELA' is at the bottom.

name: Atomkraft? Nej Tack (Nuclear Power? No Thanks)
title: ATOMKRAFT? NEJ TACK
subject: Nuclear energy, Sweden, Environmentalism
description: A Swedish version of the iconic 'Smiling Sun' button, advocating against nuclear power. The phrase 'Atomkraft? Nej tack' translates to 'Nuclear power? No thanks'. It was a major symbol during the 1980 Swedish nuclear power referendum campaign.

name: Abolish Nukes! Abolish Now!
title: ABOLISH NUKES! Abolition Now! www.abolitionnow.org
subject: Nuclear disarmament, Anti-nuclear movement, Abolition 2000
description: A political button featuring a large sunflower, the symbol of the global anti-nuclear movement Abolition 2000. The text calls for the abolition of nuclear weapons and provides the website URL for the campaign.

name: Farms Not Arms
title: FARMS NOT ARMS
subject: Agriculture, Peace movement, Military spending
description: A button featuring a farm landscape with a red barn, yellow house, and cultivated fields, featuring the slogan 'FARMS NOT ARMS'. The slogan is a call to redirect government funding from military expenditures toward agricultural and social support.

name: Less Fission More Vision
title: less fission MORE VISION
subject: anti-nuclear, environment, energy
description: Yellow button with 'less fission' in black lowercase text and 'MORE VISION' in large, stylized letters filled with a landscape drawing of a sun and fields. It is a protest button from the anti-nuclear movement.

name: Alles im Grünen Bereich? (Is Everything Okay/Under Control?) [Image of Dead Flowers in Front of Power Plant]
title: Alles im GRÜNEN Bereich?
subject: Nuclear power, Environmentalism, Green Party
description: A German political button for The Green Party (Die Grünen) featuring the phrase 'Alles im GRÜNEN Bereich?' (Is everything in the green zone?). The design includes a red 'X' over a nuclear power plant with sunflowers in the foreground, representing the party's anti-nuclear energy platform.

name: No Nukes
title: NO NUKES
subject: Anti-nuclear power, Environment, Sea turtle
description: A circular button with a blue grid background. The button features the slogan 'NO NUKES' in dark blue lettering and a graphic of a leatherback sea turtle.

name: ANC (African National Congress)
title: ANC
subject: Anti-apartheid, South Africa, African National Congress
description: A button featuring the emblem of the African National Congress (ANC), which includes a spear, a shield, a wheel, and the ANC's tricolor flag of black, green, and gold.

name: End the War Now ; Hiroshima Nagasaki Week August 2-9
title: end the war now HIROSHIMA NAGASAKI WEEK august 2-9
subject: Anti-War, Nuclear Weapons, Hiroshima/Nagasaki
description: A red and white circular button with text commemorating Hiroshima Nagasaki Week (August 2-9) and urging an end to the war, likely referencing the Vietnam War era.

name: [Peace Symbol]
title: Peace Symbol
subject: peace, anti-war, protest
description: A circular button featuring a black peace symbol over an orange background.

name: $ for Jobs Not Bombs ; Solidarity Day-Sept. 19
title: $$$$ for JOBS NOT BOMBS Solidarity Day - Sept. 19
subject: Labor, Peace, Protest
description: A red and yellow button from Solidarity Day on September 19, 1981. The button features the slogan 'Jobs Not Bombs' and four dollar signs, advocating for economic investment in employment rather than military spending during a mass protest rally organized by the AFL-CIO.

name: Stop The Profits of Doom
title: STOP THE PROFITS OF DOOM
subject: Anti-nuclear power, Corporate greed, Environmentalism
description: A black pin-back button featuring yellow text that reads 'STOP THE PROFITS OF DOOM' around the perimeter. In the center is a yellow graphic of an atom with a dollar sign ($) inside.

name: Stop It [Words in Shape of Bomb]
title: Stop It
subject: Vietnam War, Anti-war, Protest
description: A circular button featuring stylized blue text that reads 'STOP IT' and is shaped like a falling bomb. The background consists of horizontal red and white stripes reminiscent of the American flag. This design was used as an anti-war protest symbol during the Vietnam War era.

name: I Survived Three Mile Island ; I Think
title: I SURVIVED THREE MILE ISLAND I THINK
subject: Nuclear Power, Three Mile Island, Satire
description: A black button with white text that reads 'I SURVIVED THREE MILE ISLAND I THINK', referring to the nuclear accident at the Three Mile Island Nuclear Generating Station in Pennsylvania on March 28, 1979.

name: For Peace and Disarmament Put Canada First!
title: For Peace and Disarmament Put Canada First!
subject: Peace, Disarmament, Canada
description: A red button with black text and a graphic of a dove. The text reads 'For Peace and Disarmament Put Canada First!' advocating for Canadian neutrality and nuclear disarmament.

name: Stop Nuclear Investments ; Take It To Wall Street ; October 28-29
title: Stop Nuclear Investments TAKE IT TO WALL STREET October 28-29
subject: nuclear energy, anti-nuclear movement, divestment
description: Anti-nuclear protest button from the 'Take It to Wall Street' demonstrations held on October 28-29, 1979, in New York City, following the Three Mile Island accident.

name: Stop the Cruise and Pershing II Missiles ; Seneca Army Depot Oct. 22-24
title: STOP the CRUISE and PERSHING II MISSILES SENECA ARMY DEPOT OCT. 22-24
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Seneca Army Depot, Peace
description: A blue and white button featuring a peace dove, advocating against the deployment of Cruise and Pershing II missiles at the Seneca Army Depot during protests in October 1983.

name: If We Can Risk Nuclear War We can RIsk Disarmament
title: © 1982 BULBUL-DONNELLY/COLT BUTTONS BOX 188 HAMPTON CT 06247
subject: activism, button manufacturing, feminist cartoons
description: The reverse side of a square pinback button produced in 1982 by Bulbul-Donnelly/Colt Buttons, a manufacturer known for social and political activism materials based in Hampton, Connecticut.

name: If We Can Risk Nuclear War We can RIsk Disarmament
title: IF WE CAN RISK NUCLEAR WAR WE CAN RISK DISARMAMENT
subject: nuclear disarmament, peace movement, anti-nuclear
description: Square white button featuring a cartoon illustration of a person unraveling nuclear missiles into a pink knitted banner. The text on the banner reads "IF WE CAN RISK NUCLEAR WAR WE CAN RISK DISARMAMENT".

name: Long Live the Intifada
title: Long Live the Intifada
subject: Palestine, Resistance, Intifada
description: A white button with black text reading "Long Live the Intifada" surrounding a depiction of Handala, a symbol of Palestinian defiance created by cartoonist Naji al-Ali.

name: [Image of Peace Dove Above a Peace Symbol and Below a Cross]
title: Peace Symbol, Cross, and Dove
subject: Peace, Christianity, Anti-war
description: A light blue button featuring a graphic that merges a peace symbol with a cross, with a white dove holding an olive branch in flight in front of it.

name: When You've Seen One Atomic War You've Seen Them All
title: WHEN YOU'VE SEEN ONE ATOMIC WAR YOU'VE SEEN THEM ALL
subject: Anti-nuclear war, peace, disarmament
description: A round pink button with a white swirl pattern and black text that reads 'WHEN YOU'VE SEEN ONE ATOMIC WAR YOU'VE SEEN THEM ALL'. The slogan is a satirical play on a quote attributed to Spiro Agnew regarding city slums.

name: Stop Nuclear Power
title: STOP NUCLEAR POWER

name: Paix (Peace) ; Caravane De La Petition Pour La Paix ; Caravan of the Petition for Peace
title: paix CARAVANE DE LA PÉTITION pour la paix

description: This French button promotes the 'Caravane de la pétition pour la paix' (Peace Petition Caravan). The caravan was an initiative by the French Peace Movement (Mouvement de la Paix) in the early 1980s, specifically around 1982, to gather support for nuclear disarmament during the Euromissiles crisis. The design features a stylized dove holding an olive branch, with its outline also forming the profile of a human face.

name: Punch 9 ; Harold Washington for Chicago
title: PUNCH 9 Harold Washington For-Chicago
subject: Mayoral Election, Chicago Politics, African American History
description: A campaign button for Harold Washington's 1983 Chicago mayoral campaign, featuring the slogan 'Punch 9' which referred to his position on the ballot.

name: Free Palestine
title: FREE PALESTINE
subject: Palestine, Liberation, Activism
description: A political button advocating for Palestinian liberation, featuring a photo of a young person and the colors of the Palestinian flag with the slogan 'FREE PALESTINE'.

name: Sun Power Now!
title: SUN POWER NOW!
subject: Solar Energy, Environmentalism, Renewable Energy
description: A light blue button with a stylized yellow sun and red rays, containing the text "SUN POWER NOW!" advocating for solar energy development.

name: Stop the Arms Race ; 1 in a Million
title: Stop The Arms Race 1 in a MILLION
subject: anti-nuclear, disarmament, peace
description: A purple button with yellow text reading "Stop The Arms Race 1 in a MILLION" and a yellow star with the number 1, commemorating the June 12, 1982 anti-nuclear rally in Central Park, New York City.

name: A Nuclear-Free Europe
title: A NUCLEAR-FREE EUROPE
subject: Nuclear disarmament, Peace movement, Europe

name: Stop the U.S. War from the Middle East to El Salvador ; CISPES (Committee in Solidarity with the People of El Salvador)
title: STOP THE U.S. WAR from the Middle East to El Salvador CISPES

The button features gold and white text on a blue background, calling for an end to U.S. military involvement in both the Middle East (referring to the Gulf War) and El Salvador.

name: Take a Change: Care ' Life & Peace Week, 1981
title: Take a chance: care LIFE & PEACE WEEK, 1981
subject: Peace, Religion, Family
description: A circular yellow button from 1981 featuring brown graphics. It depicts a stylized dove carrying an olive branch over a cross, with silhouettes of a man, woman, and child inside the dove. The text around the edge reads 'Take a chance: care' and 'LIFE & PEACE WEEK, 1981'.

name: Countdown Acid Rain ; Environment Ontario
title: COUNTDOWN ACID RAIN ENVIRONMENT ONTARIO
subject: Environment, Pollution, Acid Rain
description: A rectangular button produced by Environment Ontario to promote the 'Countdown Acid Rain' program, a government initiative launched in 1985 to drastically reduce sulfur dioxide emissions in the province by 1994. The button features a stopwatch graphic with years marked on the dial.

name: Greenpeace
title: GREENPEACE
subject: Environmentalism, Peace, Activism
description: A circular button featuring a white dove carrying an olive branch, set against a concentric rainbow background. The word 'GREENPEACE' is written in white block letters at the bottom.

name: March for a Non-Nuclear World ; APril 26-28, 1980
title: COALITION FOR A NON-NUCLEAR WORLD
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, environmental activism, pacifism
description: The reverse side of a pin-back button produced for the Coalition for a Non-Nuclear World, featuring the organization's address in Washington, D.C. and identifying Joel Deprez as the copyright holder for the design. The coalition was notably active in organizing the 1979 March on Washington against nuclear power and weapons.

name: March for a Non-Nuclear World ; APril 26-28, 1980
title: MARCH FOR A NON-NUCLEAR WORLD APRIL 26-28, 1980
subject: Anti-nuclear, Peace, Environment
description: A green button with gold graphics and text promoting the March for a Non-Nuclear World held in Washington D.C. from April 26-28, 1980. The imagery includes a sun, a windmill, and a dove with an olive branch.

name: Stop Almelo+Grondu
title: STOP ALMELO + GRONAU

description: A pink button with black text reading 'STOP ALMELO + GRONAU' and a graphic of a missile over a stylized nuclear facility. This button expresses opposition to uranium enrichment plants located in Almelo, Netherlands, and Gronau, Germany.

name: Palestine: Let the Intifada Pave the Way for People's War
title: PALESTINE: LET THE INTIFADA PAVE THE WAY FOR PEOPLE'S WAR
party: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
subject: Palestine, Intifada, Resistance
description: A square red button with English and Arabic text calling for the Intifada to lead to a people's war, featuring a militant and Leila Khaled with rifles.

name: End the Arms Race Not The Human Race
title: END THE ARMS RACE NOT THE HUMAN RACE

name: Black by Nature Proud by Choice
title: BLACK by nature PROUD by choice
subject: Black pride, identity, pan-Africanism
description: A circular pinback button with the slogan "BLACK by nature PROUD by choice". The words are color-coded in white, green, yellow, and red against a dark background, reflecting Pan-African colors and the Black Pride movement.

name: Palestine Troops Out Now
title: PALESTINE TROOPS OUT NOW
subject: Palestine, Anti-war, Protest
description: A political protest button calling for the withdrawal of troops from Palestine. The design features a silhouette of a soldier with a rifle and incorporates the colors of the Palestinian flag (red, green, black, and white).

name: No Nukes
title: No Nukes
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, environmentalism, peace movement
description: A round white button with 'No Nukes' printed in green ink above a green line-drawing illustration of a mountain range and pine trees.

title: U.S. CUT DIPLOMATIC TIES WITH SOUTH AFRICA NCSAL

description: A circular green button featuring a black silhouette of the African continent with South Africa highlighted in red. The red text reads 'U.S. CUT DIPLOMATIC TIES WITH SOUTH AFRICA' above 'NCSAL', which likely stands for the Northern California Southern Africa Liberation Committee. This button was produced to advocate for US sanctions and diplomatic isolation of South Africa during the apartheid era.

name: Hiroshima/Nagasaki ; '45 - '81 ; Lest We Forget ; First National H/N Commemoration Aug 5-9
title: Japanese Atomic Bombing by the United States Aug. 1945

name: Hiroshima/Nagasaki ; '45 - '81 ; Lest We Forget ; First National H/N Commemoration Aug 5-9
title: Hiroshima / Nagasaki '45 N H '81 Lest We Forget First National H/N Commemoration Aug 5-9

description: A blue button featuring red 'N' and 'H' letters separated by a yellow lightning bolt. Text commemorates the 1945 atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki and advertises the First National H/N Commemoration event in August 1981.

name: End Israeli Apartheid
title: End Israeli Apartheid
subject: Palestine, Apartheid, Human Rights
description: A white circular button featuring the text 'End Israeli Apartheid' in black bold letters. In the center is an illustration of Handala, a character created by Naji al-Ali that serves as a symbol of Palestinian national identity and resistance.

name: Full Employment Now ; Martin Luter King, Jr. Center for Social Change ; Jobs and Justice ; Jan. 15, 1977
title: Full Employment Now Martin Luther King, Jr. Center for Social Change Jobs and Justice Jan. 15, 1977

name: No Nukes ; Spread the Word! from Hiroshima to Harrisburg
title: from Hiroshima to Harrisburg no nukes SPREAD THE WORD!

name: Free South Africa ; Victory to the ANC & SWAPO (African National Congress, South West African People's Organisation)
title: Free South Africa VICTORY TO ANC & SWAPO

Below is an illustration of two black arms with clenched fists, breaking free from shackles. The bottom text reads 'VICTORY TO ANC & SWAPO' with a smaller line mentioning 'US OUT OF SOUTHERN AFRICA NETWORK'.

name: Arret de Tous Les Essais Nucleaires (Stop All Nuclear Tests) ; Appel Des Cent (Call of the Hundred)

name: Free Palestine
title: Free Palestine
subject: Palestine, Liberation, Activism
description: A white button featuring the Palestinian flag in the center with the words "Free" above it and "Palestine" below it in bold black text.

name: Hell No We Won't Glow
title: HELL NO WE WON'T GLOW
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, environmentalism, activism
description: A round pinback button featuring the slogan 'HELL NO WE WON'T GLOW' in white block letters against a horizontal rainbow background. The phrase is a popular anti-nuclear power protest slogan that gained prominence in the late 1970s. The rim of the button includes contact information for a post office box in Santa Cruz, California.

name: Hands off El Salvador ; No Mas Vietnams (No More Vietnams)
title: HANDS OFF EL SALVADOR NO MÁS VIETNAMS

It includes the slogans 'HANDS OFF EL SALVADOR' and 'NO MÁS VIETNAMS,' referencing the concern that U.S. involvement in El Salvador's civil war would lead to another Vietnam-like conflict.

name: St. John's College H.S. 1983 ; Cadets March for World Peace
title: ST. JOHN'S COLLEGE H.S. 1983 Cadets March for World Peace PEACE

description: A light blue button featuring dark blue text and a graphic of a dove holding a 'PEACE' banner. It commemorates a march for world peace by the cadets of St. John's College High School in 1983.

name: Help Stop The Blood Shed In Palestine ; Enough

name: Let the Sun Shine In ; Support Solar Energy

name: Brews Not Bombs

name: CodePink Women for Peace

name: Create Peace

subject: Peace, Dove, Anti-war
description: A circular button with a white dove carrying an olive branch, set against a background divided into a blue sky and green ground. The words 'Create Peace' are printed in white text at the top and bottom of the button.

name: Stop Nuclear Power! ; Shoreham - June 3rd ; SHAD Alliance (Sound-Hudson Against Atomic Development)
title: STOP NUCLEAR POWER! SHOREHAM · JUNE 3RD SHAD Alliance

name: Free South Africa ; End Apartheid

name: AtomFrit Hav (Atomic Free Sea) ; Greenpeace

name: People Before Profits! ; No Nukes! ; Youth Against War & Fascism
title: PEOPLE BEFORE PROFITS! NO NUKES! YOUTH AGAINST WAR & FASCISM

It features a graphic of an atom with a dollar sign in the center, representing a protest against nuclear power and weapons being prioritized for corporate profit over public safety.

name: El Salvador-Nicaragua ; ¡Vencer o Morir! (Win or Die!)

title: Read My Lips... NO NEW TEXANS
subject: George H.W. Bush, political satire, labor unions
description: A satirical political button from the 1992 presidential campaign, parodying George H.W. Bush's 1988 pledge 'Read my lips: no new taxes' by changing it to 'NO NEW TEXANS.' The button features a union bug from the United Steelworkers of America (USWA) Local 2018.

name: Ben Cardin for Congress
title: Ben Cardin For Congress
subject: Ben Cardin, Congress, Maryland
description: A rectangular campaign button for Ben Cardin's run for the United States House of Representatives, featuring his name in blue and 'For Congress' in red.

name: Zeese ; U.S. SEnate '06 ; "Bring Our Troops Home" ; Peace ; Justice ; Democracy ; Propserity
title: ZEESE U.S. SENATE '06 "BRING OUR TROOPS HOME" PEACE JUSTICE DEMOCRACY PROSPERITY
subject: Iraq War, Anti-war, Election
description: Campaign button for Kevin Zeese's 2006 U.S. Senate run in Maryland, featuring anti-war slogans.

name: Dr. Dean to the White House...STAT!
title: Dr. Dean to the White House .....STAT!
subject: Howard Dean, 2004 Presidential Campaign, Medical Theme
description: A campaign button for Howard Dean's 2004 presidential run, featuring a cartoon illustration of the White House with a speech bubble that uses medical terminology ('STAT') to reference his background as a physician.

name: Sensible Gun Laws, Safe Kids ; May 14, 2000 ; Million Mom March
title: Sensible gun laws, May 14, 2000 Million Mom March safe kids
subject: gun control, Million Mom March, activism
description: A button for the Million Mom March held on May 14, 2000, in Washington, D.C., advocating for sensible gun laws and child safety.

name: Zero Population Growth ; No Vacancy [Image of Earth]
title: NO VACANCY ZERO POPULATION GROWTH
subject: overpopulation, environment, zero population growth
description: A white button with black text and illustration advocating for zero population growth. It features a globe with a "No Vacancy" sign pinned into it, symbolizing the concept that the Earth has reached its carrying capacity.

name: I'm for Yanesh for Ohio State Senate
title: I'M FOR YANESH FOR OHIO STATE SENATE
subject: Ohio, State Senate, campaign
description: Yellow and red campaign button supporting Lawrence Yanesh for the Ohio State Senate.

name: Poor People's Campaign 1968
title: Poor People's Campaign 1968
subject: Civil Rights, Poverty, Economic Justice
description: A button for the 1968 Poor People's Campaign featuring a silhouette of a mother and child. This campaign was a social movement for economic justice for poor people in the United States, organized by Martin Luther King Jr. and the Southern Christian Leadership Conference (SCLC).

name: I Partied with the Kennedys
title: I PARTIED WITH THE KENNEDYS
subject: Kennedy Family, Political Culture, Humor
description: A button featuring the text 'I PARTIED WITH THE KENNEDYS' in black block lettering on a white rectangular background, surrounded by a blue background with wavy white decorative lines. This type of button was popular as political memorabilia or satirical social commentary regarding the prominent Kennedy political dynasty.

name: Vickie Harris ; School Board
title: Vickie Harris SCHOOL BOARD
subject: Education, Election, School Board
description: Blue campaign button for Vickie Harris's run for school board, featuring a check mark logo inside a red box.

name: Gotta Vote [Image of Obama]
title: GOTTA VOTE
subject: Presidential Election, Voting, Barack Obama
description: A campaign button for Barack Obama's 2012 re-election bid, featuring a stylized portrait of Obama with the text 'GOTTA VOTE' and the website URL 'GOTTAVOTE.ORG' on a red, white, and blue background.

name: Gotta Vote [Image of Obama]
title: TIGEREYE DESIGN DEMOCRATIC STUFF
subject: Campaign buttons, Democratic Party, Manufacturing
description: The reverse side of a political button featuring a yellow circular sticker for TigerEye Design and DemocraticStuff.com, a supplier of campaign materials for the Democratic Party.

name: Spire T. Agnew ; Ony Vice President in United States History to Resign Under Duress Oct. 10. 1973
title: SPIRO T. AGNEW ONLY VICE PRESIDENT IN UNITED STATES HISTORY TO RESIGN UNDER DURESS OCT. 10, 1973
subject: resignation, scandal, vice presidency
description: A yellow button featuring a caricature of Vice President Spiro Agnew with text describing him as the only Vice President to resign under duress, following his resignation on October 10, 1973, amid criminal charges.

name: Fran Ryan for Action in Congress
title: FRAN RYAN FOR ACTION IN CONGRESS
subject: Fran Ryan, Congress, Ohio
description: A circular green campaign button with white text reading "FRAN RYAN FOR ACTION IN CONGRESS". Frances "Fran" Ryan was a Democratic politician who served on the Columbus, Ohio City Council and ran for the U.S. House of Representatives in the late 1970s.

name: President '96 ; Bill Weld
title: PRESIDENT '96 BILL WELD
subject: Presidential campaign, Bill Weld, 1996 election
description: A square political campaign button for Bill Weld's potential 1996 presidential run, featuring his portrait and the text "PRESIDENT '96". Weld ultimately chose to run for the U.S. Senate that year instead.

name: Belva Lockwood
title: BELVA LOCKWOOD
subject: Suffrage, Presidential Campaign, Feminism
description: A circular pink button featuring the name 'BELVA LOCKWOOD' in a white sans-serif font. This modern commemorative pin honors the 19th-century lawyer and activist who was the first woman to argue before the U.S. Supreme Court and ran for President in 1884 and 1888.

name: Puerto Rico Libre! ; September 5-7, 1975 ; International Solidarity
title: PUERTO RICO LIBRE ! September 5-7, 1975 INTERNATIONAL SOLIDARITY
subject: Puerto Rican independence, international solidarity, activism
description: A circular button with the flag of Puerto Rico. At the top, in blue text, it reads 'PUERTO RICO LIBRE !'. At the bottom, it reads 'September 5-7, 1975' and 'INTERNATIONAL SOLIDARITY'. This button is likely associated with the International Conference of Solidarity with the Independence of Puerto Rico held in Havana, Cuba, in 1975.

name: Egan for Mayor
title: Egan for Mayor
subject: Election, Chicago, Politics
description: A campaign button from John Egan's 1979 mayoral run in Chicago, Illinois. It features the candidate's name in bold red text with a halftone shadow effect on a white background.

name: Money for Human Needs, not the Pentagon ; Overturn the Reagan Program ; All- Peoples Congress Detroit, Oct. 16-18
title: MONEY FOR HUMAN NEEDS, NOT THE PENTAGON Overturn the Reagan Program ALL-PEOPLES CONGRESS DETROIT, OCT. 16-18
subject: Anti-Reagan, Military Spending, Social Welfare
description: A yellow and black button from the All-Peoples Congress in Detroit, October 16-18, 1981, protesting against President Ronald Reagan's economic and military policies and advocating for increased funding for human needs.

name: Ohioans for a Free Press
title: OHIOANS FOR A FREE PRESS
subject: freedom of press, journalism, first amendment
description: A circular pinback button divided into horizontal stripes of red, white, and blue. The text 'OHIOANS FOR A FREE PRESS' is printed across the three sections.

name: Continue the Reagan Legacy ; Laxalt -88
title: CONTINUE THE REAGAN LEGACY LAXALT '88
subject: Presidential Election, Paul Laxalt, Ronald Reagan
description: Campaign button for Paul Laxalt's 1988 presidential bid, featuring his portrait and the slogan 'Continue the Reagan Legacy'. Laxalt, a former Governor and Senator from Nevada and a close friend of Ronald Reagan, briefly sought the Republican nomination in 1987.

name: Ed Hughes for State Representative
title: ED HUGHES FOR STATE REPRESENTATIVE
subject: Politics, Election, Campaign
description: A red, white, and blue political campaign button for Ed Hughes, a Democrat from Council Bluffs, Iowa, who served in the Iowa House of Representatives from 1983 to 1989. The patriotic design features horizontal stripes and stars.

name: Re-Defeat Bush in 2004
title: Re-Defeat Bush in 2004
subject: Presidential Election, George W. Bush, Anti-Bush
description: A political campaign button from the 2004 United States presidential election. The button features white and red text on a purple background that says 'Re-Defeat Bush in 2004', reflecting a sentiment from critics of George W. Bush regarding the outcome of the 2000 election.

name: Join The MOB ; Mothers Opposing Bush ; www.MOB.org
title: Join the MOB Mothers Opposing Bush www.MOB.org
subject: Anti-Bush, Mothers, Protest
description: A political campaign button for the group Mothers Opposing Bush (MOB), featuring red and black text on a white background and providing a website URL for the organization.

name: Tommy ; Paid for by Thompson for Wisconsin
title: Hollings '84
subject: Ernest Hollings, 1984 Presidential Election, Democratic Party
description: The reverse side of a circular button for Ernest Hollings' 1984 presidential campaign, featuring the Badge-A-Minit manufacturer logo and contact information.

name: Richardson '08
title: Richardson '08
subject: Presidential election, Bill Richardson, Campaign button
description: Circular blue button with a large white letter 'R' followed by 'ichardson '08' in white text, representing Bill Richardson's 2008 presidential campaign.

name: Win with Waxter
title: Win With Waxter
subject: campaign, local election, Baltimore
description: A blue political campaign button for Thomas J. S. Waxter Jr., likely used during one of his successful runs for the Baltimore City Council in the 1960s.

name: John McCain 2000
title: John McCain 2000
subject: Presidential Election, 2000, McCain
description: Red circular campaign button featuring a color photograph of John McCain in the center, with his name and the year 2000 in white text.

name: A President for All America ; Rev Al Sharpton
title: A President for All America REV AL SHARPTON
subject: Presidential Campaign, Al Sharpton, Civil Rights
description: A campaign button for Reverend Al Sharpton's 2004 run for the presidency of the United States. It features a portrait of Sharpton with the slogan 'A President for All America'.

name: Dole for President in '88
title: DOLE for PRESIDENT in '88
subject: Bob Dole, Presidential Campaign, 1988
description: Yellow and maroon campaign button for Bob Dole's 1988 presidential run featuring his portrait.

name: Stop Moyniham ; fight: racism-sexism-imperialism ; Columbus Committee Against Racism
title: Stop Moynihan
subject: racism, sexism, imperialism
description: A yellow and black political button advocating against Daniel Patrick Moynihan, likely during his 1976 campaign for the U.S. Senate. It was produced by the Columbus Committee Against Racism and features slogans against racism, sexism, and imperialism.

name: Nazis Off the Street!
title: NAZIS OFF THE STREETS!
subject: Nazism, Anti-fascism, Protest
description: A red and white pin-back button with the slogan 'NAZIS OFF THE STREETS!', used by anti-fascist protesters in the late 1970s and early 1980s. Slogans like this were common during the rise of the Anti-Nazi League in the UK and during protests against neo-Nazi groups in the United States, such as those in Skokie, Illinois in the late 1970s.

name: Edwards
title: EDWARDS
subject: Politics, Campaign, Election
description: A blue and white political campaign button for John Edwards, most likely from his 2004 or 2008 presidential campaign.

name: Whitfield
title: WHITFIELD
subject: political campaign, US Congress, Kentucky
description: A round campaign button for Ed Whitfield, featuring his name in blue on a white band. The top half is red with seven white stars and the bottom half is blue with six white stars.

name: The Right President 4 America ; Gramm '96
title: THE RIGHT PRESIDENT 4 AMERICA GRAMM '96
subject: Phil Gramm, Republican Party, 1996 Presidential Election
description: A campaign button for Phil Gramm's 1996 presidential campaign featuring a portrait of Gramm with the slogan 'The Right President 4 America' and the year '96.

name: In Memoriam [Image of President McKinley]
title: Buttons made by The Whitehead & Hoag Co.
subject: Union Label, Whitehead & Hoag Co., Manufacturing
description: The reverse side of a celluloid pinback button showing the manufacturing details for The Whitehead & Hoag Co. of Newark, New Jersey, including patent dates from April 14 and July 21, 1896, and the Allied Printing Trades Union Label.

name: Washington State Labor Council ; AFL - CIO
title: WASHINGTON STATE LABOR COUNCIL AFL-CIO
subject: Labor, Unions, Washington
description: A gold-colored lapel pin shaped like the state of Washington, featuring the seal of the Washington State Labor Council, AFL-CIO.

name: Mondale ; Ferraro
title: MONDALE FERRARO
subject: campaign, election, presidential
description: A lapel pin in the shape of the United States map featuring the names Mondale and Ferraro in white lettering on a blue background. This pin is from the 1984 Democratic presidential campaign of Walter Mondale and Geraldine Ferraro.

name: Re-elect the President
title: Re-elect the President
subject: Richard Nixon, 1972 Election, Presidential Campaign
description: A political campaign button supporting Richard Nixon's 1972 re-election campaign, featuring the slogan "Re-elect the President" in red and blue text on a white background. Tiny blue text on the rim reads "CITIZENS FOR THE RE-ELECT OF PRES. NIXON".

name: March for Women's Equality and Women's Lives ; Washington, DC April 9, 1989
title: MARCH FOR WOMEN'S EQUALITY AND WOMEN'S LIVES
subject: women's rights, abortion rights, feminism
description: A circular white button with purple text and purple and gold stripes. It commemorates the March for Women's Equality and Women's Lives held in Washington, D.C. on April 9, 1989, which advocated for reproductive freedom and equality.

name: Our Bodies Our Lives Our Right to Decide ; National Abortion Campaign
title: OUR BODIES OUR LIVES OUR RIGHT TO DECIDE NATIONAL ABORTION CAMPAIGN
subject: Abortion rights, Women's health, Reproductive freedom
description: A pink and black button advocating for abortion rights, produced by the National Abortion Campaign (NAC) in the United Kingdom. It features two female silhouettes holding hands—one of whom is pregnant—surrounded by the slogan 'Our Bodies Our Lives Our Right to Decide'.

name: Anthony Brown ; Democrat for Congress
title: ANTHONY BROWN DEMOCRAT FOR CONGRESS
subject: Maryland, Congress, Democrat
description: A blue rectangular campaign sticker or button for Anthony Brown's run for the U.S. House of Representatives in Maryland. It features the candidate's name, the party affiliation, the office sought, and a white silhouette of the state of Maryland.

name: Hillary ; for U.S. Senate
title: Hillary For U.S. Senate www.hillary2000.org
subject: Hillary Clinton, Senate, Election
description: A circular blue sticker featuring the text 'Hillary For U.S. Senate' in white letters. A red, white, and blue flag graphic is positioned below the text, and the website 'www.hillary2000.org' is curved along the bottom edge.

name: Nuclear Moratorium
title: nuclear moratorium
subject: Anti-nuclear movement, Peace, Environment
description: A round button advocating for a nuclear moratorium, featuring a stylized blue bomb with a white dove silhouette and an atomic symbol, with green text.

name: Nuclear Disarment or World Blow-up ; WSP (Women Strike for Peace)
title: NUCLEAR DISARMAMENT OR WORLD BLOW-UP WSP
subject: nuclear disarmament, peace movement, Women Strike for Peace
description: An orange button featuring a black outline of a mushroom cloud in the center. The text 'NUCLEAR DISARMAMENT OR WORLD BLOW-UP' and the initials 'WSP' (standing for Women Strike for Peace) circle the central image.

name: Shut Them Down [Image of Nuclear Power Plant] ; Mobilization for Survival
title: SHUT THEM DOWN MOBILIZATION FOR SURVIVAL
subject: Nuclear Energy, Anti-nuclear, Protest
description: Anti-nuclear button produced by the Mobilization for Survival coalition featuring cooling tower silhouettes and the text 'Shut Them Down'.

name: Decision 80 NBC Radio News
title: DECISION 80 NBC Radio News
subject: Journalism, Election, Media
description: A promotional button for NBC Radio News' coverage of the 1980 election cycle, featuring the "Decision 80" logo with an American flag motif on a black background.

name: FEA (behind bars with a lock)
title: FEA
subject: Labor Unions, Education, Strikes
description: A button featuring the acronym 'FEA' (Florida Education Association) behind vertical black bars resembling a jail cell. This button was likely produced during the 1968 Florida statewide teacher strike, which was the first such strike in the United States. The bars symbolize the illegality of the strike at the time and the threat of legal repercussions for the educators involved.

name: P.O.W.'S never have a nice day
title: P.O.W.'S NEVER HAVE A NICE DAY
subject: Vietnam War, Prisoners of War, POW/MIA awareness
description: A button featuring a sad face and the text 'P.O.W.'S NEVER HAVE A NICE DAY,' which subverts the popular 'Have a Nice Day' smiley face of the early 1970s to draw attention to the plight of American prisoners of war in Vietnam.
